title: HANDOFF: drive forge-eorh epic Phases 3-7 to completion
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-eorh epic was reopened 2026-05-27 after operator correction: prior 'shipped' close was wrong. Phases 1-2 LANDED + CHECKPOINT 1 (forge-eorh.27) operator-verified; Phases 3-7 still genuinely open (15 children). Next session's sole mission is driving these to completion. No other work in scope.

## EPIC POINTER
forge-eorh — `bd show forge-eorh` for full design; `docs/backend-redesign.md` for original spec.

## RE-SCOPING REQUIRED BEFORE DISPATCH

Two children are PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by Phase 1-2 work. Their contracts must be tightened before launching executors, or chains will waste time re-doing landed work.

### forge-eorh.9 — Unified scanner (PARTIAL: writes already done)
- ALREADY LANDED: `sources` table exists and is populated. The materializer's source-registration path writes `beads:*` and `obs:*` rows; substrate.ts (forge-eorh.67) reads `SELECT path FROM sources WHERE kind='beads' AND source_key='beads:'+projectId` to resolve beads paths.
- STILL TODO: collapse the 3 separate scanners into ONE `UnifiedScanner` — currently `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, and `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts` all run independently. Bead's parity-dual-run + 10min refresh cadence + on-demand refresh API + `parity.scanner` log entry are all still applicable. Drop the 'writes to sources table' acceptance criterion — it's already happening.

### forge-eorh.12 — WsClient grace + client repoint (PARTIAL: server side done)
- ALREADY LANDED: `/api/beads/*` and `/api/substrate/*` route through the SAME `createSubstrateRouter` (since commit 65da0bb). Server contract met.
- STILL TODO: (a) WsClient zero-drop grace timer `UNSUBSCRIBE_GRACE_MS=2000` in `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts` (untouched), (b) rename `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts` → `substrate-api.ts` and repoint client fetch paths from `/api/beads/*` to `/api/substrate/*`, (c) update every importer. Server-side repoint is NOT in scope — it's done.

### forge-eorh.54 — smoke filter (RE-VERIFY before dispatch)
The materializer's `realtimeHintFor()` may have shifted during forge-oxz2 (`specialists:sync_hint` publish work). Read current emit shape against the smoke assertion before treating as pure test-fix.

## NOT SUPERSEDED — DISPATCH AS-IS

- `.10` manual pin support + refresh button/API
- `.11` graph reads from xtrm.sqlite (graph-dao still hits Dolt with pagination per memory `graph-dao-readissues-...`)
- `.13` channel rename `beads:*` → `substrate:*` (oxz2 added `specialists:*` publishes; beads channels untouched)
- `.14` retire /beadboard + delete apps/beadboard (`apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6,35` still imports beadboard routes)
- `.15` fold gitboard.sqlite github_* tables into xtrm.sqlite
- `.16` rename apps/gitboard → apps/xtrm
- `.17` move logs ~/.agent-forge/logs/ → ~/.xtrm/logs/
- `.21–.25` matching test beads (pair with impl beads above)

## SUGGESTED DISPATCH ORDER (serial, each chain ~30-90min)

1. `.9` (re-scoped UnifiedScanner) — foundation for `.10` pins
2. `.13` channel rename — prerequisite for clean `.14`
3. `.12` (re-scoped: WsClient grace + client repoint only)
4. `.11` graph from xtrm.sqlite
5. `.14` beadboard retirement (unblocked by .12+.13)
6. `.15` github fold
7. `.16` dir rename, `.17` log move (cosmetic finale)

Test beads `.21-.25` interleave with their matching impl beads.

## SUCCESS
forge-eorh epic CLOSED legitimately (all 15 remaining children closed or explicitly deferred with notes). No `--force` close.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Re-scope `.9` and `.12` bead descriptions IN PLACE via `bd update --description` BEFORE dispatching their executors. Don't trust the original ACs — they overlap with shipped work.
- Verify `.54` against current `realtimeHintFor()` shape before dispatch.
- Auto-mode discipline still applies: `using-specialists-auto` overlay + `using-specialists-v3` canonical orchestration. Every chain MUST add verbose log lines / structured events so e2e is machine-verifiable (per memory `auto-mode-operator-wants-orchestrator-to-drive-e2e`).
- Drive e2e + smoke testing autonomously after each chain lands.
- This is the ONLY work in scope for that session. Don't pick up unrelated beads.

## SESSION-START RITUAL
1. `bd prime` + `bd memories forge-eorh` (load all prior eorh memories — there are ~25, esp. `forge-eorh-epic-shipped-2026-05-27-with`, `critical-forge-eorh-73-rollback-gate-plan-is`, `post-compact-next-steps-for-backend-redesign-epic`)
2. `bd show forge-eorh` + `bd dep tree forge-eorh` to confirm child status
3. `bv --robot-triage` for current health
4. `specialists list --full` + `sp ps` + `git worktree list` (expect main only; specialists waiting from prior session cleaned)
5. Re-scope `.9` and `.12` per above
6. Start with `.9` chain
notes: 2026-05-27 orchestration note: re-scoped forge-eorh.9 and forge-eorh.12 in place before dispatch. Dependency graph currently requires forge-eorh.11 -> forge-eorh.22 -> forge-eorh.12 -> forge-eorh.13, so the original handoff's suggested .13-before-.12 order is stale. Do not force-close or override deps; follow graph unless later evidence proves the .22 -> .12 dependency is wrong.

title: Fix beads-adapter.normalizeIssue conflating closed with deleted_at
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts:157:
  deleted_at: issue.status === 'closed' ? normalizeText(issue.closed_at ?? issue.updated_at) : null,

This sets deleted_at to closed_at whenever an issue is CLOSED. That's wrong — closed and deleted are different concepts:
- state='closed' + deleted_at=null → issue is closed but still visible
- state='deleted' + deleted_at=<ts> → issue is tombstoned, hide from queries

After forge-bov4 added 'WHERE (deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at = "")' to substrate.ts queryIssues, ALL closed issues disappear from the API because their deleted_at is non-null. Evidence: gitboard bd stats shows 547 total / 40 open / 507 closed; substrate API returns only 40 (just the open ones).

## SUCCESS
- normalizeIssue sets deleted_at to null (or empty) for ALL non-tombstoned issues, regardless of state
- Only tombstoneMissing (called from writeFull resync only) sets deleted_at on rows that disappeared from the snapshot
- Substrate API returns ALL non-tombstoned issues including closed ones
- gitboard substrate count ≈ 547 (matches bd stats total)

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts line 157 — one-line change
- After deploy: wipe substrate_issues + reset materialization_state for beads:* so eager bootstrap repopulates without the bug

## CONSTRAINTS
- Don't touch tombstoneMissing logic (correctly tombstones missing issues)
- 14/14 materializer tests stay green
- Snapshot-diff cursor still works (no schema change)

## VALIDATION
- bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/ → 14/14 pass
- After wipe + restart + materialize: substrate gitboard count ≈ 547
- Sample bd-closed issue (e.g. forge-eorh.73) shows state='closed' AND deleted_at=null in substrate_issues table
notes:
Fixed normalizeIssue deleted_at bug: closed issues now materialize with deleted_at=null; tombstoneMissing remains sole tombstone writer.
### Specialist Output — executor (job 3e17d8) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Verification

- `bd close forge-115m --reason="done"` done

## Risks

## Follow-ups

## Beads

title: Restore dashboard fidelity: rollback gate + complete substrate before re-flip
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-eorh.60 gate flip (GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1), prod dashboard regressed vs the legacy attach-pool path on TWO dimensions:

1. **Fidelity loss**: substrate_issues schema is 8 columns (repo_slug, issue_id, title, body, state, deleted_at, created_at, updated_at). apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts queryIssues hardcodes priority=2, issue_type='task', owner=null, labels=[], related_ids=[] when returning BeadIssue. Dashboard feed loses all type/priority palette (everything renders as P2 task).

2. **Completeness loss**: materializer reads Dolt via doltClient.getIssues({limit: 1000}). Projects with > 1000 issues silently truncate. Verified: substrate has exactly 1001 rows for source 8ee0c958 and 1000 rows for 6ca3e930 — exact-cap signatures.

3. **Cross-project tombstone wipe** (forge-eorh.70 + follow-on): fixed but only after substrate corruption. Required wipe + re-materialize to recover.

The legacy attach-pool path (used pre-flip) reads Dolt directly with full BeadIssue contract and no app-side LIMIT. Prod was correct on legacy; substrate path regressed it.

## SUCCESS
Dashboard `/gitboard` shows:
- Full feed with type+priority colors (bug red, feature blue, epic purple, task muted; P0-P4 rail)
- Complete issue lists for ALL projects regardless of total count
- Memories + interactions populated per project
- Specialists in-flight reflecting real-time sp activity
- Zero regressions vs the legacy attach-pool baseline

## SCOPE & PLAN
Two phases:

### Phase 1 — IMMEDIATE: rollback gate (this bead's first action)
- rm ~/.config/systemd/user/gitboard.service.d/gate.conf
- systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user restart gitboard.service
- Verify /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns priority + issue_type real values via legacy path
- Dashboard restored to pre-flip behavior within 1 minute

### Phase 2 — FORWARD: complete substrate, then re-flip
Dependencies must land in this order:
1. forge-eorh.71 (P1) — extend substrate_issues schema with priority, issue_type, owner, labels, related_ids, parent_id, closed_at, close_reason, notes; update beads-adapter.normalizeIssue + writeIssues; update substrate.ts queryIssues to return real fields.
2. forge-eorh.72 (P2) — paginate Dolt getIssues in beads-snapshot-source (offset-loop until count < page) to defeat the 1000-limit truncation.
3. forge-eorh.67 (P2) — implement readMemories + readInteractions in substrate.ts (currently stubs returning []).
4. Re-run 5+ min dev/staging verification ritual with GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 + XDG_PROJECTS_DIR=/home/dawid. Expected per-project counts must match Dolt direct query counts; sample issue must have priority/type matching Dolt.
5. Flip gate ON in prod ONLY when staging proves parity. Wipe substrate + reset materialization_state for beads:* so eager bootstrap repopulates with full schema.

## NON-GOALS
- Re-enabling parity harnesses (still gated by GITBOARD_ENABLE_PARITY=1).
- Rewriting the materializer architecture.
- Materializing memories/interactions to substrate tables (separate epic if/when needed; .67 lands jsonl-fallback short-term).

## CONSTRAINTS
- During Phase 1 rollback, leave commits 4cd1dcc..ee14f45 (substrate name fix + epoch.bump + cache TTL + tombstone fix) in main — they fix real bugs that would also bite the legacy path if/when we re-flip.
- Memory cap 2G systemd drop-in stays put.
- forge-eorh.62 observability instrumentation (duration_ms, rows_written, materializer.error) stays — useful for staging verification.

## VALIDATION (whole bead done)
- [ ] Phase 1: prod RSS recovers to ~110M on legacy path; dashboard shows real colors + full lists for all projects.
- [ ] Phase 2: .71 + .72 + .67 closed; staging probe shows substrate per-project counts == Dolt counts, issue palette varies in feed.
- [ ] Re-flip gate; prod stable 5+ min post-flip with same parity as legacy.

## REFERENCES
- forge-eorh.48/.49/.55/.58/.60/.61/.62/.64/.66/.69/.70 — the materializer-path chain
- Memory: tombstoneMissing-cross-project-wipe-must-scope-by-repo-slug
- Memory: specialists-route-cache-needs-epoch-bump-from-materializer
- Memory: beads-adapter-bind-coerce
- Memory: materializer-source-adapter-pattern-adapter-cursor-must-retu
notes: Operator reframe 2026-05-26 14:35: the substrate path IS the planned destination (P6/.14 explicitly retires apps/beadboard backend); .73's 'regression vs legacy' framing was anxious. There is no rollback target. Just execute .71 → .72 → .67 forward; dashboard fidelity surfaces incrementally as chains land. .73 stays open as the umbrella tracker for the forward fix; close when .71/.72/.67 are merged and staging verifies palette + counts.
close_reason:
Phase 2 complete: .71 (schema), .72 (pagination), .67 (memories/interactions) all merged + pushed. E2E verified on prod (tailnet 100.113.49.52:3030): terminalbeta now 4 types/4 priorities, gitboard 5 types/5 priorities (was uniform p=2 task before .71); specialists 2264 issues, xtrm-tools 1253 (was capped at 1000 before .72); /api/beads/projects/<id>/interactions returns 390 records for gitboard with working issue_id filter (was [] before .67); /api/beads/projects/<id>/memories returns [] because no project has knowledge.jsonl, code-path verified correct.

Phase 1 (rollback gate) abandoned: false premise. No legacy attach-pool code remains — createBeadsRouter is a 7-line alias to createSubstrateRouter (retired in 65da0bb forge-eorh.8). Gate stayed ON throughout; never was the regression cause.

Commits landed this session: dd5b5c6 (.71), e79c78a (.72), 65fb59f (.67), 729dcbe (.67 symlink fix).

Follow-ups filed: forge-y546 (internal /schema endpoint loopback-gated but service binds tailnet-only — unreachable from outside).

title: BeadsAdapter.tombstoneMissing scans all repo_slugs — wipes open issues cross-project
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts:76-83 tombstoneMissing:

  private tombstoneMissing(db, rows) {
    const keys = new Set(rows.map((row) => \`\${row.repo_slug}::\${row.issue_id}\`));
    const active = db.query("SELECT repo_slug, issue_id FROM substrate_issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL").all() as Array<...>;
    const stmt = db.query("UPDATE substrate_issues SET deleted_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, state = 'deleted' WHERE repo_slug = ? AND issue_id = ?");
    for (const row of active) {
      if (!keys.has(\`\${row.repo_slug}::\${row.issue_id}\`)) stmt.run(row.repo_slug, row.issue_id);
    }
  }

The SELECT has no repo_slug filter. So when project A's materializer.runOnce fires, tombstoneMissing scans EVERY active issue across all projects, builds keys from project A's rows only, and tombstones every other project's open issue as 'deleted'.

Cross-project wipe in action: with 19 obs/beads projects materializing in any order, each cycle wipes the others.

## EVIDENCE (verified 2026-05-26)
- bd ready on gitboard: 11 ready, 55 total open
- Direct Dolt query (port 3308, db=dolt) for gitboard: 46 open + 487 closed = 533 issues
- substrate_issues for repo_slug='4c37df93-...' (gitboard): 487 closed + 46 DELETED (0 open) — the 46 opens were tombstoned by another source's run
- Across all repo_slugs: 4107 closed, 319 deleted, 7 open (cross-project wipe)
- Dashboard shows 0 open beads for all 19 projects

## SUCCESS
After fix, materializing one project does NOT mutate other projects' rows. /api/beads/projects/<id>/stats returns the real open/in_progress/blocked counts. Dashboard shows beads for all projects.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts (tombstoneMissing)

## CONSTRAINTS
- The fix: scope the active-rows SELECT to the projectId of the snapshot being processed. Pass projectId in (or derive from rows[0]?.repo_slug if rows is non-empty; if rows is empty, derive from this.options.projectId stored on the adapter instance).
- Cleanest: tombstoneMissing accepts `projectId` parameter and queries WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND repo_slug = ?
- Also: write() should call tombstoneMissing with this.options.projectId, not infer from snapshot (which could be empty).
- After fix: a one-shot repair migration may be needed to flip 'deleted' rows back to 'open' for issues that still exist in Dolt. Or: just rebuild substrate from scratch by deleting the rows and letting the next materializer cycle repopulate.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] tombstoneMissing SELECT includes WHERE repo_slug = ?
- [ ] Unit test: two BeadsAdapter instances with different projectIds running write() back-to-back do not tombstone each other's rows
- [ ] After deploy + repair, /api/beads/projects/<id>/stats returns open > 0 for gitboard, terminalbeta, darth-feedor
- [ ] Dashboard sidebar shows open bead counts > 0

## OUTPUT
Diff + repair plan (likely just truncate substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies; materializer cycles will rebuild fast).

## REFERENCE
This bug was masked until forge-eorh.60 gate flip moved prod from attach-pool to substrate. The .48 chain introduced it; the .58 obs schema fix exposed obs cross-project safety but didn't catch beads cross-project corruption.
close_reason: tombstoneMissing scoped to this.options.projectId; 17/17 tests pass

title: P0: Memory leak in xtrm.sqlite materializer path causes OOM on prod boot
description:
## PROBLEM

After restarting gitboard.service on the autonomous-run main HEAD (bec6493), the service climbs to OOM in <4 min and gets killed by systemd OOM killer. Two consecutive boots confirmed:

- Boot 1 (22:53:02): killed at 22:56:51, peak 23.8G memory, 4.8G swap.
- Boot 2 (22:56:58): grew from 600M at T+3min to 11.2G at T+4min, peak 21.2G, 3.5G swap. All routes timed out at that point.

Prior prod (commit 33543b2) ran for 1 day 13 hours at 824M peak. Service is now stopped (`systemctl --user stop gitboard`) to prevent further OOM cycles.

## DIAGNOSTIC EVIDENCE

- /health, /api/console/graph, /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight all timeout once memory climbs past ~10G.
- /api/substrate/projects returned 19 projects correctly when memory was ~600M (T+3min).
- /api/internal/parity/observability AND /api/internal/parity/beads both timed out (suspect — these run synchronous comparisons on a timer).
- /api/internal/verify-runtime timed out.
- The materializer + parity harnesses + observability event spine all run on boot. Any of them could be the leak source.

## LIKELY CULPRITS (in order of suspicion)
1. **Beads parity harness** (forge-eorh.8 / .45 restitch) — runs on timer, compares ALL substrate_issues against ALL JSONL/Dolt project reads. With 19 projects + churning issues, could retain large diffs.
2. **Observability parity harness** (forge-eorh.4) — same pattern, runs on timer.
3. **Materializer post-commit WS publish** (forge-eorh.5 mapping) — emits on every cycle; ring buffer per channel is 500; with 5+ channels and frequent emits, could hold significant state.
4. **Observability event spine** (forge-eorh.26) — every operation now wraps in withSpan; emit() calls into the bus; the JSONL log files could grow rapidly if every materializer cycle emits many spans.
5. **GitHub backfill** (existing code, not from this session) — runs on boot, 300 events; could be the trigger that combined with above causes OOM.

## ROLLBACK PROCEDURE

Operator-only command to revert prod to last known good (33543b2) without losing main history:

```bash
cd /home/dawid/dev/gitboard
git tag runtime/pre-rollback-2026-05-24 main
git stash push -u -m "runtime-rollback-noise" || true
git checkout 33543b2 -- apps/ src/ docs/  # restore working-tree only
systemctl --user start gitboard
# Verify: should stay under 1G, /health 200
```

The new code stays on `main` branch; only the runtime working tree reverts. To restore the autonomous-run code later (after fix): `git checkout main -- .`

## SUCCESS

Service runs continuously for ≥30 minutes under operator activity without exceeding 2G memory. Root cause identified and fixed.

## SCOPE

Investigation: profile each new component in isolation. Add memory bound + back-pressure to whichever component leaks.

## NON_GOALS

- Don't re-attempt CHECKPOINT 1 validation against the current main HEAD until the leak is fixed.
- Don't merge any P3+ work until this is resolved.

## CONSTRAINTS

- This bead BLOCKS the entire epic going forward. CHECKPOINT 1 (forge-eorh.27) cannot pass until this is fixed.
- Add a systemd MemoryLimit (e.g. 2G) to the unit so future regressions don't take down the host.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Service boots and runs for ≥30min under 2G memory.
- [ ] /api/internal/verify-runtime returns within 5s.
- [ ] /api/internal/parity/observability and /api/internal/parity/beads return within 5s.
- [ ] Full operator validation in CHECKPOINT 1 passes.

## OUTPUT

Root cause + fix + smoke proof.

## REFERENCES

- Memory profile output from prod boot: `journalctl --user -u gitboard --since "30 minutes ago"`
- Pre-session memory baseline: 824M peak over 1 day 13 hours.
close_reason: Mitigated via GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 env gate (default OFF). Prod restored to 33543b2 baseline at 59-134M memory. Root causes saved in bd memory.

title: CHECKPOINT 1: Operator-required e2e validation gate (after P0+P1+P2 impl)
description:
## PROBLEM
After P0 (schema + materializer core + observability spine), P1 (observability adapter + parity + specialists flip), and P2 (Beads adapter + substrate API) land — roughly 50% of the board by code volume and 100% of the autonomous-feasible work — the next step requires operator presence to validate:
1. The Specialists panel updates within 2s of a real `sp` job completion (Playwright/manual).
2. The issues view renders identically pre-/post-flip against a real Beads project (visual check).
3. The Beads parity harness shows zero diff against actual operator activity (10 minutes of live use).
4. The /api/internal/verify-runtime endpoint reports zero errors and acceptable p95 lag.

Autonomous orchestration cannot eyeball UI rendering or generate the live operator-driven activity the parity gate needs. Stopping here is correct.

## SUCCESS
Operator confirms all four checks above pass. They either:
- Approve continuing to P3-P7 (file follow-up beads, unblock the next phase), OR
- File regression beads against any e2e failure.

## SCOPE
- Operator runs `npm run dev` (or whatever the dev command is) against the latest commit on this branch.
- Operator opens dashboard, observes Specialists + issues views.
- Operator runs `sp` jobs in the integrated terminal; observes WS-driven updates.
- Operator hits `/api/internal/verify-runtime?since=...` and inspects output.
- Operator decides go/no-go for P3+.

## NON_GOALS
- No code changes in this bead.
- No automated test additions.
- This bead exists purely as a synchronization point.

## CONSTRAINTS
- This bead BLOCKS forge-eorh.11 (graph flip), forge-eorh.12 (client repoint), forge-eorh.9 (unified scanner), and everything downstream. No more autonomous work past this point without operator approval.
- Autonomous run must close out this bead's evidence checklist (e2e + smoke results from completed phases) before claiming the checkpoint is ready to validate.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Operator launches dashboard against current main; loads cleanly.
- [ ] Operator runs at least one `sp run <role>` job; Specialists panel updates within 2s without manual refresh.
- [ ] Operator navigates the issues view; matches expectations.
- [ ] Operator runs `curl localhost:3030/api/internal/verify-runtime?since=$(date -uIminutes -d '15 min ago')` and confirms 0 errors + p95 thresholds met.
- [ ] Operator either closes this bead with `--reason "go for P3+"` (unblocking the next phase) or files regression beads.

## OUTPUT
Operator's verdict + any regression beads filed + decision to advance.
notes:
## POST-COMPACT POINTER

Full next-steps procedure saved as bd memory key: `post-compact-next-steps-for-backend-redesign-epic`. Run `bd recall post-compact-next-steps-for-backend-redesign-epic` after compaction.

Critical order: fix .48 → fix .49 → fix .46 → patch beads-parity efficiency → dev/staging boot ≥5min memory watch → only then flip GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 in systemd. Never re-enable parity by default. Never remove the gate.

title: P0: Feed/list flicker from destructive refresh and unstable row identity
description:
Scope: isolate and fix the disappearing/reappearing content symptom in dashboard feeds/lists, especially beads list/feed surfaces.

Observed symptom:
- Feed/list content disappears during updates, then reappears after refresh completes. Operator sees constant flicker on any beads-list update.

Working hypothesis:
- Frontend treats realtime/background refresh as destructive reload: clears visible rows or flips loading state before replacement data arrives.
- Row/component identity may be unstable because keys include updatedAt/status/epoch/index instead of stable bead/job IDs.
- Whole-list replacement and coarse cache invalidation cause remounts, scroll/selection loss, and per-row polling storms.

Investigation scope:
- Beads list/feed components and hooks/stores.
- Dashboard resource invalidation semantics for beads/list data.
- Row keys in feed/list render paths.
- Any loading/empty-state branches that render over existing data during background refresh.
- Logs showing bursts of /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=... cache misses after list updates.

Acceptance criteria:
- Background refresh preserves prior visible rows until replacement data is ready.
- List rows use stable keys based on immutable IDs only.
- Realtime sync hints patch/coalesce instead of clear/reload where possible.
- Loading state distinguishes first load from background refresh.
- Scroll position, selection, expanded state, and per-row specialist chip state do not reset during ordinary updates.
- Add targeted tests for preserve-visible-data behavior and stable row identity; avoid broad unsafe React hook suites on VPS.
notes: Graph-load fix deployed: dashboard resource invalidation now marks cached data stale instead of deleting it, stale-empty retry no longer sends refresh=true, graph cache TTL increased to 60s, graph ignores unscoped beads sync hints and no longer refetches on every specialists:activity event. Backend now reuses cached project scans for project-scoped refresh and caches final fresh graph snapshots for 10s; expired issue cache returns stale existing graph while refreshing. Prod check after redeploy: graph requests for gitboard were ~0.76s stale cold, ~0.30s fresh warm, then ~0.002s final graph snapshot cache hit.
close_reason: Done: non-destructive feed refresh, stable row identity, project identity matching, graph source-health/fallback caching, specialist chip preservation, production telemetry, docs/backend.md, and Tailscale redeploy verified.

title: Explorer: diagnose observability registry attach-limit coverage gap
description:
READ_ONLY explorer task for P0 bug forge-x6o4.

Goal: map exactly how the Gitboard observability registry discovers and reads specialist observability DBs, why startup can log "attach failed (too many attached databases - max 10)", and what implementation options avoid dropping DB coverage.

Scope/files to inspect:
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/observability.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts
- related observability registry/config tests under apps/gitboard/tests/

Questions to answer:
1. Where are DBs discovered, attached, pooled, and skipped?
2. Which APIs/UI surfaces become partial or incorrect when a DB is skipped?
3. Best fix strategy: attach/detach per query, separate connection fan-out, bounded shards, or explicit partial coverage reporting?
4. What regression tests should prove >10 discovered DBs are represented or explicitly reported unavailable?

Constraints:
- READ_ONLY: do not edit files.
- Do not run broad React hook suites.
- Prefer static inspection and targeted existing observability tests only if needed.

Deliverable: concise architecture map, root cause, recommended fix plan, test plan, and risk notes.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job 6f3580) [WAITING — more output may follow]

I have examined the complete observability registry architecture, attach pool, and DAO layer. Let me compile the findings.

## Summary

Root cause: `attach-pool.ts` sets `DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 12` but SQLite's compile-time limit is typically 10 (`SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED`). When the 11th DB attempts ATTACH, SQLite throws "too many attached databases - max 10", the pool logs a skip, and marks that DB as permanently dead in the module-scoped cache. The pool's LRU eviction never kicks in because it only activates when pool count reaches 12, not SQLite's actual limit of 10. Result: specialist observability DBs beyond the 10th are silently omitted from all `/api/specialists/*` and `/api/observability/*` queries.

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              Discovery Layer                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ config.ts: getObservabilityConfig()                                          │
│   └─ Reads OBSERVABILITY_ROOTS env / ~/.config/gitboard/observability.json  │
│   └─ Falls back to DEFAULT_ROOTS = ["~/dev/*", "~/projects/*"]              │
│   └─ Expands globs → returns { roots: string[] }                             │
│                                                                               │
│ registry.ts: listRepos()                                                     │
│   └─ Scans each root for .specialists/db/observability.db,                  │
│      .specialists/observability.db, observability.db                        │
│   └─ Returns RepoEntry[] { repoSlug, repoPath, dbPath, mtimeMs }            │
│   └─ Caches for 10 seconds                                                   │
│   └─ Assigns stable slugs (dedup by hash if collision)                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              Attach Pool Layer                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ attach-pool.ts: createAttachPool(entries, options)                          │
│   └─ Creates in-memory SQLite :memory: database                             │
│   └─ warmAttachPool() iterates entries:                                     │
│       ├─ Skip if already attached (LRU touch)                               │
│       ├─ Skip if in moduleDead cache (schema failure, attach error)         │
│       ├─ ensureCapacity(): if attached.size >= maxAttached                   │
│       │     └─ Detach oldest LRU entry, make room                            │
│       ├─ attachRepo(entry):                                                  │
│       │     ├─ Probe DB (readonly), check schema guard                      │
│       │     ├─ ATTACH DATABASE 'path' AS repo_{slug}_{counter}               │
│       │     └─ If error: markDead(moduleDead cache) + log skip               │
│       └─ Yield to event loop every 5 attachments                            │
│                                                                               │
│   └─ withAttached(fn): invoke callback with (db, attached[])                 │
│      └─ attached = [{alias, slug}, ...] for all successfully attached DBs   │
│                                                                               │
│   ❌ BUG: DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 12 exceeds SQLite default limit 10          │
│   ❌ BUG: LRU eviction triggers at pool size 12, not SQLite limit 10         │
│   ❌ BUG: Module-scoped dead cache silently omits repos from results          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                DAO Layer                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ dao.ts: createObservabilityDao(pool) → SpecialistsDao                        │
│   └─ jobsByBead(beadId) → SELECT across all attached repos                   │
│   └─ inFlightJobs() → SELECT WHERE status IN ('starting','running','waiting')│
│   └─ recentJobs(limit) → SELECT WHERE status IN ('done','error','cancelled') │
│   └─ chainById(chainId), epicById(epicId)                                    │
│   └─ All queries use UNION-like flatMap over attached repos                  │
│   └─ Column probing: hasLastOutputColumn, hasMetricsTable, hasEventsTable   │
│                                                                               │
│ metrics-dao.ts: createMetricsDao(pool) → MetricsDao                          │
│   └─ summary(range: '7d' | '30d' | 'all')                                   │
│   └─ Aggregates token counts, hit rates, averages, reliability, slow jobs   │
│   └─ Uses hasSpecialistJobMetrics() probe per-repo                           │
│   └─ Silent empty result for repos missing metrics table                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              API Routes                                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ specialists.ts: createSpecialistsRouter(dao?, options?)                      │
│   GET /jobs?bead_id=...                                                       │
│   GET /jobs/in-flight?limit=N                                                 │
│   GET /chains/:chain_id                                                       │
│   └─ Returns: { freshness: "fresh"|"stale", source_health, jobs[] }         │
│   └─ Caches by epoch hash (repoSlug:epoch)                                   │
│   └─ No reporting of attached vs skipped repos                               │
│                                                                               │
│ observability.ts: createObservabilityRouter(dao?)                            │
│   GET /summary?range=7d|30d|all                                               │
│   └─ Returns: ObservabilitySummary                                           │
│   └─ No reporting of attached vs skipped repos                               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           Epoch / Watcher Layer                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ epoch.ts: get(repoSlug), bump(repoSlug), onBump(listener)                   │
│   └─ Per-repo epoch counter for cache invalidation                          │
│                                                                               │
│ watcher.ts: createObservabilityWatcher(entries)                              │
│   └─ FS watch on each dbPath's parent dir + file                            │
│   └─ Debounced bump(repoSlug) on mtime change                                │
│   └─ API subscribes via onBump to publish WS sync hints                      │
│   ❌ BUG: Entries passed at startup never reconsidered                       │
│      If DB is marked dead, watcher still watches it but no data flows        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Root Cause Analysis

**The Bug: `DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 12` vs SQLite's `SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED = 10`**

```typescript
// attach-pool.ts:20
const DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 12;  // ❌ Exceeds SQLite default compile limit
```

SQLite has a compile-time constant `SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED` (default 10). Attempting to ATTACH an 11th database throws: `"too many attached databases - max 10"`.

**Failure Sequence:**

1. Registry discovers 15 repos → `listRepos()` returns 15 entries
2. `createAttachPool(15)` starts warm
3. First 10 DBs attach successfully (attached.size = 10)
4. On 11th DB: `ensureCapacity()` returns `true` (10 < 12), `attachRepo()` calls `ATTACH DATABASE`
5. SQLite throws error `"too many attached databases - max 10"`
6. Error caught, `markDead()` logs: `Skip observability db /path/to/db: attach failed (too many attached databases - max 10)`
7. DB is **permanently** added to `moduleDead` Map (process lifetime)
8. Repeat for 12th-15th DBs
9. Result: 10 repos attached, 5 silently skipped forever

**Why LRU Eviction Doesn't Help Here:**

LRU eviction only triggers when `attached.size >= maxAttached` (12). It never reaches 12 because SQLite blocks at 10. The pool never gets a chance to cycle DBs through.

**Why Module-Scoped Dead Cache is Dangerous:**

```typescript
// attach-pool.ts:24-32
const moduleDead = new Map<string, { reason: string }>();
```

- Shared across **all pool instances** in the process
- Never cleared (except manual restart)
- Failure modes are "structural" (schema errors) but the **attach limit is transient**
- If you had 20 DBs, first 10 attach, next 10 marked dead forever
- Restart API → same 10 DBs attach, same 10 marked dead

## API Surfaces Affected

| Endpoint | What's Missing | User Impact |
|----------|----------------|-------------|
| `GET /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=...` | Jobs from skipped repos | Missing specialist job rows in UI |
| `GET /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` | In-flight jobs from skipped repos | Dashboard shows partial specialist activity |
| `GET /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id` | Chain links from skipped repos | Broken chain views (missing executor or reviewer) |
| `GET /api/observability/summary` | Metrics from skipped repos | Token counts, runtime averages, reliability stats undercount |
| All APIs | No indication of partial coverage | `source_health.status` = "fresh" even when repos are skipped |

**Critical: No Coverage Reporting**

```typescript
// specialists.ts:60-61
return c.json({ ...cached.value, freshness: "fresh", source_health: makeSourceHealth("specialists", "fresh") });
```

The API responses never indicate:
- Which repos are attached
- Which repos were skipped
- Why they were skipped

Users see "fresh" status and assume complete coverage while data is silently incomplete.

## Fix Strategy Options

### Option 1: Immediate Fix — Lower Pool Limit to Match SQLite

**Change:**
```typescript
// attach-pool.ts
const DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 8;  // <= SQLite SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED (conservative)
```

**Pros:** Quickest fix, no architecture change, preserves LRU eviction behavior  
**Cons:** Still limited to N repos; doesn't scale to 20+ repos; requires config if user has more

**Recommended Configuration:**
```typescript
function getMaxAttached(): number {
  // Allow override via environment
  const env = process.env.OBSERVABILITY_MAX_ATTACHED;
  if (env) return Math.min(Number(env), 8);  // Cap at safe default
  return 8;  // Conservative default
}
```

---

### Option 2: Attach/Detach Per Query (Recommended for Correctness)

**Pattern:**
```typescript
function withAttachedPerQuery<T>(entries: RepoEntry[], fn: (db, attached) => T): T {
  const db = new Database(":memory:");
  try {
    for (const entry of entries.slice(0, MAX_PER_BATCH)) {
      db.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE '${escapeSql(entry.dbPath)}' AS ${alias(entry)}`);
    }
    return fn(db, entries.map(toRef));
  } finally {
    for (const entry of entries) {
      db.exec(`DETACH DATABASE ${alias(entry)}`);
    }
    db.close();
  }
}
```

**Pros:** No limit on total repos; all repos queried; no persistent attach state  
**Cons:** Slower (reattach overhead per query); need batching for very large repo counts

---

### Option 3: Separate Connection Fan-Out (Recommended for Scale)

**Architecture:**
```typescript
// Create N pools, each covering subset of repos
const pools = chunk(entries, MAX_PER_POOL).map(chunk => createAttachPool(chunk));
pools.forEach(p => p.warmAttachPool());  // Parallel warm

// Query each pool in parallel
const results = await Promise.all(pools.map(p => p.withAttached(fn)));
return results.flat();
```

**Pros:** Parallel queries; unlimited repos; good for many-repo deployments  
**Cons:** More complex; need to coordinate epoch/cache across pools

---

### Option 4: Explicit Partial Coverage Reporting (Required for All Options)

Add to API response shape:

```typescript
interface SpecialistsResponse<T> {
  data: T;
  coverage: {
    attached: string[];           // repoSlugs successfully attached
    skipped: Array<{ slug: string; reason: string }>;
    total_discovered: number;
  };
  freshness: "fresh" | "stale";
  source_health: SourceHealth;
}
```

Update `makeSourceHealth`:

```typescript
if (coverage.skipped.length > 0) {
  return makeSourceHealth("specialists", "degraded", { 
    message: `${coverage.skipped.length}/${coverage.total_discovered} repos unavailable`,
    metadata: { skipped: coverage.skipped }
  });
}
```

**This makes invisible failures visible in UI.**

---

### Recommended Combined Fix

1. **Immediate:** Lower `DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 8` (conservative, works on default SQLite builds)
2. **Add configuration:** `OBSERVABILITY_MAX_ATTACHED` env override with validation
3. **Add coverage reporting:** Expose attached/skipped/total in API response
4. **Future:** Implement connection fan-out (Option 3) for users with >8 repos

## Regression Test Plan

### Test 1: Pool Capacity Limit
```typescript
it("attaches up to maxAttached repos and evicts LRU when full", async () => {
  const repos = Array.from({ length: 15 }, (_, i) => createRepo(i));
  const pool = createAttachPool(repos, { maxAttached: 4 });
  await pool.warmAttachPool();
  
  // Expect exactly 4 attached (LRU order: most recent 4 in attach order)
  const result = pool.withAttached((db, attached) => attached.map(a => a.slug));
  expect(result).toHaveLength(4);
});
```

### Test 2: Exceeds SQLite Limit Without LRU Eviction
```typescript
it("lower DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED to match SQLite SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED", async () => {
  // Verify default is safe for default SQLite builds
  expect(DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED).toBeLessThanOrEqual(10);
  
  // Verify it's conservative (leaves room for main DB)
  expect(DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED).toBeLessThanOrEqual(8);
});
```

### Test 3: Dead Cache Marks Failed DBs
```typescript
it("marks attach failures in dead cache without crashing pool", async () => {
  const good = createValidRepo(0);
  const bad = createInvalidSchemaRepo(1);  // Missing specialist_jobs table
  const pool = createAttachPool([good, bad], { maxAttached: 4 });
  
  const result = pool.withAttached((db, attached) => attached);
  expect(result).toHaveLength(1);
  expect(result[0].slug).toBe(good.repoSlug);
});
```

### Test 4: Coverage Reporting in API
```typescript
it("reports attached and skipped repos in API response", async () => {
  // Setup 15 repos, only 10 can attach
  const response = await app.request("/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight");
  const body = await response.json();
  
  expect(body.coverage.total_discovered).toBe(15);
  expect(body.coverage.attached).toHaveLength(10);
  expect(body.coverage.skipped).toHaveLength(5);
  expect(body.coverage.skipped[0].reason).toContain("attach failed");
  expect(body.source_health.status).toBe("degraded");
});
```

### Test 5: Module-Scoped Dead Cache Persists
```typescript
it("survives pool recreation (module-scoped dead cache)", async () => {
  const bad = createInvalidRepo(0);
  
  // First pool: marks dead
  createAttachPool([bad]);
  
  // Second pool: dead cache prevents reattach attempt
  const pool2 = createAttachPool([bad]);
  const result = pool2.withAttached((db, attached) => attached);
  expect(result).toHaveLength(0);
});
```

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts` | Pool with attach limit bug (line 20: `DEFAULT_MAX_ATTACHED = 12`) |
| `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts` | Repo discovery, 10s cache |
| `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts` | Root config, DEFAULT_ROOTS expansion |
| `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts` | SpecialistsDao queries, silent flatMap |
| `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/metrics-dao.ts` | Metrics summary, silent row omission |
| `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts` | API endpoints, no coverage reporting |
| `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/observability.ts` | Metrics endpoint, no coverage reporting |
| `apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/attach-pool.test.ts` | Existing tests (no capacity limit tests) |
| `apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/registry.test.ts` | Discovery tests |

## Risk Notes

1. **Silent data loss:** Users won't notice missing repos until they compare counts with filesystem
2. **Production impact:** Any deployment with >10 specialist repos will silently drop data
3. **Cache amplification:** Module-scoped dead cache means a single attach failure poisons the entire process lifetime
4. **LRU dead code:** With limit mismatch, LRU eviction never activates; first 10 repos "lock in" on startup
5. **No telemetry:** The warning log is the only signal; no metrics, no API surface shows it
6. **Cross-project:** `~/dev/*` and `~/projects/*` glob expansion can easily exceed 10 repos

title: P0: Observability registry drops specialist DBs after SQLite attach limit
description:
Startup logs show: "Skip observability db ... attach failed (too many attached databases - max 10)". This means registry discovery can silently omit specialist observability DBs once the SQLite attach pool reaches the default limit. Impact: specialists/observability dashboard coverage is incomplete for discovered repos (example skipped DB: /home/dawid/projects/mercury/website/.specialists/db/observability.db).

Acceptance criteria:
- Registry no longer drops discovered observability DBs because of SQLite attached database limit.
- Either uses bounded per-query attach/detach, separate connection fan-out, or pagination/sharding with explicit coverage reporting.
- UI/API expose partial coverage explicitly if any DB cannot be read.
- Add regression test with >10 discovered observability DBs proving all are represented or clearly reported unavailable.
- Startup logs should not report silent skipped DBs in healthy state.
notes:
Lowered attach pool default to 8, added clamped env override, transient attach-limit skip coverage, and response coverage/source_health propagation for specialists + observability. Regression tests added for >10 db coverage.

title: [next-session P0] Validate realtime health deeply, then start forge-kcxa.1
description:
## STATUS
P0 next-agent pickup. This bead exists because the audit closed `forge-3ymv`, but the app is **not fixed yet**. The previous session answered the architecture questions, shipped docs, and wired `github:event.append`; it did **not** prove the dashboard is now healthy.

## WHY THIS IS P0
The current app problems are likely systemic, not one missing event type. The audit found multiple still-open root-cause candidates:

1. **Shared `WsClient` subscription lifetime bug**: `useWebSocket` uses one shared client and one channel set. If two components subscribe to the same channel, unmounting either can send server `unsubscribe` for both. This can silently kill live updates during navigation/drawer/view changes.
2. **No client-side truth/resource layer**: `useGraphData`, `useChains`, `BeadsRepoView`, and `useGithubActivity` still each own their own cache/freshness/refetch logic.
3. **Graph/Specialists first-paint stale-empty**: cold cache can return empty stale data while warming in background, so the UI reads as broken.
4. **Observability registry coverage**: default registry roots still miss repos outside `~/dev/*`, so specialists data may only show a subset of tracked repos.
5. **Source-health fragmentation**: Graph/Beads/Specialists/GitHub all use different freshness/health vocabularies.
6. **GitHub realtime parity is incomplete**: `github:event.append` is fixed, but `github:release.upsert` is published without a client consumer, and `github:rate_limit` should be removed or folded into canonical source health.

The next agent must **not assume** the prior audit is complete or correct. Treat it as a strong hypothesis set, then verify against code, tests, and live app behavior.

## PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
Validate the realtime health diagnosis end-to-end, find any additional silent contract failures, then start the highest-leverage fix in the `forge-kcxa.*` plan — likely `forge-kcxa.1` first, unless validation finds an even more urgent blocker.

## REQUIRED RE-INSPECTION
Before implementation, inspect and record evidence for each area below:

### 1. WS transport contract
Files:
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useWebSocket.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/lib/ws.test.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/tests/api/ws/*`

Questions:
- Does the client still have no per-channel refcount?
- Does unmounting one subscriber unsubscribe the server channel while another local handler remains?
- Do tests still encode stale expectations (`type:"subscribe"` vs `action:"subscribe"`)?
- Is there any subscribe ACK or only silent success/failure?
- Does reconnect/resume preserve channel subscriptions correctly when multiple consumers exist?

### 2. Client-side source-of-truth drift
Files:
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChains.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts`

Questions:
- Which hooks still directly call `useWebSocket`?
- Which hooks refetch immediately vs coalesced vs polling fallback?
- Which hooks mutate local payloads vs treating HTTP as authoritative?
- Where can navigation cause data to disappear, be overwritten by empty responses, or fail to resubscribe?

### 3. First-paint / cold-cache behavior
Files:
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`
- Graph UI empty-state components/tests

Questions:
- For a known project with data, can first request return `{nodes: [], freshness:"stale"}`?
- Does the UI show that as loading/warming or as genuinely empty?
- Is there a bounded warm path available, or only background warm + retry?

### 4. Observability registry scope
Files:
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`

Questions:
- What roots are scanned by default right now?
- Do they cover `~/projects/*` and nested specialist DBs?
- Is discovery dynamic after server start?
- Are legacy `.specialists/observability.db` paths still supported or accidentally excluded?

### 5. GitHub realtime declared-vs-wired matrix
Files:
- `apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`

Questions:
- For every `GithubRealtimeEvent`, identify publisher(s) and consumer(s).
- Confirm `github:event.append` tests cover dedupe and non-push/PR events.
- Confirm whether `github:release.upsert` updates client releases or is ignored.
- Decide whether `github:rate_limit` is deleted or represented through source health.

## REQUIRED LIVE/TEST VALIDATION
Run the smallest useful checks; do not rely only on static reading.

Minimum expected validation:
- Focused WS client/server tests around subscribe/unsubscribe/resume.
- Focused GitHub poller test file:
  `bun test apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller.test.ts`
- Existing graph cold-cache tests if present:
  `bun test apps/gitboard/tests/core/graph-dao.cache.test.ts apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts`
- If a dev server is already running or safe to start, smoke at least:
  - graph first navigation for a known repo with beads
  - specialists drawer repo count vs actual observability DBs
  - navigation away/back while watching WS/log output

Classify failures as:
- in-scope realtime contract failure
- pre-existing test infra/baseline failure
- unrelated app issue

## EXPECTED OUTPUT
Update this bead and/or `forge-kcxa` with:

1. A **validated problem list**: confirmed, disproven, newly discovered.
2. A **declared-vs-wired matrix** for WS events/channels.
3. The chosen first implementation bead and why.
4. Any changes needed to the `forge-kcxa.*` child plan.
5. If implementing, reviewer-backed PASS and focused test evidence.

## RECOMMENDED FIRST FIX
Default to `forge-kcxa.1` unless validation proves another issue is more urgent.

`forge-kcxa.1` target: fix WS shared-client channel refcount and tests before broader source-store refactors. This is foundational because source stores will still be unreliable if transport subscriptions can silently disappear.

## NON-GOALS
- Do not claim “all app problems solved” after one narrow fix.
- Do not start broad UI redesign.
- Do not rewrite all realtime hooks in one PR unless explicitly re-planned.
- Do not add new WS event types until declared-but-unwired events are resolved.
- Do not trust specialist/audit output blindly; verify with code/tests/live behavior.

## REFERENCES
- `bd show forge-kcxa` — audit answers and child plan.
- `bd show forge-kcxa.1` through `.6` — planned fixes.
- `bd show forge-df1j` — completed `github:event.append` implementation.
- Memory: `realtime-audit-forge-kcxa-2026-05-22-shared`.
- Commit: `feab1c5 feat(realtime): audit truth model and wire github event append`.
notes:
Validation evidence (orchestrator + explorer in progress): WS transport still has Set-only subscriptions in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts; useWebSocket cleanup always calls client.unsubscribe(channel), so two consumers of same channel can lose the server subscription when either unmounts. Stale ws.test expectation confirmed: bun test apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/lib/ws.test.ts apps/gitboard/tests/api/ws fails only on expected {type:'subscribe'} vs actual {action:'subscribe'}; API WS tests pass. Github poller focused suite passes: bun test apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller.test.ts = 13 pass; event.append dedupe/non-push coverage exists. Graph cold-cache tests currently fail: graph-dao.cache.test.ts uses vi.mocked under bun test (infra mismatch) and graph route tests return empty nodes; live smoke on :3301 also showed graph initial {nodes:0,freshness:'stale'} then {nodes:0,freshness:'fresh'} for project_id=gitboard despite bd showing hundreds of issues. Specialists live smoke showed first stale-empty then fresh-empty; default observability roots list only ~/dev/* (3 repos), while adding ~/projects/* finds 11 DBs, confirming registry scope gap. GitHub declared-vs-wired: pr.upsert/issue.upsert/event.append published+consumed; release.upsert published but ignored by client; sync_hint produced by WS resume and consumed; rate_limit declared but no publisher/consumer found. First implementation remains forge-kcxa.1 because transport loss undermines all higher source-store fixes.
### Specialist Output — explorer (job 6df710) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Realtime Health Validation Report

### Validated Problem List

| Problem | Status | Evidence |
|---------|--------|----------|
| **1. WS shared-client no refcount** | **CONFIRMED** | `ws.ts:24` uses `Set<string>`; `useWebSocket.ts:23-27` calls `unsubscribe()` on any unmount, killing server subscription for all subscribers |
| **2. No client-side truth layer** | **CONFIRMED** | Each hook (`useGithubActivity`, `useGraphData`, `useChains`, `BeadsRepoView`) owns independent cache/invalidation/refetch logic |
| **3. Graph first-paint stale-empty** | **CONFIRMED** | `graph-dao.ts:140` returns `{issues:[], freshness:"stale"}` on cache miss; `useGraphData` shows empty graph then retries 750ms later |
| **4. Observability registry scope** | **CONFIRMED** | `config.ts:8` defaults to `["~/dev/*"]` only; misses `~/projects/*` |
| **5. Source-health fragmentation** | PARTIAL | Graph uses `fresh|stale|degraded`; Beads uses `available|missing|unhealthy`; different vocabularies exist |
| **6. GitHub realtime parity incomplete** | **CONFIRMED** | `github:release.upsert` published but not consumed; `github:rate_limit` is dead type |

### New Findings

1. **WS client test encodes stale expectation**: `ws.test.ts:74-76` expects `{ type: "subscribe" }` but actual client sends `{ action: "subscribe" }`. Test passes because mock doesn't validate against real server.

2. **No WS subscribe ACK**: Server silently accepts subscription; no confirmation sent back to client.

3. **Reconnect preserves seq but not refcount**: `ws.ts` correctly keeps `lastSeqByChannel` on unsubscribe (line 100 comment shows this was fixed), but the Set membership is still lost, causing double-subscribe on remount.

---

### WS Declared-vs-Wired Matrix

| Channel | Event | Declared | Publisher | Consumer | Status |
|---------|-------|----------|-----------|----------|--------|
| `github:activity` | `github:pr.upsert` | ✅ | `github-poller.ts:501` | `useGithubActivity.ts:87` | ✅ WORKS |
| `github:activity` | `github:issue.upsert` | ✅ | `github-poller.ts:462` | `useGithubActivity.ts:91` | ✅ WORKS |
| `github:activity` | `github:event.append` | ✅ | `github-poller.ts:619` | `useGithubActivity.ts:79` | ✅ WORKS |
| `github:activity` | `github:release.upsert` | ✅ | `github-poller.ts:567` | **NONE** | ⚠️ UNWIRED |
| `github:activity` | `github:sync_hint` | ✅ | Server resume path | `useGithubActivity.ts:95` | ✅ WORKS |
| `github:activity` | `github:rate_limit` | ✅ | **NONE** | **NONE** | ❌ DEAD TYPE |
| `beads:changes` | Multiple BeadsRealtimeEvent | ✅ | BeadsWatcher | Various hooks | ✅ WORKS |
| `specialists:activity` | `specialists:sync_hint` | ✅ | Epoch.bump listener | `useGraphData`, `useChains` | ✅ WORKS |

---

### Chosen First Implementation: `forge-kcxa.1`

**Correct choice**. The WS subscription refcount bug is foundational:

1. **Highest leverage**: Any source-store refactor will still be unreliable if transport silently drops subscriptions
2. **Narrow scope**: Only touches `ws.ts`, `useWebSocket.ts`, and test files
3. **Test broken anyway**: `ws.test.ts` expectation must be fixed to match actual wire format

**Implementation approach**:
```typescript
// ws.ts — change Set<string> to Map<string, number>
private subscriptions = new Map<string, number>();

subscribe(channel: string): void {
  const count = this.subscriptions.get(channel) ?? 0;
  this.subscriptions.set(channel, count + 1);
  if (count === 0) {
    this._send({ action: "subscribe", channel, version: String(REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION) });
  }
}

unsubscribe(channel: string): void {
  const count = this.subscriptions.get(channel) ?? 0;
  if (count <= 1) {
    this.subscriptions.delete(channel);
    this._send({ action: "unsubscribe", channel });
  } else {
    this.subscriptions.set(channel, count - 1);
  }
}
```

---

### Test Evidence

| Test | File | Finding |
|------|------|---------|
| WS subscribe format | `tests/dashboard/lib/ws.test.ts:74-76` | **BROKEN**: expects `type:"subscribe"` but client sends `action:"subscribe"` |
| Resume after unsubscribe | `tests/dashboard/lib/ws.test.ts:100-118` | PASS: correctly tests seq preservation and resume payload |
| Multiple subscribers | `tests/api/ws/channels.test.ts:70-77` | PASS: server handles multiple independent subscribers |
| Protocol version | `tests/api/ws/realtime-contract.test.ts:38-42` | PASS: closes on version mismatch |
| Resume buffer miss | `tests/api/ws/realtime-contract.test.ts:44-53` | PASS: emits sync_hint on boot_id mismatch |
| github:event.append | `tests/core/github-poller.test.ts:234,251,275,286` | PASS: dedupe + multiple event types covered |
| github:release.upsert | `tests/core/github-poller.test.ts` | **NO TESTS** found for this event type |

---

### forge-kcxa Child Plan Changes Needed

| Issue | Adjustment |
|-------|------------|
| `forge-kcxa.1` | **START HERE** — add refcount + fix test expectation |
| `forge-kcxa.2` | Proceed after .1; no change |
| `forge-kcxa.3` | Proceed after .2; no change |
| `forge-kcxa.4` | Add `~/projects/*` to DEFAULT_ROOTS or make env-configurable |
| `forge-kcxa.5` | Consider returning `freshness:"warming"` with loading state instead of empty stale |
| `forge-kcxa.6` | Wire `github:release.upsert` consumer; remove `github:rate_limit` dead type |

---

title: [next-session pickup] Run forge-kcxa audit with overthinker, then ship docs + event.append
description:
## STATUS
Pickup bead for the next session. Created at the close of 2026-05-22 (session report at .xtrm/reports/2026-05-22-ea8500f.md).

## CONTEXT
This session shipped 6 PRs (forge-qa5g #31, forge-afac #32, forge-7cyq #33, forge-w8ya #34, forge-nwdr #35, forge-h830 #36 — last one rolling in forge-d3ix). Each fixed a real bug; each surfaced another. The session's structural diagnosis (from overthinker 48ffaf and the audit-first epic forge-kcxa) is: the dashboard has no client-side source-of-truth layer — every hook reinvents subscribe + cache + invalidation + refetch independently.

**Do not start more dashboard fixes until forge-kcxa's 10 open questions are answered.** They cover the truth model, push/pull split, source-health vocabulary, observability-registry scope, Dolt authority, first-paint UX, test contracts, migration order, dead WS event types, and "what else is silently broken after the WS subscribe contract was confirmed broken for an unknown duration".

## PLAN

### Step 1 — Audit (forge-kcxa)
- Re-read .xtrm/reports/2026-05-22-ea8500f.md for full context.
- Dispatch `overthinker` against forge-kcxa with the 10 open questions. Give it the report and the bead description.
- Capture answers + any refined questions back into forge-kcxa.
- Once questions are answered, file concrete child issues under forge-kcxa with acceptance criteria.

### Step 2 — Documentation (forge-nrwc) — can run in parallel with Step 1
- Scope already enumerated in the bead.
- README + docs/deployment.md + env-var matrix + scanner XDG semantics + shared-server fallback + Docker path "experimental" notes.
- Native systemd run mode is now primary; docker is secondary. Document both clearly.

### Step 3 — github WS parity (forge-df1j) — only after Step 1 decisions arrive

title: Fix WebSocket reconnect/resume state loss
description:
## PROBLEM
Random disconnect/reconnect events sometimes make dashboard data vanish. Audit points to `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts`: `unsubscribe()` deletes `lastSeqByChannel`, weakening reconnect resume. `github:sync_hint` also calls `load()`, which can transiently reset/replace store data instead of preserving stale visible state.

## SUCCESS
WebSocket reconnect preserves channel sequence where safe, resumes without data loss, and sync hints trigger merge/refresh without clearing visible GitHub/beads state. Data remains visible through disconnect/reconnect.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useWebSocket.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts
- relevant websocket/store tests

## VALIDATION
- Test unsubscribe/remount does not clear last sequence for reconnect resume.
- Test github:sync_hint does not clear existing store lists while refresh pending/failing.
- Test disconnect/reconnect keeps visible GitHub PR/issues rows.
notes:

description:

title: Design high-availability dashboard data plane
description:
## PROBLEM

Gitboard still has multi-second perceived latency switching GitHub tabs and console surfaces even though GitHub DB data exists locally. The dashboard needs a high-availability data plane: always serve visible cached/local state fast, keep WebSocket realtime authoritative, and move expensive data gathering out of UI request paths.

## SUCCESS

Produce a verified architecture and implementation plan for separating dashboard UI/API reads from data ingestion/refresh. Decide whether in-process cache, SQLite materialized views, background workers, Redis, or another queue/cache is appropriate. Identify current blocking request paths and propose staged fixes with validation.

## SCOPE

- GitHub data flow: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts, core/github-store.ts, core/github-poller.ts, dashboard hooks/stores/MainPane.
- Specialists data flow: api/routes/specialists.ts, server/observability/*, specialist DB attach pool.
- Beads/graph data flow enough to explain head-of-line blocking: api/routes/beads.ts, api/routes/graph.ts, core/graph-dao.ts, dashboard hooks.
- Runtime/container deployment data paths and DB location.

## NON_GOALS

- No implementation edits in this bead unless follow-up implementation issue is created/claimed.
- No broad rewrite without phased migration plan.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WebSocket updates remain instant and authoritative.
- Dashboard API should serve stale/local data immediately when available.
- GitHub REST and specialist DB scans must not block tab/surface navigation.
- Prefer simplest reliable design; Redis only if justified over SQLite/in-process cache/background jobs.

## VALIDATION

- Map current slow paths with code evidence and log evidence.
- Verify whether /api/github/issues itself is slow or whether UI/server is head-of-line blocked.
- Produce instrumentation plan: server route timings + browser tab click/fetch/paint timings.

## OUTPUT

Architecture recommendation, phased implementation plan, risks, and verification checklist.
notes: External verification summary received: DB available and GitHub list routes DB-first; PR/issues latency likely frontend orchestration; releases cold no preload; websocket unsubscribe deletes lastSeqByChannel causing weak resume/sync_hint reload; graph/specialists/beads still block Bun event loop via sync Dolt/attach work. Recommendation: no Redis for now; single-node async Dolt + in-process SWR.
close_reason: Epic complete: all 4 planned children (forge-ma82, forge-pula, forge-5i43, forge-pbev) shipped + 7 discovered-from siblings (forge-9qmk, forge-vdda, forge-ia5u, forge-jve5, forge-ijzx, forge-cspw, forge-g598) shipped. HA layer (DB = availability, WS = freshness, REST = replenishment) structurally sound. Cold parallel <50ms, warm graph <300ms. Session report: .xtrm/reports/2026-05-21-42192f57.md

title: Replace pipe shell fallback with real PTY terminal provider
description: PROBLEM: Current fallback shell proves websocket transport but behaves poorly: no real PTY semantics, job-control warnings, weak interactive behavior. Wetty demonstrates the right architecture: real PTY + xterm + websocket. SUCCESS: Local provider uses a real PTY implementation when shell provider is explicitly enabled; falls back to disabled state if PTY dependency is unavailable; cwd/shell/env/rate-limit/security gates remain enforced. SCOPE: provider-registry/local PTY integration, dependency decision, graceful unavailable status, tests. NON_GOALS: SSH/Tailscale/tmux, multi-user auth beyond current gates. VALIDATION: terminal can run pwd/echo/interactive input without job-control warnings; provider tests pass; security tests retained.
close_reason: Done: real terminal now uses Node helper-owned node-pty with cwd/shell policy, env allowlist, origin gating, and focused validation.

title: Harden TerminalStream xterm lifecycle, focus, and resize
description: PROBLEM: Terminal UI throws xterm renderer dimension errors and input can stop working due React rerenders/remount/focus/fit races. SUCCESS: xterm is created once per mount, callbacks stay fresh without remount, focus works on click/open, fit is deferred until visible, drawer close/open does not trigger renderer exceptions. SCOPE: TerminalStream lifecycle, FitAddon timing, ResizeObserver, tests for input/focus/remount/zero-size behavior. NON_GOALS: backend/provider changes. VALIDATION: no browser console xterm dimension errors; focused TerminalStream/TerminalTabPanel tests pass; manual typing works.
close_reason: Done: TerminalStream lifecycle stabilized, font metrics hardened, resize/focus guarded, tests updated.

title: Stabilize Gitboard dev server and websocket proxy
description: PROBLEM: Browser can hit stale/wrong backend/frontend processes and Vite did not proxy /api/console/terminal/ws as websocket, causing disconnected terminal and project load confusion. SUCCESS: One documented dev startup path runs backend and dashboard from current worktree; /api/github/repos loads via Vite; /api/console/terminal/status works; /api/console/terminal/ws upgrades through Vite; no stale IPv6/old-main process confusion. SCOPE: vite.config proxy, dev scripts/docs or guards, startup verification commands. VALIDATION: HTTP/API/WS smoke checks from frontend port. NON_GOALS: production deployment redesign.
close_reason: Done: dev server stabilized with explicit terminal websocket proxy and IPv4 backend binding.

title: Terminal UX recovery and Wetty-style PTY hardening
description: PROBLEM: Post-merge terminal support is functionally unstable and visually poor. User reports project loading regressions, disconnected/invalid-message states, xterm renderer crashes, and no usable input. code-server is a broad UX benchmark; Wetty is the direct terminal-over-web reference. SUCCESS: Gitboard has a reliable, good-looking terminal drawer using a real PTY-style backend, stable websocket/protocol path, robust xterm lifecycle/focus/fit behavior, and reproducible manual/automated smoke validation. SCOPE: Decompose and fix terminal/runtime regressions from current xt/yz1j work. Cover dev server/proxy consistency, provider architecture, xterm UI/lifecycle, security invariants, and smoke tests. NON_GOALS: No full VS Code clone, no SSH/Tailscale/tmux unless explicitly planned later, no broad app redesign. VALIDATION: project APIs load, terminal connects, accepts input, shows output, survives drawer close/reopen, no xterm renderer crashes, focused tests and manual smoke pass.
close_reason: Done: terminal recovery children for runtime, PTY, lifecycle, UI polish, and regression coverage are complete.

title: Fix post-merge Gitboard loading and terminal UI regressions
description: PROBLEM: After terminal E2E merge, user reports projects do not load and Terminal UI looks wrong/broken on dev server. SUCCESS: Gitboard loads project data again and Terminal tab styling/functionality is consistent with app design. SCOPE: Diagnose current xt/yz1j server/client errors, project-loading APIs/UI, TerminalTabPanel/TerminalStream styling/wiring as needed. NON_GOALS: no broad redesign, no unrelated backend refactor. CONSTRAINTS: Manual orchestrator intervention authorized by user; still keep changes minimal and run focused validation. VALIDATION: dev server logs clean of relevant errors; project endpoints/load path works; terminal visual/functionality smoke; focused tests. OUTPUT: files changed, errors found/fixed, validation.
close_reason: Done: post-merge loading/runtime regressions fixed including IPv4 backend binding, websocket proxy, protocol envelopes, and terminal runtime smoke.

title: Final security review terminal E2E
description: PROBLEM: Prior security review forge-qdtv failed only because attach used payload.token instead of payload.reattachToken. Debugger forge-nh4e fixed it on feature/forge-lqyc.6-executor. Need final mandatory security review before merge. SUCCESS: PASS if attach payload now requires reattachToken; legacy token rejected; missing reattachToken rejected; bridge/UI/tests use reattachToken; original TERM-001/TERM-002 remain fixed: server-minted unguessable token distinct from sessionId; foreign websocket cannot attach/input/resize/exit with sessionId alone; session/token/output not persisted in localStorage; valid reconnect with token works. Files: packages/core/src/terminal/protocol.ts, packages/core/tests/terminal-protocol.test.ts, api/terminal/bridge.ts, TerminalTabPanel.tsx, shell store, terminal route/store/panel tests. VALIDATION EVIDENCE: protocol+route tests pass 12/12; UI panel needs DOM env in this harness. OUTPUT: Security verdict PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL and merge readiness.

Verified protocol requires payload.reattachToken; payload.token rejected; missing token rejected; bridge mints random reattachToken distinct from sessionId; foreign connection blocked from input/resize/exit without ownership; shell store keeps terminal session/token/output in memory only; UI reattach path and tests use reattachToken. - ID: TERM-001

title: Attack plan: implement terminal stream substrate safely
description:
PROBLEM: The embedded terminal epic forge-lqyc is ready for implementation, but a fresh orchestrator session needs a clear, safe execution path. Raw shell exposure is the highest-risk surface, so implementation must proceed in the right order with specialist review gates.
SUCCESS: Fresh orchestrator can pick this bead up, dispatch the right specialist chain in order, and avoid starting PTY/WebSocket shell work before protocol and safety contracts exist.
SCOPE: Orchestration plan for epic forge-lqyc and child beads forge-lqyc.1 through forge-lqyc.11. This is a coordination/attack-plan bead, not an implementation bead.
NON_GOALS: Do not implement code in this bead. Do not dispatch PTY work first. Do not make tmux mandatory. Do not implement SSH/Tailscale. Do not fold specialist-feed reuse into the PTY MVP.
CONSTRAINTS: Use specialists-v3 workflow. Run specialists list --full before dispatch. Re-read target bead before each chain. Use executor -> reviewer for implementation chains. Run security-auditor before any PTY/shell-capable code lands. Keep TerminalStream renderer separate from provider/session ownership. Treat shell exposure as high-risk.

ATTACK PLAN:

1. Start with protocol contract.
   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.2 Define terminal stream protocol.
   - Dispatch executor on forge-lqyc.2.
   - Scope should include shared TypeScript protocol/types/schema/tests only.
   - Must define versioned generic envelope + terminal-specific payloads, not broad RPC.
   - Must include session/stream ids, capabilities, states, error model, resize/input/output semantics, and backpressure/byte-volume notes.
   - Review with reviewer. Merge only after PASS.

2. In parallel or next, build renderer-only component.
   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.1 Create reusable TerminalStream xterm component.
   - Dispatch executor on forge-lqyc.1 once file overlap with protocol is understood.
   - Must not import provider/session/PTY APIs.
   - Must support readonly and read/write modes, ANSI output, scrollback, fit addon, resize, lifecycle cleanup.
   - Review with reviewer. Merge only after PASS.

3. Implement safety gates before PTY.
   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.7 Add shell-provider safety gates.
   - Depends on protocol.
   - Dispatch executor, then mandatory security-auditor on the executor job, then reviewer.
   - Safety must include: production disabled by default, explicit enable flag, localhost/admin intent, cwd allowlist, shell allowlist, env scrub, session caps, idle timeout, hard TTL, input/output byte limits, create/terminate audit events only, no command/input/output/secret logging, orphan cleanup, readonly feed permission separated from shell permission.
   - Do not start forge-lqyc.3 or forge-lqyc.4 before this lands.

4. Implement local PTY provider.
   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.3 Implement local PTY provider.
   - Depends on forge-lqyc.2 and forge-lqyc.7.
   - Dispatch executor after safety PASS/merge.
   - Use node-pty or vetted equivalent.
   - Provider owns process lifecycle; UI must not know PTY details.
   - Run security-auditor because this touches shell/process/input handling.
   - Review with reviewer. Merge only after PASS.

5. Add WebSocket bridge.
   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.4 Add terminal WebSocket bridge.
   - Depends on protocol, safety, PTY provider.
   - Dispatch executor after PTY provider shape is stable.
   - Must safely reject invalid session IDs and disabled providers; detach/schedule cleanup on disconnect; avoid logging user input.
   - Run security-auditor because browser-to-shell transport is sensitive.
   - Review with reviewer. Merge only after PASS.

6. Add drawer tab and end-to-end wiring.
   - Target beads: forge-lqyc.5 Add Terminal tab to bottom drawer, then forge-lqyc.6 Wire local terminal end-to-end.
   - Drawer tab can start after renderer if protocol overlap is low, but e2e wiring waits for websocket/PT provider.
   - Validate open/type/output/resize/close/reopen/exit/error/build.
   - Review with reviewer.

7. Tests.
   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.11 Terminal stream test coverage.
   - Can be split or run after substrate is stable.
   - Must cover TerminalStream mocks, resize, readonly blocking, protocol validation, PTY lifecycle, websocket bridge, safety gates, specialist-feed provider if implemented.

8. Follow-ups, not MVP blockers.
   - forge-lqyc.8 readonly specialist feed provider is now P3 follow-up after local PTY MVP proves renderer/protocol.
   - forge-lqyc.9 optional tmux evaluation after PTY stable.
   - forge-lqyc.10 SSH/Tailscale planning only; no SSH implementation.

KNOWN DESIGN DECISION FROM OVERTHINKER:
- Recommendation was ADJUST, applied.
- TerminalStream owns rendering only.
- TerminalSessionProvider owns PTY/tmux/SSH lifecycle.
- Protocol uses generic envelope + terminal event payloads.
- Local PTY provider only in MVP.
- tmux optional follow-up.
- SSH/Tailscale future provider only.
- Specialist-feed reuse follow-up, not MVP blocker.

VALIDATION FOR THIS ATTACK PLAN:
- bd show forge-lqyc and children before dispatch.
- sp ps and git worktree list before/after each dispatch.
- Reviewers must PASS before merge.
- security-auditor must run before shell-capable PTY/WebSocket changes merge.
- gitnexus impact/detect_changes requirements still apply for code edits.

OUTPUT: A future orchestrator should report which chain is dispatched first, job ids, review verdicts, merge status, and any deviations from this plan.
