title: Drawer height ignored by flex:1 on .ide-main children — opt drawer out
description:
## PROBLEM
globals.css line 4387: `.ide-main > * { flex: 1; min-height: 0; min-width: 0; }` applies `flex: 1` to ALL direct children of .ide-main, including the new `<BottomDrawer>`. flex:1 (shorthand for `flex: 1 1 0%`) overrides any `style={{ height: <px> }}` set inline. Result: BottomDrawer's height-driven resize / maximize / 75vh-default all UPDATE the store + inline style correctly but the RENDERED visual height never changes — flex distributes available space proportionally instead.

Operator-confirmed via Firefox DevTools console: move/maximize handlers fire with correct math (h=106..139 as expected, maximize toggle logs show new heights) but the drawer doesn't visually resize.

Playwright tests missed this because we only checked `element.style.height` (the inline style we wrote), never `getBoundingClientRect().height` (what flex actually renders).

## SUCCESS
- Visual drawer height matches the value set via store (drag changes visible height in real time, maximize jumps, 75vh default opens at ~75vh).
- `getBoundingClientRect().height` ≈ store drawerHeight ±2px (the ±2 accounts for sub-pixel rounding).
- Also: simultaneously fix sibling `.ide-main-content` to claim the remaining flex space (flex:1) so MainPane content stays scrollable. Without this the content area would have undefined height once drawer opts out.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
  - Add after line 4391: `.ide-main > .bottom-drawer { flex: 0 0 auto; }` — drawer claims explicit pixel height only.
  - Keep `.ide-main > * { flex: 1; ... }` for `.ide-main-content` so it expands.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx
  - REMOVE the three console.log diagnostic lines (operator confirmed module load works; logs are noise now).

## NON_GOALS
- No changes to drawer JSX, no changes to resize handler, no changes to store.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: no gitnexus_* calls.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- Playwright sanity: open drawer, getBoundingClientRect().height should equal the store drawerHeight value. Then drag, recheck — rect.height changes. Maximize — rect.height = viewport - 24.

## OUTPUT
- 2-file diff
- Confirmation that .bottom-drawer flex: 0 0 auto is set
- Confirmation that diagnostic console.log lines removed
notes:
Removed drawer diagnostic logs; opted .bottom-drawer out of .ide-main flex:1.

title: Attack plan: backend/observability/deploy sprint sequencing
description:
PURPOSE
Single source of truth for next-session execution order across the 13 open beads as of 2026-05-18. Captures triage decisions, parallel-track structure, and the rationale so any future agent (or this one cold-starting) can resume without re-deriving the plan.

CURRENT OPEN BEADS (13)
- forge-ci9   P1  Visual fix pass (collapse/black-bg) — SUSPECTED STALE post-shell-rewrite
- forge-8kdc  P2  Dockerize gitboard as VPS service + Tailscale access
- forge-a72   P2  [bug] Baseline tsc cleanup (28 errors) — unblocks sp merge gate
- forge-ftj   P2  [bug] Beads Feed ready/blocked — SUSPECTED DUPLICATE of closed forge-kz7
- forge-wvlj  P2  Dashboard bottom log drawer (UI) — blocked by forge-zpw8
- forge-zpw8  P2  Backend event-log emitter + WS feed + 7-day disk retention
- forge-1fb   P3  Beadboard bv parity gaps in native scoring
- forge-6q1   P3  [bug] Store release.tag_name separately in github_events
- forge-che   P3  Console: stats from observability.db
- forge-e23   P3  Console: Specialists tab (live job list + chain history)
- forge-ebr   P3  Add focused Beads Feed tests
- forge-0up   P4  DEFERRED: Inline EventDetail in sidebar
- forge-3r1   P4  DEFERRED: ContributionHeatmap in sidebar

STEP 0 — Triage pass (do FIRST, before claiming any work)
- forge-ci9: bd show + diff intent against current apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/{Sidebar,TopBar,MainPane}.tsx. If shell rewrite covers the goals, bd close with reason 'superseded by forge-7xu / forge-ci9 phase 2 shell rewrite'. Memory ack: 'nothing novel'.
- forge-ftj: bd show + compare scope against closed forge-kz7. The closed bead derived blocked/ready from dep graph in IssueFeed; if ftj is the same scope, close as duplicate. Memory ack: 'nothing novel'.
- Expected outcome: ~11 active beads.

STEP 1 — Two parallel tracks (disjoint file scopes; safe to run concurrently)

TRACK A: observability/deploy
  Order  Bead          Why this slot                                              Estimate
  A.1    forge-a72     Highest leverage — unblocks sp merge gate for every       half day
                       future chain. We manually merged 7 commits this session
                       because sp merge tsc-gates on these 28 baseline errors.
  A.2a   forge-zpw8    Backend logger; touches src/core/*, src/api/ws/*,         1 day
                       src/types/*. No frontend deps. CAN start in parallel
                       with A.2b — disjoint scope from Docker.
  A.3    forge-wvlj    UI drawer; depends on forge-zpw8 (consumes its event      1 day
                       names + endpoint shape). Sequential after A.2a.

TRACK B: parallel with track A
  Order  Bead          Why this slot                                              Estimate
  A.2b   forge-8kdc    Docker + Tailscale. Touches root (Dockerfile,              half day
                       compose, .dockerignore, docs/). Disjoint from forge-zpw8.
                       References /data/logs path that zpw8 also targets, but
                       zpw8 has a ./logs fallback constraint so either order
                       lands clean.
  ANY    forge-ebr     Small focused tests; can slot in during any reviewer      1-2 hr
                       wait window in either track.

PARALLEL DISPATCH RULE
- bd commit-gate is project-wide. Parallel executors are fine; the SECOND commit waits until the first chain closes + commits. Plan accordingly: close one chain's bead before starting another's commit.

STEP 2 — Console mini-epic (next session+1)
- forge-che + forge-e23 together form a coherent 'console = observability surface' feature.
- RECOMMENDATION: promote to an epic with these two as children; consider adding 1-2 more (e.g. live realtime metrics graph backed by forge-zpw8's log stream) for a complete surface.
- Lower urgency than tracks above; the log drawer (forge-wvlj) already gives operator-visible observability at the dashboard level.

STEP 3 — Long-tail (claim opportunistically)
- forge-6q1: small data-shape fix, ~1 hr; isolated to github-store.ts + tests.
- forge-1fb: scoring parity; needs domain question before scope is firm — ask operator before claiming.
- forge-0up / forge-3r1: stay deferred (P4) until operator asks.

RECOMMENDED NEXT-SESSION OPENING MOVE
1. Run Step 0 triage (close ci9 + ftj if confirmed superseded/duplicate).
2. Claim forge-a72 + forge-8kdc; dispatch both executors with --worktree --keep-alive --background.
3. Monitor per v3 cadence; close them one at a time.
4. Then claim forge-zpw8 (longest single bead, ~10 files, needs careful call-site instrumentation pass).
5. Then forge-wvlj.

CONTEXT THIS BEAD PRESERVES
- Why forge-a72 is P0-priority-effectively (compounds across all future merges).
- Why ci9 + ftj are 'suspected superseded/duplicate' rather than immediately closed (operator should confirm with current code state).
- Why Track A and Track B parallelize cleanly (disjoint file scopes + tsc-gate-only dependency).
- Why forge-wvlj sequential not parallel with forge-zpw8 (consumes its event-name contract).
- Why forge-che + forge-e23 want to be an epic (single coherent surface).

NON-GOALS for this bead
- Not a planning surface for individual bead scope changes — those edits go on the beads themselves.
- Not a status tracker — close the implementation beads as they ship; this bead can be closed when the Step 1 tracks complete.

CLOSE WHEN
- forge-a72, forge-zpw8, forge-wvlj, forge-8kdc all closed AND triage on forge-ci9 + forge-ftj completed.

REFERENCES
- This conversation's session report (will be generated next): .xtrm/reports/2026-05-18-<sha>.md
- Existing memories: realtime-envelope-uses-boot-id, realtime-envelope-version-is-string, beads-watcher-diffs-4-entity-snapshots, dolt-connection-stability-forge-1qz, beadschangewatcher-fast-path-forge-3mdw
notes:
UPDATE 2026-05-18 post-PR-#10 verification: Codex review surfaced 4 production bugs in realtime stack (filed as forge-yy27, P1). This becomes the new HIGHEST-PRIORITY implementation work — supersedes forge-a72 as Step 1.A.1. Reason: live Beads sync is silently dead in production today (Fix 1 in forge-yy27); GitHub backfill silently truncates for any repo with >100 issues (Fix 2 + 3); sync_hint recovery never runs (Fix 4). All shipped via PR #10 merged 2026-05-18 20:53 UTC. None of the reviewer-PASS chains in this session caught them because review focused on contract conformance, not on consumer-side envelope unwrapping or pagination retention.

REVISED STEP 1 ORDER:
  A.0    forge-yy27   P1  Realtime production bug fixes (NEW — was not in original plan)
  A.1    forge-a72    P2  tsc cleanup (still high leverage — sp merge gate)
  A.2a   forge-zpw8   P2  Backend logger
  A.2b   forge-8kdc   P2  Docker + Tailscale
  A.3    forge-wvlj   P2  UI drawer (blocked by zpw8)

PROCESS LESSON
Reviewer contract: from now on, contract-conformance reviewers must ALSO check at least one consumer-side integration test for the new envelope shape, not just type+payload alignment. Otherwise drift between producer and consumer slips silently. Update reviewer bead template accordingly when running observability/realtime work.
close_reason: Backbone complete: forge-yy27, forge-a72, forge-zpw8, forge-8kdc all landed on main (8aaea55). forge-ci9 + forge-ftj triage-closed. forge-wvlj superseded by forge-gud9. No remaining children.

title: forge-7xu hotfix 2: MainPane needs sized wrapper for virtualized timelines
description:
PROBLEM: GitHub tabs render blank because MainPane returns ActivityTimeline / PrTimeline / etc directly without a wrapping flex-sized element. The virtualizer needs a parent with definite height; gets zero.

FIX: Wrap MainPane content in a single <main className=ide-main> with flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:auto.

SCOPE: components/shell/MainPane.tsx, styles/globals.css (.ide-main rule).
notes:
Hotfix 2 done (Playwright-verified):
- MainPane.tsx wraps inner in <main className='ide-main' key=...> for sized parent + clean remount on swap.
- globals.css: html/body/#root height:100%; body:has(.ide-shell) overflow:hidden; .ide-shell height:100vh !important + overflow:hidden; .ide-main flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:hidden; .ide-main > * flex:1 + min-height:0.
- Verified: specialists/activity now shows full timeline with TODAY/YESTERDAY headers, ~100 events visible. shellHeight=823 matches viewport 823. mainHeight=783 (correctly constrained, scrollHeight=783).
close_reason: MainPane wrapped in sized .ide-main; shell height locked to 100vh. Specialists activity renders full timeline.

title: bd close updates lag ~2min vs ~4s for bd create — likely dolt-auto-commit=off default + uncommitted working set invisible to materializer's separate Dolt session
description:
## PROBLEM
bd create → dashboard sees the new bead in ~4 seconds (verified for forge-zqv2: created 23:19:48, dashboard saw at 23:19:52).

bd close → dashboard takes ~2 minutes to see the closed state (verified for forge-zqv2: closed 23:20:26, substrate updated at 23:22:28 = 2m02s lag).

Between the close and the materializer pickup, 3 separate materializer.run cycles fired for the gitboard project (23:20:41, 23:21:12, 23:21:42) — all with rows_written=0. The snapshot-diff thought nothing changed.

## HYPOTHESIS (from bd dolt --help)
'--dolt-auto-commit string Dolt auto-commit policy (off|on|batch). Default: off.'

bd close writes to Dolt's working set but doesn't commit. The materializer's DoltClient runs in a separate SQL session and sees the COMMITTED state, not the working set, so it observes the pre-close snapshot. Eventually (~2min, possibly via batched-commit timer or some flush) the working set commits and the materializer's next read sees the new state.

## WHY CREATES ARE FAST DESPITE THIS
Open question. Possible: INSERT is autocommitted differently than UPDATE. Or the create flow includes an explicit commit but the close flow doesn't. Investigation needed.

## SUCCESS
- bd close → dashboard sees closed within ~5-15s (same latency as create)
- Substrate state matches Dolt within 1 materializer cycle of the close

## POSSIBLE FIXES
A. Set 'dolt.auto-commit: on' in /home/dawid/dev/gitboard/.beads/config.yaml — operator-side one-line change. Verify no side effects on shared-server performance with 25+ projects.
B. Materializer-side: detect uncommitted working set on read, or force a COMMIT in the source Dolt before snapshot read.
C. bd-side: ensure close path commits explicitly.

## EVIDENCE
journalctl/log replay: /home/dawid/.agent-forge/logs/2026-05-26.jsonl
Search for 'beads:4c37df93' materializer.run rows_written transitions across 23:20-23:22.

## DISCOVERED FROM
.27 Check 3 walkthrough — operator observed 'bd open is near instant, bd close takes forever'.
notes:
Fixed shared-server port precedence in both ProjectScanner copies and added one-shot beads-snapshot source logging; lint/typecheck still hit broad pre-existing repo issues unrelated to change.

title: Materializer obs:* COMMIT doesn't publish WS sync_hint — chip surfaces only after dashboard poll cycle
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-9v9u + forge-irzl fixed the chip data pipeline, /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns the correct beadId within ~10s of a sp dispatch. But the dashboard chip surfaces only on the next poll cycle (10s+), not within .27's <2s spec.

Root cause: materializer/index.ts:89 publishes 'specialists:sync_hint' on specialists:activity + specialists:repo:<slug> channels. BUT journal grep over 60s of active dispatch+materialize+commit cycles shows NO 'sync_hint' events firing in journalctl. Either:
(a) bumpEpoch fires but the WS publish path isn't reached for obs:* sources, or
(b) emit() to log ring isn't propagating to journalctl (only published-to-clients channel), or
(c) the trigger that calls runOnce for obs:* sources isn't being invoked frequently enough to push the hint

## EVIDENCE (verified 2026-05-26)
- Dispatched explorer at 20:43:13
- Job appeared in API at 20:43:23 (10s lag)
- Dashboard chip surfaced ~same 10s lag (operator observation)
- journalctl since 60s: 0 'sync_hint' lines
- forge-eorh.64 added bumpEpoch(repoSlug.slice(4)) for obs:* in runOnce after COMMIT — should trigger WS publish via epoch.onBump listener in api/server.ts

## SUCCESS
- Dispatch a fresh sp run → dashboard chip surfaces within 2 seconds
- journalctl shows 'specialists:sync_hint' event within 1-2s of every obs:* materializer commit
- specialists:repo:<slug> channel publishes

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts (verify bumpEpoch + publish channel mapping for obs:*)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/epoch.ts (verify onBump subscribers fire)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (where epoch.onBump is wired to WS publish)
- Possibly: ensure materializer.trigger('obs:<slug>') fires on observability.db change events (file watcher) — not just on a timer

## SCRUTINY
medium

## DISCOVERED FROM
forge-9v9u (.27 step 1 validation probe)
notes:
## DIAGNOSTIC OUTCOME (post-merge investigation)

The diagnostic logging added in this fix REVEALED THE TRUTH: publishHint IS firing correctly, with ~200ms latency from observability.db write to WS broadcast.

Evidence from /home/dawid/.agent-forge/logs/2026-05-26.jsonl (publishHint events firing for obs:gitboard with ws_registry_set=true).

Reconstructed forge-faux timeline:
- 21:19:21 sp run returned job_id
- ~21:19:38 sp finally wrote first observability.db row (~17s sp bootstrap)
- 21:19:39 materializer.runOnce → publishHint → WS publish (fast!)
- Frontend chip appeared ~1s later

So the 'noticeable lag' the operator observed was sp's own bootstrap time, NOT the WS push path.

## STILL VALUE FROM THIS CHAIN
1. Diagnostic logging in publishHint now permanent — easy to debug similar issues
2. onBump fallback listener wired in api/server.ts — safety net if publishHint path ever breaks
3. Earlier journalctl confusion explained: emit() writes to /home/dawid/.agent-forge/logs/<date>.jsonl + log ring + WS, NOT to stdout. journalctl was the wrong place to look.

## NEW FOLLOW-UP NEEDED
File 'sp startup writes first observability.db row ~17s after dispatch — chip surfaces 17s late' as a separate bug in xtrm-tools / sp side (not gitboard).
close_reason: Diagnostic logging revealed: WS push path works correctly (~200ms file→broadcast). Lag was sp bootstrap (~17s). Filed forge-$SP_LAG as separate sp-side bug.

title: Dashboard chip doesn't surface for new specialist jobs: bead_id lost in xtrm.sqlite materialization + wrong COALESCE fallback in loadJobs
description:
## PROBLEM
After dispatching any specialist (e.g. `sp run explorer --bead forge-zbhd`), the dashboard feed row for that bead never shows the in-flight specialist chip. The chip data IS being recorded in the observability DB correctly, but it's getting lost between observability.db and the API response.

## EVIDENCE (verified 2026-05-26)
Test: dispatched explorer for bead 'forge-zbhd' (gitboard project).

1. observability.db has the row correctly:
   apps/.../observability.db specialist_jobs: { job_id: '7b4373', specialist: 'explorer', bead_id: 'forge-zbhd', status: 'running' }

2. xtrm.sqlite specialist_jobs LACKS the bead_id column entirely:
   PRAGMA table_info(specialist_jobs) → [job_id, status, repo_slug, specialist, chain_id, epic_id, chain_kind, worktree, last_output, created_at, updated_at, updated_at_ms]
   No bead_id. The materializer's observability-adapter doesn't carry bead_id from source.

3. apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:191 loadJobs SQL:
   SELECT j.repo_slug, j.job_id, COALESCE(l.issue_id, j.job_id) AS bead_id ...
   LEFT JOIN substrate_job_link AS l ON l.repo_slug = j.repo_slug AND l.job_id = j.job_id
   - substrate_job_link table has 0 rows (nothing populates it)
   - Fallback is j.job_id, not j.bead_id
   - So API returns beadId = job_id ('7b4373') instead of 'forge-zbhd'

4. Dashboard frontend filters chips by issue.id === job.beadId — never matches. No chip.

## ROOT CAUSE (two compounding bugs)
A. xtrm.sqlite specialist_jobs schema is missing the bead_id column. The observability-adapter at apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts maps from observability.db.specialist_jobs (which HAS bead_id) but drops it during materialization to xtrm.sqlite.

B. loadJobs SQL falls back to j.job_id instead of j.bead_id even if the column existed. The COALESCE order is wrong AND the link table is empty (nothing populates substrate_job_link).

## SUCCESS
- ALTER xtrm.sqlite specialist_jobs ADD COLUMN bead_id TEXT (ALTER-guard pattern matching .71 fix)
- observability-adapter.ts: read bead_id from source row and persist it
- specialists.ts loadJobs: COALESCE(l.issue_id, j.bead_id, j.job_id) AS bead_id
- After deploy + restart: curl /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=forge-zbhd returns the running job
- Dashboard chip surfaces on the issue row

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts (ALTER guard for new column + index on bead_id)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts (write bead_id from source)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/types.ts (MaterializedSpecialistJob shape)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:191 (COALESCE order)

## SCRUTINY
high — schema migration + materializer change + API contract

## VALIDATION (auto-mode verifiable)
- Dispatch a fresh sp run, wait 10s, curl /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=<that-bead> → 1+ jobs returned
- The /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight response has the correct beadId field (not the job_id)
- Existing materializer tests still 14/14 pass

## DISCOVERED FROM

title: Fix substrate.ts query bugs: limit cap, ordering, tombstone filter (forge-eorh.71 follow-up)
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-eorh.71 schema extension + forge-eorh.72 pagination, three bugs in apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts cause /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues to return wrong data:

1. **API limit cap = 100**: parseIssueFilters at line 197-203 defaults limit to 100; applyIssueFilters line 194 does .slice(0, filters.limit ?? 100). Projects with > 100 issues silently truncated. Evidence: market-data substrate has 689 rows (137 non-tombstoned), API returns 100.

2. **Wrong ORDER BY**: queryIssues at line 120 uses ORDER BY issue_id ASC (alphabetical). Dolt's natural order is ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at DESC. Dashboard expects priority-first ordering.

3. **Tombstones not filtered**: queryIssues WHERE clause is just repo_slug = ?. Doesn't filter rows where deleted_at IS NOT NULL. Evidence: market-data has 552 of 689 rows (80%) tombstoned but still returned by API.

## SUCCESS
- /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns ALL non-deleted issues (no implicit 100 cap; explicit limit param still works as opt-in clamp)
- Issues ordered by priority ASC, then created_at DESC (matching Dolt + bd list expectations)
- Rows with deleted_at IS NOT NULL excluded from query (safer than post-filter)
- Existing tests stay green (14/14 materializer)
- /api/beads/projects/<gitboard>/issues count matches gitboard's bd list --status=open --status=closed --limit=0 within materializer lag tolerance

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts (3 changes)
  - queryIssues SQL: add 'AND (deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at = "")' to WHERE, change ORDER BY to 'priority ASC, created_at DESC, issue_id ASC' (tie-breaker)
  - parseIssueFilters limit default: increase from 100 to a much larger cap (e.g. 100000) OR make limit truly optional (undefined → no slice)
  - applyIssueFilters: respect undefined limit (return unsliced)

## NON_GOALS
- No schema migration (deleted_at column already exists)
- No materializer changes (issue 4 — possible drift between Dolt and substrate state — is separate, after this lands)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Keep response shape stable (still {issues: [...]})
- Existing limit/status/priority/search query params still work
- Tests must stay 14/14

## SCRUTINY
medium

## VALIDATION (machine-verifiable)
- bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/ → 14/14 pass
- After restart + 5-min materializer cycle: API count for market-data ~133 (matches bd --limit=0 canonical)
- API count for gitboard ~544 substrate / matches bd open+closed count
- Sample issue priority distribution shows p=0 first, then p=1, ..., not alphabetical
- Sample issues with deleted_at NOT in API response

## OUTPUT
Diff + curl-evidence count comparison per-project (gitboard, market-data, specialists) AND a separate issue-ID diff: comma-list of IDs in bd canonical but not in substrate API, and vice versa.
notes:
Updated substrate issue query: exclude tombstones in SQL, order by priority/created_at/issue_id, and make issue-list limit optional so no implicit 100 cap.

title: Extend substrate_issues schema with priority, issue_type, owner, labels, related_ids, parent_id, closed_at, close_reason, notes
description:
## PROBLEM
substrate_issues currently has only: repo_slug, issue_id, title, body, state, deleted_at, created_at, updated_at.

apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts queryIssues hardcodes priority=2, issue_type='task', owner=null, related_ids=[], labels=[]. Every issue surfaces as P2 task → dashboard TYPE_CONFIG palette (bug red, feature royal blue, epic purple, task muted) collapses to one color, priority rail uniform, no visual differentiation in the feed.

beads-adapter.normalizeIssue at line 113-124 already receives full BeadIssue from Dolt/JSONL — it just discards priority/issue_type/etc. before writing.

## SUCCESS
substrate_issues stores full BeadIssue contract; queryIssues returns real priority + issue_type + owner + labels + related_ids + parent_id + closed_at + close_reason + notes; dashboard feed renders with type+priority colors.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts (schema: add columns + indices)
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/types.ts (MaterializedIssue type)
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts (normalizeIssue + writeIssues — bind all fields)
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts (queryIssues — return real fields instead of hardcoded defaults)
apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/beads-adapter.test.ts (fixture updates)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Schema migration via ALTER TABLE guards in createXtrmDatabase (same pattern as ensureSpecialistJobsUpdatedAtMsColumn).
- labels + related_ids stored as JSON-encoded TEXT (sqlite has no array type).
- After deploy: wipe substrate_issues + reset materialization_state for beads:* so eager bootstrap repopulates with new schema.
- Don't break the obs adapter (uses different write path).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] curl /api/beads/projects/<gitboard>/issues?status=open returns issues with real priority and issue_type (not all P2 task)
- [ ] Dashboard feed shows P0=red, bug=red, epic=purple, feature=blue, task=muted
- [ ] 17/17 materializer tests pass

## OUTPUT
Diff + curl evidence.
notes:
## SCRUTINY
high (schema migration + sensitive surface per v3 auto-escalation table for migrations/**)

## ADDITIONAL CONSTRAINTS (auto-mode logging)
Operator drives e2e + smoke testing autonomously — emit observable evidence at every step:
- ALTER TABLE migration must log each column add: 'xtrm-store: ALTER substrate_issues ADD COLUMN <name> ... [added|already-present]'
- beads-adapter.normalizeIssue must log first issue per cycle in DEBUG: '{repo_slug, issue_id, priority, issue_type, labels_len, related_ids_len}'
- beads-adapter.writeIssues must include in the existing materializer.run event: rows_with_real_priority, rows_with_real_type, rows_with_labels (counts > 0)
- substrate.ts queryIssues: NO new logging needed (read-path is hot); but add a one-shot startup log on first call: 'substrate.queryIssues: schema_columns=[<comma-list>]' so orchestrator can verify schema deploy worked
- Add a new internal endpoint GET /api/internal/substrate/schema returning {columns: [...], counts: {with_priority_nonzero, with_type_nontask, with_labels, with_related}} for autonomous verification

## DEPLOY STEPS (executor must include in OUTPUT)
After code lands and dist rebuilds:
1. systemctl --user restart gitboard.service
2. curl /api/internal/substrate/schema → confirms ALTER ran
3. Wipe + repopulate: sqlite3 ~/.agent-forge/xtrm.sqlite 'DELETE FROM substrate_issues; DELETE FROM materialization_state WHERE source_key LIKE "beads:%";'
4. Wait 60s for eager bootstrap re-materialize
5. curl /api/internal/substrate/schema → counts should show varied priority/type
6. curl /api/beads/projects/<gitboard-id>/issues | jq palette check

## VALIDATION (machine-verifiable)
- bunx tsc --noEmit (no errors)
- bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/ (17/17 pass)
- curl http://100.113.49.52:3030/api/internal/substrate/schema returns columns including priority, issue_type, owner, labels, related_ids, parent_id, closed_at, close_reason, notes
- After wipe+repopulate: GET /api/beads/projects/<gitboard>/issues shows at least 2 distinct priorities and 2 distinct issue_types
- materializer.run log event includes rows_with_real_priority > 0

title: Investigate: /gitboard dashboard shows nothing in feed + all tabs after .66 fix
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-eorh.66 landed (substrate project name fix), /api/beads/projects returns real names (gitboard, terminalbeta, etc.) and /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns issues. But user reports dashboard at /gitboard shows nothing in feed or any other tab.

Possible suspects already considered but unconfirmed:
- Bundle hash unchanged (same as May 23 — source matches bundle).
- useRepoTree at apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts:64 does Promise.all of getRepos + getRepoStats + listProjects; if any throws, catch swallows and preserves prior empty repos.
- beadsSourceFromConnection at apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts:45 has no case for status='substrate_connected' (the new status returned by apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts:56). It falls through to the error case → repo node gets beadsSource={label:'error', healthy:false} but hasBeads=true. Sidebar should still show.
- BeadsRepoView at apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx might fail on the substrate-returned issue shape (specifically missing fields like notes vs description, dependencies title resolution).
- /api/beads/projects/<id>/memories and /interactions return [] due to stubs (forge-eorh.67 filed) — but feed/triage shouldn't be empty for that.

## SCOPE
Investigation only — find the smoking gun. No code changes in this bead.

## TASK
1. Hit the live dashboard at http://100.113.49.52:3030/gitboard. Capture:
   - sidebar repo count rendered (count both github and console surfaces)
   - what shows when console surface + feed tab is selected
   - what shows when a beads-only project (e.g. terminalbeta) is selected
   - any console errors (use the bundle's source map if avail)
2. Trace useRepoTree's exact failure: cross-check API responses against expected types in apps/gitboard/src/types/beads.ts and what beadsSourceFromConnection expects.
3. Specifically check whether substrate.readConnection's return shape (status='substrate_connected', source='sqlite') is handled correctly downstream and whether BeadsConnectionStatus type validates it.

## CONSTRAINTS
Read-only. No code edits. Report findings with file:line citations.

## OUTPUT
One concrete root cause + the file:line where the bug lives.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job b3d41a) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary
Found the smoking gun: `beadsSourceFromConnection` at `useRepoTree.ts:45-55` doesn't handle `status: "substrate_connected"` or `source: "sqlite"` returned by the substrate API after forge-eorh.66. The fallthrough returns `{ label: "error", ... }` instead of recognizing it as healthy.

## Architecture
- **API chain**: `beadsApi.listProjects()` → `/api/beads/projects` → `createSubstrateRouter()` → `readProjects()` (substrate.ts:20)
- **Connection status**: `beadsApi.getConnection()` → `/api/beads/projects/:id/connection` → `readConnection()` (substrate.ts:67) returns `{ source: "sqlite", status: "substrate_connected", ... }`
- **UI chain**: `useRepoTree` → `beadsSourceFromConnection()` → `BeadsRepoView` → `IssueFeed`
- **Sidebar rendering**: `Sidebar.tsx:43` filters by `(surface === "github" ? r.hasGithub : r.hasBeads)` — `hasBeads` comes from `Boolean(beadsSide)` which is true if project matched

## Key Findings

### Root Cause Identified

**File: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts:45-55`**

```typescript
function beadsSourceFromConnection(connection: BeadsConnectionStatus | null): BeadsSourceChip {
  if (!connection) return { label: "unknown", title: "Beads source unknown", healthy: false };
  if (connection.status === "dolt_connected" || connection.status === "connected" || (connection.source === "dolt" && !connection.degraded)) {
    // Handles dolt_connected, connected, dolt source
    return { label: "dolt", title: ..., healthy: true };
  }
  if (connection.source === "jsonl" || connection.degraded || connection.status === "jsonl_fallback" || connection.status === "no_dolt") {
    // Handles jsonl fallback
    return { label: "jsonl", title: ..., healthy: false };
  }
  if (connection.status === "not_found") return { label: "missing", ... };
  
  // FALLTHROUGH: status="substrate_connected", source="sqlite" lands here
  return { label: "error", title: `Beads source status: ${connection.status}`, healthy: false };
}
```

**The substrate API returns:**
- `source: "sqlite"` 
- `status: "substrate_connected"`
- `degraded: false`
- `message: "xtrm.sqlite connected"`

**Neither matches any condition** — falls through to error case.

### Impact

1. **`beadsSource`** becomes `{ label: "error", healthy: false }` 
2. **`hasBeads: Boolean(beadsSide)`** remains `true` (project was found) — sidebar should still show projects
3. **Sidebar chip displays** `bd:X` with subtitle "error" (the `label` field)
4. **Feed/issue loading should work** — `BeadsRepoView` doesn't depend on `beadsSource.healthy`

### Why Sidebar Might Appear Empty

The task spec mentions sidebar shows nothing. Let me verify if `hasBeads` calculation could be impacted:

**`useRepoTree.ts:123`**: `hasBeads: Boolean(beadsSide)`

`beadsSide` comes from `findBeadsSide(tail, beadsByTail)` which returns a project if matched by name. This should work regardless of `beadsSourceFromConnection`.

**However**, if the entire `Promise.all` in `useRepoTree:64-76` throws (due to `beadsApi.listProjects()` failure), the catch at line 146 swallows and preserves prior empty state.

### Secondary Issue: Missing Field Handling in IssueFeed

**File: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts:83-97`**

The substrate API returns issues with:
```typescript
{
  id: String(row.issue_id),
  title: String(row.title ?? ""),
  description: row.body == null ? null : String(row.body),
  notes: null,  // Added to satisfy type
  // ...
}
```

But `BeadDependency` returned has empty `title: ""` (line 76):
```typescript
list.push({ id: dep.dep_issue_id, title: "", status: "open", dependency_type: dep.relation });
```

This is **not fatal** - dependencies with empty title can still render, just missing visual context.

## Risks
- The `beadsSourceFromConnection` function incorrectly labels healthy substrate connections as "error"
- Visual display shows error badge on sidebar chips even though data loads successfully
- If user sees "error" label, they may assume data is stale or broken

## Verification
Check `/api/beads/projects/:id/connection` response shape:
- Expected: `{ source: "sqlite", status: "substrate_connected", ... }`

Check sidebar:
1. Filter repos by `surface === "beads"` 
2. Check if `hasBeads` is true for substrate-backed projects
3. Check `beadsSource.label` — should show "error" but data still loads

## Follow-ups
- Add `status === "substrate_connected"` or `source === "sqlite"` handling to `beadsSourceFromConnection`
- Return `{ label: "sqlite", title: "Connected to substrate SQLite", healthy: true }`

## Machine-readable block

title: Beads projects API returns name='.beads' — substrate route uses wrong basename
description:
## PROBLEM
Discovered 2026-05-25 during dashboard inspection: /api/beads/projects (served by createSubstrateRouter at apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts) returns all 19 projects with name='.beads', breaking the dashboard's useRepoTree which matches beads projects to GitHub repos by tail name.

Root cause: substrate.ts line 25-30 reads xtrm.sources where kind='beads'. The stored `path` is the .beads directory path (e.g. /home/dawid/projects/mercury/terminalbeta/.beads). Line 28 does `row.path.split('/').filter(Boolean).at(-1)` which returns '.beads' for every row.

Verified via curl: every project entry has name='.beads', path='.../<project>/.beads'. Probing the underlying scanner (apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts) returns correct names like 'terminalbeta', 'darth-feedor'. The bug is purely in the substrate route's basename resolution.

## SUCCESS
/api/beads/projects returns 19 entries each with name = parent directory name (terminalbeta, darth-feedor, etc.), matching the GitHub repo tails so useRepoTree merges beads sources into the unified shell.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts (line 28 fix)
apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/substrate.test.ts (if exists, add regression assertion)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Minimal change: use `.at(-2)` instead of `.at(-1)` to skip the trailing '.beads' segment. Or use `dirname(row.path).split('/').filter(Boolean).at(-1)` for explicit clarity.
- Don't change xtrm.sources schema (path column stores .beads dir intentionally — that's where TriggerWatcher mints sources).
- Don't change /api/beads/projects/:id response shape — only the name field per project.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] curl /api/beads/projects returns name='terminalbeta' for terminalbeta's UUID, name='gitboard' for gitboard's UUID, etc.
- [ ] dashboard /gitboard shell shows beads projects under matching GitHub repos
- [ ] No other route consumer of substrate projects shape regresses
- [ ] If substrate.test.ts exists, add assertion: project.name !== '.beads'

## OUTPUT
Diff + verbatim curl response showing real project names.

## REFERENCE
Discovered post .64 fix during dashboard e2e check. Likely long-standing (substrate route was added by .48 chain).

title: Specialists in-flight cache never invalidates — epoch.bump() has no callers
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-eorh.60 e2e dispatch probe (2026-05-25 13:39 UTC) revealed that /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns frozen data even when new jobs are materialized correctly:

1. Dispatched: sp run explorer --bead forge-wrii → job 7d83e9 started at 13:39:43
2. Materializer wrote it: obs:gitboard materializer.run fired at 13:39:39 (rows=2) and 13:39:58 (rows=1), last_status='success'
3. xtrm.specialist_jobs has the row: repo_slug='gitboard', job_id='7d83e9', status='running', updated_at_ms=1779716428840
4. BUT /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight after 35+s still does NOT return 7d83e9; instead returns 4 explorer jobs from May 14 (10 days stale)

Root cause: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:269-272 builds cacheKey using epoch.get(repoSlug). apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:274-277 readCache returns cached value on key equality with NO TTL check. apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/epoch.ts exports bump() but grep across apps/gitboard/src/ (excluding tests + verifier) shows ZERO call sites. Epoch never bumps -> cache key never changes -> stale forever.

Memory `epoch-ts-onbump-pattern-forge-7cyq` says this was wired to the legacy observability fs-watcher pre-flip. The .48/.49/.58/.61/.62 chain replaced that path with the materializer but did not re-wire epoch.bump on materializer.runOnce success.

## SUCCESS
After materializer.runOnce success for obs:<slug>, the specialists in-flight cache invalidates within one HTTP request cycle. New sp jobs visible in /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight within ~2s of materializer firing (which is itself within COALESCE_MS=1500ms of the source write).

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts (runOnce — call epoch.bump after upsertMaterializationState for obs:* sources)
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (optionally: add a 5s TTL to readCache as safety net)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Don't bump epoch on every realtime event; once per materializer.runOnce success is enough since the route's cache key bundles all repos.
- Optional: drop stale 'running' rows that never reached 'done' (May 14 zombies). Separate from this fix.
- Pattern: import { bump as bumpEpoch } from "../../server/observability/epoch.ts" at top of index.ts; call after the COMMIT in runOnce when sourceKey.startsWith("obs:"), passing the repoSlug.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Repeat the e2e probe: sp run explorer → /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight shows the new job within 2-5s
- [ ] cacheKey reflects updated epoch; readCache miss on next request after materializer.runOnce
- [ ] Materializer tests still pass
- [ ] No regression in jobs/bead-id cache (which has same staleness pattern — bumping the epoch invalidates it too)

## OUTPUT
Diff + repeat e2e probe with timing evidence.

## REFERENCE
Discovered 2026-05-25 during forge-eorh.60 follow-up e2e verification. Memory `epoch-ts-onbump-pattern-forge-7cyq` documents the pre-flip wiring that the materializer chain replaced.

title: Fix beads adapter binding error — Binding expected string... when writing substrate_issues
description:
## PROBLEM
With GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 in prod (2026-05-25 01:49 flip), 11 of 12+ beads:* sources failed with "Binding expected string, TypedArray, boolean, number, bigint or null" in materialization_state.last_error. The 12 obs:* sources succeeded (14084 jobs materialized cleanly). beads:* failed on the writeIssues or writeDependencies INSERT — one of the row fields is being passed as an object (not a primitive).

apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts writeIssues binds:
  stmt.run(row.repo_slug, row.issue_id, row.title ?? null, row.body ?? null, row.state, row.deleted_at ?? null, row.created_at ?? null, row.updated_at ?? null)

writeDependencies binds:
  stmt.run(row.repo_slug, row.issue_id, row.dep_issue_id, row.relation, row.created_at ?? null)

normalizeIssue maps: body = issue.description ?? issue.notes ?? null. Either description, notes, title, or some other field has an object/array shape in real .beads/issues.jsonl rows.

Failing source_keys are UUID project IDs (e.g. beads:073c25b8-6723-42b7-8bca-1bee1dbc1a3d) — those are likely shared-server Dolt projects, not the local gitboard project (which presumably worked because the smoke tests exercise that shape).

## SUCCESS
Gate flip to GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 leaves zero last_status='error' rows in materialization_state. All known beads sources materialize cleanly.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts (writeIssues, writeDependencies, normalizeIssue)
apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/beads-adapter.test.ts (add regression fixture with non-string description/notes)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Coerce non-primitive fields to string via String() or JSON.stringify before binding. Title and body should be String(...) since they're user-visible text; if undefined, null.
- Add a defensive normalizer that walks the field list once and stringifies anything that's not (string | null | number | bigint | boolean | TypedArray).
- Keep the substrate_issues schema unchanged.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Regression test: BeadIssue with description=object or notes=array writes successfully (coerced to string), no throw.
- [ ] Re-run gate flip: GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 in dev shell, after 30s materialization_state has zero error rows.
- [ ] Existing 31 materializer/specialists/beads-parity tests still pass.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test counts, staging probe with zero errors.

## REFERENCE
Discovered 2026-05-25 during forge-eorh.60 prod gate flip (rolled back). Original flip plan still good after .61 lands.

title: Fix obs adapter cursor() — reads materialization_state from wrong db
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts:34-62. createObservabilityAdapter opens the obs.db readonly, and adapter.cursor() calls readCursor(db, repoSlug) which queries 'SELECT cursor FROM materialization_state WHERE source_key = ?' against the OBS db. But materialization_state lives only in the xtrm sink db. Obs.db has no such table — query throws 'no such table: materialization_state', the materializer catches it silently via SourceQueue.drain() empty catch, and no materialization_state row is ever written for obs:* sources.

Verified live during forge-eorh.49 verification ritual: with GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 and 12 obs sources registered + eager bootstrap firing, after 2+ minutes materialization_state stays empty and specialist_jobs count = 0. Direct probe of adapter.cursor() reproduced: 'ERR: no such table: materialization_state'.

## SUCCESS
With GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 in a staging shell, after 30s materialization_state has 12 obs:* rows with last_status='success' (one per discovered obs source) and specialist_jobs is populated.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts

## CONSTRAINTS
- adapter.cursor() must return the ZERO cursor {updated_at_ms: 0, event_rowid: 0} when there's no prior state. The materializer's runOnce() already handles xtrm-side cursor restoration via getCursor; adapter.cursor() is only the seed.

- changesSince/snapshot are unchanged.

title: Harden beads-parity.ts: pool reuse, 50-issue cap, field-diff, 5min interval
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts ran on a 30s timer creating new DoltClient per project, reading 1000 issues per project, and JSON.stringify-comparing them each cycle. This was the root cause of forge-eorh.47 prod OOM. Even though GITBOARD_ENABLE_PARITY is now opt-in (default OFF), the harness must be hardened before it can ever be re-enabled.

## SUCCESS
Harness runs at 5min default interval, reuses pooled DoltClient via doltPoolKey, caps issue reads at 50 per project, uses field-by-field diff. Memory delta over 10 cycles bounded (no accumulation).

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts

## CONSTRAINTS
- Keep gate behavior identical (GITBOARD_ENABLE_PARITY=1 to start; default OFF).
- Pool reuse: keep one DoltClient per doltPoolKey(host, port, db) across cycles; close/disconnect on stop().
- Issue cap: 50 per project (current 1000).
- Field-diff: compare only id, title, status, priority, issue_type, owner, updated_at, closed_at — not full JSON.stringify. Other fields drift naturally and produce noise.
- Default intervalMs: 300_000 (5min) instead of 30_000 (30s). Allow caller override.
- Tests: add apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/beads-parity.test.ts covering pool reuse (same client returned on second call), issue cap (limit 50 passed to getIssues), field-diff (changing untracked field e.g. body does NOT add to diffs), interval default.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Default intervalMs = 300_000
- [ ] DoltClient instances are cached by doltPoolKey
- [ ] readLiveIssues calls client.getIssues({limit: 50})
- [ ] compareIssues uses field-by-field diff (not JSON.stringify)
- [ ] New test file passes
- [ ] No tsc errors scoped to apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test pass evidence, diff stat.

  - `compareIssues()` now field-diff only on:

title: Cold-start UX: /api/specialists/* empty until first materializer trigger
description:
## PROBLEM
Even with the materializer working correctly (after forge-eorh.48 fix), the route returns empty data on first request after boot because the materializer is event-driven (triggered by observability watcher mtime changes). If no sp activity is running, watcher doesn't fire, materializer doesn't run, xtrm.sqlite stays empty. Operator sees blank Specialists panel.

## SUCCESS
On boot, /api/specialists/* returns the current state of every known obs source within 2s of first request.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (eager materialize on app start).
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (graceful fallback when xtrm has 0 rows but materializer hasn't run yet).

## CONSTRAINTS
Two complementary fixes:
1. Eager bootstrap: `queueMicrotask(() => obsKeys.forEach(k => materializer.trigger(k)))` after register, so initial materialization runs in background on boot.
2. Hybrid fallback: when xtrm has 0 rows AND materialization_state has no success for that source yet, fall through to attach-pool live read (current GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK=1 forces it unconditionally; we want it automatic on cold start only).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] After boot with no sp activity, /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns the current state of all known sources within 2s.
- [ ] After sp activity starts, the materializer takes over and the response reflects new data.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, boot-time API trace.
notes:
## Post-claim inspection (2026-05-25, after .48 landed in bdfd462)

### Current state
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:65-71 registers obs adapters per repo but never triggers initial materialization. Materializer only runs when TriggerWatcher (line 74) detects an fs change.
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:61-67 resolve() is all-or-nothing: if xtrmDao exists and liveFallbackEnabled=false, ALWAYS use xtrmDao (even when empty). The liveFallbackEnabled flag, when set, ALWAYS uses attach-pool (defeating the materializer).
- XtrmSpecialistsDao already exposes materializationState() at types.ts:42 returning {source_key, last_status, last_success_at}[].

### Exact changes
1. apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts — after the for-loop at line 70, add eager bootstrap:
   ```
   queueMicrotask(() => {
     for (const repo of listRepos()) materializer.trigger(`obs:${repo.repoSlug}`);
   });
   ```
   So first request after boot has data within 1-2 materializer cycles (no operator wait for fs change).

2. apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts — replace the resolve() chain at lines 63-67 with HYBRID logic:
   - If `dao` (test injection) → use it.
   - Else if xtrmDao exists:
       - Check xtrmDao.materializationState() for entries with last_status='success' for at least one obs source.
       - If at least one success row exists → use xtrmDao.
       - Else fall back to getDefaultBundle(repoLister, epochGetter) — attach-pool live read.
   - Else → default bundle.
   - Remove or repurpose GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK (current semantics: force live; new semantics: optional override to force live regardless of mat state).

3. Add unit test:
   - apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts (extend existing) — verify that a fresh xtrmDb with no materialization_state success rows falls back to attach-pool; with at least one success row, uses xtrmDao.
   - Add a server.ts integration test (or extend existing): after createApp with xtrmDb + 1 fake obs source, queueMicrotask runs the trigger; materialization_state has 'success' entry within 50ms.

### Validation
- Boot with GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 in dev shell.
- curl http://127.0.0.1:3030/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight within 2s of boot. Should return data (either via eager bootstrap or fallback).
- Check journalctl for materializer.run events on obs:* sources during cold-start window.
- Existing apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts still green.

### Non-goals
- No new realtime channels.
- No change to materializer write path (.48 already landed).
- No removal of GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH gate.
- No re-enabling parity harnesses.

### Hard rules
- Run materializer + specialists tests via bun --bun vitest run.
- Do NOT touch .worktrees/ (executor's tmp).
- Do NOT add logs/* to commit (gitignored after .48; double-check).
### Specialist Output — executor (job cac928) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary
Cold-start specialists flow fixed. Eager boot trigger added, plus hybrid fallback now uses live attach-pool only until first successful materialization.

## Status
partial

## Changes
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
  - Cached `obsRepos` once.
  - Added `queueMicrotask(() => materializer.trigger(...))` for each known obs source after register.
  - Reused same `obsRepos` for observability watcher.
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`
  - Added `hasSuccessfulObsMaterialization()`.
  - `resolve()` now:
    - uses injected `dao`
    - else uses xtrm DAO only if live override set or at least one `obs:*` source has `last_status='success'`
    - else falls back to attach-pool default bundle
- `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts`
  - Added cold-start fallback/success tests.
  - Added boot-trigger integration test for eager materialization.

title: Materializer.writeIssues hard-codes substrate shape; obs adapter can't write specialist_jobs
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts:writeIssues() and replaceDependencies() write to substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies columns hard-coded. The observability adapter calls runOnce() with rows containing specialist_jobs shape (job_id, status, updated_at_ms, etc.) which don't match. INSERT throws, transaction rolls back, markFailure called — but no obs:* entry ever appears in materialization_state because the exception happens before the upsertMaterializationState call.

Verified on prod boot: substrate_issues has 640 rows (beads adapter works), specialist_jobs has 0 rows (obs adapter never writes), materialization_state has 19 beads:* entries and 0 obs:* entries.

## SUCCESS
Both substrate and obs sources materialize correctly through the materializer; specialist_jobs gets populated; materialization_state has obs:* entries with status='success'.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts (writeIssues, replaceDependencies, runOnce).
Possibly apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/types.ts (adapter return shape).

## CONSTRAINTS
Two design options:
A) Split into writeIssues/writeJobs methods, dispatch based on source_key prefix.
B) Push the write into the adapter (give adapter a `write(db, snapshot)` method, materializer just orchestrates transactions + cursor).
Pick B — better separation; materializer stays generic.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] After P0/0 fix lands and GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 is set, materialization_state has obs:* entries with status='success'.
- [ ] specialist_jobs count > 0 within 30s of boot.
- [ ] /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns non-empty when there's actual specialist activity.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, sample materialization_state after boot, sample specialist_jobs query.
notes:
## Post-claim analysis (2026-05-25)

### Current state
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts:95-100 hard-codes substrate_issues INSERT inside Materializer.writeIssues()
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts:102-106 hard-codes substrate_dependencies INSERT inside Materializer.replaceDependencies()
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts:111-122 LOSSILY converts JobRow → MaterializedIssue (title=specialist, body=last_output, drops chain_id/epic_id/chain_kind/worktree/updated_at_ms) so the obs adapter's data CAN technically land in substrate_issues but loses 5 columns
- apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts:40-54 defines a rich specialist_jobs table that is NEVER WRITTEN TO by the materializer

### Design (option B from bead)
Add `write(db, snapshot)` to MaterializerAdapter interface. Beads adapter writes to substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies; obs adapter writes to specialist_jobs. Materializer keeps BEGIN/COMMIT/cursor advance.

### Exact changes
1. apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/types.ts — add `write(db: Database, snapshot: MaterializerSnapshot): void` to MaterializerAdapter. Snapshot stays as `{rows, dependencies?}` for beads; obs uses a new richer snapshot type (probably add a discriminated MaterializerSnapshot union: BeadsSnapshot{rows,dependencies} | ObsSnapshot{jobs}). Cleaner: keep MaterializerSnapshot/Delta generic over a `payload` slot and let each adapter type its own.
2. apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts — delete writeIssues/replaceDependencies/deleteDependencies; runOnce + resync delegate to `adapter.write(this.db, next)`. tombstoneMissing also moves into adapter (only beads needs it currently).
3. apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts — implement write() with the existing substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies INSERTs lifted from index.ts. Also tombstoneMissing.
4. apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts — drop toMaterializedIssue lossy mapper; implement write() to INSERT INTO specialist_jobs with the full JobRow shape (12 columns). cursor() / changesSince() / snapshot() now return JobRow[] in their `rows` field via the snapshot payload slot.
5. Update Snapshot/Delta type to carry the per-adapter payload shape (generic, or per-adapter Snapshot types). Pick whichever keeps the call sites simple.

### Validation
- Boot prod with GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1 (dev/staging first per discipline)
- `sqlite3 ~/.agent-forge/xtrm.sqlite "SELECT source_key, last_status FROM materialization_state ORDER BY source_key"` shows BOTH beads:* AND obs:* with last_status='success'
- `sqlite3 ~/.agent-forge/xtrm.sqlite "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM specialist_jobs"` returns > 0
- Existing tests apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/*.test.ts still pass (update fixtures as needed)
- Add a unit test for obs-adapter.write() that verifies specialist_jobs is populated with full row shape

### Test plan
- Update apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/observability-adapter.test.ts (or create) to construct a fake observability.db with one row, run obs adapter through the materializer, assert specialist_jobs has the row.
- Update apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/beads-adapter.test.ts similarly for substrate_issues.
- Smoke: apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p2-beads-adapter.ts and p1-parity-cycle should still pass with new adapter.write() shape.

### Non-goals
- No new realtime channels.
- No changes to MaterializerAdapter.cursor() / changesSince() / snapshot() contracts beyond adding write().
- Don't touch attach-pool fallback path.

### Hard rules
- Do NOT remove the GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH gate.
- Do NOT enable parity harnesses by default.
- Tests must run via 'bun --bun vitest run' (matches project convention).

title: Debugger-restitch: forge-eorh.8 onto post-.43 main
description:
## CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS

Fork off CURRENT MAIN (which has forge-eorh.43 merged with schema column `specialist_jobs.updated_at_ms` added). Verify by running before any commits:
```
grep "updated_at_ms" apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts
```
If that finds the column declaration, you're on the right base.

## PROBLEM

forge-eorh.8 (substrate API + Beads parity) was authored on a branch forked from main PRIOR to forge-eorh.43 being merged. The naive merge regresses .43's work in three places:

1. `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts`: .8 branch DELETES 8 lines that .43 added (the `updated_at_ms INTEGER` column on `specialist_jobs` + the ALTER TABLE guard migration). Without this column the observability adapter cursor breaks.

2. `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts`: .8 branch RE-ADDS 42 lines of `CREATE TABLE` statements that .43 removed (clashes with schema migration).

3. `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-kill.ts`: same regression — 42 lines of CREATE TABLE re-added.

## SUCCESS

A new branch `feature/forge-eorh.8-restitch` based on current main with all of these PRESERVED + ADDED:

PRESERVE (already on main from .43 — DO NOT TOUCH any of these files):
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts` (KEEP exactly as-is on main; do not delete updated_at_ms or the ALTER guard)
- `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts` (KEEP as-is — already insert-only, no CREATE TABLE)
- `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-kill.ts` (KEEP as-is — already insert-only)

ADD (.8's intent on top — fetch via `git show feature/forge-eorh.8-executor:<path>`):
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts` (new file — copy as-is from .8 branch)
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts` (new file — copy as-is)
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts` (apply .8's 4-line addition: `Materializer.publishHint`, `Materializer.realtimeHintFor` symbols)
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (apply .8's additions: mount /api/substrate, mount /api/internal/parity/beads, register Beads parity harness)
- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` (apply .8's MASSIVE -509 line reduction making it a thin shim — but DO NOT proceed if the shim depends on substrate router; verify the chain works)

## SCOPE

apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts (new)
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts (new)
apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (additive)
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts (additive)
apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts (replace with shim)

Reference `feature/forge-eorh.8-executor` for symbol shapes only — do NOT cherry-pick or merge that branch. Re-implement on integration's current state.

## NON_GOALS

- DO NOT touch xtrm-store.ts (it's correct on main)
- DO NOT touch p1-specialists-fallback.ts or p1-specialists-kill.ts (they're correct on main)
- No new features beyond what .8 introduced

## CONSTRAINTS

- Single named conventional commit at end (not a checkpoint commit).
- Verify the diff is ADDITIVE for xtrm-store and the smoke files: `git diff main...feature/forge-eorh.8-restitch -- apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-*.ts` should be EMPTY.
- Smokes must still pass after restitch.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] `git diff main...feature/forge-eorh.8-restitch -- apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts` returns EMPTY
- [ ] `git diff main...feature/forge-eorh.8-restitch -- apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-kill.ts` returns EMPTY
- [ ] `bun apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts` exits 0 (proves .43 preserved)

title: P0/0: Observability event spine — typed event bus + lag verifier
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator policy: every runtime event must be programmatically observable, and any error or lag must be detectable without manual log-grepping. Today's logger (`apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts`) emits ad-hoc JSONL + WS but lacks span/duration semantics, correlation IDs across the materializer→adapter→API call chain, and a verifier that produces a pass/fail report against thresholds.

This is the foundation for every downstream bead: every materializer state transition, every adapter cursor advance, every snapshot-diff hash compute, every WS publish, every API request, every parity diff MUST emit through the typed bus. Without this, we cannot answer "did anything fail in the last hour" or "where is the p95 lag" without operator inspection.

## SUCCESS
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/{event-types.ts,spans.ts,verifier.ts}` exist:
  - `EventType` union covers `materializer.*`, `adapter.*`, `parity.*`, `ws.publish.*`, `api.request.*`, `scanner.*`, `app.*`, plus pass-through for the legacy component-tagged events.
  - `withSpan(component, event, attrs, fn)` wraps an async/sync operation, captures `started_at`, `duration_ms`, `outcome` (`ok`|`error`), `error_message` if thrown, plus a `span_id` and optional `correlation_id` (job_id / source_key / request_id). Always emits exactly one bus event on completion, even on throw (re-throws after emit).
  - `correlate(correlation_id, fn)` runs a function with a contextual correlation ID picked up by nested `withSpan` calls (use `AsyncLocalStorage`).
  - `Verifier`: queries today's JSONL (and optionally rolls forward across rotated logs) and returns `{by_component, by_event, error_count, p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms, breaches: [{component, event, threshold, observed}]}`.
  - Default thresholds in `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/thresholds.ts` per component (e.g., `materializer.run` p95 < 2000ms, `parity.diff` p95 < 500ms, `api.request` p95 < 200ms, `ws.publish` p95 < 50ms). Configurable via JSON file.
- New endpoint `GET /api/internal/verify-runtime?since=<iso>&until=<iso>` returns the verifier output as JSON. Localhost-gated like other internal routes.
- Existing `emit()` and `makeLogEntry()` remain functional and unchanged (backward-compat). New spans go through the bus, which still calls `emit()` internally so logs land in the same JSONL files.
- A constraint document `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/CONTRACT.md` (or inline JSDoc on `EventType`) names which events every downstream bead MUST emit and at what severity.

## SCOPE
- New files only: `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/{event-types.ts,spans.ts,verifier.ts,thresholds.ts,CONTRACT.md}`.
- New file: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-verify.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts` (re-export `EventType`; keep `emit` and `makeLogEntry` unchanged; bus calls into `emit()`).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (mount `/api/internal/verify-runtime`).

## NON_GOALS
- No Prometheus/OpenMetrics push (JSONL + queryable endpoint is enough for single-user).
- No distributed tracing across processes (single-process).
- No retention policy change beyond existing daily JSONL rotation.
- No log shipping / SaaS integration.
- No automated alerting (verifier is pull-based).

## CONSTRAINTS
- BACKWARD-COMPATIBLE: existing `emit()` / `makeLogEntry()` callers must continue to function without code changes.
- `withSpan()` emits EXACTLY ONE event on completion, never on entry; this keeps event volume linear in operation count, not 2x.
- `correlate()` MUST use `AsyncLocalStorage` to survive `await` boundaries — verify in tests.
- Thresholds file is config; verifier reads at request time so operator can edit without restart.
- Internal verify route is localhost-only (same host-gate as `/api/internal/logs`).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: `withSpan` captures duration on success AND on throw; re-throws original error; emits exactly one bus event.
- [ ] Unit: `correlate` propagates correlation_id through await boundaries (verify with two nested awaits + a `setTimeout` between).
- [ ] Unit: Verifier aggregates by component + event, computes p50/p95/p99 from a synthetic JSONL fixture, returns breaches sorted by severity.
- [ ] **Background smoke** (mandatory): synthetic workload — 1000 spans across 3 components with seeded duration distribution; verifier output matches expected percentiles within 1ms; one injected error event surfaces in `error_count` and `by_component`.
- [ ] **API smoke**: `curl localhost:3030/api/internal/verify-runtime?since=$(date -uIminutes -d '5 min ago')` returns parseable JSON with expected shape.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, smoke evidence (synthetic workload + verifier JSON output), the CONTRACT.md spec downstream beads will read, residual risks (e.g. log volume under burst load).

title: Test P0: Schema + materializer core (unit + crash-recovery + EchoAdapter smoke)
description:
## PROBLEM
The materializer core is the foundation: a bug here cascades into every adapter, every parity diff, every API response. Tests must prove the cursor invariant under crash, the per-source isolation under failure, the queue coalesce under burst load, and idempotent re-apply.

Layer: core (queue, coalesce, hashing) + boundary (SQLite tx, WAL).
Strategy: unit + crash-recovery integration + background-pipeline smoke (EchoAdapter).
Covers: forge-eorh.1, forge-eorh.2.

## SUCCESS
All assertions below pass in CI, AND the EchoAdapter smoke run shows zero double-applications across 100 triggered events.

## SCOPE
- New tests under `apps/gitboard/tests/core/xtrm-store/*.test.ts` and `apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/*.test.ts`.
- A test-only `EchoAdapter` lives at `apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/fixtures/echo-adapter.ts`.

## NON_GOALS
- No coverage of real Beads/observability adapters (T-P1, T-P2).
- No API tests (T-P1, T-P2 cover API flips).
- No UI tests in this layer.

## CONSTRAINTS
- No shared mutable state between tests (each test opens its own tmp xtrm.sqlite via mkdtemp).
- Deterministic fixtures — no `Date.now()` without injection; no random.
- Crash-recovery test must inject a real throw mid-tx, not a mock.

## VALIDATION

Assertions required (core layer DoD):
- [ ] Positive: schema migration creates every table + index; re-running is no-op.
- [ ] Positive: composite-key upsert `(repo_slug, issue_id)` deduplicates correctly.
- [ ] Negative: tombstone `deleted_at` round-trips; tombstoned rows do not reappear after re-apply.
- [ ] Property: snapshotHash is deterministic across runs and key orderings (sort-by-key invariant).
- [ ] Edge: COALESCE_MS dedup — 5 triggers in 100ms produce ≤2 runs (initial + coalesced).
- [ ] Crash-recovery: throw between writes and cursor advance → cursor stays old, partial writes rolled back, next run completes.
- [ ] Per-source isolation: source A throwing does not pause source B's queue.
- [ ] Post-commit hook: WS hint fires exactly once per successful commit; zero hints on rollback.

**Background-pipeline smoke** (mandatory per operator policy):
- [ ] EchoAdapter fed 100 events with random `(repo_slug, key)` distribution → all 100 land in xtrm.sqlite, exactly one cursor advance per coalesced batch, exactly one WS hint per commit, zero double-apply on artificial mid-run kill + restart.
- [ ] Smoke results captured as `tests/smoke/p0-materializer-smoke.log` and referenced in PR.

## OUTPUT
Test pass output, EchoAdapter smoke log, residual risks (e.g. SQLite WAL behavior under aggressive concurrent reads).

## Critical paths covered
- Cursor same-tx invariant — risk: silent data loss if cursor advances past unapplied rows
- Per-source try/catch isolation — risk: one bad source halts entire materializer
- Idempotent re-apply via composite key + tombstone — risk: duplicate or zombie rows on restart

## Known deferred paths
- Multi-writer race (only relevant if we ever break the single-writer invariant) → not testing; architectural invariant guards
- SQLite corruption recovery → out of scope; rely on backup
close_reason: All VALIDATION assertions satisfied by merged tests in .1 (xtrm-store.test.ts: schema migration, idempotency, composite-key uniqueness, tombstone roundtrip) and .2 (materializer.test.ts: COALESCE_MS dedup, crash-recovery via injected throw, per-source isolation, post-commit hook count) plus the EchoAdapter background-pipeline smoke (apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p0-materializer-smoke.ts: rowCount=100 cursorAdvances=1 hints=1). Property snapshotHash assertion is properly relocated to T-P2 since snapshot-diff lives in .6.

title: P0/1: Schema + xtrm.sqlite bootstrap
description:
## PROBLEM
Nothing else can land without the owned store and its tables. The materializer needs a writeable target; the API will read from this; cursors live here.

## SUCCESS
- New SQLite file `xtrm.sqlite` is created in `GITBOARD_DATA_DIR` on startup, alongside `gitboard.sqlite` (sidecar — both coexist until P7).
- WAL mode enabled.
- All tables created with idempotent migration runner (re-runs cleanly on every startup):
  - `substrate_issues`, `substrate_dependencies` (composite key `(repo_slug, issue_id)`; tombstone `deleted_at`)
  - `specialist_jobs`, `specialist_job_events`, `substrate_job_link` (composite key `(repo_slug, job_id)`)
  - `sources` (`source_key`, `kind`, `path`, `origin` ∈ {discovered, manual}, `status`, `discovered_at`, `last_seen_at`)
  - `materialization_state` (`source_key` PK, `cursor` JSON (per-source shape — see §8.1), `last_run_at`, `last_success_at`, `last_status`, `last_error`)
- A repo-bootstrap helper exists in `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts` mirroring the shape of `core/store.ts`.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts` (schema migrations + Database open helper).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/index.ts` (open second DB; pass to createApp).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (accept second DB in createApp signature; thread to future materializer module).

## NON_GOALS
- No materializer logic in this task.
- No API reads from `xtrm.sqlite` yet.
- No deletion or schema change to `gitboard.sqlite` / `github_*` tables.
- No data migration from `gitboard.sqlite`.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Cursor column in `materialization_state` is JSON (per-source shape varies: Beads = `{snapshot_hash}`, observability = `{updated_at_ms, event_rowid}`).
- Use `(repo_slug, issue_id)` and `(repo_slug, job_id)` composite keys exactly — per-repo IDs are not globally unique.
- Tombstones, not hard deletes, on substrate_issues.
- Migrations must be idempotent.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] `bun test apps/gitboard/tests/core/xtrm-store.test.ts` — schema creation, idempotency, composite-key uniqueness, tombstone column present.
- [ ] Boot the service; verify `xtrm.sqlite` appears next to `gitboard.sqlite`; verify `sqlite3 xtrm.sqlite '.schema'` shows all tables.
- [ ] **Smoke**: `curl /health` returns ok after boot with both DBs open (background-feature smoke — proves the second DB doesn't break startup).

## OUTPUT
Changed files, sqlite3 schema dump, test pass, smoke evidence.

title: Backend redesign: materializer-based xtrm.sqlite (per docs/backend-redesign.md)
description:
## PROBLEM

The console request path still reads Dolt + per-repo `observability.db` + JSONL live on every API call, wrapped in a fragile degradation machine (circuit breaker, warm timeouts, fallback chains). The github path is the reference implementation of "own the local copy" and is the clean part of the system. The two-pass validation in epic forge-6f0a (closed) approved the redesign in `docs/backend-redesign.md` with mods that are now applied: a single background materializer copies upstream data into one owned store `xtrm.sqlite` and the API serves only from that local copy. WS becomes pure invalidation hint.

## SUCCESS

- `xtrm.sqlite` exists, is owned and written only by xtrm, contains substrate (issues + deps), specialist (jobs + timeline), `sources`, and `materialization_state` tables.
- Materializer is the only writer; serializes per source, advances cursor in same tx as data, emits WS hint post-commit.
- Beads adapter uses snapshot-diff floor (Q1 resolved); observability adapter uses `(updated_at_ms, event_rowid)` cursor pair (Q2 resolved).
- `/api/substrate/*` is the authoritative substrate surface; `/api/specialists/*` reads from `xtrm.sqlite`; graph reads from `xtrm.sqlite`.
- `freshness` and `source_health` derived from `materialization_state`, not from request-time warm timeout.
- Dual scanners collapsed to one; dual Dolt clients collapsed to one (or shared helper).
- `/beadboard` route + `apps/beadboard` backend retired only after every consumer migrated.
- Renames (`gitboard.sqlite → xtrm.sqlite` file consolidation, `apps/gitboard → apps/xtrm`, log dir move) come last, after behavior is stable.
- E2E + smoke tests cover every visible UI surface and every background pipeline (materializer, WS spine, scanner). Unit tests alone do not gate phases — e2e/smoke must be green per phase.

## SCOPE

- New: `xtrm.sqlite` schema, materializer core, observability adapter, Beads adapter, unified scanner, `/api/substrate/*`, `sources` table API.
- Modified: `/api/specialists/*`, `/api/console/graph`, `/api/console/observability/summary`, WS channel publish points, `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/{resource.ts,ws.ts,beads-api.ts}`, `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/**`, `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx`.
- Retired: `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/{beads-change-watcher,dolt-client,project-scanner}.ts`, `apps/beadboard/**` frontend, `/beadboard` compat route, `gitboard.sqlite` file.

## NON_GOALS

- Substrate as a native issue-tracking engine. Backing source stays Beads; far-future native engine deferred indefinitely.
- Issue write-back from the UI. Materializer is read-only; the only writer of upstream state is the operator's external Beads CLI.
- Launching specialists from the UI. The integrated terminal already covers this.
- Multi-user / auth. Single-user on Tailscale is assumed.
- Renaming `forge-*` issue ID prefixes. Treat as immutable cross-system FK.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Shadow-mode first: every adapter writes to `xtrm.sqlite` alongside the live read path before the API flips. Parity is gated by a diff harness.
- `xtrm.sqlite` is created as a **sidecar** to `gitboard.sqlite` in `GITBOARD_DATA_DIR` from P0; the two files coexist until P7 folds them.
- Materializer writes + cursor advance happen in the same transaction. Crash mid-run never advances cursor past unapplied data.
- WS publish is strictly post-commit. No data is carried in WS messages that the client relies on.
- Beads cursor: snapshot-diff floor mandatory. Commit hash usable only as a *coarse negative skip signal* and only when JSONL mtime is also unchanged. Never as the actual cursor.
- Observability cursor: pair `(obs_updated_at_ms, obs_event_rowid)` advanced atomically. Job state from mutable `specialist_jobs` rows, never from folding events.
- `/beadboard` route + `apps/beadboard` backend retire only after `App.tsx`, `beads-api.ts`, and `server.ts` are all migrated.
- DB / dir renames come in the final phase. No mid-refactor renames.
- E2E + smoke tests are required gates; unit-only validation is insufficient per operator policy.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Per phase: e2e/smoke green on the surfaces touched in that phase.
- [ ] Materializer parity harness: zero diff between shadow `xtrm.sqlite` reads and current live-read path for at least one full session before each API flip.
- [ ] `bd dep cycles` clean before any phase merge.
- [ ] `sp epic status forge-7000` (replace with actual ID) reports `merge_ready` before publish.
- [ ] Final epic close: every `/api/beads/*` consumer is on `/api/substrate/*`, `apps/beadboard` is deleted, `gitboard.sqlite` is gone, log dir moved, all tests green.

## OUTPUT

Single integrated PR set (one PR per phase, or one PR per task if a phase has parallel chains). Each PR's description summarizes what landed, what the parity diff showed, which e2e/smoke ran, and any residual risk. Epic close report references each child handoff and the cumulative integration evidence.

## REFERENCES

- `docs/backend-redesign.md` (post-validation, revised §18)
- Memory: `backend-redesign-md-validation-forge-6f0a-may-2026`
- Closed validation epic: `forge-6f0a` (E1-E4 + 2 overthinker passes)
- `docs/backend.md` (current-state reference, partially stale; trust source code)
notes: Reopened 2026-05-27: prior close was operator-directive misread. Phases 1-2 (schema/materializer/adapters/UX wave + checkpoint 1 sign-off) DONE; Phases 3-7 still genuinely outstanding (15 open children: .9-.17 .21-.25 .54).

### Phase 2 — Devil’s Advocate

Remaining contradictions matter:

1. **§8.1 still says observability cursor = max event seq.**  
   Conflicts with §9.2 and §17: job state cursor must be `updated_at_ms` high-watermark + touched-row re-read. Event seq only timeline append.

   Conflicts with Q1: snapshot-diff floor mandatory; commit hash only possible negative signal; working-set fast-path only after upstream proof.

   Step says cursor columns: `beads_snapshot_hash`, `obs_updated_at_ms`. But §9.2 also needs event rowid/seq cursor for timeline append. Without it, timeline duplication/gaps risk.

- Q2 could regress into event-seq job-state cursor.

1. **Patch spec first.** Apply edits above. Convert future-state sections to target language. Resolve Q1/Q2/Q7 inline.

- **Wrong:** “same project can get different IDs.” Current scanners use same ID expression.

- **Unproven:** forge ID generator in repo source.  
- **Future, not current:** manual pins.

- WS-as-hint is right invariant.  
- Persist-then-broadcast is right invariant.  
- Tombstones protect job↔bead joins.  
- Freshness/source_health split fixes current warm-timeout confusion.  
- Keeping `resource.ts` avoids unnecessary client-cache rewrite.
