- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (.pr-*, .issue-*, .activity-* row classes — REUSE these prefixes naming for .release-*; same tokens, same sizes, same density)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (GithubState + useGithubStore — EXTEND this store, do NOT add a parallel one)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (ApiClient class — ADD getReleases method following the getPrs/getIssues signature exactly)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (existing /api/github/* endpoints — ADD /releases handler in this same file, do NOT create new router file)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (getEvents/getRepos helpers — ADD getReleases(db, filters) here, do NOT create a new store module)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubEvent shape with type/repo/actor/title/body/url — ADD GithubRelease interface here, derive from ReleaseEvent payload)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (verify ReleaseEvent payload shape — what fields are populated for type='ReleaseEvent' rows; tag/name/body live in payload)

SUCCESS
- New "Releases" tab appears in GithubPanel TabBar, sibling to Activity/PRs/Issues. Same styling (font, accent underline on active). Tab count shows current release count for the active filter.
- Clicking Releases renders <ReleaseTimeline releases={releases} /> in the same content area where PrTimeline/IssueTimeline mount.
- ReleaseTimeline.tsx:
  - Uses @tanstack/react-virtual virtualizer (mirror PrTimeline structure)
  - Uses buildDateGroupedItems + DayHeader from timeline-utils (mirror PrTimeline rendering of day groups)
  - Each row shows: TagIcon (left, --accent-blue/green tone), tag/name, repo, actor, relative time
  - Expand-in-place dossier: shows release body via renderPrBodyText (IMPORTED from PrTimeline), plus "Open on GitHub" external link

- ReleaseTimeline test: smoke for empty + 1 row + expand renders body via the same renderPrBodyText path (mock via existing test patterns; see PrTimeline.test.tsx if it exists, else IssueTimeline.test.tsx)
- /api/github/releases endpoint: GET ?repo=...&limit=...&offset=... returns { releases: GithubRelease[] }. Default limit 50, ordered created_at DESC. 400 if no repo on bad params (mirror /api/github/prs error handling).
- github-store.ts: new getReleases(db, filters) selects events where type='ReleaseEvent', parses payload JSON to a GithubRelease shape.
- stores/github.ts: extends GithubState with `releases: GithubRelease[]`, `setReleases(...)`, fetched the same way activity/prs/issues are fetched (see existing fetch effects in App.tsx or whatever mounts the store).
- client.ts: new getReleases(params) following getPrs signature pattern.
- types/github.ts: new GithubRelease interface (id, tag_name, name, body, html_url, author_login, published_at, repo_full_name).

SCOPE

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- IMPORT renderPrBodyText from PrTimeline.tsx; do NOT write a new markdown renderer.
- IMPORT buildDateGroupedItems, formatRelativeTime, truncateBody from timeline-utils.ts; do NOT inline copies.
- IMPORT @primer/octicons-react TagIcon; do NOT add other icon families.
- REUSE existing CSS tokens (var(--accent-*), var(--text-*), var(--border-*)); add NO new variables or palettes.

NON_GOALS

CONSTRAINTS

VALIDATION

title: Console rail polish: derive owner/repo for beads + display name + click-to-select
description:
PROBLEM
Post-z6j.1 the /console rail renders beads-only entries as UUIDs (e.g. "0494245c-...") and the github/beads merge never collides because beads ProjectScanner uses uuid as project.id while github uses owner/repo. Rows are also non-clickable so the empty right pane never changes. Result: the scaffold renders but feels broken to the user.

REFERENCES
- Parent: forge-z6j (epic)
- Sibling: forge-z6j.1 (scaffold; SHIPPED)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (mergeConsoleRepos uses project.id as full_name — line 20 — wrong key)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (ProjectScanner; check if it already reads git remote)
- apps/console/src/App.tsx (rail renders repo.full_name verbatim; no click handler)
- apps/console/src/types/console.ts (ConsoleRepoEntry shape)
- apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (existing .console-repo-row class)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (merge test to extend)

SUCCESS
- Beads project entries in /api/console/repos return a `full_name` derived from the project's git remote when available (`owner/repo` parsed from `git -C <path> config --get remote.origin.url` OR by reading `.git/config` directly — no new dependency). Fall back to `project.name` when no remote.
- When github and beads point at the same `owner/repo`, the response has ONE entry with both `github` and `beads` populated.
- New `display_name` field on ConsoleRepoEntry: github.display_name || beads.name || full_name (computed server-side).
- /console rail renders `entry.display_name` (NOT raw `full_name`), with `full_name` as a smaller mono subtitle when they differ.
- Clicking a rail row sets a `selectedRepo` state in App.tsx; the right pane shows the selected repo header (display_name + full_name + a "Modules" placeholder block listing future tabs as disabled chips: Activity, PRs, Issues, Releases, Beads, Reports).
- Existing tests still pass; new test asserts a beads project whose path has remote `Jaggerxtrm/gitboard` merges with the github repo of the same full_name.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (derive owner/repo from beads project; populate display_name; rewrite mergeConsoleRepos to key on derived full_name)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/types/console.ts (add display_name?: string)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/App.tsx (display_name in rail, click handler, selectedRepo state, right-pane header + disabled module chips)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (active row state, subtitle style, repo header, disabled chip cluster)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (cover remote-derivation merge + display_name)
- NEW (if needed): apps/gitboard/src/core/git-remote.ts (tiny helper: read .git/config or invoke `git -C <path> remote get-url origin`, return parsed owner/repo or null)

NON_GOALS
- No actual module content (PRs/Issues/Releases/Beads/Reports tabs still come from later beads — render disabled chips only).
- No edits to ProjectScanner core behavior (read-only helper; do not change project.id semantics in DB).
- No new deps. Use Bun.file / fs / child_process built-ins.
- No CSS framework swap.
- No new icon usage beyond what apps/console already imports.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode.
- Octicons only (already imported); do not add other icon families.
- Mono font only on the small full_name subtitle when full_name has a "/" (i.e. looks like owner/repo); never on the display_name.
- Single --accent token.
- Git remote parsing must handle both `https://github.com/owner/repo.git` and `git@github.com:owner/repo.git`; strip trailing `.git`.
- If reading .git/config: use Bun.file (this is a Bun project, see apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md). Avoid `child_process` if a file read suffices — faster + no shell escape risk.
- Selected repo state may be in a URL query param too (?repo=owner/repo) so deep links work; if scope permits, sync state with location.search.
- Disabled chips must use existing tokens (e.g. border-color: var(--border-muted); color: var(--text-muted)).

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` green; new merge test passes asserting a beads project at a path with remote Jaggerxtrm/gitboard merges with the github repo of full_name Jaggerxtrm/gitboard into one ConsoleRepoEntry with both `github` and `beads` set.
- `cd apps/console && bun run build` green.
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build` green.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` shows no new errors attributable to scope files.
- Manual smoke (orchestrator runs): /api/console/repos response has at least 1 entry with BOTH github AND beads (will hit because Jaggerxtrm/gitboard exists both ways).

OUTPUT
- File diff list + key snippets
- /api/console/repos sample showing one merged entry (github+beads)
- Confirmation that the rail click sets the right pane and the disabled chips render
- Any residual risks (e.g. repos without remote stay keyed by project.name; non-github remotes show full URL or fall back)

title: Unify gitboard + beadboard under one /console shell
description:
PROBLEM
gitboard and beadboard run as two separate apps under apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/. The user has to switch URL/tab to move between GitHub work and beads work, and any shared chrome (rail, topbar, search, density) drifts between the two. A pre-existing bd memory describes a planned /console shell but no /console app exists in apps/.

REFERENCES
- bd memory: xtrm-console-now-uses-console-as-the-primary (stale until this epic lands)
- bd memory: xtrm-console-visual-contract-bg-181818-single-accent
- Current row + dossier primitives shipped on this branch: forge-l85, forge-wky, forge-zg3, forge-7n6, forge-8it
- Existing apps: apps/gitboard/ (Bun+Hono+Vite, port 3010), apps/beadboard/ (Vite-only, currently served via gitboard server in production mode)
- Existing mounting: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts serves /gitboard, /beadboard SPA HTMLs when NODE_ENV=production

SUCCESS
- A new apps/console/ workspace builds and is mounted at GET /console by apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts.
- /console has one shell: single topbar (brand + repo search + status), single unified rail (GitHub repos + beads projects, merged by repo full_name), and module tabs: Activity · PRs · Issues · Releases · Beads (Feed/Board/Triage/Closed) · Reports.
- All modules render through the row + dossier primitives already on this branch (no per-module CSS drift).
- /gitboard and /beadboard remain mounted as legacy redirects to the corresponding /console module + repo filter.

SCOPE (this is the EPIC — child beads carry SCOPE files)
- apps/console/ (new workspace, vite+react)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (add /console mount, redirect /gitboard /beadboard)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (new: /api/console/repos merging /api/github/repos + /api/beads/projects keyed by repo full_name)
- packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (already canonical; no changes expected)

NON_GOALS
- No new auth.
- No backend re-architecture; keep Bun+Hono+single process model.
- No DB schema changes; merging happens at API layer.
- No removal of apps/gitboard or apps/beadboard until /console reaches parity (legacy redirects stay).
- No mobile-first rework; desktop dark UI only.

CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse existing components (IssueFeed, TriagePanel, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ActivityTimeline, RepoSidebar) — adapt props, don't fork.
- Single accent (--accent), no extra palettes.
- No charts (per existing contract).
- Octicons only (per forge-hl1).
- Mono restricted (per forge-m38).

VALIDATION
- bun run build:dashboard green for apps/console.

OUTPUT

title: Beadboard: dossier width inconsistent because module-list grid doesn't span
description: .module-list uses display:grid without grid-template-columns, so each implicit grid row sizes to fit content (longest child). Dossier 75% width is then computed against the row's narrower box, not viewport. Fix: grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) (or switch to flex column).
close_reason: module-list now grid-template-columns minmax(0,1fr); rows fill row width

title: Beadboard refinements: single-line rows, text types, centered dossier, fix \n escape
description: User feedback bundle. (1) Row anatomy single-line by default — title and meta inline, wrap only on narrow viewports. (2) Replace type-mark Octicon with the actual text label (Bug/Task/Epic/Feature/Chore). (3) Expanded dossier: drop the right sidebar; single centered column ~720px max; order = Metadata → Description → Notes → Labels → Dependency tree. (4) Markdown: unescape literal '\\n' in description (some sources store JSON-escaped newlines). (5) Make sure backtick code and **bold** render in description, not only notes.
close_reason: Single-line rows, text type label, centered single-col dossier (metadata→description→labels→tree), JSON-escaped newline unescape

title: Beadboard: dep tree id wraps + child titles empty
description: Two issues visible after forge-7n6: (1) Long bead ids in the dep tree wrap to two lines and the title overlaps — need white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + ellipsis on .bead-dep-tree-id (or max-width). (2) Child dep rows show id + [kind] but title column is empty. Check API response for dependencies — may need to fetch full issue per dep id or accept that title can be empty and hide the title cell when blank.
close_reason: id no longer wraps; dep titles resolved from loaded issues map

title: Beadboard: show dep IDs + tree like bd dep tree
description: User feedback: rows show '1 deps' but not which bead. Mimic bd dep tree output. (1) In the row meta-right, replace 'N deps' with up to 2 short bead-id chips + kind hint (↪ for blocks, ↩ for blocked_by, ⊃ for parent-child). (2) In the dossier, add a 'Dependency tree' block: root = current issue, children = each dep with kind label like [blocks] [blocked_by] [parent-child] [discovered-from], status check/dot, tree connectors. (3) If a dep id matches a loaded issue, recurse one level. Octicons only; no charts.
close_reason: Inline dep chips show bead IDs + kind glyph; dossier has dependency tree

title: Beadboard: Triage segment backed by bv-style signals
description: Add a new 'Triage' segment to the module-header alongside Feed/Board/Closed/Memories. Renders from loaded data: 1) Top picks ranked by priority+staleness+open count (replicate bv's score heuristic client-side), 2) Quick wins (open, low priority cost, no blockers), 3) Stale issues (no activity 7d+), 4) Velocity (closed in last 7/30 days from closedIssues), 5) Project health counts by status/type/priority. No backend changes required — all computed from existing endpoints.

title: Epic: Normalize Gitboard and Beadboard visual system
description:
## Overview

Normalize Gitboard and Beadboard to the xtrm Console Minimal FUI contract before any further shell unification. This is a visual-system convergence pass: both apps should look like tabs of one professional operations console while remaining separate, stable routes during the migration.

## Goals

- Gitboard and Beadboard share the same #181818 base, square edges, border language, typography, spacing, and row rhythm.
- PR rows and bead issue rows use the same list-first anatomy.
- PR expanded detail and bead expanded dossier use the same section rhythm.
- Existing /gitboard and /beadboard functionality remains stable.

## Non-goals

- No /console route resurrection in this epic.
- No iframe embedding.
- No shared repo rail yet.
- No backend data-model rewrite.
- No mutating GitHub or Beads actions.

## Success criteria

- [ ] docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md is followed.
- [ ] Gitboard and Beadboard no longer have conflicting palettes or rounded-card styling.
- [ ] Beadboard feed visually matches Gitboard PR/activity list spacing and shapes.
- [ ] Both dashboard builds pass.
- [ ] Preview confirms /gitboard and /beadboard remain functional independently.

## Context

Visual contract: docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md
Key areas: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard, apps/beadboard/src/dashboard

title: Fix Beadboard feed fidelity gaps
description: PROBLEM: Current Beadboard feed still misses key UX requirements from user review: issue bodies/notes are not rendered as clean markdown sections, feed/dossiers do not visually match Gitboard expandable list behavior, project scanner includes worktrees/nested repos instead of base repos only, epic children are not visually nested under epics, and dependency/blocked-by/blocks relationships are not shown clearly like the reference Beads viewers. SUCCESS: Beadboard shows a Gitboard-like collapsed inline feed by default with clean expandable dossiers, markdown-rendered description/notes split into clear sections, base-repo-only project discovery, epic child indentation/grouping, and explicit dependency panels for blocked-by/blocks/related/parent-child. SCOPE: apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts, apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts, apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts, apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts, apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts, apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx, apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/*. NON_GOALS: no mutating Beads actions, no broad Gitboard redesign, no reference repo edits. CONSTRAINTS: preserve Board tab, preserve source fallback behavior, no worktree/nested repo pollution, no emojis, match grounded Gitboard FUI. VALIDATION: bun run --cwd apps/beadboard build:dashboard; inspect API detail payload has notes/deps/children where available; manually verify feed collapsed by default, expands inline, markdown renders, epics group children, dependencies visible.
close_reason: Done: fixed Beadboard feed fidelity gaps, markdown/dossier sections, dependency/children visibility, base-repo scanner filtering, production asset serving, scan timeout, and compact #181818 polish.

title: Create beadboard app - Beads Issue Tracking Dashboard
description:
Phase 2 of monorepo expansion:

## Goals
- Create apps/beadboard/ as a second dashboard for beads issue tracking
- Implement multi-project discovery (find .beads/ directories)
- Build Kanban board UI matching gitboard design system
- Add closed issues section
- Add memory panel (bd memories)
- Implement PR auto-linking from worktrees/branches
- Track agent sessions from interactions.jsonl

## Tech Stack (matching gitboard)
- Runtime: Bun
- HTTP: Hono
- UI: React 19 + Vite + Tailwind v4
- State: Zustand
- Icons: @primer/octicons-react
- Use @omniforge/core, @omniforge/ui packages

## Key Features
1. Project sidebar - list discovered beads projects
2. Kanban board - Open → In Progress → Blocked → Closed
3. Closed issues section - collapsible, shows recent closed
4. Memory panel - bd memories integration
5. Agent badges - show which agent worked on issue
6. PR links - auto-detect from worktrees and commits

## DDD Approach
- Domain: BeadIssue, BeadsProject, Memory, AgentSession, LinkedPr
- Application: ProjectScanner, BeadsReader, PrLinker, AgentTracker
- Infrastructure: SQLite metadata, dolt connection, file watcher
close_reason:
Beadboard app complete with all key features:
- Project sidebar: Discovers .beads/ directories via ProjectScanner
- Kanban board: 4-column layout (Open/In Progress/Blocked/Closed)
- Closed issues section: ClosedIssuesPanel with agent badges
- Memory panel: Reads from knowledge.jsonl
- Agent badges: Shows Claude/Qwen/Gemini badges on cards
- Real dolt connection: DoltClient queries live database
- 40 tests passing (core + store + dolt-client)

Remaining features filed as separate issues:
- forge-1kv: PR auto-linking from worktrees
- forge-yhm: WebSocket live events

notes:
### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job e360b8) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/tests/core/unified-scanner.live.test.ts`, `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/sources.test.ts`, `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/settings-sources-panel.e2e.test.tsx`, `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-unified-scanner-smoke.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/settings/SourcesPanel.tsx`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/sources.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/settings-sources-panel.e2e.test.tsx`
  - Issue: global DOM/window swap not fully restored. `beforeEach` overwrites `globalThis.window/document/navigator/...`, but `afterEach` only restores `fetch`.
  - Why it matters: later tests in same worker can inherit dirty DOM state, causing cross-test flake.
  - Suggested executor instruction: save original globals before overwrite and restore all of them in `afterEach`.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-unified-scanner-smoke.ts`
  - Issue: fixed `setTimeout(..., 180)` sleep drives timer assertion.
  - Why it matters: timer smoke can go flaky under slower CI or loaded runners; 180ms is tight for event-loop jitter.
  - Suggested executor instruction: poll for source row with bounded retry/backoff instead of single hard sleep.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/settings-sources-panel.e2e.test.tsx`
  - Issue: mocked fetch is very broad and stateful; one shared branch mutates `sources` for all requests, no request-shape assertions.
  - Why it matters: test can pass even if UI sends wrong payload/order, and hidden coupling makes future changes harder to debug.
  - Suggested executor instruction: narrow mock per endpoint with explicit request assertions for pin/refresh/delete payloads.

## Non-findings
- Did not flag runner split (`bun test` for node + vitest/happy-dom for UI): reasonable boundary for current mix of node-only smoke and DOM-heavy component flow.
- Did not flag `as unknown as typeof fetch` / typed query casts: test-only boundary, ugly but not production risk here.

## Handoff
Proceed to reviewer with 3 concrete stability fixes if executor can still patch. If no more edits, reviewer can gate on these flake risks.

description: Read-only smell/type-safety pass for forge-eorh.10 executor diff (job 791ced): pin CRUD endpoints, settings Sources panel, App tab wiring, focused tests. Check for broad App rewrites, brittle path/source_key handling, and maintainability. Do not edit files.

- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx:24-36`
  - Issue: `App()` mutates shell store during render off `window.location.pathname`.
  - Why it matters: render-phase state writes can retrigger renders, make routing brittle, and hide update loops when path changes.
  - Suggested executor instruction: move pathname→store sync into `useEffect` or routing layer; keep render pure.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/sources.ts:13-29, 60-66`
  - Issue: source_key parsing uses raw `kind:path` split on first `:`.
  - Why it matters: path/source_key handling breaks if path contains `:` or other delimiter edge cases; delete/history logic can misclassify source kind/path.
  - Suggested executor instruction: stop deriving semantics from raw `source_key`; store kind/path separately in delete/history flow or escape/encode key components before concatenation.

## Non-findings
- Not flagging broad `SourcesPanel` fetch/reload flow; simple enough.
- Not flagging test shape; focused CRUD coverage is fine.
- GitNexus blast-radius check unavailable in current tool set; file-level inspection used instead.

Proceed to reviewer after fixing 2 smells. If executor wants, rescan after render-pure routing and safer source-key handling land.

## Summary

Scoped audit done for `UnifiedScanner` and `/api/sources`. Verdict: **FINDINGS**. Main risk: local filesystem path disclosure plus unauthenticated expensive refresh path.

## Status

findings

## Security Audit Summary

- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/sources.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts`, legacy compare in `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, tests in `apps/gitboard/tests/core/unified-scanner.test.ts`
- Overall risk: **medium**
- Mode(s) used: static secure-code review, bounty-style exploitability triage
- Commands/sources used:
  - `find`, `grep`, `read`
  - `bun test apps/gitboard/tests/core/unified-scanner.test.ts --runInBand`

## Findings

- ID: SA-001
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-200 Exposure of Sensitive Information
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/sources.ts:8-18` returns scanner rows verbatim
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts:77-78, 111, 158, 186-187` stores and returns absolute `path` / `dbPath`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:120, 150, 214-216` enables global CORS, mounts `/api/sources`, binds `0.0.0.0` in production by default
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - No auth check on `GET /api/sources`
  - Any reachable browser/client can read repo paths, `.beads` locations, observability DB paths if server exposed
- Impact:
  - Leaks local usernames, repo layout, mounted directories, worktree naming, observability DB placement
  - Good recon for later attacks, privacy leak in multi-user/shared-host setups
- Recommended fix:
  - Gate `/api/sources` behind same admin/local policy as other sensitive console endpoints
  - Remove raw absolute paths from API by default; return stable IDs plus redacted/relative labels
  - Tighten CORS for sensitive routes
- Verification after fix:
  - Unauthenticated `GET /api/sources` returns 401/403 or redacted payload
  - Response no longer contains absolute host paths

- ID: SA-002
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/sources.ts:15-18` unauthenticated `POST /refresh`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts:68-74, 81-87, 103-117, 134-149` refresh triggers full beads + observability scan and DB upsert
  - `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts:10, 12-19, 51-60` default observability roots expand `~/dev/*` and `~/projects/*`
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Remote caller can spam refresh endpoint
  - Scan breadth depends on host filesystem size under configured roots
- Impact:
  - Repeated disk walks, SQLite churn, log noise, latency spikes
  - Worse on developer hosts or prod nodes with many repos/worktrees
- Recommended fix:
  - Require auth/admin/local-only access for `/api/sources/refresh`
  - Add rate limit / debounce / single-flight guard around `refresh()`
  - Bound roots explicitly in prod; avoid broad wildcard defaults for server mode
- Verification after fix:
  - Repeated refresh requests get 401/403 or 429
  - Concurrent refresh calls collapse to one active scan
  - Large repo roots no longer cause unbounded request-time scan cost

- ID: SA-003
- Severity: low
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-668 Exposure to Wrong Sphere / hardening gap
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts:103-117` recurses by directory name only
  - Missing legacy worktree checks present in `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts:61-63, 116-137`
  - Unified scanner excludes literal `.worktrees`/`worktrees` names at `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts:35, 115`, but does not detect git worktree repos via `.git` file indirection
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Not request-parameter traversal
  - Triggered by filesystem layout under scan roots
- Impact:
  - Can index git worktree clones or alternate repo layouts legacy scanner skipped
  - Increases accidental exposure of transient/private worktree paths through `/api/sources`
  - Adds extra scan breadth and duplicate source noise
- Recommended fix:
  - Port legacy `isWorktreePath` and `isGitWorktree` logic into `UnifiedScanner`
  - Consider `realpath`-based root containment and explicit symlink/worktree deny rules
- Verification after fix:
  - Scanner skips repos whose `.git` is worktree indirection file
  - Tests cover `.worktrees`, `worktrees`, git worktree clones, symlinked dirs

## Dependency Advisory Triage

- Package: none audited in this scoped pass
- Installed version: n/a
- Advisory/source: n/a
- Vulnerable range: n/a
- Fixed version: n/a
- Reachability: unknown
- Recommendation: no dependency CVE triage needed for this source-only scope

## Non-findings / Dropped Noise

- No request-driven path traversal found in `/api/sources`. Route takes no user path input.
- No command injection sink found in scanner code.
- Direct symlink traversal not proven from current code path; `Dirent.isDirectory()` usually excludes symlink entries. Risk kept on worktree-detection gap, not generic symlink claim.
- Root expansion in observability config comes from env/settings/admin config, not request input. Dangerous if misconfigured, but not standalone vuln in this scope.

## Risks

- `/api/sources/refresh` allows unauthenticated expensive scan
- Unified scanner misses legacy git-worktree skip logic

## Follow-ups

- Add auth/local-only guard to `/api/sources` and `/api/sources/refresh`
- Redact or remove absolute paths from API payload
- Port legacy worktree detection into `UnifiedScanner`
- Add focused tests for route auth, refresh throttling, git worktree skip, redaction

title: Confirm gitboard backend-redesign work fits package-default chain_templates + inventory custom specialists
description:
MANDATE: This is the PER-REPO investigation. Specialists repo defines DEFAULT chain_templates using only config/specialists/ (package-tier) roles. This investigation tests whether gitboard's heavy backend-redesign work (forge-eorh epic with 30+ children) fits those defaults cleanly, or whether gitboard needs per-repo custom chain_templates.

INPUTS:
- .specialists/user/*.specialist.json — list all repo-custom specialists with name, permission, model, description (likely empty or minimal)
- .xtrm/reports/*.md — mine ALL reports (especially the recent forge-eorh.* heavy work in 2026-05-21..26) for chain shapes that recurred
- Cross-reference docs/design/specialists-friction-audit.md §3, §12 (sp epic decoration — gitboard heavily affected by sp epic merge failures per the friction patterns in §12.3)
- substrate-review §25.3 (initial 6 package-default templates as STARTING POINT)

OUTPUTS: Findings memo with these sections:
(1) Custom specialist inventory in .specialists/user/ — if empty, say so; if any exist, name + description
(2) Chain shapes observed in forge-eorh work + other recent backend-redesign chains — frequency table, especially looking for: executor + code-sanity + reviewer baseline (code-standard fit?); executor + ONLY reviewer (code-quick degenerate?); chains skipping advisory passes; chains using debugger as bookend
(3) Verdict: do the 6 package defaults (code-quick, code-standard, code-with-advisors, debug, quant-validation, security-deep) cover gitboard's needs adequately? Identify any MISSING template that gitboard would benefit from (e.g., 'observability-instrumentation' for the materializer work, or 'cold-start-bootstrap' for runtime work)
(4) sp epic merge failure patterns from gitboard specifically — cross-reference §12.3 of the friction audit. Were there cases gitboard had to manual-git after sp epic merge wedged? How often?
(5) Recommendations: if gitboard would benefit from per-repo chain_templates, propose 1-2 with bd-formula-compatible name + extends + added steps. If gitboard fits defaults cleanly, say so.

SCOPE: read-only investigation in this repo (~/dev/gitboard). Cite file:line for every claim from reports. Cap at 30 reports if total exceeds.

NON_GOALS: do NOT design new specialists; do NOT write the actual .formula.yaml files; do NOT mine reports from OTHER repos.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job ce88c7) [WAITING — more output may follow]

Now compile the findings memo. I have sufficient evidence from the reports.

## Summary

Gitboard backend-redesign work (forge-eorh epic, 30+ children) heavily uses the executor+reviewer baseline. No repo-custom specialists exist. The 6 package defaults cover most patterns, but observability-materializer chains showed consistent schema-alignment complexity. sp merge fails intermittently due to bd auto-export race on `.beads/issues.jsonl` — documented in ~50% of chains.

## Findings Memo

### (1) Custom Specialist Inventory

**Result: EMPTY**

- `.specialists/user/` path does not exist in the worktree
- No `*.specialist.json` files found anywhere in the repo
- All specialist roles are package-tier defaults from `.xtrm/registry.json`

**Verdict:** gitboard uses only default specialists — no repo-custom roles defined.

---

### (2) Chain Shapes Observed (forge-eorh + Recent Backend-Redesign)

**Source:** `.xtrm/reports/2026-05-{21,22,23,26}*.md` (forge-eorh, forge-vi4j, forge-3ue5)

| Chain Shape | Frequency | Examples |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| executor + code-sanity + reviewer | 6-8 chains | forge-eorh.48/.49/.55 (multi-file materializer diffs) |
| executor + reviewer only | 15+ chains | forge-eorh.58/.61/.62/.64; forge-vi4j 10/11 chains |
| executor only (reviewer skipped) | 2 cases | forge-eorh.66/.70 (one-char fix, CSS-only) |
| executor + debugger + reviewer | 2-3 chains | forge-eorh.68 (explorer → chip mapping fix) |
| security-auditor (multi-pass) | 3 chains | forge-lqyc.8 terminal security audit (FAIL→PARTIAL→PASS) |
| code-sanity advisory only | 5+ chains | forge-eorh.50/.52/.56 (advisory dispatch, findings applied) |

**Key observations:**

- **code-standard (executor + code-sanity + reviewer)** used for multi-file production diffs with schema alignment complexity
- **code-quick (executor + reviewer)** dominates — 70%+ of chains
- **Chains skipping advisory passes:** Most chains skip code-sanity; used selectively for schema-complex diffs
- **Debugger as bookend:** forge-eorh.68 used explorer/debugger for investigation before fix chain

**Evidence from 2026-05-26 (forge-eorh):**
```
| forge-eorh.48 | executor cc5fcc + code-sanity d6eacc + reviewer 7b3775 | PASS |
| forge-eorh.49 | executor cac928 + code-sanity 52b037 + reviewer a77dc8 | PASS 96 |
| forge-eorh.55 | executor d759c9 + code-sanity e1812c + reviewer 95e7f8 | PASS |
| forge-eorh.58 | executor 581197 + reviewer 0040bf | PASS 95 |
| forge-eorh.61 | executor 7b66ea + reviewer 95e7f8 | PASS |
| forge-eorh.62 | executor 8eb681 + reviewer be6d42 | PASS 95 |
```

---

### (3) Verdict: Do Package Defaults Cover Gitboard's Needs?

**Partial coverage — gaps identified.**

**COVERED:**
| Template | Gitboard Usage Evidence |
|----------|------------------------|
| code-quick | ✓ 15+ chains, dominant pattern |
| code-standard | ✓ 6-8 chains for multi-file schema diffs |
| debug | ✓ forge-eorh.68 explorer chain |
| security-deep | ✓ forge-lqyc.8 terminal security audit (3-pass) |

**MISSING / WEAK FIT:**

| Gap | Evidence | Rationale |
|-----|----------|-----------|
| **observability-materializer** | forge-eorh.48/.49/.58/.61/.62/.64 | Schema-alignment issues (obs.db REAL schema vs assumed), cursor semantics, epoch.bump wiring — all required multiple fix loops |
| **cold-start-bootstrap** | forge-eorh.49 (eager bootstrap), forge-9qmk (listRepos cache), forge-ijzx (attach pool blocking) | Performance/HA chains with specific lazy-eager patterns |
| **gate-rollback** | forge-eorh.73 (P0) | Gate flip → regression → rollback → fix → re-flip; operational flow no template covers |

**Recommended Per-Repo Templates (if package defaults are extendable):**

**1. `observability-materializer`**
```yaml
name: observability-materializer
extends: code-standard
added_steps:
  - verify_obs_db_schema_alignment  # Check mutable DB reads match REAL schema
  - epoch_bump_wiring_check         # Ensure cache invalidation is wired
constraints:
  - multi_file_diff: required       # Always code-sanity + reviewer
```

**2. `gate-rollback`** (optional, lower priority)
```yaml
name: gate-rollback
extends: code-quick
added_steps:
  - staging_verify_5min             # 5+ minute prod-like verification
  - gate_flip_command               # GITBOARD_XTRM_PATH=1
  - rollback_on_regression_detected
```

---

### (4) sp Epic Merge Failure Patterns (Gitboard Specific)

**Cross-reference note:** The `docs/design/specialists-friction-audit.md` file does not exist in this worktree. Cannot directly cite §12.3.

**Evidence from reports (§12.3 equivalence):**

**2026-05-26 (forge-eorh epic):**
- `.xtrm/reports/2026-05-26-ee14f45.md:~180-200`
- `sp merge forge-eorh.48` failed with "Merge conflict" even though `git merge-tree` clean
- Root cause: **bd auto-export race** — `.beads/issues.jsonl` written mid-operation despite being in `MERGE_DIRTY_IGNORE_PREFIXES`
- Resolution: manual squash-merge for 5/9 chains
- Pattern recurred: forge-eorh.48/.49/.58/.61/.62

**2026-05-21 (forge-vi4j HA epic):**
- `.xtrm/reports/2026-05-21-42192f57.md:~150`
- `sp merge` rejected every chain with "Merge conflict while merging"
- Fell back to manual squash-merge for all 11 chains
- Memory saved: `sp-merge-intermittently-fails-with-merge-conflict-while`

**2026-05-19 (forge-3ue5 epic):**
- `.xtrm/reports/2026-05-19-9509215.md:~420` (main orchestrator section)
- `sp merge` failed on `.beads/issues.jsonl` conflict after every successful chain
- Orchestrator mutated main's bd state while specialist worktree open
- Resolution: selective `git checkout <executor-checkpoint> -- <paths>`

**Failure frequency table:**

| Session | Epic | sp merge success | sp merge fallback | Fallback rate |
|---------|------|------------------|-------------------|---------------|
| 2026-05-26 | forge-eorh | 4/9 | 5/9 | 56% |
| 2026-05-21 | forge-vi4j | 0/11 | 11/11 | 100% |
| 2026-05-19 | forge-3ue5 | ~0 | 23/23 | ~100% |
| 2026-05-18 | forge-dgd chain | 0 | 1 | 100% (tsc gate) |

**Manual-git after sp epic wedged:**
- **Yes, consistently.** Reports explicitly cite:
  - "fell back to manual squash-merge for all 11 chains"
  - "selective git checkout per chain; never tried sp merge again this session"
  - "manual squash-merge pattern (per saved memory `sp-merge-intermittently-fails-with-merge-conflict-while`)"

**Root cause consistent across sessions:**
- bd auto-export writes `.beads/issues.jsonl` during sp merge's index check
- Orchestrator-side bd mutations (bead create/close/deps) race with worktree-side writes
- `MERGE_DIRTY_IGNORE_PREFIXES` does not prevent the race

---

### (5) Recommendations

**Verdict: gitboard would benefit from 1-2 per-repo chain_templates.**

**Proposal 1: `observability-materializer`** (extends code-standard)
- **Trigger:** Multi-file diff touching `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/`, `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`
- **Added steps:**
  1. Schema alignment verification (check mutable DB reads match REAL obs.db schema)
  2. epoch.bump wiring check (ensure cache invalidation wired after COMMIT)
  3. Stale-empty recovery test (probe + verify chips preserved)
- **Rationale:** 6+ chains in forge-eorh had schema-alignment issues; code-standard baseline passed but didn't catch REAL schema drift

**Proposal 2: `gate-rollback`** (extends code-quick)
- **Trigger:** Beads labeled `gate-flip`, `rollback`, or prod-config changes
- **Added steps:**
  1. 5+ minute staging verify with `XDG_PROJECTS_DIR` override
  2. Prod probe checklist (errs=0, success count, substrate count)
  3. Explicit rollback command sequence documented in bead
- **Rationale:** forge-eorh.73 (P0) shows this pattern; currently ad-hoc

**Alternative: If per-repo chain_templates are not supported, document these patterns in `CLAUDE.md` or `.xtrm/config/chain-conventions.md`.**

**Non-recommendation:**
- No new specialist roles needed — package-tier executor/reviewer/code-sanity/debugger/security-auditor cover the role surface
- No need for `quant-validation` or `code-with-advisors` templates — gitboard chains don't match these shapes

---

title: Feed flicker still happens on bd create + sp dispatch — forge-dymv fix didn't cover all refetch paths
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-dymv landed (SWR refetch in BeadsRepoView.load()), the operator still observes the feed blanking + reappearing on a fresh bd create + sp run dispatch sequence.

The dymv fix preserved issues/memories/interactions on transient API failures during the load() function (BeadsRepoView.tsx:170-190). But a flicker is still visible — meaning there's at least one more refetch path that still wipes the list.

## EVIDENCE
Operator: 'just saw the feed blank and reappear on this bd create + dispatch.' Observed at ~23:45 CEST on 2026-05-26, immediately after creating forge-221d-executor probe bead.

## HYPOTHESES
1. listProjects() at BeadsRepoView.tsx:155 still throws/returns-empty momentarily → findProject returns undefined → state goes to INITIAL (which DOES preserve on non-switch refetch since the dymv fix line 165, but there might be a code path where isProjectSwitch is incorrectly true)
2. A different component re-mounts on refetch (e.g., IssueFeed or KanbanBoard) and reinitializes its own state
3. A separate hook (useChain, useRepoTree) refetches and clears its own state visible in the UI
4. The reloadKey++ pattern triggers a full re-render that briefly shows the initial state before useEffect resolves

## SCOPE TO INVESTIGATE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx — check if isProjectSwitch can be true on a same-project reloadKey++
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts — the repo tree refetch path
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx — if sidebar re-renders affect the feed
- Look for any 'loading: true' setState calls that show an empty/spinner state

## SUCCESS
- bd create / bd close from terminal causes no visible flicker
- React DevTools: confirm no setState that empties issues during refetch path

## SCRUTINY
low

## DISCOVERED FROM
forge-dymv close-out walkthrough
notes:
## ROOT CAUSE (located 2026-05-26 23:55)

After the forge-dymv SWR fix, BeadsRepoView.load() correctly preserves state on transient failures. But the flicker still happens on **every successful refetch** because:

1. load() calls setState with a fresh issuesResult — a brand new array reference (even when content is identical to before).
2. IssueFeed has many useMemos with 'issues' / 'closedIssues' references in their dep arrays: inProgressIssues, topLevelIssues, activeChildren, blockingChildren, closedChildren, completedIssues. ALL of these recompute on every refetch because the issues reference changed.
3. The  useMemo depends on all of those — also recomputes.
4. @tanstack/react-virtual's useVirtualizer sees a new items array → re-renders all visible rows briefly (mount/unmount cycle).
5. The visible artifact: rows disappear for ~1-2 frames, then reappear with the same content. 'feed disappeared then reappeared'.

Plus: load() fires every ~30s (BeadsChangeWatcher poll) AND on every sync_hint. So flickers happen constantly during active use.

## FIX (stale-while-revalidate with content equality)

In BeadsRepoView.load(), when API succeeds, compare new arrays against current.issues / current.memories / current.interactions by content. If contents are identical, KEEP THE PREVIOUS REFERENCE so the downstream useMemos don't invalidate.

Implementation:
- Helper: `function sameIssues(a, b): boolean` that compares length, then per-row id+status+priority+updated_at+labels (a stable content signature).
- In setState:
  ```ts
  issues: issuesResult == null
    ? (current.project?.id === project.id ? current.issues : [])
    : sameIssues(issuesResult, current.issues)
      ? current.issues  // KEEP REFERENCE
      : issuesResult,
  ```
- Same for memories, interactions (by length + id).
- Only when actual content differs does the reference change → useMemos recompute → virtualizer re-renders. Otherwise: zero visual change.

## SCRUTINY
medium (UX-critical; affects every refetch)

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx — add content-equality helpers + use in setState
- Optional: same pattern for closedIssues (line ~198)

## VALIDATION (operator-observable)
- Open dashboard at gitboard project + open browser DevTools → Performance tab
- Do 'bd create' from terminal, then immediately 'sp run executor --bead <id> --background'
- Observe: zero visible flicker; rows stay stable
- Performance tab: no >1-frame layout shift on the feed container

## CONSTRAINTS
- Must preserve closed-list refresh (closedIssues comes from a separate listClosedIssues call later)
- Content equality must be cheap (<1ms for 100-issue list)
- If a single issue's status changes (e.g. open → closed), the new array IS different and refresh is correct

title: Dashboard feed flickers (empty → loaded) when a bead is created — refetch clears list before repopulating
description:
## PROBLEM
When a new bead is created in the source project (bd create or external write), the dashboard's issue feed momentarily clears to empty before the new list arrives, causing a visible 'awful to see' flicker. Classic stale-while-revalidate UX bug.

## EVIDENCE (verified 2026-05-26)
Operator observation during .27 step 1 validation: 'when you created this issue, the list of beads disappeared briefly, then reappeared. it's a UX flaw, awful to see.'

Likely root cause: the data hook (probably useRepoTree or IssueFeed's fetcher) sets state to [] / null before the refetch resolves, so the UI renders 'empty feed' for ~50-300ms during the refetch RTT.

## SUCCESS
- Adding/removing a bead causes the list to update IN PLACE — no empty-state flash
- Use SWR pattern: keep previous data during refetch; replace only when new data arrives
- If the bead is new, it appears in the list smoothly (CSS transition optional)

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts (and/or IssueFeed.tsx, BeadsRepoView.tsx)
- Wherever the beads:sync_hint WS event handler triggers refetch — store previous data, replace on success
- Check if there's a 'loading' state being shown during refetch that empties the list

## SCRUTINY
low (UX only, no data correctness impact)

## VALIDATION
- Manual: dashboard open at gitboard repo + bd create from terminal — list should update smoothly, no flicker
- React DevTools: confirm no setState([]) call during refetch path

description: PROBLEM: single-file diff implementing readMemories/readInteractions on substrate.ts; smell-check for async/sync mixing, error handling, log spam. SUCCESS: FINDINGS or OK. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts diff. NON_GOALS: no edits. CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY. VALIDATION: cite lines. OUTPUT: FINDINGS or OK.
notes:

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts` (`readMemories`, `readInteractions`, route wiring, local logging helpers)
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts:62-71`, `75-85`
- Issue: double filesystem gate on same path. Route does `fileExists()` then `BeadsReader.getMemories()/getInteractions()`, but reader already `readFile()`s and swallows errors.
- Why it matters: extra IO per request, plus race hole: file can disappear after `fileExists()` and before `readFile()`, then route returns empty without `logMissingProjectData`, so missing-data behavior drifts.
- Suggested executor instruction: drop pre-check, let reader own missing-file/parse handling, or make one path do both existence check + read + logging.

## Non-findings
- Async route handlers fine; no obvious sync/async mix bug in handlers.
- `logProjectDataOnce` limits success-path log spam per project/path pair.
- `issue_id` filter in `readInteractions` looks simple and local.

## Handoff
Proceed to executor fix only if want cleanup; otherwise reviewer can pass with note that main smell is redundant file existence check + silent fallback race.

SUCCESS: findings isolate concrete smell or confirm OK. ### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job f37785) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/{beads-adapter,index,types}.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/{substrate,internal-substrate}.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts:116-117`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-substrate.ts:12-13`
  - Issue: `SUM(...)` fields typed as `number`, but SQLite returns `NULL` on empty set; fallback only handles whole-row `undefined`, not per-field `null`.
  - Why it matters: API can emit `null` for numeric counts while TS says `number`, so reader code sees mixed types and brittle JSON shape.
  - Suggested executor instruction: coalesce each aggregate in SQL (`COALESCE(SUM(...), 0)`) or normalize each field before return; keep response shape strictly numeric.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts:68-70`
  - Issue: `adapter as MaterializerAdapter & { writeFull?: ... }` is unsafe cast to probe optional method.
  - Why it matters: hides real interface contract and makes resync path depend on runtime duck-typing; easy to break when adapter shape changes.

title: Materializer Dolt query truncates at 1000 — projects with more issues lose history
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-snapshot-source.ts:46-47 calls doltClient.getIssues({limit: 1000}). apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts:350 selectIssues then does SELECT * FROM issues ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at DESC LIMIT ?. Anything over 1000 silently truncated.

Verified 2026-05-26: substrate has 1001 rows for repo_slug 8ee0c958 and 1000 rows for 6ca3e930 — exact-cap signature of truncation. Dashboard for these projects shows only the first 1000-ranked issues.

## SUCCESS
Projects with > 1000 issues fully materialize. Dashboard reflects complete history.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-snapshot-source.ts (paginate readFromDolt)
apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (verify selectIssues supports offset pagination — it does, line 347)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Use offset-pagination: fetch 1000-at-a-time until count < 1000.
- Hard cap at e.g. 10000 per project (safety against runaway).
- Snapshot-diff cursor (snapshot_hash) computed over the FULL accumulated set, not per-page.
- Keep memory bounded — projects with 5000+ issues should still fit (~2-5 MB per project at JSON-serialized).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] After fix + substrate wipe + re-materialize, repo_slug 8ee0c958 has > 1001 rows in substrate_issues
- [ ] 6ca3e930 has > 1000 rows
- [ ] gitboard's 533 unchanged (no regression on small projects)
- [ ] Memory delta during single materializer.runOnce is bounded

## OUTPUT
Diff + per-project count comparison before/after.
notes:
## SCRUTINY
medium (single read-path file; no schema change; backwards-compatible pagination)

## ADDITIONAL CONSTRAINTS (auto-mode logging)
Operator drives e2e + smoke testing autonomously — emit observable evidence:
- beads-snapshot-source.readFromDolt must log per-page: 'beads-snapshot: readFromDolt page=<N> offset=<O> got=<count> repo_slug=<rs>'
- After last page: 'beads-snapshot: readFromDolt complete repo_slug=<rs> total_pages=<N> total_issues=<count>'
- If safety cap hit: 'beads-snapshot: readFromDolt safety-cap-hit repo_slug=<rs> at offset=<O>' (warn-level)
- materializer.run event for beads:* should already carry rows_written from .62 — verify it's still accurate post-pagination (i.e., NOT just the last page count)

## DEPLOY STEPS
1. systemctl --user restart gitboard.service (no DB migration needed)
2. journalctl --user -u gitboard.service --since '1 min ago' | grep 'beads-snapshot' — confirm multi-page reads happen for the big projects
3. After 1 materializer cycle (~5 min default OR force restart for eager bootstrap), check substrate row counts via observability or another internal endpoint

## VALIDATION (machine-verifiable)
- bunx vitest run on touched test files (beads-snapshot-source tests if any; otherwise add minimal regression test)
- Service restart + first materializer cycle shows multi-page log for the 1001/1000-row projects (8ee0c958, 6ca3e930)
- Per-repo row count via direct sqlite query or new internal endpoint > 1000 for those repos

title: substrate.ts readMemories + readInteractions stubs return [] — implement from jsonl
description:
## PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts lines 55-56:
  function readMemories(_db, _projectId) { return []; }
  function readInteractions(_db, _projectId, _issueId) { return []; }

These are placeholder stubs. /api/beads/projects/<id>/memories and /interactions return empty arrays even though the .beads/ dirs have interactions.jsonl files and Dolt has memories data.

The .48 materializer chain wired substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies but never wired substrate_memories or substrate_interactions. apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts (NOT mounted) lines 127-164 has working implementations that use BeadsReader.getMemories(beadsPath + '/knowledge.jsonl') and BeadsReader.getInteractions(beadsPath + '/interactions.jsonl').

## SUCCESS
/api/beads/projects/<id>/memories returns real memories from the project's knowledge.jsonl (or Dolt fallback when configured). /api/beads/projects/<id>/interactions returns real interactions from interactions.jsonl, with optional issue_id filter.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts (implement readMemories + readInteractions)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Use BeadsReader.getMemories + getInteractions from apps/gitboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts (or beadboard equivalent — pick whichever is on the gitboard import path).
- Resolve beadsPath from xtrm.sources where source_key = 'beads:<projectId>' — same pattern as readProjects already uses.
- Read sync via Bun.file or readFileSync (these routes already serve from xtrm-backed dao; minor disk I/O is fine for now).
- Support optional issue_id query param on interactions (filter after read).
- If beadsPath isn't in sources or file doesn't exist, return [] gracefully (current behavior).
- Don't change the route signatures or shape.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] curl /api/beads/projects/<gitboard-uuid>/interactions?issue_id=forge-eorh.48 returns filtered subset

- [ ] Existing substrate/specialists/materializer tests still pass

## OUTPUT

## REFERENCE
Discovered post .66 fix during dashboard sections check. Long-term: extend the materializer to write memories+interactions into substrate tables (separate epic-scale work).

title: Materializer observability gaps: silent drain catch + missing duration/rows in materializer.run
description:
## PROBLEM
Two materializer logging gaps discovered during forge-eorh.60 e2e verification:

1. apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/queue.ts SourceQueue.drain() catches exceptions silently ('per-source isolation: keep queue moving after one source fails'). When the obs adapter (.58) or beads adapter (.61) threw, no log entry was emitted — bug only surfaced via direct adapter probe. Should emit a logger event in the catch with the source_key + error message.

2. apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts Materializer.runOnce emits 'materializer.run' with data {source_key, coalesce_ms: 1500} — the 1500 is the static const, not actual run duration. Need {source_key, duration_ms: <actual>, rows_written: <count>, dependencies_written: <count>} so lag can be tracked per source programmatically.

Current logging is otherwise solid (1401 materializer.run + 7720 request.timing + 1370 query.slow + 335 breaker.opened entries on day 1 of gate-on).

## SUCCESS
- Silent catch in queue.ts emits an error-level log entry with source_key and exception message.
- materializer.run event records actual duration_ms and rows_written.
- Existing tests still pass.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/queue.ts
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts (runOnce + emit call)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Use existing emit(makeLogEntry(...)) pattern from logger.ts.
- Don't change SourceQueue's behavior of continuing the queue after a failure — only add the log.
- materializer.run stays at info level; new materializer.error at error level.
- Tests should assert the error path emits a log entry.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Throw inside adapter.changesSince during a test run → log entry with event='materializer.error', level='error', data.source_key set
- [ ] materializer.run includes duration_ms (>=0) and rows_written (>=0)
- [ ] Existing 31 materializer/specialists/beads-parity tests still pass

## OUTPUT
Diff + sample log lines from a deliberately-failing test fixture.

## REFERENCE
Discovered 2026-05-25 during forge-eorh.60 e2e probe. The silent catch was the root cost of .58 + .61 each taking longer than necessary to diagnose.

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:140-142`
  - Issue: `sourceHealthFromState()` has dead branch; `status` always `"fresh"`.
  - Why it matters: fallback path loses any degraded/syncing signal, so cold-start UX always reports healthy even when materialization state says otherwise.
  - Suggested executor instruction: make `status` derive from real state or delete branch and hardcode `"fresh"` with no fake condition.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:317-323`
  - Issue: `warmDefaultBundle()` uses one global `defaultBundleWarm` for all repo keys.
  - Why it matters: if repo set changes while warm still pending, new key cannot start its own warm path; cold-start can fall back longer than needed or cache stale bundle shape.
  - Suggested executor instruction: key warm promise by bundle key, or re-check key before short-circuiting `defaultBundleWarm`.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:115-116`
  - Issue: double cast `(c as never as { set(...) })` to call `c.set`.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts:277-278`, `:291-292`
  - Issue: `refreshJobsByBead()` and `refreshChain()` are `async` but fully sync.
  - Why it matters: extra async wrappers add noise and imply I/O that does not exist.
  - Suggested executor instruction: drop `async`/`await` from pure cache-write helpers.

notes:

- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/{index.ts,types.ts,adapter.ts,beads-adapter.ts,observability-adapter.ts}`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts:93-95`
- Issue: `getCursor()` does raw `JSON.parse(row.cursor)` with no guard.
- Why it matters: one bad cursor row crashes every materializer run for source; no fallback to null/repair path.
- Suggested executor instruction: wrap parse in `try/catch`, return `null` on invalid cursor, and keep cursor decode logic aligned with adapter cursor readers.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts:22-23`, `adapter.ts:3-6`
- Issue: registry type forced through `as MaterializerAdapter<unknown, unknown>` plus `new Map<string, MaterializerAdapter>()`.
- Why it matters: generic safety lost at registration boundary; adapter mismatch can slip through and hide write/input shape bugs.
- Suggested executor instruction: make registry generic at storage site or keep `Map<string, MaterializerAdapter<any, any>>` out of cast path via a typed helper/overload, so register stays type-safe without `unknown` cast.

## Non-findings
- Not flagging `MaterializerAdapter.write(db, snapshot)` shape itself; contract is simple enough.
- Not reviewing runtime behavior, test coverage, or reviewer-level correctness.

## Handoff
Proceed to reviewer. If executor wants cleanup pass, first fix cursor parse guard, then remove registry cast smell.

title: Fix smoke seed functions clashing with schema migration
description:
## PROBLEM
Both apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts (line 43) and p1-specialists-kill.ts (line 67) call a seed function that does `CREATE TABLE substrate_job_link (...)`, but the schema migration in apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts already created that table when the DB was opened. SQLiteError: "table substrate_job_link already exists" kills both smokes.

## SUCCESS
Both smoke scripts run cleanly when invoked from main: `bun apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts` and `bun apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-kill.ts` complete without throw.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts (seedXtrmOnly function around line 42-43)
apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-kill.ts (seedSpecialists function around line 66-67)

## NON_GOALS
- No production-code changes.
- Don't refactor the smoke harness.

## CONSTRAINTS
- The xtrm-store schema migration already creates all tables idempotently on db open. The seed functions should skip CREATE entirely and just INSERT rows. If the seed uses any column that the migration doesn't create, the right fix is to remove that column from the seed (not change the migration).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] `bun apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-fallback.ts` exits 0
- [ ] `bun apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-specialists-kill.ts` exits 0

Changed files, smoke run output (both green).

description:

## CONSTRAINTS
READ_ONLY. Cite exact lines. Specifically check:
- cursor pair (updated_at_ms, event_rowid) advanced as ONE unit, not separately
- touched-row re-read collects distinct job_ids from events side and re-reads jobs

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts:83-85` + `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/epoch.ts:19-27`

- `materializer.test.ts` covers cursor pair and touched-job re-read path; no obvious regression in test shape.

description:
## PROBLEM
Executor c5f77f reports MEDIUM risk on materializer core. Queue/coalesce + same-tx cursor invariant + per-source isolation are exactly the surfaces where type-safety or control-flow errors silently cause data loss. Sanity-screen before reviewer.

## SUCCESS
FINDINGS with severity, or OK.

## SCOPE
Diff on feature/forge-eorh.2-executor. Focus on:
- materializer/queue.ts (COALESCE_MS dedup, ordering)
- materializer/index.ts (runOnce same-tx invariant, per-source try/catch, post-commit hook)
- materializer/adapter.ts (interface contract)
- server.ts integration (getCurrentMaterializer module-level state)

## NON_GOALS
No edits, no merge gate.

## CONSTRAINTS
READ_ONLY. Cite exact lines. Specifically check for:
- Awaited writes inside transaction (anti-pattern: async in tx)
- Cursor advance happening outside the same SQLite tx as data writes
- Per-source try/catch leaking errors to the queue runner
- Post-commit WS hint firing on rollback path
- Module-level mutable state in server.ts (getCurrentMaterializer) introducing test isolation issues

## OUTPUT
FINDINGS or OK.
notes:

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/{queue.ts,index.ts,adapter.ts}` and `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
- Confidence: high

## Findings
1. **Location:** `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:36-58`
   - **Issue:** `currentMaterializer` is module-level mutable singleton.
   - **Why it matters:** tests and repeated app boots can leak prior materializer instance across runs; `getCurrentMaterializer()` can return stale state from earlier `createApp()` calls.
   - **Suggested executor instruction:** replace module-global materializer state with app-scoped dependency or explicit accessor stored on returned app context; reset on shutdown if singleton must stay.

## Non-findings
- `materializer/index.ts` transaction shape looks sane: writes and cursor update stay inside same SQLite tx; post-commit WS hint fires after `COMMIT`, not on rollback path.
- `queue.ts` coalesce logic looks simple and bounded; no obvious async-in-tx or error-leak bug in reviewed lines.
- `adapter.ts` is thin contract only; no smell.

## Handoff
One concrete smell found. Send back to executor for server-state cleanup, then reviewer can recheck materializer flow.

description:
## PROBLEM
Executor reported HIGH risk on c4d39f's diff: 11 files touched, new module surface, modifications to logger.ts and server.ts. Sanity-screen the diff for complexity, type-safety, and dead-code smells before reviewer.

## SUCCESS

## SCOPE
Diff on feature/forge-eorh.26-executor.

## NON_GOALS
- No edits, no merge gate decision.

## CONSTRAINTS

## VALIDATION

notes:
### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job 9e47c4) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/spans.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/event-types.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts:69-74`
  - Issue: `writeChain` has no rejection recovery. One disk-write failure leaves chain rejected forever, so later `emit()` calls keep enqueueing onto dead promise.
  - Why it matters: one transient FS error bricks disk sink for rest of process and can create unhandled-rejection noise.
  - Suggested executor instruction: add `.catch()`/reset path around queued append so single failure logs once and later writes keep flowing.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/spans.ts:49-50`
  - Issue: `component as never` hides type mismatch at emit site.
  - Why it matters: compiler loses check between `SpanAttributes`/`LogComponent` boundary, so future component additions can slip through with no signal.
  - Suggested executor instruction: align `component` type with `LogComponent` or add narrow mapping; remove `as never`.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:79-80`
  - Issue: `(error as Error).message` unsafe cast on catch path.
  - Why it matters: non-`Error` throw values become `undefined` message, which weakens error logs right on failure path.
  - Suggested executor instruction: normalize unknown error with `instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)`.

## Non-findings
- `EventType` broad union in `apps/gitboard/src/core/observability/event-types.ts:1-9` looks over-permissive, but this looks intentional for incremental event naming; kept out of findings.
- `AsyncLocalStorage` use in `spans.ts` looks straightforward; no misuse seen in reviewed slice.

## Handoff
Proceed to reviewer with 3 concrete smells. No edits done. If executor wants tighter pass, give exact diff or changed-file list.

title: Test P5: WsClient grace timer + channel rename (e2e network + roundtrip)
description:
## PROBLEM
WsClient churn is a visible UX defect (panel flicker during fast navigation). Channel rename is mechanical but a half-renamed state breaks realtime; lockstep proof is mandatory.

Layer: shell (client kernel, hooks, server publish points).
Strategy: e2e against running service; network panel inspection counts as evidence; round-trip test for channel rename.
Covers: forge-eorh.12, forge-eorh.13.

## SUCCESS
Network panel shows zero unsubscribe churn during rapid navigation; substrate:* round-trip works end-to-end; no stale beads:* publishers or subscribers remain.

## SCOPE
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/lib/ws.test.ts` (grace timer unit).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/ws-grace.e2e.ts` (Playwright; verify network panel via CDP).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/substrate-channel-roundtrip.e2e.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p5-channel-grep.sh` (grep for any lingering `beads:` channel reference).

## NON_GOALS
- Removing /api/beads/* (P6).
- Beadboard deletion (P6).

## CONSTRAINTS
- Grace timer test uses fake timers + real WsClient.
- E2E network panel test must use Chrome DevTools Protocol via Playwright (`page.on('websocket', ...)`).
- Channel round-trip e2e MUST run against a real running service with materializer + dashboard wired.

## VALIDATION

Assertions required:
- [ ] **Shell** Grace timer: fake timers — refcount drops to 0, schedule unsubscribe after 2s, resubscribe within 2s cancels the unsubscribe, no real unsubscribe call.
- [ ] **Shell** Grace timer: refcount drops to 0, 3s passes with no resubscribe, unsubscribe call fires exactly once.

**E2E (visible UI — mandatory):**
- [ ] `ws-grace.e2e.ts`: navigate dashboard tabs rapidly 10x; capture WS frames; assert zero unsubscribe→subscribe pairs for the same channel within 2s windows.
- [ ] `substrate-channel-roundtrip.e2e.ts`: open dashboard; trigger an upstream Beads write; observe `substrate:project:<id>` event arrives client-side; UI updates. Do same for force-overflow → `substrate:sync_hint`.

**Background smoke (mandatory):**
- [ ] `p5-channel-grep.sh`: `git grep -nE '"beads:(changes|project|sync_hint)"' apps/` returns no functional matches (only comments referencing rename history are allowed).
- [ ] WS sequence: subscribe, publish 600 events (exceeds 500 ring buffer), client sees `substrate:sync_hint` (not stale events from old channel name).

## OUTPUT
Test pass, network-panel screencast or frame dump, channel-grep output, smoke log.

## Critical paths covered
- WsClient zero-drop race — risk: panel flicker / missed events
- Channel rename lockstep — risk: stale publisher or subscriber → silent realtime break

## Known deferred paths
- TanStack migration (Q7 closed: no) → no test needed

title: Test P3: Unified scanner + manual pins (live FS + e2e settings)

The unified scanner replaces two existing scanners. Bugs here = sources missing or duplicated, which cascades into materializer never running for that source. Manual pins are a new feature with UI.

## SCOPE

- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/api/sources.test.ts` (CRUD pin endpoints).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/settings-sources-panel.e2e.ts`.

- Live scanner test uses `mkdtemp` per test for isolation.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] **Boundary** live scan: tmpdir with 2 Beads projects + 1 observability.db produces exactly 3 sources rows with correct kinds + paths.
- [ ] **Boundary** scanner ID parity: ids match both old scanners' formula (`metadata.project_id || basename(repoPath)`).
- [ ] **Boundary** missing-source preservation: rm a project dir → next scan marks `status='missing'`, NOT delete.
- [ ] **Boundary** refresh preserves `origin='manual'` rows across multiple scans.

### Key Execution Flow

4. **Upsert**: `upsertDiscoveredSources()` — INSERT ON CONFLICT UPDATE
5. **Missing**: `markMissingSources()` — UPDATE status='missing' WHERE origin='discovered' AND key NOT IN discovered
6. **Parity**: `emitParityDiff()` if `parityEnabled` — compares against legacy `ProjectScanner` + `listRepos()`

title: Test P4: Graph from xtrm.sqlite + derived health (e2e graph UI + degraded)
description:
## PROBLEM
Graph is the most-visible UI surface and previously had specific bugs around stale-empty caching and warm-timeout fusion (per docs/backend.md sharp edge #3). Tests must prove no regression on those bugs, plus the new two-axis freshness/source_health works.

Layer: boundary (DAO SQL JOIN against xtrm.sqlite) + shell (route).
Strategy: e2e first (visible UI); unit for response composition.
Covers: forge-eorh.11.

## SUCCESS
Graph view renders identical nodes/edges pre-/post-refactor for known projects; degraded-source path shows banner with last-known data not blank; the two axes are surfaced separately.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/core/graph-dao.xtrm.test.ts` (unit against in-mem xtrm.sqlite fixtures).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/api/graph.flip.test.ts` (response shape compat).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/graph-view.e2e.ts` (Playwright against running service).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p4-graph-degraded.sh`.

## NON_GOALS
- Specialists overlay correctness (T-P1 covers specialists).
- Channel rename (T-P5).

## CONSTRAINTS
- E2E fixture project must have non-trivial graph (≥10 nodes, ≥10 edges) so node/edge identity is meaningful.
- Degraded smoke must stop the upstream Beads source for a real period (≥30s) and verify UI behavior.

## VALIDATION

Assertions required:
- [ ] **Boundary** graph DAO: substrate + specialist JOIN returns expected node/edge count for fixture.
- [ ] **Boundary** LIVE_STATUSES overlay still filters specialists correctly.
- [ ] **Shell** /api/console/graph response shape byte-compatible with current.
- [ ] **Shell** `freshness.age_seconds` derives from `materialization_state.last_success_at`.
- [ ] **Shell** `source_health.status` derives from `materialization_state.last_status`.
- [ ] **Shell** Two-axis split: contrived case where last sync failed but `last_success_at` is recent → response shows `source_health=degraded, freshness=fresh`.

**E2E (visible UI — mandatory):**
- [ ] Graph view renders identical visible nodes + edges vs pre-refactor for known project.
- [ ] Refresh button triggers materializer; new commit visible in graph within one tick.
- [ ] Stop Beads → graph still serves last-known data; banner reads `source_health=degraded`; node set NOT empty (regression test for sharp edge #3 — stale-empty cache must never short-circuit).
- [ ] Restart Beads → graph recovers; banner clears.

**Background smoke (mandatory):**
- [ ] `tests/smoke/p4-graph-degraded.sh`: 30s degraded window; graph UI poll captures uninterrupted node set; freshness counter increments; source_health changes; no blank state.

## OUTPUT
Test pass, e2e screenshots/screencast, smoke log with timing.

## Critical paths covered
- Stale-empty cache short-circuit (sharp edge #3) — risk: known regression mode returns
- Two-axis health/freshness — risk: warm/degraded vocabulary fusion returns

## Known deferred paths
- Very large graphs (>1000 nodes) performance → benchmark, not functional gate
