title: Test P2: Snapshot-diff + Beads adapter + substrate API (unit + live + e2e)
description:
## PROBLEM
P2 builds the Beads side. Tests must prove the snapshot-diff utility is correct (it's reused for any future adapter), the Beads adapter handles Dolt + JSONL fallback correctly, and the /api/substrate/* + /api/beads/* shim returns byte-compatible payloads.

Layer: core (snapshot-diff hashing) + boundary (Dolt + JSONL reads, xtrm.sqlite writes) + shell (new API surface, shim).
Strategy: unit (diff util) + live contract (Beads) + e2e (issues view).
Covers: forge-eorh.6, forge-eorh.7, forge-eorh.8.

## SUCCESS
Snapshot-diff unit tests prove correctness for all four delta types; live Beads adapter test shows zero diff between fresh snapshot and materialized state; /api/beads/* shim byte-matches pre-flip responses; e2e shows identical issues UI.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/snapshot-diff.test.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/beads-adapter.live.test.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/api/substrate.test.ts` and `apps/gitboard/tests/api/beads-shim.compat.test.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/beads-issues-view.e2e.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p2-jsonl-fallback.sh` (kill Dolt; verify JSONL path).

## NON_GOALS
- Channel rename (T-P5).
- Graph (T-P4).
- UI manual refresh (P5).

## CONSTRAINTS
- Snapshot-diff tests have ZERO I/O — pure function tests only.
- Beads live test uses a real `.beads/` fixture (a known Beads project checked-in to `tests/fixtures/sample-beads-project/`).
- /api/beads/* shim compat test compares against a pre-recorded response fixture from the live Beads route (pre-flip baseline).

## VALIDATION

Assertions required (mixed-layer DoD):
- [ ] **Core (snapshot-diff)** Positive: add/update/delete/no-change all produce correct deltas; counts add up.
- [ ] **Core** Property: snapshotHash deterministic across reorderings of input rows.
- [ ] **Core** Edge: empty snapshot, all-new snapshot, all-deleted snapshot.
- [ ] **Boundary** live Beads: Dolt path → expected substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies match source; JSONL fallback → same expected output; both paths produce identical hash.
- [ ] **Boundary** `shouldSkipRead` only returns true when commit hash AND JSONL mtime both unchanged. NEVER on commit-hash only.
- [ ] **Boundary** tombstone path: delete an issue upstream; materializer next run tombstones it (not hard-deletes).
- [ ] **Boundary** /api/beads/* shim returns byte-compatible JSON vs recorded baseline for at least 3 fixture projects.
- [ ] **Boundary** /api/substrate/* returns the same data shape via the new namespace.
- [ ] **Shell** Beads parity harness emits zero diffs over 10 minutes of live operator activity.

**E2E (visible UI — mandatory):**
- [ ] Dashboard issues view renders identical issue list pre-/post-flip for a known project (Playwright snapshot).
- [ ] Create an issue via external `bd create` in a watched project; verify it appears in dashboard within 2s.
- [ ] Close an issue via `bd close`; verify it tombstones (disappears from active view; appears in closed view if shown).

**Background smoke (mandatory):**
- [ ] `tests/smoke/p2-jsonl-fallback.sh`: stop Dolt; verify materializer falls back to JSONL; substrate_issues rows still update; UI continues to render.
- [ ] Re-start Dolt; verify materializer resumes Dolt path; cursor advances correctly.

## OUTPUT
Test pass, live evidence, e2e screenshots, smoke logs, byte-compat diff (or proof of zero).

## Critical paths covered
- Snapshot-diff floor (Q1 RESOLVED) — risk: commit-diff regression silently misses uncommitted writes
- Tombstone preservation — risk: lost job↔bead JOIN if hard-deleted
- /api/beads/* shim byte-compat — risk: dashboard breaks because shape changed

## Known deferred paths
- Multi-project ordering (snapshot-diff is per-project; cross-project ordering not tested) → architectural invariant guards
close_reason: All non-operator-required VALIDATION items met. Core snapshot-diff covered by apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/snapshot-diff.test.ts (add/update/delete/no-change deltas + hash stability). Beads adapter covered by apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/beads-adapter.test.ts. shouldSkipRead edge cases in beads-snapshot-source.test.ts (commit+mtime both-unchanged-only short-circuit; null signal handling). p2-beads-adapter.ts smoke has a flaky waitFor (follow-up filed as forge-eorh.46) but the materializer itself is proven via 26 tests + 5 smokes. E2E items in browser (dashboard issues view byte-compatible, external bd write reflects within 2s, tombstone on close) remain operator-required and are owned by CHECKPOINT 1.

title: Test P1: Observability adapter + parity + specialists flip (live contract + e2e + degraded smoke)
description:
## PROBLEM
P1 flips the user-visible Specialists panel onto the materializer. Bugs here = UI regression. Live observability.db contract tests + dashboard e2e + degraded-source smoke prove the flip is safe.

Layer: boundary (observability.db SQL, xtrm.sqlite reads) + shell (API route + WS publish + UI render).
Strategy: live-first contract, e2e for visible UI, smoke for background pipelines and failure modes.
Covers: forge-eorh.3, forge-eorh.4, forge-eorh.5.

## SUCCESS
Live contract tests pass against a real observability.db fixture; dashboard e2e shows identical Specialists panel pre-/post-flip; degraded-source smoke shows graceful UI.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/server/observability/adapter.live.test.ts` (live contract).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/api/specialists.flip.test.ts` (route flip behavior).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/e2e/specialists-panel.e2e.ts` (Playwright or similar against running service).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p1-degraded-source.sh` (kill upstream Dolt mid-session).

## NON_GOALS
- Beads adapter testing (T-P2).
- Graph testing (T-P4).
- Channel rename (T-P5).

## CONSTRAINTS
- Live contract tests use a copy of a real observability.db checked into `tests/fixtures/` (or symlinked from a stable path).
- E2E test runs against a real `bun run dev`-served service, not a mocked HTTP server.
- Degraded smoke must use the real fallback flag `GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK=1` to verify both paths.

## VALIDATION

Assertions required (boundary + shell DoD):
- [ ] Boundary live contract: shadow xtrm.sqlite specialist_jobs matches source observability.db `specialist_jobs` after one full materializer cycle (zero diffs).
- [ ] Boundary: touched-row re-read picks up event-only updates (turn count change without updated_at_ms move).
- [ ] Boundary: updated_at_ms watermark picks up direct status flip (running → done) without an event row.
- [ ] Cursor pair advances atomically — never one advances without the other.
- [ ] Parity harness emits zero diffs over 10 minutes of live operator activity.
- [ ] Post-commit WS hint: `specialists:activity` fires after commit, not before; subscribed client sees exactly one hint per commit.
- [ ] `freshness` field derives from `materialization_state.last_success_at`; not from request-time warm timeout.
- [ ] `source_health.status='degraded'` when last sync failed AND `freshness=fresh` when data is still recent — two-axis split verified.

**E2E (visible UI — mandatory):**
- [ ] Dashboard Specialists panel renders identical job list pre-/post-flip (Playwright snapshot or HAR diff).
- [ ] Spawn a new sp job via terminal; verify the panel updates within 2s of completion (WS-driven, no manual refresh).
- [ ] Stop sp / kill the upstream observability.db writer; verify UI shows last-known jobs with a degraded badge — NOT blank.

**Background smoke (mandatory):**
- [ ] `tests/smoke/p1-degraded-source.sh`: kill the materializer mid-run, confirm API still returns last-known specialists data; restart materializer, confirm it resumes from old cursor; assert zero double-application.
- [ ] Rollback smoke: with `GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK=1`, verify the old attach-pool path still returns valid data — escape hatch works.

## OUTPUT
Test pass output, parity log excerpt, e2e screenshots/HAR, degraded smoke log, rollback smoke log.

## Critical paths covered
- Updated_at_ms cursor (Q2 RESOLVED) — risk: silent job-state staleness if regressed to event-seq
- Post-commit WS publish — risk: client sees data that's not yet committed
- Degraded-source UI behavior — risk: blank panel scares operators

## Known deferred paths
- Concurrent multi-repo materialization at scale (>20 repos) → follow-up: file P3-time
close_reason: All non-operator-required VALIDATION items met. Boundary contracts proven by apps/gitboard/tests/api/specialists.xtrm.test.ts (xtrm.sqlite reads, response shape, freshness/source_health derivation). Background pipeline smoke p1-parity-cycle.ts shows 5/5 convergence + injected divergence detection. Failure-mode smoke p1-specialists-kill.ts proves data preserved across materializer failure (initial=1, after_failure=1). Rollback smoke p1-specialists-fallback.ts proves GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK env flag works (fresh_jobs=1, fallback_jobs=0). E2E items in browser (dashboard renders identical job list, WS hint arrives within 2s, degraded badge UI) remain operator-required and are owned by CHECKPOINT 1 (forge-eorh.27).

title: P6/14: Retire /beadboard route + delete apps/beadboard backend
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §16, beadboard retires only after every consumer is migrated. After P2/8, P5/12, and P5/13, the dashboard no longer references /beadboard or /api/beads/*. This task removes the compat surface and deletes the deprecated app.

## SUCCESS
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:9,17` no longer imports anything from `apps/beadboard/src/...`. The relative-path bridge is gone.
- `/beadboard` redirect route removed from server.ts and index.ts.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx:21-22,135,162-163` iframe + reference removed.
- /api/beads/* router unmounted from server.
- `apps/beadboard/` directory deleted entirely.
- Build still passes; dashboard still functions.

## SCOPE
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/index.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx`.
- Removed: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/substrate-api.ts` deprecation shim (any stragglers will fail loud after this PR).
- Deleted: `apps/beadboard/**` (entire directory).
- Deleted: any workspace package.json reference to beadboard.

## NON_GOALS
- DB / dir renames (P7).
- New features.
- Restoring any beadboard functionality.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Cannot land until P5/12 and P5/13 are merged AND no /api/beads/* or /beadboard reference remains in `apps/gitboard/**`.
- `git grep -E 'beadboard|/api/beads|/beadboard'` in `apps/gitboard/` must be empty (modulo migration notes / comments referencing history).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] `git grep -E 'beadboard|/api/beads|/beadboard' apps/gitboard/src/` returns nothing functional.
- [ ] `bun run build:dashboard` passes.
- [ ] **E2E**: full dashboard regression — every visible feature renders + behaves as before.
- [ ] **Smoke**: `curl /beadboard` returns 404 (not redirect, not 200); `curl /api/beads/projects` returns 404.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, deleted file list, grep evidence, e2e + smoke proof.

title: P7/15: Fold gitboard.sqlite github_* tables into xtrm.sqlite
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §7 / §18 step 11, the single-owned-store model has ONE file. After everything else is stable, fold the github_* tables from gitboard.sqlite into xtrm.sqlite and delete the old file.

## SUCCESS
- A one-time migration runs on next startup if `gitboard.sqlite` exists:
  1. Open both DBs.
  2. Copy `github_events`, `github_prs`, `github_issues`, `github_releases`, `github_event_etags` rows into xtrm.sqlite (matching schemas).
  3. Verify row counts match.
  4. Rename gitboard.sqlite to `gitboard.sqlite.migrated.<timestamp>` (do NOT delete in this PR — operator removes after one stable session).
- New writes go to xtrm.sqlite exclusively.
- `core/store.ts` and `core/github-store.ts` are repointed to xtrm.sqlite.
- After migration, `apps/gitboard/src/index.ts` opens only one DB.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/migrations/fold-gitboard-sqlite.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/index.ts`.

## NON_GOALS
- Deleting gitboard.sqlite (operator does this after verification).
- Schema redesign of github_* tables.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Migration must be idempotent (re-running with renamed source file is a no-op).
- Row-count verification before flipping writes.
- No data loss; the renamed `.migrated.*` file is the safety net.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: migration runner handles fresh install (no gitboard.sqlite), pre-migrated install (.migrated.* present), and active install (gitboard.sqlite present).
- [ ] **Smoke**: on a real install, boot the service; verify both files exist initially; verify migration runs; verify row counts identical; verify subsequent reads/writes hit xtrm.sqlite; verify github poller continues to function.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, migration log, row-count evidence, post-boot file listing.

title: P5/12: WsClient zero-drop grace timer + hooks repointed to /api/substrate/*
description:
## PROBLEM
The dashboard `WsClient` unsubscribes immediately when refcount drops to zero, causing churn during fast unmount/remount and navigation. A small grace timer eliminates zero-refcount subscription churn. Separately, dashboard client API calls must move from `/api/beads/*` to `/api/substrate/*` before `/beadboard` can retire.

## ALREADY LANDED / DO NOT RE-IMPLEMENT
- Server side is done: `/api/beads/*` and `/api/substrate/*` route through the same `createSubstrateRouter` since commit `65da0bb`.
- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` is already just a thin alias: `createBeadsRouter = createSubstrateRouter`.
- Do not add a server-side repoint or rollback gate here; it is out of scope and obsolete.

## SUCCESS
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts` implements `UNSUBSCRIBE_GRACE_MS = 2000`.
- When a channel refcount drops to 0, `WsClient` schedules unsubscribe after the grace window.
- If a new subscribe arrives during the grace window, the pending unsubscribe is canceled and the existing subscription remains active.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts` is renamed to `substrate-api.ts`.
- Client fetch paths move from `/api/beads/*` to `/api/substrate/*`.
- Every importer is updated to import the renamed substrate API module.
- Optional compatibility shim from old `beads-api.ts` is allowed only if it fails loudly or warns during development; no new code should import it.

## SCOPE
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts`.
- Renamed/modified: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts` -> `substrate-api.ts`.
- Modified: every dashboard importer of `beads-api.ts`.
- Tests for grace-timer cancel/fire behavior and route repoint behavior.

## NON_GOALS
- Server-side `/api/beads/*` work; already done.
- Channel rename; handled by `forge-eorh.13`.
- Removing `/api/beads/*` server compatibility; handled by beadboard retirement work.
- TanStack Query migration.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Grace timer must cancel cleanly on resubscribe; no double-unsubscribe, no leaked timers.
- The hook split is preserved: invalidation-only hooks keep using sync hints; live-data hooks keep stream subscriptions.
- Channel names stay `beads:*` in this task; channel rename is `forge-eorh.13`.
- Add observable log/event evidence for subscription churn and route usage so e2e/smoke can be machine-verified.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: `WsClient` cancel-on-resubscribe within 2000ms.
- [ ] Unit: `WsClient` sends unsubscribe only after grace timeout when no resubscribe occurs.
- [ ] E2E: rapid dashboard tab navigation does not produce WS sub/unsub churn.
- [ ] E2E/network evidence: pages that previously hit `/api/beads/*` now hit `/api/substrate/*` and render identically.
- [ ] Search check: no production dashboard importer still imports `beads-api.ts` except an intentional compatibility shim if kept.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test output, network evidence before/after, and residual risk notes.
notes: Implemented WsClient 2000ms unsubscribe grace with cancel-on-resubscribe and reconnect zero-ref skip; renamed dashboard beads-api client to substrate-api and repointed client fetches to /api/substrate/*. Evidence: bun test --cwd apps/gitboard tests/dashboard/lib/ws.test.ts tests/dashboard/lib/substrate-api.test.ts tests/dashboard/hooks/useWebSocket.test.tsx => 18 pass; bunx vitest run dashboard BeadsRepoView/BeadSideDrawer/Specialists/perf => 12 pass; touched-file tsc grep clean; dashboard rg for beads-api/beadsApi//api/beads clean. Gates: obligations 752193 CLEAN, code-sanity 01b216 OK after reconnect fix, security 457c30 pass/no findings. Reviewer b8cbce could not produce valid PASS because forced stale-base injected unrelated diff; corrected packet was supplied but result stayed stale. Full browser/HAR proof remains covered by downstream forge-eorh.23.
close_reason: Done: implemented 2000ms WsClient unsubscribe grace with cancel/fire/reconnect coverage, renamed dashboard client to substrate-api, repointed dashboard fetch paths to /api/substrate/*, updated importers/tests/perf path fixtures. Validation green; full browser/HAR remains downstream forge-eorh.23.

title: P5/13: Channel rename beads:* → substrate:* (server + client lockstep)
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §11.1, the rename is `beads:changes → substrate:changes` and `beads:project:<id> → substrate:project:<id>`. Renames must happen in a single change set covering server publish points, channel registry types, and every client subscriber simultaneously — half-renamed state breaks the realtime spine.

## SUCCESS
- Server `ChannelName` union (`apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts:3-14`) updated.
- Materializer Beads adapter publishes on `substrate:changes` + `substrate:project:<id>` post-commit.
- WsHandler sync_hint mapping updated (`beads:sync_hint` → `substrate:sync_hint`).
- Every client subscriber updates channel names: `useGraphData`, `useChains`, `BeadsRepoView` (or whatever it gets renamed to), substrate-api hooks.
- `boot_id` bump on deploy ensures clients clear cached subscriptions cleanly.

## SCOPE
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`, `handler.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts` (publish names).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/{useGraphData,useChains,...}.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx` (channel subscribe + sync_hint event names).

## NON_GOALS
- Renaming the other channel families (github:*, specialists:*, session:* etc. all stay).
- Renaming the route (already done in P2/8).

## CONSTRAINTS
- Single PR — no half-state.
- No backward-compat ghosts (no `beads:*` republish for one release). Client and server move together.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: handler sync_hint maps substrate:* correctly on buffer miss.
- [ ] **E2E**: subscribe to substrate:changes in a fresh browser session; modify an issue externally; verify the substrate event arrives; UI updates.
- [ ] **E2E**: force a buffer miss (publish > 500 events fast); verify substrate:sync_hint fires; client refetches; UI converges.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, e2e evidence, residual risks (any stale clients on old channel names).

title: P4/11: Graph reads from xtrm.sqlite + derive freshness/source_health from materialization_state
description:
## PROBLEM
The graph DAO currently lives in `apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts` and reads Dolt live with TTL+epoch caches and 750ms warm timeout (per §2.1 #3, #4). After P2/8 lands, the graph can read substrate_* + specialist_* from xtrm.sqlite via a single SQL JOIN. Freshness/source_health must come from `materialization_state`, not from request-time warm signals.

## SUCCESS
- `getGraphSnapshot(projectId, includeClosed)` reads substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies + specialist_jobs (filtered to live overlay) from xtrm.sqlite via SQL. No Dolt / JSONL calls on the request path.
- `freshness` is derived from `materialization_state.last_success_at` (per §12); `source_health` is derived from `last_status`. The 750ms warm timeout is REMOVED from the graph route.
- The two axes are surfaced separately in the response (per §12): `source_health.status` can be `degraded` while `freshness.age_seconds` is fresh.
- `POST /api/console/graph/invalidate` triggers a materializer trigger for the project (not a stale-mark on a local TTL cache).

## SCOPE
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts` (replace project-scan + Dolt + JSONL read paths with xtrm.sqlite query; remove warm timeout; remove issue cache + graph snapshot cache TTLs).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts` (freshness/source_health source).
- Removed: gitboard's Dolt client + jsonl-reader from graph hot path (file may stay but is no longer imported by graph; can be removed after Beads adapter doesn't need them either — likely P6).

## NON_GOALS
- Channel rename (P5).
- Removing graph-dao.ts entirely.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Response shape must be byte-compatible with current graph route (dashboard hook unchanged).
- Two-axis freshness + source_health must replace the fused warm/stale vocabulary.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: graph composes correctly from substrate + specialist tables; LIVE_STATUSES overlay still applied.
- [ ] **E2E**: open dashboard graph view; verify identical node/edge set vs pre-refactor for a known project; verify `freshness=fresh` and `source_health.status=fresh` in the response.
- [ ] **E2E**: stop the upstream Dolt → verify graph still serves last-known data with `source_health.status=degraded` and increasing `freshness.age_seconds`. UI shows banner; no blank state.
- [ ] **Smoke**: invalidate triggers materializer; new commit shows up in graph within one tick.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, e2e evidence, sample response (degraded + stale), residual risks.
notes: Scoped re-review complete. Verified current worktree graph route no longer calls dao.invalidate on GET refresh for xtrm-backed dao, POST /graph/invalidate now gates via isAllowedMutationRequest + canRefreshSources cooldown, and xtrm source_health metadata omits raw last_error/source_key. Re-ran targeted tests: bun test --cwd apps/gitboard tests/core/graph-dao.xtrm.test.ts tests/api/routes/graph.xtrm.test.ts tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts => 12 pass.
close_reason: Done: graph route now uses xtrm.sqlite path in createApp, composes nodes/edges/specialists from substrate tables, splits freshness from source_health, protects materializer invalidation behind POST auth/cooldown, and redacts internal health details. Validation: 12 targeted graph tests pass, touched-file tsc grep clean, obligations CLEAN, code-sanity OK, security PASS.

title: P3/10: Manual pin support + discovery refresh button/API
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §10.2 / E2, manual pins are a NEW feature in this refactor. Today there is no pin registry — only config roots and excludes. Operators need a way to add/remove pinned sources without editing config.

## SUCCESS
- New API: `POST /api/sources/pin` `{path, kind}` upserts into sources with `origin = 'manual'`; `DELETE /api/sources/pin/:source_key` removes (or marks `status='unpinned'` if it has historical data).
- Unified scanner respects pins: pinned sources are preserved on every refresh; refresh does NOT overwrite or remove manual rows.
- Settings UI surface: a small "Sources" panel under settings with list + add/remove buttons.
- "Refresh sources" button hits POST /api/sources/refresh.

## SCOPE

- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts` (preserve `origin='manual'` rows on refresh).
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/settings/SourcesPanel.tsx` (list + add/remove + refresh button).
- Modified: settings page wiring.

## NON_GOALS
- Editing pin paths after creation (just delete + re-add).
- Auth on pin API (single-user / Tailscale assumed).

## CONSTRAINTS
- `origin = 'manual'` rows must be preserved across refresh cycles.
- Pin API is internal-only (localhost gate).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: pin upsert/remove, refresh-preservation.
- [ ] **E2E**: pin a path via UI; verify it appears in sources list; verify it persists across refresh; remove it; verify historical materialized data still visible if any existed.

## OUTPUT

title: P2/8: /api/substrate/* surface + /api/beads/* shim onto xtrm.sqlite
description:
## PROBLEM
After Beads adapter parity (mirror P1/4 harness here), the substrate API becomes the new authoritative public surface. /api/beads/* must remain mounted as a backward-compat shim — but reading from xtrm.sqlite, not from Dolt live — until the dashboard migrates in P5.

## SUCCESS
- New `/api/substrate/projects`, `/api/substrate/projects/:projectId/issues`, `/api/substrate/projects/:projectId/issues/:issueId`, etc. — reads from xtrm.sqlite only.
- Existing `/api/beads/*` routes return the same data shape but ALSO read from xtrm.sqlite (no more Dolt live calls). Implemented as thin alias of the substrate router.
- A Beads parity harness `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts` (mirror of observability parity) runs in shadow mode comparing xtrm.sqlite reads vs current Dolt+JSONL live reads. Gate criterion: zero diffs for 10 minutes of live operator activity before flipping `/api/beads/*` shim.
- WS `beads:changes` and `beads:project:*` publishes happen post-commit in the materializer.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads-parity.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` (now thin shim to substrate router; reads xtrm.sqlite).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (mount `/api/substrate`; mount `/api/internal/parity/beads`).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts` (post-commit WS hint publishing).

## NON_GOALS
- Removing /api/beads/* entirely (waits for P6 retire).
- Dashboard client migration (P5).
- Channel rename (P5/13).

## CONSTRAINTS
- /api/beads/* response shape must be byte-compatible with current responses (dashboard depends on it).
- Parity gate must be green before the /api/beads shim flips off Dolt.
- WS publish post-commit only.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: substrate routes round-trip correctly for issues/projects/deps queries.
- [ ] Unit: /api/beads/* shim returns identical shape to pre-flip output for a fixture project.
- [ ] **Background smoke**: parity harness shows zero diff for 10 minutes of activity.
- [ ] **E2E**: open dashboard; navigate beads view; verify identical UI behavior pre-/post-flip.
- [ ] **E2E**: write an issue via external bd; verify WS hint arrives; client refetches; new issue appears within 2s.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, parity log, e2e evidence, residual risks.

title: P3/9: Unified scanner writes sources table (parity-dual-run)
description:
## PROBLEM
Three scanners still run independently and can diverge in source-health/runtime behavior: `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, and `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts`. Phase 1-2 already landed the `sources` table and the materializer source-registration writes, so this bead is now narrowed to collapsing discovery into ONE `UnifiedScanner` while preserving current consumers during parity dual-run.

## ALREADY LANDED / DO NOT RE-IMPLEMENT
- `sources` table exists and is populated.
- Materializer source-registration already writes `beads:*` and `obs:*` rows.
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/substrate.ts` already reads `SELECT path FROM sources WHERE kind='beads' AND source_key='beads:'+projectId` to resolve beads paths.
- Drop the old acceptance criterion that this bead must make the materializer write the `sources` table; that is shipped work.

## SUCCESS
- New `UnifiedScanner` in `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts` discovers:
  - Beads projects, replacing the beadboard + gitboard project scanner discovery responsibilities.
  - Observability databases, replacing `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts` scanning responsibilities.
- The unified scanner upserts discovered rows into `sources` with `origin = 'discovered'`, `status`, `path`, `kind` in {`beads`, `observability`} using the already-landed schema/path.
- Missing sources are marked `status = 'missing'`, NOT deleted, so historical materialized data stays visible.
- Periodic refresh uses a low cadence, default 10 minutes.
- On-demand refresh API exists.
- Parity dual-run keeps old scanners feeding existing consumers for now and emits `parity.scanner` when the unified scanner's discovered set differs from legacy scanners.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` to start unified scanner while keeping old scanners feeding their consumers.
- New/modified: sources refresh route if absent, expected as `GET /api/sources` and `POST /api/sources/refresh`.
- Preserve existing materializer registration behavior; integrate with it rather than replacing shipped code.

## NON_GOALS
- Removing the old scanners; that waits for adapters/consumers to migrate fully to `sources`.
- Rebuilding materializer source registration; shipped in Phase 1-2.
- Manual pin support; handled by `forge-eorh.10`.
- Dolt client consolidation unless required for scanner correctness.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Do not duplicate shipped source table writer logic.
- The sources table is the SSOT for materializer registration.
- Missing sources stay `status='missing'`; never delete them during scanner refresh.
- Parity log must capture full diff: added, removed, changed.
- Add observable log/event output for meaningful scanner refresh steps so e2e/smoke can be machine-verified.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: unified scanner identity formula matches old scanners (`metadata.project_id || basename(repoPath)` per E2 evidence).
- [ ] Unit: missing source flips to `status='missing'` without deleting historical rows.
- [ ] Background smoke: dual-run for 10 minutes across known projects; `parity.scanner` shows zero diff in source set + key fields.
- [ ] E2E: move a project directory aside; status flips to `missing`, historical specialist data remains visible; restore directory; status flips back to `active`.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, parity log sample, smoke evidence, sources table dump, and residual risk notes.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job 0efec7) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary

**Three independent scanners** with divergent discovery logic currently populate in-memory caches and write to `sources` table separately. Phases P0-P2 landed the `sources` table schema, Materializer core, and adapters — **source-registration is shipped** (TriggerWatcher for beads, createApp for observability write `sources` on startup). This bead (P3/9) must **collapse discovery into one UnifiedScanner** class while preserving existing consumers through parity dual-run mode. Missing sources are marked `status='missing'` not deleted.

## Architecture

### Current State: Three Scanners
| Scanner | Location | Discovers | ID Formula | Writes To |
|---------|----------|-----------|------------|-----------|
| Beadboard ProjectScanner | `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts` | `.beads/` directories | `metadata.project_id \|\| basename(repoPath)` | In-memory cache only |
| Gitboard ProjectScanner | `apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts` | `.beads/` directories | Same formula + `sourceHealth[]` | In-memory + `sources` (via TriggerWatcher) |
| Observability Registry | `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts` | `observability.db` files | `slugify(basename) + hash-collision-suffix` | In-memory cache + `sources` (in createApp) |

### Source Registration Already Landed
P0-P2 shipped these writes:
- **TriggerWatcher** (`apps/gitboard/src/server/beads/trigger-watcher.ts:42-47`): Scanner scan → `INSERT INTO sources (source_key='beads:...', kind='beads', ...) ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`
- **createApp** (`apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:77-84`): `listRepos()` → `INSERT INTO sources (source_key='obs:...', kind='observability', ...) ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`

### Target Architecture
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  UnifiedScanner (NEW)                                            │
│  apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts                        │
│                                                                   │
│  ┌───────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│  │ Periodic Scan │───▶│ DiscoveredSource[]                  │     │
│  │ (10min default)│   │ { source_key, kind, path, status }  │     │
│  └───────────────┘    └────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│         │                          │                              │
│         ▼                          ▼                              │
│  ┌───────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│  │ Upsert sources│    │ Parity Comparison                  │     │
│  │ (discovered)  │    │ vs legacy scanner outputs          │     │
│  │ Mark missing  │    │ → emit parity.scanner log           │     │
│  └───────────────┘    └────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         │                           │
         ▼                           ▼
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ sources table    │     │ Existing Consumers (unchanged)       │
│ xtrm.sqlite      │     │ - TriggerWatcher reads sources      │
│                  │     │ - createApp reads sources           │
│                  │     │ - Substrate routes read sources     │
└─────────────────┘     └──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Key Files for Implementation

### New Files
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `apps/gitboard/src/core/unified-scanner.ts` | Core scanner class with periodic refresh |
| `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/sources.ts` | `GET /api/sources`, `POST /api/sources/refresh` |

### Modified Files
| File | Change Required |
|------|------------------|
| `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` | Start UnifiedScanner alongside existing scanners; emit parity.scanner |
| `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts` | No schema change needed (already has `sources` table) |

### Key Symbols to Read
| Symbol | Location | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `ProjectScanner.scanAll()` | `apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts:31-52` | Beads discovery algorithm |
| `ProjectScanner.loadProject()` | `apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts:82-120` | Beads project metadata extraction |
| `listRepos()` | `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts:28-39` | Observability discovery + caching |
| `scanRoot()` | `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts:49-62` | Root expansion and scanning |
| `assignSlugs()` | `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts:67-76` | Slug generation + dedupe |
| `TriggerWatcher.registerAndTrigger()` | `apps/gitboard/src/server/beads/trigger-watcher.ts:40-47` | Current `sources` write pattern |
| `CREATE TABLE sources` | `apps/gitboard/src/core/xtrm-store.ts:85-92` | Schema: source_key PK, kind, path, origin, status, discovered_at, last_seen_at |

## Execution Flows

### UnifiedScanner.scan()
1. Collect beads roots: `config.beadsRoots ?? process.env.XDG_PROJECTS_DIR ?? [process.env.HOME/projects, process.env.HOME/dev]`
2. Collect obs roots: `config.obsRoots ?? getObservabilityConfig().roots`
3. For each beads root: walk directories to `maxDepth: 3`, find `.beads/` folders, extract `metadata.json` → `projectId = metadata.project_id || basename(repoPath)`
4. For each obs root: expand glob patterns, find `.specialists/db/observability.db` | `.specialists/observability.db` | `observability.db`
5. Return `DiscoveredSource[]` with `source_key`, `kind`, `path`, `status`

### UnifiedScanner.writeToSources(db)
1. `SELECT source_key, kind, path FROM sources WHERE origin='discovered'` — get existing discovered
2. For each new discovery: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(source_key) DO UPDATE SET path=excluded.path, status='active', last_seen_at=CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`
3. For each existing discovered source no longer found: `UPDATE sources SET status='missing' WHERE source_key=?`
4. Preserve `origin='manual'` rows entirely (forge-eorh.10 handles pinning)

### Parity Dual-Run
1. UnifiedScanner runs **in parallel** with existing scanners
2. After each UnifiedScanner scan, compare discovered set with:
   - `TriggerWatcher.scanner.scanAll()` result (beads)
   - `listRepos()` result (observability)
3. If sets differ: `emit(makeLogEntry("system", "parity.scanner", "warn", undefined, { added, removed, changed }))`

## Acceptance Criteria (from bead spec)

1. **Unit: Identity formula matches** — `metadata.project_id || basename(repoPath)` for beads; `slugify(basename) + hash` for obs
2. **Unit: Missing status flip** — Verify `UPDATE sources SET status='missing'` does not delete rows
3. **Smoke: Parity dual-run** — Run 10 minutes; `parity.scanner` log shows zero diffs
4. **E2E: Directory aside** — Move `.beads/` aside → status flips to `missing`, substrate data visible; restore → status back to `active`

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Scanner timing differences cause spurious parity diffs | Coalesce parity check with 500ms tolerance, compare sorted sets |
| Config root expansion differs between scanners | Reuse `getObservabilityConfig()` and `expandRoot()` exactly |
| Worktree/nested repo pollution (Codex forge-w8ya) | Apply same `isWorktreePath()` filter both scanners use |
| Manual pins clobbered on refresh | Filter `WHERE origin='discovered'` when marking missing |

title: P2/7: Beads adapter (Dolt + JSONL via snapshot-diff floor)
description:
## PROBLEM
Materialize Beads per-project state into substrate_issues + substrate_dependencies using the snapshot-diff utility from P2/6. Per §9.1, snapshot-diff is the cursor floor; tombstones cover removals.

## SUCCESS
- `BeadsAdapter` in `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts` per source key `beads:<project_id>` implements:
  - `cursor()` returns `{snapshot_hash}` from `materialization_state`.
  - `changesSince(_)` reads a fresh snapshot via `BeadsSnapshotSource`, diffs against current local state, returns upserts + tombstones, advances cursor to new hash.
  - `snapshot()` returns full normalized issues for resync.
- Watcher polls (current `BeadsChangeWatcher`) become triggers only — they call `materializer.trigger(source_key)`, no longer reading snapshots or publishing WS themselves.
- One source registered per discovered Beads project (from sources table).

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts` (or new wrapper in apps/gitboard/) — trigger-only; remove snapshot read + WS publish from the watcher itself.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (register adapter per discovered project).

## NON_GOALS
- API flip (P2/8).
- Channel rename to substrate:* (P5/13).
- Removing the dual scanner / Dolt client (P3 / P7).
- Removing the watcher file — only the snapshot-read + WS-publish responsibilities move.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Snapshot-diff floor; commit-hash + JSONL-mtime are skip signals only (per P2/6 `shouldSkipRead`).
- Tombstones, not delete.
- `(repo_slug, issue_id)` composite key.
- Idempotent re-apply (snapshot-diff naturally is).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: adapter delegates correctly to snapshot-diff + snapshot-source; cursor advances only on success.
- [ ] Fixture: stale Dolt → JSONL fallback path produces same shadow result as live Dolt path.
- [ ] **Background smoke**: write an issue via external `bd create` in a watched project; verify materializer triggers within `ACTIVE_POLL_MS`, upserts into substrate_issues, advances cursor, emits one WS hint. Close the issue → tombstone appears.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test pass, smoke evidence (timing + cursor before/after), residual risks.

title: P2/6: Snapshot-diff utility + Beads cursor strategy
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §9.1 (Q1 RESOLVED), Beads has no commit path in this repo. The Beads adapter cursor must be snapshot-diff. The Dolt commit hash and JSONL mtime are at most coarse negative skip signals. We need a reusable, well-tested snapshot-diff utility before the adapter can land.

## SUCCESS
- `snapshotDiff(prev, next, keyFn)` in `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/snapshot-diff.ts` returns `{upserts, tombstones, unchanged_count}`.
- Stable hashing helper `snapshotHash(rows, keyFn)` produces a deterministic digest used as the cursor `{snapshot_hash}` value.
- A `BeadsSnapshotSource` helper reads the per-project snapshot:
  1. Try Dolt via the shared client.
  2. Fall back to JSONL via `apps/gitboard/src/core/jsonl-reader.ts`.
  3. Either way, returns a normalized `Issue[]` (with `dependencies[]` embedded) suitable for snapshot-diff.
- Skip-decision helper `shouldSkipRead(source_key)` returns true ONLY when BOTH:
  - Dolt commit hash unchanged since last successful materialization, AND
  - `.beads/issues.jsonl` mtime unchanged since last successful materialization.
  Falsey otherwise — no commit-hash-only skip.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/snapshot-diff.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-snapshot-source.ts`.

## NON_GOALS
- The adapter itself is a separate task.
- No new Dolt client (uses the existing gitboard pooled client for now; consolidation comes later).
- No commit-hash-only skip logic anywhere.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Snapshot-diff is the floor. Commit-hash-only skip is FORBIDDEN (Q1 resolution).
- JSONL fallback prefers live `.beads/issues.jsonl` first, backup second (matches current `readIssuesFromJsonl` behavior).
- Hashing must be deterministic across runs and Bun versions (use a sorted-key stable JSON serializer + sha256).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: snapshot-diff covers add/update/delete/no-change; hash stability across re-runs.
- [ ] Unit: shouldSkipRead only returns true when both signals match.
- [ ] Fixture: feed a known beads project; verify snapshot-diff against captured snapshots matches expected deltas.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test pass, sample snapshot_hash for a real project.

title: P1/5: Flip /api/specialists/* to xtrm.sqlite + post-commit WS emit
description:
## PROBLEM
After parity is green (P1/4), the specialists API must read from xtrm.sqlite. The WS hint emit must move from the watcher's epoch bump to the materializer's post-commit hook. Done correctly, the request hot path no longer touches observability.db live.

## SUCCESS
- `/api/specialists/jobs`, `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight`, `/api/specialists/chains/:id` read from xtrm.sqlite (substrate_job_link + specialist_jobs + specialist_job_events).
- Old attach-pool path remains behind env flag `GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK=1` (default off in this task) for emergency rollback.
- WS publish on `specialists:activity` and `specialists:repo:<repo_slug>` happens from the materializer post-commit, not from the watcher epoch bump.
- `freshness` and `source_health` derived from `materialization_state` (per §12); 750ms warm timeout removed from specialists route.
- Observability watcher's epoch bump becomes purely a materializer trigger (no direct WS publish).

## SCOPE
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts` (read from xtrm.sqlite; remove warm-timeout; derive freshness from materialization_state).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts` (trigger only; no WS publish).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts` (channel name mapping per source kind: obs:* → specialists:* hints).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/observability.ts` (also flip to xtrm.sqlite — same data, one source of truth).
- Removed (or env-gated): attach-pool consumers in specialists route hot path.

## NON_GOALS
- Channel rename (substrate:*) — separate task in P5.
- Beads materialization — P2.
- Delete the attach-pool code (kept behind flag this phase).

## CONSTRAINTS
- Cannot flip until P1/4 parity is green for ≥10 minutes of live activity.
- Backward-compatible JSON shape for /api/specialists/* responses (existing dashboard hooks must keep working unchanged).
- Sub-channel publishes (`specialists:repo:<repo_slug>`) must continue to use the existing channel-key shape.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: specialists route reads xtrm.sqlite + composes existing response shape.
- [ ] **E2E**: dashboard `useInFlightJobs` hook + Specialists panel render the same set of jobs as before the flip; verify in browser against a running sp job.
- [ ] **E2E**: WS `specialists:sync_hint` arrives after a job completes; client refetches; UI updates.
- [ ] **Smoke**: kill the materializer mid-run, confirm dashboard returns last-known specialists data (not blank), then materializer resumes and dashboard recovers.
- [ ] **Smoke**: with `GITBOARD_SPECIALISTS_LIVE_FALLBACK=1`, prove old path still works as escape hatch.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, e2e evidence (screenshot or HAR), WS message timing, rollback-flag smoke proof.

title: P1/4: Parity diff harness for shadow observability
description:
## PROBLEM
Before we flip /api/specialists/* to read xtrm.sqlite, we need automated proof that the shadow materializer produces the same result as the current attach-pool live read. Without parity evidence, the flip risks silent UI regressions.

## SUCCESS
- A diff harness `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/parity.ts` runs on a timer (default 30s) in shadow mode and compares:
  - `inFlightJobs()` from current DAO (attach pool) vs the same query on xtrm.sqlite.
  - `jobsByBead(beadId)` for each known bead vs xtrm.sqlite.
  - `recentJobs(100)` ordering + payload.
- Diffs (count mismatch, missing rows, field-level deltas) are logged as `parity.observability` JSONL entries with severity tags.
- A new debug endpoint `GET /api/internal/parity/observability` returns the most recent diff summary (counts + first N diffs) for inspection.
- A `parity_ok_count` counter the harness exposes — used as the gate signal for P1/5.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/parity.ts`.
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-parity.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (mount parity router on `/api/internal`; start parity timer on boot).

## NON_GOALS
- No API flip (P1/5).
- No silencing or filtering of diff data — we want to see everything during this phase.
- No fixes to attach-pool path. The current path stays authoritative until flip.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Parity runner is READ_ONLY against both DBs.
- Parity endpoint is internal-only (localhost gate, same as existing internal routes).
- Diff format must be machine-readable (JSONL `parity.observability` events).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit: parity comparator handles missing-row, extra-row, field-delta, and ordering cases.
- [ ] **E2E smoke**: run sp dispatch; confirm parity log shows zero diffs after one materializer cycle.
- [ ] **Background smoke**: with parity running for 10 minutes covering live sp activity, verify diff count converges to zero and stays there.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, parity logs sample, smoke evidence (10-min run summary), gate criteria for P1/5 flip.

title: P1/3: Observability adapter (updated_at_ms + event_rowid cursor pair)
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §9.2 (Q2 RESOLVED), observability.db is hybrid (mutable specialist_jobs + side-table specialist_events). The adapter must materialize job state from updated_at_ms watermark + timeline from event rowid, with touched-row re-read.

## SUCCESS
- `ObservabilityAdapter` in `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts` per source key `obs:<repo_slug>` implements:
  - `cursor()` returns `{updated_at_ms, event_rowid}`.
  - `changesSince(cursor)`:
    1. Select `specialist_jobs WHERE updated_at_ms > cursor.updated_at_ms` (with 1s overlap to absorb skew).
    2. Select `specialist_events WHERE rowid > cursor.event_rowid`.
    3. Collect distinct `job_id` from (2); re-read those rows from `specialist_jobs` even if updated_at_ms was missed in (1).
    4. Returns upserts (jobs) + appends (events) + advances cursor to `(max(updated_at_ms), max(rowid))`.
  - `snapshot()` returns full current `specialist_jobs` (for resync / first run / tombstone GC).
- One source per discovered observability.db (from sources table).
- Wired into the materializer registry on startup; runs on observability watcher trigger.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/observability-adapter.ts`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts` (trigger materializer.trigger(source_key) instead of bumping epoch).
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (register adapter per repo).

## NON_GOALS
- No event-seq watermark for job state (FORBIDDEN — per Q2 resolution).
- No API flip yet (separate task).
- No new channel publishes (the watcher trigger still bumps the old epoch path during shadow mode).

## CONSTRAINTS
- Cursor is the PAIR. Advance atomically.
- Touched-row re-read is mandatory; do not skip when events arrive for a job whose updated_at_ms didn't move.
- Composite key `(repo_slug, job_id)` everywhere.
- Tombstones, not hard delete.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit tests: cursor pair semantics, touched-row re-read, no event-seq job-state regression, idempotent re-apply.
- [ ] Fixture test against a real observability.db copy: shadow xtrm.sqlite specialist_jobs matches source after one materializer run.
- [ ] **Background-pipeline smoke**: run a real `sp` job; verify materializer picks up updated_at_ms transitions (running → done) without an event row, picks up event-only updates (turn count changes), and never advances cursor past unapplied rows on crash.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test pass, smoke evidence, sample materialization_state row.

title: P0/2: Materializer core engine (single-writer queue + cursor invariant)
description:
## PROBLEM
Per §8 of the redesign, one and only one writer touches xtrm.sqlite, serializing per source, advancing the cursor in the same transaction as the data writes, isolating per-source failures. No adapter can land without this core in place.

## SUCCESS
- A `Materializer` class in `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/index.ts` owns:
  - A per-source queue with `COALESCE_MS = 1500` dedup (same source triggered twice while running coalesces to one re-run).
  - An adapter registry: `register(source_key, adapter)` where adapter implements `cursor() / changesSince(cursor) / snapshot()`.
  - `trigger(source_key)` enqueues; `runOnce(source_key)` does the actual work in a single SQLite transaction.
  - Same-transaction writes + cursor advance into `materialization_state`. On error, rollback; cursor stays old.
  - Per-source try/catch — one source failing never blocks another.
  - Post-commit hook: emits a WS hint **only after** the transaction commits.
- Full-resync API: `resync(source_key)` — reads `snapshot()` from adapter, upserts all, tombstones missing.
- A stub `EchoAdapter` used in tests to exercise the queue/cursor invariants without depending on real adapters.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/{index.ts,queue.ts,adapter.ts,types.ts}`.
- Modified: `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` (instantiate materializer with xtrm.sqlite handle; expose to adapters).

## NON_GOALS
- No real adapter implementations (observability/Beads are separate tasks).
- No API read path changes.
- No WS channel renames.

## CONSTRAINTS
- One writer to xtrm.sqlite at all times (use a single Database handle in WAL mode; queue serializes).
- Cursor advance MUST be in the same `BEGIN/COMMIT` as the data writes.
- Mid-run process crash must be safe: next start re-runs with old cursor; idempotent keyed upsert + tombstone make re-apply safe.
- WS hint is the LAST step after commit, not before.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit tests for queue coalesce + cursor invariant + per-source isolation (apps/gitboard/tests/core/materializer/*.test.ts).
- [ ] Crash-recovery test: kill mid-run via thrown error after writes but before cursor advance → state rolls back; next run re-applies.

title: GitHub realtime parity: event.append, release consumer, rate-limit cleanup
description:
## PROBLEM
GitHub realtime parity is incomplete: `github:event.append` is declared and consumed but not published; `github:release.upsert` is published but not consumed by `useGithubActivity`; `github:rate_limit` is declared with no consumer and should be folded into source health or removed.

## SCOPE
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts`
- existing `forge-df1j` and `forge-5o3o` should be reconciled with this decision

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- New user events inserted via `insertEvent` publish `github:event.append` only when newly inserted.
- Release upserts update the client release store or intentionally trigger an HTTP reload; no published event is ignored.
- Standalone `github:rate_limit` is removed from the union OR replaced by the canonical source-health event from `forge-kcxa.3`.
- Tests prove all declared GitHub realtime event types have at least one publisher and either a consumer or an explicit documented reason.

## NON_GOALS
- No new GitHub entity ingestion beyond existing events/PRs/issues/releases.
notes: Implementation ready on feature/forge-kcxa.6-github-parity. Blast radius checked with GitNexus for GithubPoller, useGithubActivity, useGithubStore, GithubRealtimeEvent. Changes: github:event.append publisher already present and now covered by poller tests; useGithubActivity consumes github:release.upsert via new store upsertRelease; GithubPoller publishes github:source_health on rate-limit degraded/fresh transitions; no github:rate_limit references remain. Validation: bun test core/github-poller + github store/upsert + useGithubActivity + source-health => 42 pass/0 fail. Full typecheck still exits 2 on known unrelated baseline errors only (MainPane github tab, graph-dao fixture, useWebSocket tests).
close_reason: Done: GitHub realtime parity completed. event.append publisher covered; release.upsert consumed by client store; rate-limit state uses github:source_health. Validated targeted GitHub realtime/store/hook/source-health tests (42 pass/0 fail).

title: Observability registry: dynamic roots and missing repo coverage
description:
## PROBLEM
The specialists observability registry defaults to `~/dev/*`, so repos under `~/projects/*` are invisible. `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` therefore reports only a small subset of tracked repos, making the drawer/graph overlays look broken.

## SCOPE
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts`
- route/cache interactions in `api/routes/specialists.ts` and graph overlay if needed

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Defaults cover at least `~/dev/*` and `~/projects/*`, while preserving `OBSERVABILITY_ROOTS` and config-file override semantics.
- Registry can discover new `observability.db` files after server start via TTL refresh and/or root watcher.
- Discovery remains bounded: no expensive recursive scan on every request.
- Tests cover both modern `<repo>/.specialists/db/observability.db` and legacy `<repo>/.specialists/observability.db` paths if currently supported.

## NON_GOALS
- No write/migration to observability DBs.
- No cross-process fanout infrastructure.
notes: Implementation ready on feature/forge-kcxa.4-observability-registry. Blast radius checked with GitNexus for getObservabilityConfig/listRepos/createObservabilityWatcher: direct callers are observability registry, specialists routes, observability routes/watch setup, and existing registry/config tests; no HIGH/CRITICAL risk reported. Changes: default roots now include ~/projects/*; registry scans bounded root + immediate child repo candidates for modern .specialists/db/observability.db, legacy .specialists/observability.db, and direct observability.db; cache TTL still enables post-start discovery without per-request recursive scans. Validation: bun test registry/config suites => 8 pass/0 fail; bun snippet confirms mercury projects under ~/projects/mercury now discovered. Full typecheck still exits 2 on known unrelated baseline errors only.
close_reason: Done: observability defaults now include ~/projects/*; registry discovery is bounded to root + immediate repo candidates and supports modern .specialists/db/observability.db plus legacy .specialists/observability.db. TTL cache still discovers new repos after server start. Validated with registry/config tests (8 pass) and local discovery snippet showing mercury projects.

title: Source health SSOT across GitHub, Beads, Specialists, Graph
description:
## PROBLEM
The dashboard has no canonical source-health model. Graph says `fresh|stale|degraded`; Beads says `available|missing|unhealthy` and shell chips map to `dolt|jsonl|missing|error|unknown`; GitHub rate-limit state is log-only.

## SCOPE
- Shared source-health type(s) under `apps/gitboard/src/types/`.
- Mapping points in graph, beads connection/status, specialists freshness, GitHub poller/rate-limit state.
- Minimal UI surface only if needed to prove the contract.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Canonical enum is `fresh | stale | degraded | unhealthy | missing`.
- Per-source metadata is preserved without fragmenting the top-level health vocabulary.
- GitHub rate-limit/degraded state is represented through source health, not a standalone speculative `github:rate_limit` event.
- Existing route responses continue to be backwards-compatible or have explicit migration tests.

## NON_GOALS
- No big visual redesign.
- No Dolt repair actions.
notes: Implementation ready on feature/forge-kcxa.3-source-health-ssot. Blast radius checked with GitNexus for GithubRealtimeEvent/ProjectSourceHealth/GraphResponse plus route symbols; no HIGH/CRITICAL risk reported. Added canonical SourceHealthStatus vocabulary fresh|stale|degraded|unhealthy|missing in apps/gitboard/src/types/source-health.ts. Graph/specialists/beads routes now expose backwards-compatible freshness/status plus source_health metadata. GraphResponse includes optional source_health. BeadsConnectionStatus includes canonical health while preserving existing status/source/degraded fields. Replaced speculative github:rate_limit event with github:source_health payload type for next parity bead. Validation: source-health + graph/specialists route tests 20 pass/0 fail; full typecheck still exits 2 on known unrelated baseline errors only.
close_reason: Done: added canonical source health vocabulary and route metadata while preserving backwards-compatible fields; replaced speculative github:rate_limit type with github:source_health for follow-up parity work. Validated targeted source-health/graph/specialists tests.

title: github WS parity: publish github:event.append when poller inserts events
description:
## PROBLEM
\`github:event.append\` is declared in \`apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts\` (GithubRealtimeEvent union) and the dashboard subscribes to it in \`apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts\` line 79 — but NO server-side site publishes it. New activity events show up in the dashboard only after a poll cycle round-trip (because pr.upsert/issue.upsert sync hints fire), not as their own push.

## SCOPE
- Find every site in \`apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts\` that calls \`insertEvent(this.db, ...)\` (user-events ingestion path).
- After each insert, publish \`github:event.append\` with the inserted event payload on \`github:activity\`.
- Watermark/dedup: insertEvent already has uniqueness on event.id; only publish for the newly-inserted ones (test \`changed\` or rely on insertEvent return signal — extend the function if needed).

## NON_GOALS
- Refactoring the user-events backfill flow itself.
- Multiplexing event types into separate channels.

## OUTPUT
- Live activity timeline updates push-driven; new pushes / issue comments / fork events surface in the dashboard within seconds of a poll cycle ingesting them.
notes:
Published github:event.append from GithubPoller ingestEvents after insertEvent success, with compare/PR enrichment reflected in payload before publish. Added poller realtime tests for push/PR append publishes.

title: github poller: no /releases ingestion — releases only captured as events from user feed (300-event window)
description:
## PROBLEM
The dashboard's release view is silently incomplete on most repos. Investigation:

- There is no \`github_releases\` table in the schema (\`apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts\`).
- Releases are captured *only* as \`ReleaseEvent\` rows in \`github_events\`, sourced from the \`/users/<user>/events\` stream.
- That stream is bounded: most recent ~300 events, ~90-day sliding window.
- Current DB on the VPS has **2 ReleaseEvent rows total** across all 33 tracked repos. Real release count is far higher (e.g. \`gh release list --repo Jaggerxtrm/gitboard\` shows v0.2.0 + earlier).
- Anything older or not currently in the user-events stream is invisible to the dashboard.

This is unrelated to the realtime/WS layer — releases are a poller/storage gap. Fixing the WS push (forge-7cyq) doesn't help here.

## SCOPE
- Add \`github_releases\` table to \`createDatabase\` (\`apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts\`): columns roughly \`(repo, tag_name PK, name, body, html_url, author, published_at, created_at)\`.
- Add \`pollReleases(repo)\` to \`apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts\` — single \`GET /repos/<owner>/<repo>/releases?per_page=100\` per tracked repo per poll cycle. Use ETag like the existing pollIssues/pollPullRequests to keep the cost free on cached responses.
- Upsert into the new table; on insert/change publish \`github:release.upsert\` over \`github:activity\` (the event type already exists in \`types/realtime.ts\` — \`GithubRealtimeEvent\` union, \`GithubReleaseUpsert = GithubRelease\`).
- Expose \`/api/github/repos/:owner/:repo/releases\` route and update the dashboard's release surface to read from it.

## NON_GOALS
- Backfill of release \`body\` markdown beyond what GitHub returns on \`/releases\`.
- Pre-release / draft filtering UX — surface all states with a chip.
- Asset/binary metadata.

## OUTPUT
- DB shows all releases on tracked repos, not just the 2 that happened to land in the user-events window.
- Dashboard release view populates fully; new releases trigger \`github:release.upsert\` in real time.
close_reason:
Implemented dedicated /repos/<x>/releases polling. New github_releases table (PK repo+tag_name) populated by pollReleases() inside the standard pollRepo loop, with published_at watermark and ETag gating. Result on VPS: 239 releases across 13 repos vs prior 2 ReleaseEvent rows in github_events.

- store.ts: github_releases table + migrations for new last_release_published_at / release_etag columns on github_repo_poll_state.
- github-store.ts: upsertRelease(); getReleases() now reads from the new table (preserving the existing GithubRelease[] return shape so the dashboard surface is untouched).
- github-poller.ts: pollReleases() mirrors pollPullRequests structure (per-page ETag, watermark on published_at, skip drafts). Wired into pollRepo() so it runs every cycle alongside pollIssues + pollPullRequests. Publishes github:release.upsert on changes.
- routes/github.ts: dropped the 'repo is required' constraint on /releases since the new path supports cross-repo listing, matching /prs and /issues.

Three github WS event types remain declared-but-unpublished — github:release.upsert (now fixed), github:event.append, and github:rate_limit. The latter two are separate gaps worth their own beads if push parity is wanted across the github surface.

title: perf(graph): cache inFlightJobs() at module level — current per-call SQL adds ~250ms to every /graph request
description:
## PROBLEM
`getGraphSnapshot` calls `dao.inFlightJobs()` synchronously on every graph request (graph-dao.ts:55). That call runs a fresh SQL query against the observability DB (`SELECT ... FROM jobs WHERE status IN (...)`). For specialists the result set is 134 rows; the request consistently takes ~260-420ms even when both the project scan cache and the issue cache are hot.

Recent perf work (forge-cspw, forge-ijzx, forge-9qmk, forge-5i43) cached `listRepos()` and chunked DAO queries but left `inFlightJobs` per-call. With the issue cache TTL at 10s and the project cache TTL at 30s, the inFlightJobs query is the dominant cost on warmed graph reads.

## SCOPE
- Cache `inFlightJobs()` output at the dao or graph-dao level with a short TTL (e.g. 1-2s) — short enough that the "live" overlay still feels live, long enough to amortise across rapid graph repaints.
- Alternative: precompute a per-project `Map<projectId | name, SpecialistJob[]>` once per TTL window so the buildGraph filter is O(1) instead of O(134).
- Confirm cache invalidation on observability writes if write paths exist (otherwise stale specialist overlay).

## OUTPUT
- Warmed graph latency drops from ~300ms → <50ms.
- Live specialist overlay still updates within ~2s.
notes: Superseded by the WS-push approach. The TTL cache is no longer needed once the graph hot path is event-driven. Closing this in favour of the new bead.
close_reason: Superseded by forge-7cyq. The TTL/queueMicrotask cache approach treats the symptom; the real architectural fix is to make the graph endpoint NOT be a hot path by replacing polling with WS push. Implemented in forge-7cyq — graph endpoint is now only hit on user navigation or on specialists:activity / beads:changes WS hints, so the per-call inFlightJobs() SQL cost no longer matters.

title: Documentation sweep: README + deploy/runbook docs
description:
## PROBLEM
Many docs are stale or missing after the recent deploy work and accumulated changes. README is thin; deployment.md still describes the docker-only path (which we've moved away from); the new native systemd + Tailscale run mode is undocumented and non-obvious to re-derive from code.

## SCOPE — needs to be added/refreshed
- **README.md** — high-level overview, current run modes, links to deeper docs
- **docs/deployment.md** — replace/augment the docker-centric content with the native run mode that actually deployed (forge-qa5g)
- **Native systemd user service run mode** (the path that won):
  - `~/.config/systemd/user/gitboard.service` unit shape (Type=simple, ExecStart=bun, env vars: HOST, PORT, XDG_PROJECTS_DIR, DOLT_HOST, LOG_DIR)
  - `loginctl enable-linger <user>` requirement so service runs without an active session
  - Pre-build step: `bun install && cd apps/gitboard && bun run build:dashboard` on the host before first start
  - `systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user enable --now gitboard`
  - Restart / logs cheat sheet (`systemctl --user restart gitboard`, `journalctl --user -u gitboard -f`, `tail -F ~/.agent-forge/logs/$(date +%F).jsonl`)
- **Tailscale-only access pattern**:
  - Tailscale on the host, `tailscale up` for auth
  - Bind server to the tailscale interface IP (`HOST=<tailnet-ip>`) — no ufw, no NAT, no public exposure even with no firewall
  - Why this beats `tailscale serve` for the personal-VPS case (no HTTPS cert opt-in needed; one less moving part)
- **Env var matrix**: HOST, PORT, AGENT_FORGE_DB, XDG_PROJECTS_DIR, DOLT_HOST, LOG_DIR, GITHUB_TOKEN, SKIP_GITHUB_POLLER, LOG_LEVEL — what each does, defaults, when to override
- **Scanner behaviour**: XDG_PROJECTS_DIR semantics; what happens for nested layouts (~/dev + ~/projects); shared-server config fallback (`dolt.shared-server: true` → reads ~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port)
- **Docker path status**: keep Dockerfile/compose in tree but document them as "experimental / not the primary deploy" — note the known bugs (vite v7 outDir resolves under root, host.docker.internal needs DOLT_HOST override, custom bridge subnets may lack NAT on shared hosts)

## NON_GOALS
- Multi-user/multi-tenant deployment docs
- Cloud / k8s deploy guides
- TLS/HTTPS termination docs (Tailscale handles transport on the personal path)

## OUTPUT
- Updated README.md with current run modes and quickstart
- New/refreshed docs/deployment.md covering the native systemd + Tailscale path
- Audit + refresh of other docs in docs/ that have drifted
notes:
Refreshed README quickstart/run modes and rewrote docs/deployment for native systemd + Tailscale primary deploy. Documented env matrix, scanner fallback, and Docker path status.

title: Graph chip: move priority left rail internal to avoid arrow overlap
description:
## PROBLEM
Recently added priority left rail on graph chips is implemented as 'border-left: 3px solid var(--graph-priority-N)' on .g-node (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css:5419-5438). React Flow Handle at Position.Left attaches to the same outer edge, so arrow tips overlap the colored rail.

## SUCCESS
- Priority rail is visually INSIDE the chip box (not on the outer border).
- Arrow tips attach to a clean uniform 1px border, no overlap with the colored rail.
- Chip outer dimensions preserved (or close enough that react-flow layout doesn't shift visibly).
- All 5 priority colors (P0-P4) and the active-pulse animation still render.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css: .g-node base rule + .g-node[data-p='N'] rules (lines ~5419-5439).
- No .tsx changes expected.

## DESIGN
- Change .g-node border-left from '3px solid var(--text-muted)' to '1px solid var(--border-subtle)' (matches the other 3 borders for a uniform outer box).
- Increase padding-left to compensate for the lost 2px of left space (was 7px + 3px border = 10px effective inner offset; new is 10px padding + 1px border = 11px — close enough; or use 9px padding to match exactly).
- Add a ::before pseudo: 'content: ""; position: absolute; left: 1px; top: 1px; bottom: 1px; width: 3px; background: var(--text-muted); border-radius: 2px 0 0 2px;' (sits just inside the 1px outer border).
- Update .g-node[data-p='N'] rules to set '::before background' instead of 'border-left-color'.

## NON_GOALS
- Don't redesign the chip layout, colors, or animations.
- Don't touch BeadNode.tsx unless padding compensation requires it (it shouldn't — CSS only).

## VALIDATION
- Visual: open the graph, verify rail is inset and arrows touch a clean border.
- Hover/active states still render.
- All 5 priorities show distinct colored rails.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, residual visual notes.

title: Graph empty-state UX: distinguish 'loading' (freshness=stale) from 'truly empty' (freshness=fresh)
description:
## PROBLEM
When opening the graph for a project for the first time, the API correctly returns freshness='stale' with nodes=[] (cache cold; background refresh in flight). The UI renders this as 'no beads in this project' — same message as a project that genuinely has zero beads. After switching tabs and back, the cache is warm, the response returns freshness='fresh' with the real nodes, and the graph populates.

Two issues:
1. Wrong messaging: a cold/stale empty response is mistakenly shown as definitively empty.
2. Suboptimal flow: the UI does not auto-refetch on stale, leaving the user to manually switch tabs to trigger a second request.

## SUCCESS
- When response.freshness === 'stale' (or absent) and nodes.length === 0: show 'Loading project graph…' or a spinner + 'Background refresh in progress'.
- When response.freshness === 'fresh' and nodes.length === 0: show 'No beads in this project' (current message).
- When response.freshness === 'degraded' and nodes.length === 0: show 'Graph data unavailable — last refresh failed' with a retry affordance.
- When response.freshness === 'stale': the client should schedule one auto-refetch ~500-1000ms later (or once the WS broadcasts the corresponding project update) to pick up the warmed cache without the user switching tabs.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/Graph/ (or equivalent — the graph empty-state component; find it via the existing 'no beads in this project' string).
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts (auto-refetch on stale).
- Tests for the empty-state component covering all 3 freshness states.

## NON_GOALS
- Changing the API contract (already returns freshness).
- Changing the cache layer.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Do not break the case where the API response is missing 'freshness' entirely (treat as stale for safety).
- Refetch must not loop: max 1 retry per stale response; if second response is still stale, surface 'loading' UI and rely on WS broadcast or user-initiated refresh.

## VALIDATION
- Unit test: empty-state component renders correct message for each freshness value.
- Unit test: useGraphData schedules one refetch when first response is stale; does not loop on repeated stale.
- Manual: cold-load gitboard graph; should see 'loading' briefly then populate without tab switch.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test evidence.
notes:

title: Graph project resolution returns fallback:selected-repo:projects on valid UUID
description:
## PROBLEM
GET /api/console/graph?project=<gitboard-project-uuid> returns:
  {"project_id":"","repo_slug":"","project":"fallback:selected-repo:projects","nodes":[],"edges":[],"freshness":"fresh"}

Even when the UUID matches a valid project from /api/beads/projects (e.g. 4c37df93-55bf-44d2-8ddb-9c9d0f237587 = gitboard). resolveProject() in graph-dao.ts falls back instead of matching.

User-visible effect: switching to the gitboard graph in the dashboard shows the cached/empty fallback ('no beads in this project') even though beads/projects clearly lists gitboard with active beads.

This is independent of the HA work landed in forge-5i43 — the caching layer is correctly serving 'fresh' results; the resolver itself returns nothing.

## SUCCESS
- /api/console/graph?project=<uuid> returns the matching project's nodes/edges/specialists.
- /api/console/graph?project=<name> (e.g. 'gitboard') also works.
- 'fallback:selected-repo:projects' only appears when no project matches AND no selected-repo is set.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts: resolveProject() and the project-id matching logic.
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts: query-param parsing path.
- Test: graph-dao unit test for resolveProject with UUID, name, and missing id cases.

## NON_GOALS
- Changing the caching/freshness layer (works correctly).
- UI changes — only API.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve current freshness contract.
- Backwards-compat: any callers passing project name (not UUID) must still work.

## VALIDATION
- curl /api/console/graph?project=<gitboard-uuid> returns non-empty nodes for gitboard.
- Unit test asserts UUID lookup, name lookup, and fallback path.

## OUTPUT
Diagnosis (which lookup field is wrong), changed files, test evidence.
notes:

title: Observability DB schema_version 0 incompatible — attach-pool refuses .specialists/observability.db
description:
## PROBLEM
On dev startup the API logs:
  Skip observability db /home/dawid/dev/specialists/.specialists/observability.db: schema_version 0 incompatible

Source: apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts emits this via logger.warn when the SQLite PRAGMA schema_version (or expected app-level migration version, check exact pragma in code) reads 0 on attach. The DB exists and is reachable but attach-pool marks it dead, so the SpecialistsDao falls back to emptySpecialistsDao() permanently.

User-visible effect: /api/specialists/* always returns empty data — even with the listRepos cache fix (forge-9qmk) and the serve-stale layer (forge-5i43) working correctly. Specialists tab shows nothing.

(Also: the log message is misformatted — emits 'n ${pragma} incompatible' instead of '${pragmaName} incompatible'. Minor log-string bug, fix alongside if cheap.)

## SUCCESS
- API attaches the observability DB and serves real specialists data, OR
- The schema check is correctly identifying a real version mismatch and the bead documents the upgrade path / migration the user needs to run.
- Log message renders the pragma name correctly.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts (schema check + log string)
- Possibly apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts / migration logic if a real migration is needed.
- Test: attach-pool unit test confirms behavior on schema_version=0 and on a known-good DB.

## NON_GOALS
- HA / caching work (already handled by forge-5i43, forge-9qmk).
- Rewriting the observability DB schema unless this turns out to be a missing-migration bug.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Investigate first: is the DB actually at v0, or is the pragma read wrong? Schema_version=0 is the SQLite default when no app-level migrations have run, so it may indicate the migration runner never executed against this DB.
- If the DB needs migrating, do it idempotently and log clearly.

## VALIDATION
- Restart API; observability DB attaches without warning.
- /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns real data (or a clear empty when truly empty).
- Unit test for the schema check path.

## OUTPUT
Diagnosis, fix path, changed files, test evidence.
notes:
Found fix path: schema guard too strict at min=1; zero-version observability dbs with specialist_jobs should attach. Added attach-pool unit coverage for user_version 0 and known-good db. Typecheck still noisy on pre-existing repo-wide TS issues unrelated to this change.
