## Summary
Hono/Bun dashboard cache plan: short TTL + SWR + in-flight dedupe + epoch invalidation. Keep live overlay fresh, stop rescans and attach churn.

## Sources
- Hono timeout middleware: https://hono.dev/docs/middleware/builtin/timeout
- Hono cache middleware: https://hono.dev/docs/middleware/builtin/cache
- Bun `server.timeout(req, seconds)` / idle timeout / SSE: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/http/server
- Bun WebSocket upgrade: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/http/websockets
- Bun SQLite prepared statements + WAL: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/sqlite
- GitHub TTL + inflight patterns:
  - MetaMask perps cache: https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/ui/providers/perps/perps-cache.ts
  - OpenClaw cache helper: https://github.com/TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-center/blob/main/src/runtime/usage-cost.ts
  - OpenClaw insights cache: https://github.com/TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-center/blob/main/src/runtime/openclaw-cli-insights.ts

## Recommendations
- Graph endpoint:
  - key: `projectId + includeClosed + repoEpoch`
  - TTL: 250-500ms
  - SWR: 1-2s
  - invalidate on epoch bump, WS live-status event, repo change
- Specialists in-flight:
  - key: `repoSlug + limit + repoEpoch`
  - TTL: 250ms
  - SWR: 750ms
  - invalidate on job write, watcher bump, WS event
- Project scan list:
  - key: roots hash
  - TTL: 1-2s
  - invalidate on filesystem watcher, roots config change
- Attach pool:
  - keep current dead-cache
  - reuse prepared statements per attach alias
  - do not re-probe dead DBs on mtime tick
- Client `useGraphData`:
  - kill forever `Map`
  - use same TTL/epoch rule
  - refresh on focus + visibility + WS epoch

## Unsafe caching
- live specialist status
- graph response with live overlay
- `generated_at`
- cache keyed only by `projectId`
- long cache on attach-pool failures

## Route/timeouts
- Hono `timeout` middleware on slow JSON routes
- Bun `AbortSignal.timeout()` for outbound fetch
- Bun `server.timeout(req, 0)` only for streaming/SSE

## Test criteria
- cache hit within TTL
- stale serve during SWR, refresh in background
- single upstream call under concurrent miss
- epoch bump forces refresh
- WS update invalidates client cache
- dead DB stays skipped across mtime churn
- live overlay never hidden past explicit TTL

## Final
Brief done. No edits. No infra change.

## Test criteria
- cache hit within TTL
- stale serve during SWR, refresh in background
- single upstream call under concurrent miss
- epoch bump forces refresh
- WS update invalidates client cache
- dead DB stays skipped across mtime churn
- live overlay never hidden past explicit TTL

title: Research Dolt/mysql2 pool, timeout, and circuit-breaker best practices
description:
## PROBLEM

The epic identified a likely Dolt performance bug: a singleton mysql2 runtime shares pool, breaker, probe, and metrics across project ports/databases. We need current best practices for efficient MySQL/Dolt access from Bun/Node, especially per-key pools, query timeouts, health probes, circuit breakers, and fallback behavior.

## SUCCESS

Produce evidence-backed guidance for implementing per-project/per-pool-key Dolt connections safely and efficiently. Include whether mysql2 pool patterns are correct, how to implement timeout/cancellation without leaking work, how to structure health probes, and whether to use a circuit-breaker library or keep a small custom breaker.

## SCOPE

- External research only: mysql2 docs/source examples, Dolt SQL server docs if available, Node/Bun timeout patterns, circuit-breaker examples.
- Local context from `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`, beads route fallback, and graph DAO use.
- Output should map to child beads `forge-ojn4.2`, `forge-ojn4.6`, and `forge-ojn4.9`.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not edit files.
- Do not require a live Dolt server.
- Do not propose broad storage migrations away from Dolt.

## CONSTRAINTS

- One broken project source must not poison other project sources.
- Fallback to JSONL/SQLite must remain fast and visible.
- Prefer solutions that are testable with fakes/mocks.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Current mysql2 pool docs/examples consulted.
- [ ] Dolt SQL server behavior/docs checked where possible.
- [ ] At least two GitHub snippets/patterns for pool maps or breakers checked.
- [ ] Recommendation includes concrete runtime-state shape and failure-mode tests.

## OUTPUT

Return a concise research brief with sources, recommended runtime design, timeout/breaker details, testing strategy, and red flags to avoid.
notes:
### Specialist Output — researcher (job 7bd546) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary
Singleton `runtime` bad shape. Need `Map<poolKey, runtime>` so one broken project no poison other project.

## Status
External evidence checked:
- mysql2 pool + timeout docs
- Node AbortSignal / timers cancel docs
- Dolt MySQL-server docs
- breaker library docs
- GitHub patterns for keyed caches, pool setup, breaker state

## Changes
None. Research only.

## Evidence
- **mysql2 pool**
  - `mysql.createPool()` canonical.
  - `connectionLimit`, `maxIdle`, `idleTimeout`, `enableKeepAlive`, `keepAliveInitialDelay` supported.
  - query `timeout` is **client-side inactivity timeout** and **destroys connection** on hit. Good for fail-fast, no stale conn reuse.
  - `connection.release()` and `pool.end()` required.
  - Source: `ctx7 /websites/sidorares_github_io_node-mysql2`
- **Node cancellation**
  - `AbortSignal.timeout(ms)` exists.
  - `AbortSignal.any([...])` exists.
  - `timers/promises.setTimeout(..., { signal })` aborts with `AbortError`.
  - Source: `ctx7 /nodejs/node`
- **Dolt**
  - Dolt SQL server speaks MySQL-compatible client protocol.
  - Connect via host/port/user like MySQL.
  - Source: `ctx7 /dolthub/dolt`
- **Breaker library**
  - `opossum` gives `timeout`, `errorThresholdPercentage`, `resetTimeout`, `volumeThreshold`, `fallback`, stats.
  - Good if want canned metrics/fallback.
  - Source: `ctx7 /nodeshift/opossum`
- **GitHub patterns**
  - `n8n-io/n8n/packages/nodes-base/nodes/MySql/v2/transport/index.ts` uses `mysql2/promise.createPool(...)`.
  - `n8n-io/n8n/packages/cli/src/utils/circuit-breaker.ts` uses `CLOSED | OPEN | HALF_OPEN`.
  - `n8n-io/n8n/packages/@n8n/instance-ai/src/mcp/mcp-client-manager.ts` caches keyed clients in `Map<string, ...>` and cleans up by key.
  - `HeyPuter/puter/src/backend/clients/database/MySQLDatabaseClient.ts` keeps distinct primary/replica pools.
- **Local code**
  - `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts` has module singleton `runtime` shared across all `DoltClient` instances.
  - `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` already routes fallback `dolt -> sqlite -> jsonl`, but Dolt state still global.

## Recommended runtime design
Use one runtime bucket per pool key:

```ts
type PoolKey = string; // host:port/database/user

type DoltRuntime = {
  pool: Pool | null;
  breakerOpenUntil: number;
  consecutiveFailures: number;
  probeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null;
  probing: boolean;
  metrics: { ok: boolean; latencyMs: number; at: string }[];
  lastHealthyAt: number;
};

const runtimes = new Map<PoolKey, DoltRuntime>();
```

Key = `host:port/database/user`.
Rules:
- one broken key stays local
- pool + breaker + probe + metrics all bucketed by key
- `disconnect()` closes only that key
- stale key rotation ends old pool before new pool lives
- probe timer `unref()`

## Timeout / cancellation
Best path:
- use mysql2 query-level `timeout` first
- wrap user request with `AbortSignal.timeout()` or `AbortSignal.any([reqSignal, AbortSignal.timeout(...)])`
- on abort, destroy in-flight connection, not only reject wrapper promise

Why:
- pure `Promise.race` hides latency but lets query keep running
- mysql2 `timeout` kills connection, so work cannot leak into reused conn
- for pooled connection, timeout should poison only that conn, not whole project bucket

## Health probe
Keep probe small:
- `SELECT 1`
- no heavy commit hash or join
- single probe in flight per key
- skip probe if breaker open
- probe interval `unref()`
- success resets failure count + opens breaker closed
- failure increments counter and records latency

## Breaker choice
- **Need simple, testable, tiny state machine?** keep custom breaker.
- **Need built-in stats, fallback hooks, richer knobs?** use `opossum`.
- For this app, custom breaker wins: fewer deps, easier per-key map, easier fake tests.

## Testing strategy
Must add tests for:
- key A fail does not trip key B
- key rotation closes old pool, opens new pool
- query timeout destroys conn / marks failure
- breaker open skips DB and returns fast fallback
- probe success closes breaker
- probe failure opens breaker after threshold
- JSONL/SQLite fallback still visible and fast

## Red flags
- module-global singleton runtime
- `Promise.race` timeout with no destroy path
- one breaker for all projects
- probe interval without `unref()`
- fallback that hides degraded state

## Child bead map
- **forge-ojn4.2**: per-key pool map + lifecycle
- **forge-ojn4.6**: timeout/cancel path + no leaked work
- **forge-ojn4.9**: probe/breaker/fallback behavior + tests

## Verification
- mysql2 docs consulted
- Dolt docs consulted
- Node timeout docs consulted
- GitHub snippets checked
- Local runtime shape reviewed

## Follow-ups
Need if want sharper answer:
- current `dolt-client.test.ts`
- any prod load numbers
- any Dolt server config file for timeout knobs

## Beads
Claimed then closed `forge-ojn412`.

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "summary": "Research done. Singleton Dolt runtime bad shape; per-key map with mysql2 query timeout, abort-aware cancel, small probe, local breaker best.",
  "files_changed": [],
  "follow_ups": [
    "Add tests for per-key isolation, timeout destroy, probe recovery, breaker-open fallback."
  ],
  "risks": [
    "Promise.race timeout alone leaks DB work.",
    "Global breaker/metrics still poison unrelated project ports."
  ],
  "verification": [
    "mysql2 pool and query-timeout docs checked.",
    "Node AbortSignal.timeout / AbortSignal.any docs checked.",
    "Dolt MySQL-compatible server docs checked.",
    "GitHub patterns checked for pool usage and breaker state."
  ]
}
```

---

title: Research React dashboard stale-while-revalidate + WebSocket merge patterns
description:
## PROBLEM

The realtime dashboard performance epic needs current external guidance for React state/data-loading patterns that keep cached data visible, refresh in the background, and merge WebSocket updates without stale HTTP responses overwriting newer events. The current app uses React, Zustand, manual fetch calls, and `@tanstack/react-virtual`, but not a dedicated query/cache library.

## SUCCESS

Produce evidence-backed recommendations on whether to keep a small custom Zustand cache or introduce a library such as TanStack Query/SWR for this dashboard. Recommendations must specifically address instant WebSocket upserts, stale-while-revalidate, tab-level lazy queries, abort/cancellation, request dedupe, and avoiding unrelated refetches on tab navigation.

## SCOPE

- External research only: current docs, GitHub snippets, GitWiki/DeepWiki/context7 where useful.
- Local context from epic `forge-ojn4`, especially `useGithubActivity`, `MainPane`, `stores/github`, and `useGraphData`.
- Output should map recommendations to child beads `forge-ojn4.1`, `forge-ojn4.4`, and `forge-ojn4.10`.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not edit files.
- Do not implement the recommended library.
- Do not review unrelated UI aesthetics.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WebSocket updates must stay instant and authoritative over older HTTP responses.
- Prefer minimal dependency changes unless evidence strongly supports a library.
- Cite current docs/snippets and distinguish docs from opinion.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Current React/Zustand/TanStack Query/SWR docs consulted.
- [ ] At least two real-world GitHub implementation snippets or repo patterns checked.
- [ ] Recommendation includes pros/cons and migration cost for this repo.
- [ ] Recommendation includes test strategy for WS-vs-HTTP freshness.

## OUTPUT

Return a concise research brief with sources, recommended approach, rejected alternatives, code patterns to copy/adapt, and specific acceptance criteria to add to the implementation/test beads.
notes:
### Specialist Output — researcher (job 677776) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary
Recommendation: **TanStack Query for server state, small Zustand for UI/live overlay**.  
Why: current dashboard needs cached data visible, background refresh, dedupe, abort/cancel, and WS wins over stale HTTP. TanStack Query docs cover all of that directly; SWR covers some, but Query fits multi-tab, multi-source merge better.

## Status
- React docs checked
- TanStack Query docs checked
- SWR docs checked
- Zustand docs checked
- Real-world GitHub snippets checked: `langflow-ai/langflow`, `TryGhost/Ghost`, `coder/coder`, `multica-ai/multica`

## Evidence
### Docs
- **React**: `useEffect` cleanup/ignore flag stops stale async result overwrite.  
  Source: https://github.com/reactjs/react.dev/blob/main/src/content/learn/synchronizing-with-effects.md
- **TanStack Query**:
  - `staleTime` marks fresh vs stale
  - `gcTime` keeps inactive cache 5 min by default
  - `enabled` gates lazy/dependent queries
  - `placeholderData: keepPreviousData` keeps old data on key change
  - `queryFn` gets `AbortSignal`
  - `cancelQueries`, `setQueryData`, `invalidateQueries`
  - in-flight request dedupe via shared promise
  Sources:
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/reference/QueryClient.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/caching.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/important-defaults.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/query-cancellation.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/dependent-queries.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/placeholder-query-data.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/updates-from-mutation-responses.md
- **SWR**:
  - `mutate` updates cache + revalidate
  - `dedupingInterval` dedupes
  - `keepPreviousData` keeps old data on key change
  - `revalidateOnFocus` default on
  Sources:
  - https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/blob/main/content/docs/mutation.es.mdx
  - https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/blob/main/content/docs/revalidation.mdx
  - https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/blob/main/content/blog/swr-v2.es.mdx
- **Zustand**:
  - selector subscriptions + shallow equality good for client state
  Sources:
  - https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand/blob/main/README.md
  - https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand/blob/main/docs/reference/middlewares/subscribe-with-selector.md

### Real-world snippets
- **Langflow**: live message stream merges into cache with `queryClient.setQueryData(cacheKey, (old: Message[] = []) => ...)`.  
  https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/main/src/frontend/src/components/core/playgroundComponent/chat-view/utils/message-utils.ts
- **Ghost**: keyed cache patching with `setQueryData(queryKey, ...)` for live account/activity updates.  
  https://github.com/TryGhost/Ghost/blob/main/apps/activitypub/src/hooks/use-activity-pub-queries.ts
- **coder/coder**: `cancelQueries` before optimistic writes to stop old snapshot overwrite.  
  https://github.com/coder/coder/blob/main/site/src/api/queries/chats.ts
- **multica-ai/multica**: same anti-overwrite pattern with `cancelQueries` + `setQueryData`.  
  https://github.com/multica-ai/multica/blob/main/packages/core/issues/mutations.ts

## Recommendation
### Pick
- **Use TanStack Query**
- **Keep Zustand only for**:
  - selected tab / UI state
  - unread flags / instant WS overlay if needed
  - small local graph cache if single-consumer

### Reject
- **SWR-only**: good for simple fetch cache, but Query gives cleaner cancel/refetch/invalidate toolbox for this dashboard shape.
- **Zustand-only**: doable, but means rebuilding stale flags, request dedupe, cancellation, background refetch policy, and cache key semantics by hand.

## Copy/adapt patterns
- **WS upsert**: `queryClient.setQueryData(key, old => mergeById(old, incoming))`
- **Stale HTTP guard**: carry `updated_at`, `version`, or `seq`; drop older payloads
- **Background refresh**: `staleTime` > 0 + `isFetching` indicator
- **Lazy tab loads**: `enabled: activeTab === 'prs'`
- **No sibling tab refetch**: separate query keys per tab/repo
- **Abort stale fetch**: pass `signal` into `fetch`
- **Avoid flicker on tab switch**: `placeholderData: keepPreviousData`

## Bead mapping
- **forge-ojn4.1**: activity feed / WS merge
  - WS event wins over older HTTP
  - merge by id
  - sync hint = targeted invalidate, not blind full reload
- **forge-ojn4.4**: repo tab data loading
  - lazy query per tab
  - keep old data visible on switch
  - no unrelated refetch on hidden tabs
- **forge-ojn4.10**: graph / secondary console data
  - keep small cache if single consumer
  - if reused across panes, move to Query
  - focus refetch only for active view

## Acceptance criteria
- Late HTTP response cannot overwrite newer WS event
- Switching tabs does not refetch inactive tab data
- Same query key across components makes one network request
- In-flight request aborts on tab change/unmount
- Cached data stays visible during background refresh
- Tests cover WS-before-HTTP and HTTP-before-WS order

## Verification
- Current docs consulted: yes
- Real-world snippets consulted: yes
- Migration cost understood: moderate for TanStack Query, high for custom Zustand-only

- `setQueryData` for WS upserts

**Small Zustand store** for:

- **WS wins over HTTP**

  ignore response if `seq < latestSeq` or `updated_at` older than cache
- **Tab switch no flicker**  
  `placeholderData: keepPreviousData`
- **Tab lazy load**  
  `enabled: activeTab === "prs"`

- no refetch on unrelated tab switch

Graph / secondary pane
- keep current custom cache only if single-consumer
- if shared across panes, move to Query
- focus/refetch only for visible graph view

- WS update applied instantly, stays authoritative over older HTTP

- Hidden tab does not refetch on navigation
- Duplicate mounts share one in-flight request
- Request aborts on unmount / key change
- Background refresh happens without blank state
- Tests cover:
  - HTTP late vs WS early
  - WS late vs HTTP early
  - tab switch no data loss
  - cancel old request before new query

## Risks
- Query migration cost moderate: need query keys, fetchers, cache write paths
- Mixed state model needs discipline: server state in Query, UI state in Zustand
- If WS payload lacks version/seq, merge safety weaker

title: specialist:security-auditor
notes:
### Specialist Output — security-auditor (job 6ed699) [DONE]

## Summary

Scoped audit run on uncommitted terminal changes. Result: **FAIL**.  
Main blocker: terminal WebSocket opens shell path with no request auth, no origin check, no per-user admin verification. If shell provider enabled, reachable user can open PTY. Reattach/session logic itself mostly okay, but access gate broken before token matters.

## Status

findings

## Changes

None. Read-only audit only.

## Verification

- Read source:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/node-pty-helper.cjs`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/terminal/TerminalStream.tsx`
- Checked uncommitted diff with `git diff`, `git status --short`
- Used line-numbered review with `nl -ba`

## Risks

- Unauthenticated shell access when provider enabled
- Admin-only gate not tied to requester
- Secret-bearing env inherited into spawned shell beyond tiny scrub list

## Follow-ups

- Gate `/api/console/terminal/ws` with real requester authz before upgrade
- Bind verified-admin to request/session identity, not process env
- Expand env scrub or switch to explicit allowlist env for PTY child
- Add origin check for WebSocket upgrade if browser clients only

## Security Audit Summary

- Scope reviewed:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/node-pty-helper.cjs`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/terminal/TerminalStream.tsx`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts` for reachability/session checks
- Overall risk: high
- Mode(s) used:
  - static secure-code review
  - bounty-style exploitability triage
- Commands/sources used:
  - `git status --short`
  - `git diff --stat`
  - `git diff -- ...scoped files...`
  - `sed -n`
  - `nl -ba`
  - local source evidence only

## Findings

- ID: GB-TERM-001
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-306 Missing Authentication for Critical Function; CWE-1385 Missing Origin Validation in WebSockets
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:129-140` upgrades any WebSocket request with no auth/origin check
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:147-163` routes `/api/console/terminal/ws` straight into `terminalBridge`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:77-107` `open()` creates PTY session for any connected socket
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx:58` client opens provider kind `"pty"`
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Direct. Browser or script can connect to `/api/console/terminal/ws`.
  - CORS middleware does not protect WebSockets.
  - If operator enables shell provider and host reachable, attacker gets interactive shell.
  - Even localhost-only setup still exposed to cross-site WebSocket from malicious webpage opened by local user.
- Impact:
  - Full command execution in repo-root PTY.
  - Read repo, modify worktree, pivot via inherited env/secrets.
- Recommended fix:
  - Enforce authz before `server.upgrade()` for `/api/console/terminal/ws`.
  - Check `Origin` against trusted UI origin.
  - Reject unauthenticated or non-admin callers before socket creation.
- Verification after fix:
  - Unauthenticated WebSocket upgrade to `/api/console/terminal/ws` returns 401/403.
  - Cross-origin browser attempt rejected.
  - Authenticated admin path still opens PTY.

- ID: GB-TERM-002
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-285 Improper Authorization
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:33-35` marks requester verified admin from process env: `env.GITBOARD_SHELL_PROVIDER_ADMIN_VERIFIED === "1"`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts:70-89` admin-only decision depends on `context.isVerifiedAdmin`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:124` registry created once from process env, not per request/connection
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Direct design flaw. Admin-only control not bound to socket owner/session/user.
  - If process env sets verified flag, every terminal client becomes verified admin.
  - If env not set, no one can use admin-only feature. Both cases break intended policy.
- Impact:
  - Admin-only shell restriction collapses into global server toggle.
  - Any connected client can inherit admin shell capability once env flag enabled.
- Recommended fix:
  - Move provider enable/authz decision to request-time.
  - Pass authenticated caller context into terminal open/attach flow.
  - Remove `GITBOARD_SHELL_PROVIDER_ADMIN_VERIFIED` process-wide trust for user authorization.
- Verification after fix:
  - Non-admin authenticated client gets 403 even when shell provider globally enabled.
  - Verified admin client can open session.
  - Two simultaneous users see different authz outcomes.

- ID: GB-TERM-003
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-200 Exposure of Sensitive Information
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts:33` scrub list only removes `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `SSH_AUTH_SOCK`, `SSH_AGENT_PID`, `NPM_TOKEN`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:192-200` child env clones almost all `process.env`, then re-adds `PATH`, `HOME`, `SHELL`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/node-pty-helper.cjs:10-17` helper passes config.env straight into PTY spawn
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Reachable through GB-TERM-001. Interactive shell can read inherited env immediately.
  - Also risks leaking tokens with variant names: `GH_TOKEN`, `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, cloud creds, internal service vars.
- Impact:
  - Secret exposure inside terminal session.
  - Turns shell access into credential exposure/pivot.
- Recommended fix:
  - Replace denylist scrub with explicit allowlist env for PTY child.
  - Keep only minimal vars: `HOME`, `PATH`, `SHELL`, `PWD`, `TERM`, maybe locale vars.
- Verification after fix:
  - `env` inside spawned shell shows only approved keys.
  - Known secret vars absent.

## Dependency Advisory Triage

- Package: `node-pty`
- Installed version: `1.1.0`
- Advisory/source: none reviewed in this scoped audit
- Vulnerable range: unknown
- Fixed version: unknown
- Reachability: direct
- Recommendation:
  - Run package advisory check in separate dependency-audit bead if merge depends on supply-chain signoff.

## Non-findings / Dropped Noise

- `cwd` allowlist check present. `provider-registry.ts:174-181`. Fixed cwd resolves to repo root only. No user-controlled cwd path seen.
- Shell allowlist present. `provider-registry.ts:184-189`, `shell-provider-policy.ts:32`. zsh support intentional. No direct injection sink.
- Node-pty helper IPC uses JSON lines + base64 payloads. `node-pty-helper.cjs:22-43`, `provider-registry.ts:130-166`. No shell interpolation or command-string injection found in helper channel.
- Reattach token exists and required for attach. `bridge.ts:113-124`. Token stored in memory only, not localStorage. Good.
- Session ownership enforced for `input`, `resize`, `detach`, `exit`. `bridge.ts:127-171`. No ownership bypass found after attach.
- Dev gate logic exists. `shell-provider-policy.ts:75-87`. But auth blockers above still fail task because gate/auth not enough when feature enabled.

## Residual Risk and Follow-ups

- Block merge until GB-TERM-001 and GB-TERM-002 fixed.
- After fix, run narrow re-audit on:
  - WebSocket upgrade auth/origin checks
  - request-bound admin verification
  - PTY env allowlist contents
- Optional next bead:
  - dependency audit for new `node-pty` addition

## Beads

- No bead id provided in prompt. No `bd` command run.

## Machine-readable block

title: Add UI realtime smoke tests for stale-while-revalidate dashboard behavior
description:
## PROBLEM

The central product constraint is that the dashboard stays realtime: HTTP caching and lazy loading must not make WS updates feel delayed or allow stale HTTP responses to overwrite fresh WS state. This needs explicit UI-level regression coverage.

## SUCCESS

UI tests or a reliable smoke harness prove that cached/stale data can render immediately, loading is panel-scoped, tab switches avoid unrelated fetches, and simulated WS messages update visible GitHub/beads state instantly.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts` tests or component tests.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx` / GitHub tab tests.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx` tests for WS open/closed transitions if feasible.
- Test helpers for fake fetch and fake websocket messages.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not require a real browser if component/hook tests cover the behavior.
- Do not test every visual detail of timelines/cards.
- Do not replace backend boundary tests.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WS events must be simulated at the same abstraction used by existing hooks/components.
- Tests should assert visible/dashboard state, not just internal store calls.
- Fetch-count assertions should remain stable across harmless implementation refactors.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] GitHub tab switch test proves only tab-relevant endpoints are fetched.
- [ ] GitHub WS event test proves event/PR/issue upsert is visible without manual refresh.
- [ ] Stale HTTP response test proves older fetch results do not remove newer WS upserts.
- [ ] Beads close/upsert WS test proves issue moves between lists without full reload where feasible.
- [ ] `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` or targeted tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return test files, commands, and the realtime behaviors covered vs. left for manual smoke.

## APPROACH NOTES

This is the explicit guardrail for the user requirement: keep WS instant refresh and dashboard feel while optimizing slow HTTP/data paths.
notes:
Research integration:
UI realtime tests should assert the core invariant: WS updates are instant and authoritative over stale HTTP/cache.
- With TanStack Query: simulate an older HTTP response arriving after a WS upsert; visible state must retain the WS-updated record.
- Tab switch fetch-count tests should prove only active tab data is requested, with cached previous data visible during refresh.
- Beads close/upsert WS tests should move items between open/closed lists without full reload.
- Graph/specialist UI should show cached data immediately and refresh via WS/epoch invalidation rather than focus-triggered full reloads.
close_reason: Done: UI realtime smoke coverage added for GitHub and beads stale-while-revalidate behavior.

title: Add boundary regression tests for Dolt, beads, graph, and GitHub latency paths
description:
## PROBLEM

The optimization work touches API boundaries, source fallback, route matching, and remote GitHub fan-out. Without boundary tests, future changes can reintroduce slow full-list fetches, route-order 404s, global Dolt breaker coupling, or unbounded GitHub detail waits.

## SUCCESS

Boundary regression tests cover the key backend failure modes and can run in CI/local development without requiring live GitHub or live Dolt where mocks/fakes are sufficient.

## SCOPE

- `apps/beadboard/tests` or colocated route/client tests for beads route order and Dolt runtime isolation.
- `apps/gitboard/tests` for graph route cache/invalidation, GitHub detail timeout/partial response, and poller scheduling.
- Test fixtures/fakes only; no product code except minimal testability hooks if approved by implementation beads.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not create E2E browser tests here; UI realtime tests are separate.
- Do not depend on live external GitHub or Dolt servers for deterministic tests.
- Do not overfit tests to implementation-private details unless needed for the bug class.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Tests must be deterministic and reasonably fast.
- Prefer public route/client behavior assertions over deep internals where possible.
- If product code needs testability hooks, keep them small and non-invasive.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Test fails against the old `/issues/closed` route-order bug.
- [ ] Test covers per-pool Dolt runtime isolation or equivalent behavior.
- [ ] Test covers graph cache avoiding repeated scan calls.
- [ ] Test covers GitHub detail timeout/partial results.
- [ ] Test covers poller no inter-repo sleep and prompt WS publication.

## OUTPUT

Return test files, command output, and any remaining untested risk that needs manual smoke coverage.

## APPROACH NOTES

This test bead should be wired after the related implementation beads but can be developed incrementally as each fix lands.
notes:
Research integration:
Boundary regression tests should cover:
- Dolt Map<poolKey,runtime>: one key timeout/breaker does not affect another.
- Beads `/issues/closed` route order and open/closed payload split.
- Graph cache: repeated request avoids repeated scan; WS/epoch invalidates.
- GitHub REST: 304 no-op, persisted ETag use, watermark stop, 403/429 backoff, PR detail partial timeout, SHA cache invalidation.
- Hono/Bun cache: TTL, SWR stale return, in-flight dedupe, timeout handling.
close_reason: Done: backend performance boundary coverage contract added and target suites pass.

title: Rework GitHub poller scheduling for bounded concurrency and persisted freshness
description:
## PROBLEM

`GithubPoller.pollRepos()` serializes repos and sleeps between repos based on a computed active window. With many repositories, a cycle can take a very long time, keeping dashboard GitHub state stale even though WebSocket updates are meant to feel instant. ETags are partly modeled in DB but runtime ETag use is not fully persisted.

## SUCCESS

GitHub polling checks due repositories with bounded concurrency, no inter-repo sleep inside the loop, persisted ETags/watermarks, rate-limit-aware backoff, and WebSocket publication of changed PRs/issues as soon as they are discovered.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts` only for backward-compatible poll-state schema additions if needed.
- Tests for scheduling/watermark/ETag behavior.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not replace the current GitHub REST ingestion pipeline wholesale.
- Do not alter dashboard rendering directly.
- Do not increase GitHub API usage beyond safe bounded limits.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Respect rate limits and pause states.
- Persist enough poll state that restarts do not force unnecessary backfill.
- WS `github:pr.upsert` and `github:issue.upsert` events must publish promptly when records change.
- Run GitNexus impact on `GithubPoller` before editing.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Tests prove `pollRepos()` does not sleep once per repo in production scheduling path.
- [ ] Tests prove due repos can be processed with bounded concurrency.
- [ ] ETag/watermark state survives through `github_repo_poll_state` where applicable.
- [ ] Changed PR/issue records still publish WS events immediately.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return scheduling model, concurrency/backoff values, DB state changes if any, and validation evidence.

## APPROACH NOTES

Current `pollRepos()` uses `getRepoActiveWindow()` then awaits a timeout inside the repo loop. That should become per-repo next-due scheduling, not a blocking delay between repos.
notes:
Research integration from job f41b36:
- GitHub polling should use conditional GET with persisted ETags/If-None-Match, obey X-Poll-Interval where present, and respect Retry-After/x-ratelimit-reset on 403/429.
- Use per_page=100 and stop pagination on old watermark or short page; do not rely only on fixed 3-page caps.
- Octokit core + paginate-rest + throttling + retry is recommended if adding a library; raw fetch is acceptable only if header/rate-limit behavior remains explicit.
- Poll due repos with bounded concurrency and no inter-repo sleep; publish WS only when DB delta occurs. 304/no-change must not emit.
close_reason: Done: bounded due-repo GitHub polling, persisted ETags, rate-limit backoff, and regression tests.

title: Reduce specialist jobs endpoint latency without breaking live overlays
description:
## PROBLEM

Logs show `/api/specialists/jobs` and `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` repeatedly taking multiple seconds. These endpoints feed graph/console overlays and can make the dashboard feel blocked when navigating, even though live specialist status should be lightweight.

## SUCCESS

Specialist job endpoints reuse expensive DAO/attach resources, cache summaries safely, and expose live status quickly enough for dashboard overlays. Realtime or epoch-based invalidation keeps running/waiting job state fresh.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts` only where profiling identifies expensive repeated work.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useAllSpecialistJobs.ts`, `useInFlightJobs.ts`, or graph overlay hooks only if client refresh behavior needs adjustment.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not redesign specialist data model.
- Do not remove live specialist overlays from graph/beads UI.
- Do not change specialist CLI behavior.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Running/waiting job status must not be stale beyond the chosen short TTL or epoch invalidation rule.
- Avoid repeated attach-pool construction per request when safe.
- Keep endpoint response shapes backward-compatible.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Local timing evidence shows `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` avoids repeated multi-second work for cached/unchanged state.
- [ ] Live running/waiting jobs still update in graph/beads overlays.
- [ ] Tests or smoke cover cache invalidation via epoch/repo changes.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return bottleneck source, cache/reuse strategy, timing evidence, changed files, and residual freshness risks.

## APPROACH NOTES

Current default DAO bundle TTL is 2s and may recreate attach resources often. Start by profiling before changing DAO internals.
notes:
Research integration from job bcbd2a:
- For specialist endpoints, use short in-process TTL, stale-while-revalidate, and in-flight dedupe around expensive attach-pool/DAO reads.
- Prefer epoch/file-watcher invalidation over polling-only freshness. Running/waiting jobs may need shorter TTL than historical job lists.
- Cache keys should include repo set / query params / epoch. Avoid response-shape changes.
- Do not add Redis or external infrastructure; this is a local dashboard cache.
close_reason: Done: specialist job endpoints use short epoch-aware caches and longer reusable DAO bundle with tests.

title: Cache and progressively load GitHub PR/repo detail fan-out
description:
## PROBLEM

GitHub detail endpoints can fan out heavily: PR detail fetches comments, reviews, review comments, commits, files, and timeline with up to three pages each; reports listing fetches full content for every report just to parse frontmatter; markdown/report tabs fetch on every open. These detail paths are secondary but currently can make navigation feel blocked.

## SUCCESS

PR details and repo content panels load progressively with timeouts and short-lived cache. Opening a detail view shows cached or partial data quickly, then fills slow sections without blocking the rest of the dashboard.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoContentPanels.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts`
- Optional SQLite cache additions in `apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts` only if needed.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not migrate GitHub detail fetching to GraphQL in this bead.
- Do not change timeline/comment rendering semantics.
- Do not block the main Activity/PR list on detail hydration.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Use request timeouts/AbortController or equivalent for remote GitHub calls.
- Preserve partial-result behavior with explicit per-section errors.
- Cache invalidation must account for PR `updated_at`, report SHA, README SHA, or TTL.
- Avoid consuming GitHub rate limit aggressively.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] PR detail route has bounded latency under a failing/slow GitHub subrequest.
- [ ] Detail UI can show partial/cached data while slow sections load.
- [ ] Reports list does not fetch every full report body before first paint.
- [ ] Tests cover timeout/partial error behavior where practical.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return cache strategy, timeout values, partial response behavior, changed files, and validation evidence.

## APPROACH NOTES

One PR detail request can currently produce up to 18 GitHub REST calls. Consider section-level fetches or server-side cache keyed by repo/number/updated_at.
notes:
Checked local GitHub fetch/poll paths plus GitHub REST, Octokit, pagination, rate-limit, ETag, and contents docs. Found current PR detail route fans out 6 REST pages; markdown/report fetches eagerly parse file content; poller has in-memory ETag only and repo_poll_state ETag fields unused.
Research integration from job f41b36:
- PR detail should be progressive and cache-backed; avoid one UI action waiting on all subresources.
- Recommended cache key: pr:${repo}#${number}:${updated_at}:${head_sha}; invalidate on updated_at/head_sha/state/subresource-error change.
- Markdown/report cache key should include repo/ref/path/sha; report metadata should be cached by SHA and body fetched only when opened.
- Use per_page=100 and Link/paginate iteration where paging is needed, with hard caps as safety not primary logic.
- Use timeouts and partial section errors so one slow GitHub endpoint does not block the whole detail panel.
Reset to open: forge-ojn4.5 depends on forge-ojn4.4 and no implementation work is being done in this session yet.
close_reason: Done: bounded PR detail fan-out with partial errors/cache, directory-first reports list, readme/report fetch timeouts, and regression tests.

title: Fix beads closed-issue routing and reduce full-list payload work
description:
## PROBLEM

`apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` defines `/projects/:id/issues/:issueId` before `/projects/:id/issues/closed`, so closed-list requests can be interpreted as an issue detail request. The gitboard frontend works around this by fetching up to 1000 issues and splitting closed/open client-side, which increases Dolt hydration and payload work.

## SUCCESS

Closed issue list requests reliably hit the closed route, clients can request only the data they need, and the dashboard no longer needs the “backend `/issues/closed` returns 404” workaround for Dolt-backed projects.

## SCOPE

- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts` if client behavior needs adjustment.
- Route regression tests in beadboard/gitboard test surface.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not change issue card rendering or triage scoring.
- Do not redesign beads API filtering beyond closed/open payload partitioning.
- Do not remove the all-issues endpoint; keep compatibility.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Route order fix must be backward-compatible.
- WS `beads:issue.*` updates must continue to update open/closed collections instantly.
- If reintroducing separate closed fetches, do not block initial open issue render on closed history.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] `/api/beads/projects/:id/issues/closed` returns closed issues, not 404 detail lookup.
- [ ] Open feed can render without fetching all closed history synchronously.
- [ ] WS close/upsert events move issues between open and closed lists without refresh.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return API behavior before/after, changed frontend fetch pattern, and regression test evidence.

## APPROACH NOTES

This is a small boundary fix but high leverage because it removes one cause of overfetching around Dolt hydration.
notes:
Research integration:
- Route-order and overfetch fix supports the wider SWR plan: clients should request exactly the visible slice and let WS events patch open/closed state.
- Avoid using the all-issues endpoint as a workaround for `/issues/closed`; closed history should be lazy/non-blocking.
- UI tests should prove `beads:issue.close` and `beads:issue.upsert` move items between visible lists without a full reload.
Implementation output 2026-05-20:
- Moved `/projects/:id/issues/closed` above `/projects/:id/issues/:issueId` in `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`.
- `BeadsRepoView` now requests active statuses with limit 100 for initial render, then loads closed history separately via `/issues/closed` without blocking the open feed.
- Added `apps/beadboard/tests/api/routes/beads-routes.test.ts` route regression coverage.
- Extended `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.test.tsx` for non-blocking closed load and realtime close movement.
Validation:
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/beadboard test -- tests/api/routes/beads-routes.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.test.tsx`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint`
- Code-sanity 9617ae was stopped after stale/no findings; targeted tests cover the small route/UI diff.
close_reason: Done: fixed closed-issue route order, removed full-list overfetch workaround, and added route/UI realtime regression tests.

title: Sidebar repo tree: replace 10s poll with WS-driven updates
description: Currently useRepoTree (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts) polls every 10s and fires ~2N backend calls per tick (getStats + getConnection for every beads project). With ~30 projects that's ~63 calls / 10s — saturates the API and causes projects to flicker in/out mid-navigation. Short-term fix bumps interval to 60s and moves getConnection to mount-only; this issue tracks the proper end-state: WS-driven sidebar with zero steady-state polling load.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Initial mount: one-shot fetch of /api/github/repos + /api/github/repo-stats + /api/beads/projects + per-project getStats + getConnection. (2) Subscribe to WS channels: beads:changes, beads:source_health, github:activity. (3) On beads:changes envelope: debounce ~500ms then refetch getStats(projectId) only for that project. (4) On beads:source_health envelope: update beadsSource chip directly from the envelope payload (no refetch). (5) On github:activity envelope: refetch repo-stats for the affected full_name only (debounced). (6) Honor since_seq/boot_id reconcile on WS reconnect per realtime-envelope contract. (7) Remove setInterval / REFRESH_MS. (8) Sidebar counts and chips visibly update within ~1s of the underlying change (verified via manual e2e: claim a bead, watch sidebar count tick).
notes: Related memories: realtime-envelope-uses-boot-id-uuid-per-server, beads-watcher-forge-igg-diffs-4-entity-snapshots, gitboard-realtime-websocket-server-expects-client-messages-w, gitboard-sidebar-beads-source-chip-userepotree-polls-api. Predecessor: short-term fix on PR 12 / fix/forge-live-specialist-observability already lands attach-pool dead-cache + shared-server scanner routing. This bead is the polling-pressure follow-up.

title: Specialists tab: live cockpit (chain cards + structured detail pane)
description:
## PROBLEM
The Console > Specialists tab is currently a placeholder. Operators have no full-page surface for "show me every chain across all repos right now, grouped by root bead, with status badges, click-to-expand turn logs." The existing BottomDrawer Specialists panel is the glance view (last 50 jobs, terminal-style rows); the Console tab needs the deep view: chain cards grouped by root bead, filter chips, click-card → structured detail pane on the right.

## SUCCESS
- Console > Specialists renders a two-pane layout:
  - LEFT (60% width): chain card list, one card per chain_id, grouped by root bead. Each card shows: root bead id (JBM) + title (Inter, truncated), chain length count, dominant status badge (running / waiting / done / error / cancelled — pick the latest non-done state), list of role chips (executor, reviewer, etc.) with status dots, elapsed time of the chain, last-message excerpt (1 line).
  - RIGHT (40% width): structured detail pane. Empty state when no card is selected (small octicon + "Select a chain to see details"). When a card is selected: chain timeline (rows of jobs in chain order with status icon, role, job_id, elapsed, turns, tools, model), full last-output excerpt of the most recent job, "Open bead" button (calls useBeadSideDrawer.open(bead_id)).
- Filter chips above the card list: All · Running · Waiting · Done · Error · Cancelled. Multi-select OR-combined.
- Auto-refresh: poll /api/specialists/chains every 5s while the tab is visible. Pause when hidden (visibilitychange listener).
- Per-bead detail: when a chain card is clicked, the detail pane fetches /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id for the full job list.
- Live pulse on currently-running cards (same animation as Graph node).
- Empty state: "No specialist chains for this project yet" with a small dim octicon when the API returns an empty array.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.tsx (REPLACE the placeholder with real cockpit)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainCard.tsx (NEW) — single card primitive
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainDetailPane.tsx (NEW) — right pane structured view
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/FilterChips.tsx (NEW) — top filter chip row
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChains.ts (NEW) — polls /api/specialists/chains (new endpoint if absent) or aggregates from /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight + /api/specialists/jobs/recent. PREFER aggregating from existing endpoints first — do NOT add a new backend endpoint in this task unless aggregation is impossible.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChainDetail.ts (NEW) — fetches /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id on selection
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (MOD — .console-specialists-* classes, card layout, two-pane split)

## NON_GOALS
- No xterm specialist-feed pane in this task. The structured detail pane is the v1 deliverable. A future task tracks forge-lqyc.8 to add the xterm pane to the right side of the detail pane.
- No kill/stop/resume actions — read-only.
- No editing of bead notes from this view.
- No new backend endpoints if existing endpoints suffice. PREFER aggregating /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight + /api/specialists/jobs/recent (already returns chain_id) over adding /api/specialists/chains.
- No drag-to-resize on the pane split (60/40 fixed for v1).
- No multi-host coordination — single-host only.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- Visual contract: same flat surface as Feed. No card shadows, no rounded corners (radius 0). 1px var(--border-subtle) between cards. Hover background var(--row-hover).
- Each card height ~80px (header line + role chips line + footer line).
- Card selected state: 2px var(--accent) left border replacing the default 1px border-subtle.
- Status badges: small octicon + label. Use existing color tokens — no new colors. Map: running=PlayIcon (--accent), waiting=ClockIcon (--text-secondary), done=CheckIcon (--text-muted), error=AlertIcon (--text-secondary, just text, no red flood — operator's neutralized palette doesn't have a hot red), cancelled=XCircleIcon (--text-muted).
- Filter chips reuse the same `.drawer-log-chip` class pattern from LogsTabPanel.tsx (toggleable, active=is-active, monospace).
- Detail pane scrolls independently from the card list.
- Polling MUST pause when document.visibilityState !== 'visible'.
- All chain_id and bead_id values render in JBM. All titles in Inter.
- Touch-friendly: cards have 8px vertical padding so they're scannable but not cramped.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] Manual: Console > Specialists renders the two-pane layout with at least 1 chain card visible.
- [ ] Manual: clicking a chain card populates the detail pane with chain timeline.
- [ ] Manual: filter chips work — selecting "Running" hides done/error/cancelled chains.
- [ ] Manual: during a live specialist run, the chain card pulses; chain stops pulsing when status flips to done.
- [ ] Manual: "Open bead" button in detail pane opens the BeadSideDrawer (from forge-f6qk.4).
- [ ] Network tab: polling pauses when tab is hidden.
- [ ] Empty state renders when no chains exist (test with a fresh project).

## OUTPUT
- 7-file diff stat (1 replaced + 4 new + 1 CSS + 1 type)
- Playwright screenshot of Specialists tab with 1 chain selected and detail pane showing.
- Confirmation that no new backend endpoint was added (or, if absolutely required, the new endpoint's contract documented in OUTPUT).
- Confirmation that the existing BottomDrawer Specialists panel still works (no regression in drawer behavior).

## REFERENCES
- forge-gud9 (closed) — drawer Specialists panel pattern (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistsTabPanel.tsx). REUSE patterns from there but DO NOT touch that file.
- forge-lqyc.8 — future readonly specialist-feed xterm provider. After it lands, a follow-up task will mount it inside ChainDetailPane.tsx as a tab next to the structured view.

## APPROACH NOTES
- Aggregation strategy: /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight already returns `{in_flight, recent_history}`. Both have `chain_id`. Group by chain_id in the frontend to form chains. For chain detail, /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id already exists (per forge-3ue5.7).
- ChainCard's "dominant status" = the most recent job's status in that chain. If chain has any running/waiting job → use that. Otherwise → latest by updated_at.
- Roles in the chain card: derive from the union of `specialist` field across that chain's jobs.
- Detail pane chain timeline: render jobs sorted by chain_kind (executor first, then reviewer/sanity/etc., then any post-impl roles), then by updated_at ASC within each kind.
- For "last-message excerpt" — use the latest job's `last_output` (already in /jobs response, truncate to 80 chars stripped of newlines).
- Performance: chain list re-renders only when /jobs response data hash changes. Use a single useMemo on `chains` derivation.

title: Bead side-drawer: reusable right-slide overlay for bead context
description:
## PROBLEM
Graph nodes and Feed rows both need a way to drill into a single bead's full context (description, notes, dossier sections, live + past specialists) without losing place. A modal kills the cross-tab pulse and scroll position; a full-page nav loses the graph; expanding inline breaks the graph layout. A right-slide side-drawer (separate from the existing bottom drawer) is the canonical "context pane" pattern — bead lives on the right, the active tab stays underneath. Once it exists, Graph node-clicks and Feed row-clicks both target the same overlay.

## SUCCESS
- New component `BeadSideDrawer` renders a right-slide overlay (slide in from right, half-width, opaque) over the Console MainPane content.
- Opens with `useBeadSideDrawer().open(beadId)`; closes with `.close()` or ESC.
- Content layout: bead id (JBM), title (Inter), classification metadata strip (priority chip, type, status), specialist owner chip if live + run-count chip if any history; dossier sections (Description, Notes, Labels, Related, Audit log, Dependency tree, SPECIALIST ACTIVITY) reusing the existing `DossierSection` primitive from IssueFeed.tsx.
- Reuses existing hooks: `useSpecialistOwnership(beadId)`, `useSpecialistHistory(beadId)`. No new fetching layer.
- Closes on: ESC key, click outside the drawer, an explicit X button in the header.
- Persistent header with X button + bead id; body scrolls; footer hosts a "Open in Feed" button that switches Console tab to Feed and scrolls the row into view.
- Closing the drawer fires onClose callback; component manages its own open state via the shared hook.
- BottomDrawer (Ctrl+J) continues to coexist — both can be open simultaneously without z-index fighting. SideDrawer is z-index 50; BottomDrawer is z-index 40.
- Mobile-friendly: viewport < 768px → drawer becomes full-screen overlay instead of half-width.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/BeadSideDrawer.tsx (NEW)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadSideDrawer.ts (NEW — Zustand or local-context-shaped store with .open / .close / .currentBeadId)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (MOD — .bead-side-drawer classes, slide animation, backdrop)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (MOD — mount <BeadSideDrawer /> at shell root inside ShellApp, sibling to MainPane)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (MOD — wire row-click to open the side-drawer instead of inline-expanding. KEEP the inline-expand toggle as an option — the side-drawer is a NEW affordance, not a replacement.)

## NON_GOALS
- No bead editing inside the side-drawer. Click "Open in Feed" to mutate.
- No drag-to-resize on the side-drawer (fixed half-width on desktop, full-screen on mobile).
- No multi-bead navigation (back/forward arrows) in v1.
- No xterm pane inside the side-drawer in v1 — same forge-lqyc.8 deferred polish.
- No replacement of the existing inline-expand dossier in IssueFeed — both options coexist.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- HARD: do NOT modify the existing BottomDrawer. It stays independent.
- Slide animation: transform translateX, 200ms ease-out (faster than the bottom drawer's 80ms because side-drawer slides further).
- Backdrop: rgba(0,0,0,0.4) overlay on the rest of the screen, click → close.
- Visual contract: same as Feed — var(--surface-primary) bg, JBM on IDs, Inter on title/sections, no new hex.
- Header height: 40px, X icon button (XIcon from @primer/octicons-react), aria-label "close bead drawer".
- ESC key handler must NOT conflict with the BottomDrawer's Ctrl+J (different key).
- Drawer state lives in module-scope (Zustand or singleton) so any tab can call `open(beadId)` without prop drilling.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] Manual: clicking a row in Feed opens the side-drawer with that bead's dossier.
- [ ] Manual: pressing ESC closes the side-drawer.
- [ ] Manual: clicking the backdrop closes the side-drawer.
- [ ] Manual: opening the side-drawer + Ctrl+J (bottom drawer) → both visible, no z-index fight.
- [ ] Manual: Open-in-Feed button switches Console tab to Feed if not already there and scrolls the row into view.
- [ ] Mobile viewport (< 768px): drawer goes full-screen.
- [ ] Playwright integration: useBeadSideDrawer.open is callable from outside React tree.

## OUTPUT
- 5-file diff stat.
- Playwright screenshot of side-drawer open over Feed tab.
- Playwright screenshot of side-drawer + bottom drawer co-open.
- Confirmation that IssueFeed.tsx's inline-expand still works (no regression in run-count chip or SPECIALIST ACTIVITY section).

## APPROACH NOTES
- Mount point: <ShellApp> in App.tsx, sibling of <MainPane>. The drawer needs to live above MainPane to receive backdrop clicks.
- Hook shape: `useBeadSideDrawer(): { beadId: string | null; open(id: string): void; close(): void }`. Backed by Zustand for cross-tree communication.
- Reuse `<IssueDossier />` exported from IssueFeed.tsx — same component that renders the inline-expand body. Pass the same props (issue, detail, projectId, issueById).
- For Open-in-Feed: use existing `useShellStore` setSurface/setTab + a DOM scroll-into-view by bead-id selector on the .row element.
- Don't reinvent dossier rendering inside the drawer — DossierSection + SafeMarkdown are already exported from IssueFeed.tsx.

title: Graph frontend: layered SVG renderer with full-relationship layout + node pulse + interactions
description:
## PROBLEM
The Graph backend (forge-f6qk.2) ships a payload of nodes + edges + live specialist overlay. The frontend needs to turn that into a single SVG canvas that lays out 250+ nodes legibly, encodes 9 distinct edge types visually, pulses the currently-running agent on its bead, and supports hover-highlight + click-to-side-drawer. The reference design (docs/graph/reference.html) shows the visual contract for 8 nodes; this bead scales it to the operator's real bead set and rewires the palette/fonts to the app's existing token system.

## SUCCESS
- New page mounted at Console > Graph (replaces the placeholder from forge-f6qk.1).
- Fetches /api/console/graph?project_id=<current-repo> on mount + on focus.
- Computes node positions via a layered LR layout that uses ALL 9 edge types as inputs (not blocks-only). `blocks` and `supersedes` are weighted heavier as scheduling primitives; `parent-child` and `related` are weighted lighter so they don't force layer changes. Specific algorithm: weighted dag-layered layout (e.g. dagre or a lightweight in-app port), with edge weights {blocks: 5, supersedes: 3, parent-child: 0.5, discovered-from: 2, validates: 2, caused-by: 2, tracks: 1, until: 1, related: 0.2}. Multi-edges between the same pair get arc routing (top + bottom) and are NEVER merged into a single line.
- Each node rendered per the reference HTML's anatomy: 3px priority band (left edge, color per priority: P0 red, P1 amber, P2-P4 dim), state glyph (◯ ready, ◐ in_progress, ◇ blocked, ◈ epic, ✓ closed, ✕ superseded), JBM bead-id (suffix bright, prefix dim), Inter title (truncate with ellipsis), small live-pulse dot on the left of the id when the bead has a running specialist.
- Pulse animation is 1.6s ease-in-out, applied to the dot AND a small ring around the agent badge — ONLY animation in the graph (per detail.md rule).
- Edges encoded per the reference HTML's palette but using app tokens for color (operators see the same neutral grays as Feed; the 9 edge colors are local SVG `stroke` values derived from the reference HTML's hex set, namespaced as CSS custom properties --graph-edge-blocks / --graph-edge-tracks / etc. so future theme work can override).
- Arrowhead direction is source→effect for blocks, supersedes, caused-by, validates, discovered-from, parent-child, tracks, until. `related` has no arrowhead.
- Hover a node → all incident edges get full opacity, all non-incident edges + nodes drop to 30% opacity. Hover off → restore.
- Click a node → fires `onNodeClick(beadId)` callback. The Graph component itself does not mount the side-drawer; it just emits the event. forge-f6qk.4 wires the side-drawer at the Console-tab level.
- Closed beads render at 45% opacity with dashed node border; superseded adds strikethrough on the title and shows the supersedes arrow toward the new bead.
- Pan/zoom via mouse drag + scroll wheel. Reset-view button (octicon SyncIcon) in the top-right corner of the panel.
- Performance target: 250 nodes / 400 edges render < 200ms after data arrival; hover-highlight is instant (no re-layout, only opacity).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx (REPLACE the placeholder with the real component)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts (NEW) — pure layout algorithm; takes nodes + edges + weights, returns positioned nodes + routed edges
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/GraphSvg.tsx (NEW) — pure SVG renderer; takes positioned data + interaction callbacks, no fetching
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edge-styles.ts (NEW) — exports color/stroke-width/dash-pattern per edge type
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts (NEW) — fetch + cache + on-focus refetch for /api/console/graph
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (MOD — add .graph-* classes + --graph-edge-* tokens + pulse keyframes; tiny additions, keep flat with Feed style)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts (NEW) — pure unit tests for the layout function with fixture data

## NON_GOALS
- No xterm pane on node click — that's a future polish tracking forge-lqyc.8. Click opens the structured side-drawer only.
- No graph editing / bead mutations from this view.
- No real-time WS subscription. Refetch on focus + on visibility change is sufficient for v1.
- No filtering chips (per edge type / per priority / per agent) — those are explicit follow-ups for the v2 polish bead, not this one.
- No mini-map / overview pane.
- No node grouping by parent-child collapse / expand.
- No new dependency on a graph library beyond what's already in deps. If dagre is needed, prefer porting a 200-line layered-layout function inline.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- HARD visual contract:
  - Background: var(--surface-primary) (matches Feed bg)
  - Node card background: var(--surface-primary) — same as bg
  - Node border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle); priority band on the left is the only accent
  - Title font: Inter (var(--font-ui))
  - ID font: JBM (var(--font-mono))
  - Active/hover state border: var(--accent)
  - NO new colors outside --graph-edge-* CSS custom properties and the existing app tokens
- HARD layout rule: layered LR, all 9 edge types weighted. Document the weights in a comment at the top of layout.ts.
- HARD multi-edge rule: same-pair edges get top-arc + bottom-arc routing; reference HTML lines 633-652 show the pattern.
- HARD: arrowhead direction is invariant source→effect for all 8 directed types; related has no arrowhead.
- Pulse animation must be the only animation in the graph component. No hover transitions on nodes beyond opacity. No edge animations.
- Closed beads in graph: same node, 45% opacity, dashed border. Do not remove them; they're context.
- Component must be SSR-safe — guard window/document access.
- For repos with no beads: render an empty-state with "No beads in this project" + a small octicon, NOT a blank canvas.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts passes (covers: layered ordering, multi-edge top/bottom arc, closed/superseded preservation, weight-tuned column assignment).
- [ ] Manual: Console > Graph for the gitboard project renders all current beads with at least 3 distinct edge types visible.
- [ ] Manual: hover any node → incident edges highlight; non-incident dim.
- [ ] Manual: click any node → console logs (or stubs) the bead id (side-drawer wiring in forge-f6qk.4).
- [ ] Manual: a bead with a known running specialist (during a test dispatch) pulses live.
- [ ] Playwright: graph canvas DOM contains >= 100 SVG nodes for the gitboard project after first fetch.
- [ ] No new hex colors outside --graph-edge-* (grep sweep: `rg -n '#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}' apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/` matches only inside edge-styles.ts and the CSS).

## OUTPUT
- File diff list with LoC.
- tsc + vitest tails.
- Playwright screenshot of the rendered graph for the current gitboard project with at least one node showing the pulse.
- Layout perf measurement: time to compute + render for the current bead count.
- Confirmation that no new top-level deps added (check package.json + bun.lockb).

## APPROACH NOTES
- Reference HTML is the source of truth for visual STRUCTURE (anatomy, glyphs, badge layout, multi-arc, dashed external chips, pulse rule). For COLOR, use the reference's edge palette via CSS custom properties so theming hooks exist later.
- IssueFeed.tsx is the source of truth for ROW/CHIP typography and density. Match the JBM-on-IDs / Inter-on-text rhythm.
- detail.md captures the invariants in prose — re-read before claiming.
- Implement layered layout from scratch unless dagre@latest is already in bun.lockb. Hand-rolled keeps the diff small and the algorithm comprehensible.
- For performance: memoize layout per (nodes hash + edges hash). The graph data is paginated by project, so a single repo's layout cache hit rate is essentially 100% across re-renders.
- Pan/zoom: use a single SVG transform on the root <g>. Track tx/ty/scale in component state; mousemove with mousedown updates tx/ty. Wheel updates scale (clamp [0.3, 3]).

title: Graph backend: /api/console/graph endpoint with 9 edge types + specialist join
description:
## PROBLEM
The Graph tab needs a single GraphQL-shape endpoint that returns the full topology for the selected repo: every bead, every relationship, plus a live specialist overlay so the frontend can paint the agent-pulse on running beads without a second round-trip. Today the dashboard would have to call bd via subprocess or hit beads-api per bead — neither scales to 250+ bead graphs. Need one endpoint, one fetch, payload < 250kb for typical repos.

## SUCCESS
- New endpoint: GET /api/console/graph?project_id=<id>&include_closed=true|false (default false).
- Returns JSON:
  ```
  {
    project_id: string,
    repo_slug: string,
    generated_at: string (ISO),
    nodes: Array<{
      id: string,              // bead id, e.g. "forge-gud9"
      title: string,
      type: "task"|"bug"|"feature"|"epic"|"chore"|"decision"|"molecule",
      priority: 0|1|2|3|4,
      status: "open"|"in_progress"|"blocked"|"closed"|"deferred",
      assignee: string|null,
      closed_at: string|null,
      superseded_by: string|null,   // resolved from supersedes edge if exists
    }>,
    edges: Array<{
      from: string,            // source bead id
      to: string,              // target bead id
      type: "blocks"|"tracks"|"related"|"parent-child"|"discovered-from"|"validates"|"caused-by"|"until"|"supersedes",
    }>,
    specialists: Array<{
      bead_id: string,
      job_id: string,
      role: string,            // executor / reviewer / etc.
      status: "starting"|"running"|"waiting"|"done"|"error"|"cancelled",
      updated_at: string (ISO),
    }>
  }
  ```
- All 9 bd edge types preserved verbatim in the `edges` array.
- The `specialists` overlay only includes jobs with status IN {starting, running, waiting} (live set). Closed runs come from a separate per-bead query, not this endpoint.
- Endpoint scoped to a single project (`?project_id=...`). Default project resolution falls back to the selected repo's beads project name.
- Endpoint reads bd via the existing beads-api bridge (same path /api/beads/* uses), NOT via subprocess.
- New endpoint mounted under /api/console/* (consistent with /api/console/observability already in place).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts (NEW)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (MOD — mount the new router at /api/console/graph)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts (NEW) — pure query layer; reads from beads-api + observability attach-pool
- apps/gitboard/src/types/graph.ts (NEW) — shared type definitions for the response shape above
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts (NEW) — endpoint integration test with fixture project

## NON_GOALS
- No frontend changes — forge-f6qk.3 consumes this endpoint.
- No graph layout computation (positions, edge routing) — also forge-f6qk.3 responsibility.
- No write endpoint — read-only.
- No real-time WS feed of graph changes — the frontend will refetch on tab focus + on bead state change events.
- No mass-edit endpoint — Graph cannot mutate beads in this epic.
- No payload pagination — assume single-project graphs fit in one response.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- Use the beads-api bridge already in apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts to query beads + edges. Do NOT shell out to `bd`.
- For the specialist overlay, reuse the existing observability dao (apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts) — its inFlightJobs() method returns exactly the shape needed. Filter to the current project's repo_slug.
- Endpoint must return 200 with an empty graph (nodes=[], edges=[], specialists=[]) when the project has no beads — never 404.
- Edge `type` strings must match bd's exact identifiers (use hyphenated forms: "parent-child", "discovered-from", "caused-by"). No camelCase, no enum aliases.
- Closed beads are excluded by default; include_closed=true opts in. Superseded beads always come along because they're referenced by supersedes edges.
- Response must serialize cleanly to JSON; no circular references, no Map/Set leaking through.
- For repos that have NO observability.db, the `specialists` array is empty (not undefined, not error). Gracefully skip.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts passes (fixture has 6 beads, 4 edges across at least 3 distinct edge types).
- [ ] Manual: curl localhost:3000/api/console/graph?project_id=gitboard returns nodes+edges+specialists.
- [ ] Manual: payload size for the current gitboard project (250+ beads) is under 250kb.
- [ ] include_closed=false vs true returns differing node counts; include_closed=false still includes any supersedes-edge targets even if closed.

## OUTPUT
- Diff list (2 new files + 1 modification + 1 type file + 1 test).
- Sample response payload pasted in the close note (truncated to ~30 lines).
- Confirmation that all 9 edge types appear in the bd source schema (run `bd link --type=<each>` smoke test before close).
- Payload size benchmark for current gitboard project.

## APPROACH NOTES
- beads-api bridge: see apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6 for the `beadsRoutes` import + mounting. Same DB client surface is reusable in graph-dao.ts.
- bd's JSON output via `bd dep list <id> --json` shows the `dependency_type` field — that's the source of edge type names. Confirmed in this session.
- The observability dao already returns `{jobId, repoSlug, beadId, status, updatedAt, specialist}` — strip to the 5 fields needed for the specialists overlay.
- A clean way to fetch the FULL edge set across a project is to query the beads-api with `--json` for the project's bead list, then bulk `bd dep list <id1> <id2> ...` with --json. Verify the batch syntax in graph-dao.ts.

title: IA restructure: drop Beads surface, flatten Console tabs
description:
## PROBLEM
Current shell has three Surfaces (github, beads, console). Beads holds its own sub-tabs (feed, triage, memories), Console holds its own (observability, specialists placeholder). Operator wants a single operational surface — drop Beads as a Surface, and put feed/triage/memories alongside observability/specialists/graph as Console's flat tab strip. Until this restructure lands, there's nowhere for the new Graph and live Specialists tabs to mount that makes IA sense.

## SUCCESS
- TopBar surface switcher renders exactly two buttons: GitHub, Console.
- Console renders a tab strip with six labels in this order: Feed, Triage, Memories, Graph, Observability, Specialists.
- Tab order is canonical (defined in types/shell.ts), not free-form in the JSX.
- Selecting Feed renders the existing BeadsRepoView in 'feed' mode; Triage → kanban; Memories → memories list. Behaviorally identical to today's Beads sub-tabs.
- Graph + Specialists tabs render placeholder panels that say "Coming in forge-f6qk.3 / forge-f6qk.5" with a small octicon. (Real content lands in later children.)
- Observability tab unchanged (still renders pages/console/Observability.tsx).
- Legacy URL deep-links resolve correctly on first load:
  - /gitboard/beads → Console > Feed
  - /gitboard/beads/feed → Console > Feed
  - /gitboard/beads/triage → Console > Triage
  - /gitboard/beads/memories → Console > Memories
  - /gitboard/console → Console > Observability (unchanged default)
  - /gitboard/console/graph → Console > Graph
- localStorage selection key 'forge-5w9:selection' continues to persist surface+tab+repo. Operators with persisted surface='beads' get auto-migrated to surface='console' with tab=feed on first load.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts — change `Surface = "github" | "beads" | "console"` to `Surface = "github" | "console"`. Define new `ConsoleTab = "feed" | "triage" | "memories" | "graph" | "observability" | "specialists"`. Update `TabId` union accordingly. Update `DEFAULT_TAB.console` from "observability" to "feed" (so Console opens to the most-used tab). Update `CONSOLE_TABS` export array to the 6 entries in canonical order with octicons.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TopBar.tsx — remove Beads tab from the surface tablist. Render Console tab strip from CONSOLE_TABS array using existing tab rendering pattern. Each tab gets an octicon next to its label (Feed → ListUnorderedIcon, Triage → ColumnsIcon, Memories → BookIcon, Graph → ShareAndroidIcon, Observability → PulseIcon, Specialists → GearIcon).
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx — replace the surface→component swap. When surface==='console', render based on tab:
  - "feed" → <BeadsRepoView repo={repo} tab="feed" />
  - "triage" → <BeadsRepoView repo={repo} tab="triage" />
  - "memories" → <BeadsRepoView repo={repo} tab="memories" />
  - "graph" → <Graph repo={repo} /> (placeholder until forge-f6qk.3 lands)
  - "observability" → <Observability /> (existing)
  - "specialists" → <Specialists /> (placeholder until forge-f6qk.5 lands)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — extend the route detection to map /gitboard/beads* paths to selection.surface='console' with the correct tab. Migrate persisted localStorage selection.surface==='beads' to 'console' on first load.

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.tsx — NEW placeholder file (same shape).
