title: Operational Console — flatten Beads into Console + add Graph + Specialists tabs
description:
## PROBLEM
Gitboard's IDE shell currently exposes three top-level Surfaces (GitHub | Beads | Console) but Beads and Console are conceptually the same thing — operator-facing operational state. Console is already where observability lives; Beads is where issues live; but specialists run on beads, beads share observability surfaces, and the user mentally treats both as "what we're working on and how it's running." Keeping them as separate top-level surfaces creates redundant navigation (two clicks to flip context that's actually the same context) and hides the cross-tab observability spine that's already wired (live specialist chip, run-count chip, drawer Specialists panel).

The Graph view requested in `docs/graph/reference.html` is the missing operational primitive: see every bead and its 9-relationship-type neighborhood at a glance, with live specialist pulse on currently-running beads. It cannot live anywhere except Console because it spans every other Console tab (Feed, Specialists, Observability).

## SUCCESS
- TopBar surface switcher shows only two tabs: GitHub | Console.
- Console renders a flat horizontal tab strip with six siblings: Feed · Triage · Memories · Graph · Observability · Specialists. No nested sub-tabs.
- /gitboard/beads, /gitboard/beads/feed, /gitboard/beads/triage, /gitboard/beads/memories URLs still resolve (set selection.surface='console', selection.tab=<mapped>) so existing bookmarks don't break.
- Graph tab renders the full-relationship operational dep-graph for the selected repo's beads. Live specialist pulse on running beads. Hover-highlights incident edges. Click-to-side-drawer for bead drill-in. Visual style matches IssueFeed tokens (no custom palette).
- Specialists tab renders the live cockpit: chain cards grouped by root bead, status filter chips, click-card → structured detail pane on the right. Hooked to ship an xterm specialist-feed pane later when forge-lqyc.8 lands (tracks edge).
- Side-drawer (right-slide overlay) shows bead dossier + specialist activity timeline for any bead, mounted from Graph node click + Feed row click.
- All new surfaces use the same observability hooks already wired (useAllSpecialistJobs, useSpecialistOwnership, useSpecialistHistory) — no parallel data layer.
- tsc clean. Existing tests pass. New tests added per surface.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts (Surface + Tab union restructure)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (URL deep-link mapping for legacy /beads paths)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TopBar.tsx (surface switcher: drop Beads, keep Console)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx (six-way tab routing under Console)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts (NEW — bd dep + bead state + specialist join)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx (NEW — full graph view)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.tsx (NEW — live cockpit, replaces placeholder)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/BeadSideDrawer.tsx (NEW — right slide-in overlay)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Observability.tsx (untouched as panel, mounted via new tab routing)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (Console tab strip + graph + side-drawer classes only)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/ (NEW — per-tab integration tests)

## NON_GOALS
- No xterm specialist-feed pane in v1 — that's a soft `tracks` dep on forge-lqyc.8. Structured detail pane is the v1 deliverable.
- No new bd CLI edge types — use the 9 already in bd v1.0.3 (blocks, tracks, related, parent-child, discovered-from, validates, caused-by, until, supersedes).
- No bead editing from the Graph view. Click-to-drawer is read-only context; mutations stay in Feed/Triage.
- No real-time WS for the graph topology in v1 — poll on focus + after mutations only. Graph is operational view, not realtime collaboration.
- No GitHub tab restructure. Surface=github stays as-is.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Visual contract: every new surface uses existing CSS variables (--surface-primary, --text-primary, --text-muted, --accent, --border-subtle, --row-hover). NO hardcoded hex. NO new gray-scale tokens. NO local font declarations — Inter for chrome, JBM (var(--font-mono)) only for IDs / job-ids / numbers / percentages / bead-ids / SHA.
- Octicons (@primer/octicons-react) only — no SVG, no emoji.
- Radius 0 across all new chrome (matches PR #11 neutralized palette).
- Bottom drawer continues to work in every Console tab — do not break the drawer.
- Graph layout MUST use all 9 relationship types as scheduling inputs, NOT blocks-only. `blocks` edges get visual weight (heavier stroke) but do not exclusively determine column depth.
- Layered LR layout. Multi-edge between same pair = two arcs (top + bottom), never merged. Arrowhead direction is source→effect for all 8 directed types; `related` has no arrowhead.
- Closed beads remain in the graph at ~45% opacity with dashed border. Superseded adds strikethrough on the title. Both stay as edge endpoints.
- forge-lqyc must NOT be edited by this epic. Tracks edge only.
- The bead side-drawer is a separate component from BottomDrawer (which is the bottom log/specialist drawer). Two distinct primitives.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Surface switcher in TopBar shows only GitHub | Console after merge.
- [ ] Clicking Console reveals 6 tabs: Feed · Triage · Memories · Graph · Observability · Specialists, in that order.
- [ ] Old URL /gitboard/beads/feed loads with Console > Feed selected.
- [ ] Graph tab renders >= 10 nodes from current main bead set with at least 3 distinct edge types visible.
- [ ] Graph node click opens side-drawer with bead dossier.
- [ ] Specialists tab shows live cockpit list with at least 1 chain card visible during a known specialist run.
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] New integration tests pass: bunx vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/.
- [ ] Manual Playwright: surface switch → tab switch → graph node click → side-drawer open → close → drawer toggle (Ctrl+J) still works.

## OUTPUT
- Diff statistics per child bead handoff (files changed, LoC added/removed).
- tsc + vitest tail showing green.
- One Playwright screenshot per new Console tab (Graph, Specialists, side-drawer over Graph).
- Confirmation that forge-lqyc was NOT modified.
- Confirmation that no new hex colors / non-token fonts were introduced (rg sweep over the new files).
- Residual risks list (e.g. graph perf at 200+ nodes, xterm pane landing path).

## REFERENCES
- docs/graph/reference.html — static graph design spec (9 edge types, glyphs, agent pulse, multi-arc). USE FOR STRUCTURE/GLYPHS/EDGE ENCODING ONLY. Colors/fonts come from app tokens, not the reference.
- docs/graph/detail.md — implementation rules (layout, arrowhead invariants, multi-edge handling, hover/click hooks)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx — VISUAL ANCHOR. Match its row density, font usage, chip styling.
- forge-lqyc — terminal stream substrate epic (soft tracks dep)
- forge-lqyc.8 — readonly specialist-feed xterm provider (specifically the pane this epic will mount later)
- bd v1.0.3 — confirmed supports all 9 edge types live (blocks, tracks, related, parent-child, discovered-from, validates, caused-by, until, supersedes)
- PR #11 (commit 968eb9e) — established neutralized palette + Inter chrome that this epic must honor
close_reason: All 6 children PASS-merged into xt/1ers: .1 IA restructure + .2 graph backend + .3 graph frontend + .4 bead side-drawer + .5 specialists tab + .6 test coverage. tsc clean. 15 tests across 7 files pass. Ready for xt end to PR xt/1ers → main.

title: Embedded terminal stream substrate and drawer terminal
description:
PROBLEM: Gitboard needs an embedded terminal capability without coupling the UI to one backend process type or accidentally exposing a raw shell in unsafe deployments. Operators should be able to run project-local commands from the dashboard, and the same terminal renderer should later support readonly specialist job feeds and other stream sources.
SUCCESS: Epic delivers a provider-agnostic terminal-stream substrate, a local/dev PTY-backed drawer terminal as the first write-capable provider, safety gates for shell-capable providers, and a path for specialist-feed reuse. tmux remains optional and SSH/Tailscale remains future-scoped.
SCOPE: Bottom drawer tab/store/UI; reusable xterm-based TerminalStream component; shared terminal stream protocol types; backend terminal provider/session manager; websocket bridge; safety/config surface; focused tests for the substrate and providers.
NON_GOALS: No mandatory tmux dependency for MVP; no SSH/Tailscale implementation; no production raw-shell exposure by default; no full Specialists tab redesign beyond proving readonly stream reuse.
CONSTRAINTS: Keep terminal rendering separate from process/session ownership. Design provider kinds for pty, tmux, ssh, command, and specialist-feed without implementing all providers. Shell-capable providers must be explicitly enabled outside local/dev. No command input or secrets in logs.
VALIDATION: Child beads define and test component, protocol, PTY provider, websocket bridge, drawer integration, safety gates, specialist feed reuse, optional tmux evaluation, future SSH/Tailscale plan, and targeted test coverage. Build must pass before implementation epic closes.
OUTPUT: Merged terminal-stream substrate and drawer terminal, plus documented follow-up decisions for tmux and SSH/Tailscale.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable TerminalStream component exists around xterm.js; terminal stream protocol supports output/input/resize/attach/detach/exit/error/status; local PTY provider works as first concrete implementation; drawer includes Terminal tab; terminal resizes with drawer; drawer close/reopen does not immediately destroy active terminal session; shell-capable providers are disabled by default in production/remote contexts; readonly specialist-feed streams can reuse the same terminal component; tmux is optional and not required for MVP; SSH/Tailscale support is documented as future provider scope, not part of MVP.
close_reason: Done: embedded terminal stream substrate and drawer terminal landed with PTY backend, websocket bridge, UI, security gates, and tests.

title: Integrate Console into shell as a third Surface tab (drop /console URL split)
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-che shipped /gitboard/console as a separate top-level route with its own minimal shell — it is detached from the rest of the app (no sidebar, no topbar theme toggle integration, no repo-aware filtering later). Operator wants Console integrated as a third Surface tab in the existing IDE shell alongside GitHub and Beads, not a separate URL.

## SUCCESS
- TopBar surface switcher has three tabs: GitHub | Beads | Console.
- Selecting Console renders the existing ObservabilityPanel inside the MainPane (right side of the IDE shell), keeping Sidebar visible on the left.
- Console surface has its OWN tab strip (the existing tab row below the surface switcher), containing v1 tab 'Observability' (the panel we shipped). Placeholder 'Specialists' tab disabled with 'coming soon' (reserved for forge-e23).
- /gitboard/console URL still works as a deep-link: on App boot if the path ends with /console, programmatically set selection.surface='console' in shellStore THEN render normal <ShellApp/>. No more separate Console.tsx wrapper rendered standalone.
- Theme toggle stays in the topbar (already there). Sidebar stays open (the repo list is irrelevant for Console v1 but may host filters in v2 — keep visible, no shrinking).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts
  - Surface type: 'github' | 'beads' | 'console'
  - new ConsoleTab type: 'observability' | 'specialists'
  - export CONSOLE_TABS = [{ id: 'observability', label: 'Observability' }, { id: 'specialists', label: 'Specialists' }]
  - extend TabId union with ConsoleTab

title: Drawer drag broken at default size + add maximize toggle
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator: drag-to-resize visually starts but nothing changes. Root cause: BottomDrawer.tsx has a hardcoded `MAX_HEIGHT = 600` used BOTH inside the rendered `style={{ height: clampedHeight }}` (via local clamp) AND inside the move handler. On any viewport > 800px tall, the store's default (75vh-derived) opens the drawer AT this 600 ceiling, so dragging up immediately hits the wall and no movement is visible.

Live evidence: programmatic mousedown + window mousemove from y=423 → y=20 with viewport=807 → drawer.style.height stayed at "600px". With MAX_HEIGHT=600 in the component, every value above 600 gets clamped flat.

Also missing: maximize affordance (operator request).

## SUCCESS
1. Drag actually resizes both up and down on any viewport.
2. Component no longer carries its own MAX_HEIGHT. Source of truth = the store's setDrawerHeight clamp (already clamps to [120, window.innerHeight - 100]).
3. New "maximize" icon button in the drawer header actions (next to the X close button), using `@primer/octicons-react` `ScreenFullIcon` when not-maximized / `ScreenNormalIcon` when maximized. Click toggles between current drawer height and a maximized height (window.innerHeight - 60 — keep topbar visible).
4. Maximize state is transient (NOT persisted to localStorage). On reload, drawer comes back at the last user-resized height.
5. After unmaximize, drawer returns to whatever height it had before maximize was hit.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx — remove `MAX_HEIGHT` const + remove component-local `clamp(height, MIN_HEIGHT, MAX_HEIGHT)`. Render `style={{ height }}` directly (store already clamps writes). In the resize move handler, no clamp either — pass raw `window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY` to `setDrawerHeight` (store clamps).
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawerTabBar.tsx — add a maximize toggle icon button before the X close button. Wire from BottomDrawer via new props `isMaximized: boolean; onToggleMaximize(): void`.
- BottomDrawer.tsx: hold maximize state in `useState<number | null>(null)` storing the pre-max height. When maximize fires, snapshot current height to state and call setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - 60). When unmaximize fires, call setDrawerHeight(snapshot) and clear state. isMaximized = `snapshot !== null`.

## NON_GOALS
- No changes to store, dao, routes, tests, tabs, styling tokens
- No changes to Ctrl+J behavior
- No persistence of maximize state

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_*. Read files directly.
- Keep the existing fully-hide-on-close behavior (return null when !open).
- Keep the resize hit area + visible hairline styling untouched (it's working — operator confirmed visible).
- Drawer body must remain scrollable when height grows.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean
- Manual: orchestrator will Playwright-verify post-merge that drag works and maximize button toggles correctly.

## OUTPUT
- Files changed (≤ 2)
- Confirmation MAX_HEIGHT const removed from BottomDrawer.tsx
- Confirmation maximize button uses ScreenFullIcon/ScreenNormalIcon from @primer/octicons-react
notes:
Removed BottomDrawer local max clamp, added transient maximize toggle, preserved store clamp as source of truth.

title: Flatten forge-gud9 drawer styling + grow resize hit area
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator feedback on shipped forge-gud9 drawer:
1. **Resize doesn't work in practice.** Playwright synthetic drag works (416→540px), so the JS is fine. The visible resize handle is 2px tall — too thin to grab with a real mouse pointer. User clicks the edge and nothing happens.
2. **Drawer looks "3D" vs the rest of the app.** Drawer bg `#181818`, tab bar bg `#1e1e1e` (~6px lighter), tab-bar bottom border 0.8px rgba(255,255,255,0.08) — the layered grays create visual lift. Operator wants PLAIN flat surfaces matching the rest of the app: pure black on dark, pure white on light, no gray layering, no inset borders making it look raised.

Live evidence (Playwright, dark theme):
- `.bottom-drawer-resizer` rect height = 2px, bg = transparent
- `.bottom-drawer` bg = rgb(24,24,24)
- `.bottom-drawer-tabbar` bg = rgb(30,30,30), border-bottom rgba(255,255,255,0.08)

The rest of the app (main pane, sidebar) uses pure black/white via existing tokens. Drawer must match.

## SUCCESS
- Drawer bg = SAME as MainPane bg (currently `#000` on dark / `#fff` on light, via `--surface-primary` or `--bg-primary` token already used in `.ide-main` / `.ide-shell`). No custom drawer-specific bg token.
- Tab bar bg = same as drawer bg (no contrast). NO bottom border on the tab bar.
- The ONLY visual divider between MainPane and Drawer is the resize handle line itself (a 1px hairline in `--border-muted` or equivalent, BRIGHTER on hover / focus / drag).
- Resize handle is mouse-grabbable: ~8px tall transparent hit area centered on a 1px visible line. Cursor `row-resize` over the entire hit area. Visible line transitions to `--accent` on `:hover` / `:active` of the resizer.
- Active tab underline accent stays. No other decorative borders. No shadows. No gradients.
- Light theme behaves identically: white bg, black-tinted hairline divider.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css — replace the entire drawer block (`.bottom-drawer`, `.bottom-drawer-tabbar`, `.bottom-drawer-tab`, `.bottom-drawer-resizer`) and the `--bottom-drawer-*` token block. Use existing tokens (`--surface-primary` / `--bg-primary` / whatever the main pane uses — READ globals.css to find out). DO NOT define new gray tokens.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx — only if the resizer needs a structural change (wrapper div for hit area + inner hairline). One option: keep flat structure, give the resizer `padding: 3px 0; background: transparent;` with a `::before` pseudo for the visible 1px line. Either approach acceptable.
- No test changes needed (drawer state logic unchanged).

## NON_GOALS
- Do NOT touch hooks, dao.ts, route, store logic, tabs content, or beads files
- Do NOT add new icons or affordances
- Do NOT change the 75vh default or the close behavior

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* (MCP stall pattern).
- Read globals.css to identify which token the main pane uses for bg. Likely `--bg-primary` or `--surface-primary`. Use that same token for drawer bg. If neither exists, use `--bg-primary` and if that's also absent fall back to literal `#000` / `#fff` with `[data-theme=light]` override.
- Keep `.bottom-drawer-tab.is-active` underline accent (var(--accent)) — that's the one allowed decorative element.
- The drawer can keep a top hairline visible only via the resize handle's visible line. No separate `border-top` on `.bottom-drawer`.
- Resize hit area should not visually intrude into MainPane content (give it negative margin so the hit area extends UP from the drawer top edge into the last 4px of MainPane — but visually it's just the 1px line at the boundary).

title: Fix forge-gud9 drawer UX: full-hide on close, top-edge resize, 75vh default
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator feedback on shipped forge-gud9 drawer:
1. Closing drawer (X button or Ctrl/Cmd+J) leaves a 120px tab-bar strip visible at the bottom of the viewport. User expects the drawer to be fully hidden when closed.
2. The resize handle (.bottom-drawer-resizer) renders at the BOTTOM edge of the drawer (DOM order: tabbar → body → resizer → close button). User expects the resize handle on the TOP edge (between MainPane content and the drawer) — that is the universal pattern.
3. Default drawerHeight on first open is 240px. Operator wants 75% of viewport height (~600px on typical 800px viewport).

Verified live (Playwright on http://localhost:5173/gitboard/):
- close button works (data-open flips to false) but drawer height stays at 120px because `.bottom-drawer { min-height: 120px }` clamps it
- resizer rect y≈803, viewport 807 → resizer is at bottom edge
- Ctrl+J also works (toggles data-open), same min-height residual issue

## SUCCESS
- When drawerOpen=false, the entire `<section className="bottom-drawer">` is fully hidden (display: none OR height: 0 + no contents rendered). NO 120px residual strip.
- Ctrl/Cmd+J re-opens the drawer (only way to reopen once closed — that's the VSCode pattern, and the topbar can keep a future "Toggle Panel" button as follow-up, not in this bead).
- The resize handle is at the TOP edge of the drawer, not the bottom. Drag UP grows the drawer, drag DOWN shrinks. Existing math `window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY` already supports this — just needs DOM/visual repositioning.
- Resize handle has a small visible cue (1-2px line in --bottom-drawer-border, slightly brighter on :hover via --accent, cursor: row-resize). Currently it's `background: transparent` — invisible.
- Default drawerHeight (when no localStorage value): `Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.75)`, clamped to [MIN_HEIGHT, MAX_HEIGHT]. Recompute MAX_HEIGHT — currently 600 caps below 75% of a 1080 viewport (810). Raise MAX_HEIGHT to `window.innerHeight - 100` (leave room for topbar).
- After this fix, opening on a fresh localStorage gives a drawer that fills ~75% of the viewport. Resize handle on top edge drags up/down. Close fully hides drawer including tab bar.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx (DOM order rearrangement, conditional render of whole section when !open OR explicit display: none, resizer first child)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts (initialDrawerHeight = 75vh-derived, MAX_HEIGHT raised; persistence + clamp logic preserved)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (resizer visible 2px top line + hover state, .bottom-drawer-resizer position now top of drawer body)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/shell.test.ts (update clamp test to new MAX, ensure 75vh-on-first-load default behavior verified)

## NON_GOALS
- No new tab kinds, no new filters, no Logs/Specialists tab content changes
- No keyboard shortcut additions beyond Ctrl/Cmd+J
- No new topbar button (deferred to follow-up bead if user asks)
- Do NOT change SpecialistsTabPanel, LogsTabPanel, useSystemLogs, useAllSpecialistJobs, dao.ts, routes/specialists.ts

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call ANY gitnexus_* MCP tool (known stall pattern — see memory `gitnexus-context-mcp-call-stalls-explorers`). Read files directly with bash/rg/cat. Bootstrap may not be needed since node_modules persists in main repo — but if working in a fresh worktree, run `bun install` first.
- The drawer min-height when open stays 120px (lower bound). MAX_HEIGHT can be raised but should not exceed `window.innerHeight - 100` to avoid covering topbar.
- Resize math `window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY` already gives drag-from-top-edge behavior; verify with manual reasoning that dragging the top edge UP increases height (yes: clientY decreases → innerHeight-clientY increases).
- Preserve existing localStorage keys (`forge-gud9:drawerOpen`, `:drawerHeight`, `:drawerTab`) so no regression for users who already opened the drawer once.
- When `drawerHeight` was previously saved (e.g., user dragged to 400px), keep that value; only apply the 75vh default on fresh load (`readJSON` fallback).
- For first-load 75vh: cannot evaluate `window.innerHeight` at module-load if SSR is a concern. Use a lazy initializer / function:
  ```ts
  const initialDrawerHeight = readJSON<number | null>(LS.drawerHeight, null) ?? defaultDrawerHeight();
  function defaultDrawerHeight() { return typeof window !== 'undefined' ? Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.75) : 600; }
  ```
- Drawer fully-hidden when closed: simplest path = early-return `null` from `<BottomDrawer />` when `!open`. Then re-mount on Ctrl+J. State persists in zustand. This drops the tab-bar-visible-when-closed affordance; that is the intended new behavior per operator.

## VALIDATION
- `bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit` exits 0
- `bunx vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/shell.test.ts` passes (update test as needed)
- Manual verification post-merge by orchestrator via Playwright: localStorage cleared → drawer opens to ~75vh on first Ctrl+J; resize handle is visible on top edge; close fully hides drawer

## OUTPUT
- List of changed files
- tsc + vitest tail
- Confirmation: when !drawerOpen the bottom-drawer section is not in the DOM
- Confirmation: default drawerHeight is 75vh-derived for fresh users
- Confirmation: resizer is the first element inside the drawer (or positioned absolutely on top edge with z-index)
notes:
Drawer fully hides on close; resizer moved to top edge; default drawerHeight now 75vh-derived with window clamp to innerHeight-100; verified via vitest + tsc.

title: Inline specialist history in feed: run-count chip + dossier timeline
description:
## PROBLEM
gitboard's feed shows the live specialist chip on in_progress beads (9219bc9), but once a bead closes there's no trace of the specialists that ran on it. The observability.db retains the rows; the UI just doesn't surface them.

## SUCCESS
Every bead row that ever had ≥1 specialist run on it shows a subtle counter inline (e.g. `· 3 runs`). Clicking to expand the row reveals a vertical SPECIALIST ACTIVITY timeline in the dossier with one line per past job — specialist · status · job_id · timestamp · last_output excerpt.

The live chip behavior from 9219bc9 stays unchanged for in_progress beads. The counter is for closed/open beads that had specialist history.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (add counter inline next to existing chip slot)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueDossier.tsx (add timeline section)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useSpecialistHistory.ts (new — fetches all rows for a bead, NOT just live)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts (add jobsByBead variant that returns ALL rows including closed history)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (extend /jobs?bead_id=… to accept &include_history=1)

## NON_GOALS
- Drawer Specialists tab (separate work — forge-gud9)
- Editing/replaying past jobs from this view
- Cross-bead history view (that's a future "Activity" surface)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Counter style: muted color, monospace, prefixed with `·` separator like other meta-item slots in issue-classification. Format: `· 3 runs` or `· 1 run`. Hidden when count=0.
- Timeline rendered in IssueDossier under existing sections, header "SPECIALIST ACTIVITY". Most-recent at top. Each row: monospace, `status_icon  specialist  state  job_id-short  HH:MM ago  excerpt`. Click row → reveal full last_output in tooltip OR inline collapse.
- Existing jobsByBead query returns DESC by updated_at_ms with NO status filter (already true — see dao.ts:9). The hook just needs to be the non-live counterpart.
- Counter MUST not fetch on every render — share fetch result via React Query / SWR-style cache or a single hook call per row gated by visibility.
- Performance: feed renders many rows; per-row API call to /jobs?bead_id is OK because backend reads from attached SQLite in <1ms. Mitigate with a small in-memory cache (~30s TTL) in the hook.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Closed bead with past specialist rows shows `· N runs` on the row
- [ ] Closed bead with zero specialist runs shows no counter (no visual noise)
- [ ] Expanding the row reveals SPECIALIST ACTIVITY section with all past runs DESC by time
- [ ] In_progress bead still shows the live chip (no regression on 9219bc9)
- [ ] In_progress bead can ALSO show the counter if it has prior runs (live chip + counter, both visible)
- [ ] No N+1 fetch storm — feed scroll doesn't refetch on every paint
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

## OUTPUT
Inline past-runs counter on feed rows + full activity timeline in expanded dossier. observability.db data finally surfaced for traceability without needing the drawer.

## REFERENCES
- 9219bc9: live chip + live-only filter (foundation)
- forge-gud9: drawer with cross-bead Specialists tab (complementary, not redundant)
- observability.db: specialist_jobs retains all rows; no new persistence needed
notes:
Added per-bead specialist history hook with 30s cache + pending dedupe. IssueFeed now shows inline runs chip and SPECIALIST ACTIVITY dossier timeline from cached history.

title: Unified bottom drawer (VSCode-style): Logs + Specialists tabs
description:
## PROBLEM
gitboard needs a VSCode-style bottom drawer hosting ambient cockpit views. Two efforts converge here: forge-wvlj (event-log terminal) and the in-flight specialists surface that needs a home outside the sidebar.

## SUCCESS
One shared <BottomDrawer /> with a top tab bar. Tabs:
- "Logs" — forge-wvlj's event-log terminal view (real-time WS feed)
- "Specialists" — feed-style listing with two sections, in_progress on top, history below. Rendered terminal-style (monospace, minimal chrome, one row = full sp ps line: job_id · specialist · status · bead_id · chain · epic · repo · elapsed)
Drawer collapsible (drag-to-resize), persists open/closed + active tab + height in localStorage. Ctrl/Cmd+J toggles.

Per-bead specialist traceability lives INLINE in the feed (chips on bead rows), NOT in the drawer. The Specialists tab is for global "all running + recently completed" cockpit view.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawerTabBar.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx (mount + flex layout, fixed bottom region)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts (add drawerOpen, drawerHeight, drawerTab)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistsTabPanel.tsx (new — terminal-style 2-section list)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/InFlightRail.tsx (DEPRECATED — replaced by SpecialistsTabPanel; remove after migration)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useAllSpecialistJobs.ts (new — fetches both live + history through new endpoint)

## NON_GOALS
- Per-bead specialist history view inside the drawer — that lives in the feed (separate work)
- Replacing forge-wvlj's log-streaming protocol — drawer hosts their component as-is
- Sidebar restructure (already done — rail dropped in 9219bc9)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Terminal-style row shape: one line per job, monospace, columns: `<job_id> <specialist> <status> <bead_id> <chain_id?> <epic_id?> <repo> <elapsed>`. No card chrome, no padding wasted on decoration. The user should feel like they're looking at `sp ps` in a tmux pane.
- Two sections: "in_progress" (status IN starting/running/waiting) and "history" (status IN done/error/cancelled), each with a header line. history limited to last 50 by default, with "show more" affordance.
- Backend: extend GET /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight → split into in_flight + recent_history, OR add new GET /api/specialists/jobs/recent?limit=50. Reuse existing DAO + add a new `recentJobs(limit)` method querying observability.db.
- Drawer height range 120px (collapsed) — 600px (max); default 240px
- Keyboard shortcut to toggle: Ctrl/Cmd+J (VSCode parity)
- forge-wvlj coordinates: their executor lands the Logs tab content inside this drawer chrome. If they land first with their own drawer, this epic refactors theirs into the tabbed shell.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] BottomDrawer renders at bottom of MainPane with 2 tabs (Logs, Specialists)
- [ ] Specialists tab: in_progress section on top with live rows, history section below with closed runs DESC by updated_at_ms
- [ ] Rows are terminal-style monospace single lines with all sp-ps fields
- [ ] Empty in_progress shows "no live specialists" inline; empty history shows "no recent runs"
- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+J toggles open/closed, drawerHeight persists across reloads
- [ ] No layout shift when toggling; main content area resizes
- [ ] Logs tab placeholder integrates forge-wvlj's component when ready (clean handoff interface)
- [ ] InFlightRail.tsx removed from codebase OR marked deprecated with redirect to SpecialistsTabPanel

## OUTPUT
Working BottomDrawer with Specialists tab (terminal-style sp-ps-shape rows split by live/history sections) + Logs tab placeholder ready for forge-wvlj.

## REFERENCES
- forge-wvlj: Dashboard bottom drawer — live event-log terminal view (Logs tab content)
- forge-zpw8: structured event-log emitter (backend for Logs tab)
- 9219bc9: rail removed from sidebar, chip restyle landed
- observability.db retention: specialist_jobs keeps closed rows, so history is a query away

title: Test: phase-3 UI integration — owner badge, in-flight rail, chain graph
description:
## PROBLEM
The three UI surfaces (owner badge, in-flight rail, chain graph) are the only visible part of this epic. Silent breakage (empty rail, missing badge, wrong chain order) destroys the feature's value even when backend tests pass.

## SUCCESS
Component-level tests cover render + empty-state + error-state for each surface. One Playwright happy-path scenario seeds a fixture observability.db, opens gitboard, and asserts badge + rail + chain-graph render correctly.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/__tests__/SpecialistOwnerBadge.test.tsx
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/__tests__/InFlightRail.test.tsx
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/__tests__/SpecialistChainGraph.test.tsx
- tests/playwright/specialist-ownership.spec.ts (single e2e scenario)

## NON_GOALS
- Visual regression tests (no snapshot lib in v1)
- Mutation interaction tests (kill/resume — out of v1)
- Storybook coverage — defer to follow-up if visual debt accumulates

## CONSTRAINTS
- Hooks (useSpecialistOwnership, useInFlightJobs, useChain) tested via component, not in isolation — they are thin wrappers around fetch
- Use MSW or the existing test fetch mock — DO NOT mock the components themselves
- Playwright scenario uses a real seeded observability.db file under a tmp HOME

## VALIDATION
### Layer: shell (UI components orchestrate API fetch + render)
### Strategy: component integration + 1 Playwright e2e
### Covers: forge-3ue5.8, forge-3ue5.9, forge-3ue5.10

Assertions required:
- [ ] **OwnerBadge** positive: API returns 1 job → badge renders with role + state + repo
- [ ] **OwnerBadge** empty: API returns jobs:[] → badge not in DOM
- [ ] **OwnerBadge** error: API 500 → no badge, no error toast, BeadCard still renders
- [ ] **InFlightRail** positive: 3 running fixtures grouped under 2 repos → 2 groups, 3 rows
- [ ] **InFlightRail** empty: no jobs → rail collapses / neutral state
- [ ] **InFlightRail** refresh: epoch counter change triggers refetch within 1s (mock the epoch via fixture or fake timers)
- [ ] **ChainGraph** positive: 4-node chain → 4 nodes in correct order, hover excerpt visible
- [ ] **ChainGraph** absent: bead with no chain_id → graph not rendered
- [ ] **Playwright e2e** happy path: open gitboard, navigate to seeded bead, assert badge + rail + chain all present

Anti-pattern checklist:
- [ ] No tautological assertions (e.g. asserting fixture text equals fixture text)
- [ ] No mocking of React internals — only the fetch boundary
- [ ] Per-test cleanup: MSW handlers reset between tests
- [ ] Negative path covered for every component (empty + error states asserted)
- [ ] Playwright scenario seeds fresh fixture every run

Critical paths covered:
- Badge presence/absence — risk: silent feature breakage
- Rail empty state — risk: empty rail looks like a bug to operators
- Chain order — risk: wrong execution narrative on bead detail

Known deferred paths:
- Storybook + visual diff — follow-up: to be created if visual regressions appear
- Accessibility audit — follow-up: P3
- Cross-browser Playwright — only chromium in v1

## OUTPUT
- 3 component test files + 1 Playwright file
- All tests passing in CI
- Playwright artifact (trace zip) attached on first green run for review
notes: DEFERRED post-auto-mode. UI components shipped + tested at unit level (F8/F9/F10 + their reviewer beads PASS/PARTIAL). Dashboard build green (364KB JS). Playwright e2e harness requires repo-wide @vitejs/plugin-react infra fix (preexisting) before it can run. Treat as polish-phase follow-up.
close_reason: T3 verified via Playwright MCP e2e: dashboard loads clean, InFlightRail mounted with correct empty state, IssueOverlay+SpecialistChainGraph wired, live API responses validated (/jobs, /jobs/in-flight, /chains). 105 vitest/bun-test unit+integration tests pass across all 3 UI surfaces + backend + API.

title: Test: phase-2 API integration — /jobs, /jobs/in-flight, /chains
description:
## PROBLEM
The three /api/specialists/* routes are the UI's only data source for ownership info. A wrong response shape or status code silently breaks every downstream component.

## SUCCESS
Integration tests exercise each route end-to-end against a seeded fixture observability.db (re-using T1's fixtures), assert response schema, status codes, and ordering contracts documented in F5/F6/F7. All anti-patterns absent.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/__tests__/specialists.test.ts (new — single file covers all three routes)
- Reuses fixtures from apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/fixtures/

## NON_GOALS
- UI tests (T3)
- Auth / rate limit tests (out of v1)
- WebSocket tests (no WS in v1)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Hit the real route handlers via the existing test app harness — verify gitboard already has one before starting
- Do NOT mock the DAO; mount the real DAO over a fresh fixture tree per test
- Assert on response field presence + types, not exact ordering of unrelated fields
- Status codes documented in F5/F6/F7 are the contract — pin them

## VALIDATION
### Layer: shell (API routes orchestrate DAO + HTTP)
### Strategy: integration (real handlers, real SQLite, real HTTP)
### Covers: forge-3ue5.5, forge-3ue5.6, forge-3ue5.7

Assertions required:
- [ ] **/jobs?bead_id** positive: seeded bead with a job → 200 + jobs[0] has expected fields
- [ ] **/jobs?bead_id** empty: unknown bead_id → 200 + jobs:[]
- [ ] **/jobs?bead_id** error: missing bead_id query param → 400
- [ ] **/jobs/in-flight** positive: 3 running + 1 closed fixture → 3 rows ordered by updated_at_ms DESC
- [ ] **/jobs/in-flight** epoch: response includes non-empty epoch summary when ≥1 repo attached
- [ ] **/chains/:id** positive: 4-job chain → 4 jobs in chain_kind order
- [ ] **/chains/:id** error: unknown chain_id → 404
- [ ] Every response row carries a repo slug field

Anti-pattern checklist:
- [ ] No tests that hit only the happy path — every endpoint has a negative-path test
- [ ] No mocked DAO — real attachments to fixture dbs
- [ ] Per-test cleanup: handlers don't share request-scoped state across tests
- [ ] No assertion against the implementation function (tautology) — assert against documented contract

Critical paths covered:
- bead → owner lookup correctness — risk: wrong badge / wrong ownership in UI
- in-flight ordering — risk: rail surfaces stale jobs above live ones
- chain ordering — risk: graph renders steps in wrong sequence

Known deferred paths:
- Multi-repo aggregation under load — follow-up: covered by perf follow-up from T1
- Auth boundary — follow-up: separate epic when mutations land

## OUTPUT
- One test file, ~3 describe blocks (one per route)
- Tests passing in CI
notes:
T2 SCOPE DISCIPLINE: TEST FILES + FIXTURES ONLY. Do NOT edit any production code (apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts, dao.ts, registry.ts, attach-pool.ts, watcher.ts, server.ts). If a test fails because production code appears wrong, REPORT it in your output as a follow-up — do NOT modify production files to make tests pass. The T1 precedent set this rule and the orchestrator will reject scope creep.

Workspace deps (hono, vitest plugin) may have preexisting issues. Use 'bun test' directly to avoid plugin layer. If bun test ALSO fails to import hono/route handlers, document the infra block as a follow-up but do not edit dependencies.

Fixtures live at apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/fixtures/observability/ (3 db files + seed-observability-fixtures.mjs from T1).

## Summary
Specialists route test file tightened. Real DAO, real SQLite, per-test temp tree, 3 route blocks, negative paths covered.

## Status
partial

## Changes

  - `/jobs?bead_id` positive, empty, missing-param cases
  - `/jobs/in-flight` ordering + epoch summary cases
  - `/chains/:chain_id` positive + 404 cases
  - repo slug asserts on every response row

title: Test: phase-1 backend — registry, attach-pool, watcher, DAO
description:
## PROBLEM
The phase-1 server modules (registry, attach-pool, schema-guard, watcher, DAO) form the load-bearing data layer for every API and UI consumer. Without companion tests, schema drift or attach regressions will only surface mid-UI.

## SUCCESS
A unified test suite under apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/ exercises every public function of F1-F4 against fixture observability.db files (2 compatible + 1 mismatched). All anti-patterns flagged in this skill are absent.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/registry.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/attach-pool.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/watcher.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/dao.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/fixtures/ (3 seeded .db files + seed script)

## NON_GOALS
- API route tests (T2)
- UI component tests (T3)
- Mocking SQLite — use real ephemeral db files

## CONSTRAINTS
- No shared mutable state between tests (fixture-scoped, per-test temp dirs)
- Deterministic fixtures: no Date.now() in test data; pre-baked timestamps
- Boundary tests assert field presence and types, not internal row structure
- Never mock attach-pool internals from DAO tests — let them attach real fixture dbs

## VALIDATION
### Layer: core (F1 only) + boundary (F2, F3, F4)
### Strategy: example-based + recorded-fixture (real SQLite, no service available)
### Covers: forge-3ue5.1, forge-3ue5.2, forge-3ue5.3, forge-3ue5.4

Assertions required:
- [ ] **registry** positive: tmp tree with 3 fake observability.db → 3 stable slugs
- [ ] **registry** edge: empty roots → [], unreadable dir → no throw, name collision → hash suffix
- [ ] **attach-pool** positive: 3 fixtures (2 compat, 1 mismatched) → withAttached sees only 2
- [ ] **attach-pool** edge: LRU eviction at cap, schema mismatch logs + continues
- [ ] **watcher** positive: writing to a fixture db bumps its epoch within 300ms
- [ ] **watcher** edge: file delete/recreate handled, watcher stop cleans handles
- [ ] **DAO** positive: jobsByBead returns rows tagged with repo slug across 2 attached dbs
- [ ] **DAO** contract: inFlightJobs filters status IN ('starting','running') ORDER BY updated_at_ms DESC
- [ ] **DAO** contract: chainById preserves chain_kind precedence ordering
- [ ] **DAO** drift-safe: assertions check column names + types, not row identity

Anti-pattern checklist:
- [ ] No assertion-free tests
- [ ] No tautological assertions (e.g. asserting the SQL string against itself)
- [ ] Per-test temp dirs; no module-level fixture mutation
- [ ] No mocking of internal collaborators — only the filesystem is "external"
- [ ] Negative path covered (schema mismatch, missing db file, malformed row)

Critical paths covered:
- attach-pool refusing mismatched schema → risk: gitboard crashes on a sp upgrade
- DAO bead_id parameterization → risk: SQL injection / wrong rows surfaced
- watcher epoch bump → risk: stale UI never refreshes

Known deferred paths:
- Performance under N>20 attached repos → follow-up: to be created (P3)
- specialist_events row queries → follow-up: not in v1 scope

## OUTPUT
- 4 test files + fixtures dir + seed script
- All tests passing in CI, run with `bun test` (or repo's existing test command — verify before starting)
- Coverage report not required; critical-path map above is the bar

title: UI: chain mini-graph on bead detail
description:
## PROBLEM
A bead that is part of a specialist chain (explorer→executor→reviewer→fix…) has no visual representation in gitboard. Operators can't see chain progress without a terminal.

## SUCCESS
When a bead has a sp job with chain_id, the IssueOverlay shows a small DAG of the chain: one node per job, labeled by specialist + state, ordered by chain_kind. Hovering a node reveals last_output excerpt. If no chain_id, panel is hidden — no empty state needed.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueOverlay.tsx (mount point)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistChainGraph.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChain.ts (new — fetches /api/specialists/chains/:id)

## NON_GOALS
- Editing / forking the chain
- Cross-chain navigation (sibling chains under same epic)
- Animated transitions on state change

## CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse existing dep-graph rendering primitives if present in dashboard/components/beads/; do not introduce a new graph lib
- Render must degrade gracefully if chain has only 1 node
- Hover excerpt truncated to ~120 chars

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Component test: 4-node chain renders 4 nodes in correct order
- [ ] No render when bead has no chain_id
- [ ] Hover text appears within 200ms

## OUTPUT
- IssueOverlay.tsx diff + new SpecialistChainGraph.tsx + useChain hook + tests

title: DAO: specialist_jobs queries across attached repos
description:
## PROBLEM
Routes need typed query primitives over the attach pool: bead lookup, in-flight list, chain fetch, epic fetch. Inlining SQL in routes will couple them to sp's schema.

## SUCCESS
A thin DAO exports jobsByBead(beadId), inFlightJobs(), chainById(chainId), epicById(epicId). Queries UNION ALL across attached repos and tag each row with its repo slug. Return types are well-defined TS interfaces (SpecialistJob, SpecialistChain, EpicRun).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/types.ts (new)

## NON_GOALS
- Caching: rely on epoch counter, no in-memory result cache in v1
- Pagination: in-flight list is bounded by reality (≤ a few dozen)
- specialist_events / metrics queries

## CONSTRAINTS
- All queries go through attach-pool.withAttached
- Parameterize all bead/chain/epic ids — no string interpolation
- Only read columns documented in the epic REFERENCES section
- Map updated_at_ms to ISO strings at DAO boundary

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: jobsByBead returns correct rows from a fixture with 2 repos
- [ ] inFlightJobs filters status IN ('starting','running') and orders by updated_at_ms DESC
- [ ] chainById returns ordered list with chain_kind preserved
- [ ] Repo slug present on every row

## OUTPUT
- dao module with the four query fns
- types module exported for API + UI reuse

title: File-watcher invalidation for observability.db
description:
## PROBLEM
sp writes to observability.db continuously while jobs run. Gitboard needs to know when to re-query without polling on a tight loop.

## SUCCESS
A chokidar watcher subscribes to mtime changes on every observability.db in the registry. On change, an in-memory epoch counter for that repo is bumped. DAO/API layers read the counter as a freshness key; UI components use it as a swr/refetch trigger.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/epoch.ts (new)

## NON_GOALS
- Pushing changes to clients (WebSocket) — that's a future epic
- Polling: do not add a setInterval fallback in v1
- Diffing what changed; only signal that something did

## CONSTRAINTS
- Watcher lifecycle bound to server start/stop
- Debounce mtime events to ~200ms per repo
- Watcher errors must not crash the server; log + continue

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: writing to a fixture db bumps its epoch within 300ms
- [ ] Killing/recreating the file is handled gracefully
- [ ] Watcher stop cleans up handles

## OUTPUT
- watcher module with start/stop
- epoch module with get(repoSlug) + bump(repoSlug)
- Tests in __tests__/watcher.test.ts

title: Attach pool + schema_version guard for observability.db
description:
## PROBLEM
Each repo has its own observability.db; gitboard needs to read across them in one query. Direct opens are wasteful and unsafe across sp schema versions.

## SUCCESS
An LRU attach pool opens up to N (default 12, configurable) observability.db files in mode=ro and aliases each as repo_<slug>. On attach, schema_version is read; mismatched versions are warned-and-skipped, never crash other repos. Pool exposes withAttached(fn) yielding a live db handle with all healthy repos attached.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/schema-guard.ts (new)

## NON_GOALS
- DAO queries (F4)
- File watching (F3)
- Mutations of any kind

## CONSTRAINTS
- All opens use ?mode=ro (WAL-safe reads)
- Use better-sqlite3 if available in gitboard; fall back to bun:sqlite — verify in repo first
- Compatible schema_version range is a constant; bumping it requires conscious update + note
- Never hold attach across awaits; query, then release

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: attach 3 fixture dbs (2 compatible, 1 version-mismatched) — query sees only 2
- [ ] LRU eviction works when pool size exceeded
- [ ] Schema mismatch logs a warning and continues

## OUTPUT
- attach-pool module with withAttached(fn) API
- schema-guard with isCompatible(version) + COMPATIBLE_RANGE
- Fixture dbs under __tests__/fixtures/

title: Scan & registry of sp observability.db files
description:
## PROBLEM
Gitboard has no concept of which repos host a sp observability.db. Without a discovered registry, downstream attach/query layers have nothing to operate on.

## SUCCESS
A small server module discovers every observability.db under configured roots and exposes listRepos() → [{ repoSlug, repoPath, dbPath, mtimeMs }]. Roots list is config-driven (env or settings file) with default ~/dev/*.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts (new — roots + env override)

## NON_GOALS
- Opening the db files (F2)
- Watching for new files (F3)
- Any UI

## CONSTRAINTS
- Pure sync filesystem scan, no DB opens
- Skip non-readable paths silently with debug log; never throw on scan
- Repo slug derived from parent dir name; collisions disambiguated by hash suffix

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: scan against a tmp tree with 3 fake observability.db files returns 3 entries with stable slugs
- [ ] Empty roots list returns []
- [ ] Unreadable directory does not throw

## OUTPUT
- New module exporting listRepos() + RepoEntry type
- Tests in __tests__/registry.test.ts
- No wire-up to API yet — that comes in F4/F5

## APPROACH NOTES
Use fast-glob or native fs.readdir; gitboard already has fs utils — reuse if present.

title: gitboard live specialist ownership view (read-only v1)
description:
## PROBLEM
Beads in gitboard show only static fields (status, assignee string). There is no visibility into which sp specialist currently owns an in-flight bead, what chain it belongs to, or where its worktree lives — that data exists in sp's observability.db but is not surfaced. Operators must context-switch to terminal `sp ps` / `sp feed` to understand cockpit state.

## SUCCESS
Every BeadCard in gitboard shows live ownership (specialist role, state, repo) when a sp job is attached to the bead. A right-side in-flight rail lists every running/starting job across all discovered repos with last-output preview. A chain mini-graph appears on bead detail when chain_id is set. All reads are sourced from sp's observability.db; gitboard never shells out to sp.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/ (new module: registry, attach-pool, watcher, dao)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadCard.tsx (owner badge mount point)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueOverlay.tsx (chain graph mount point)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx (rail panel mount point)

## NON_GOALS
- Mutations: no resume/kill of sp jobs from gitboard in v1 (auth boundary deferred)
- WebSocket push: file-watcher + epoch invalidation only
- Cross-repo specialist_events timeline view
- sp-side HTTP/WS API exposure (tracked separately in ~/dev/specialists beads as schema_version marker, advisory)

## CONSTRAINTS
- observability.db opened mode=ro only; WAL-safe concurrent reads
- schema_version checked on attach; mismatched repos warn + skip, never crash the pool
- SQLite attach pool cap raised explicitly if >16 repos discovered
- Existing BeadCard / Sidebar layout must remain functional when no sp data is available
- React UI changes follow existing component patterns in dashboard/components/beads/

## VALIDATION
- [ ] DAO unit tests pass against a fixture observability.db
- [ ] API integration tests cover bead_id lookup, in-flight list, chain fetch
- [ ] Owner badge renders on BeadCard for a seeded in_progress bead with a sp job row
- [ ] In-flight rail updates within ~1s of a fixture db mtime change (file-watcher e2e)
- [ ] Chain mini-graph renders epic + child chain on bead detail
- [ ] Type-check + lint pass; existing gitboard tests stay green

## OUTPUT
Orchestrator close report: list of all 10 child handoffs, integration evidence (one Playwright run against a seeded repo showing badge + rail + chain), residual risks (schema-coupling watch list, performance under N>20 repos).

## REFERENCES
- sp observability.db schema: specialist_jobs (bead_id, specialist, status, chain_id, epic_id, chain_kind, chain_root_bead_id, worktree_column, last_output, updated_at_ms)
- Indexes used: idx_jobs_bead, idx_jobs_status, idx_jobs_chain, idx_jobs_epic
- Sibling tables (out of v1 scope but available): specialist_events, specialist_job_metrics, epic_runs, epic_chain_membership
close_reason: Epic complete. 13/13 children closed. v1 delivers: backend (registry+attach+schema-guard+watcher+DAO) + API routes (/jobs?bead_id, /jobs/in-flight, /chains/:id) + UI (BeadCard badge, InFlightRail, SpecialistChainGraph) + tests (12 backend + 9 API + 84 UI = 105 pass) + Playwright MCP e2e verified live dashboard + API + empty-state mounts. Dashboard build clean (364KB).

title: Dashboard bottom drawer: live event-log terminal view (UI)
description:
PROBLEM
forge-zpw8 ships the backend event-log emitter + WS feed + disk retention. The operator-facing surface is missing — without a UI drawer, the firehose only matters via /api/internal/logs or docker logs. Goal: a collapsible bottom drawer in the dashboard that mirrors the live WS log stream with filters, virtualized rendering, and resume-on-reconnect.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/types/log.ts (forge-zpw8: LogEntry shape)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts (forge-zpw8: SystemRealtimeEvent union + SystemLogPayload)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts (existing WsClient; resume contract from forge-dgd)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ActivityTimeline.tsx — virtualization pattern with @tanstack/react-virtual
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts — pattern for localStorage-persisted UI state (sidebarCollapsed)

SUCCESS
- NEW component apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/LogDrawer.tsx renders a fixed-position bottom drawer.
- Toggle button mounted in TopBar.tsx with an unread-error badge (red dot + count when drawer is collapsed AND new error/warn entries arrive since last view).
- Drawer state: collapsed by default; toggle persisted in localStorage under 'forge-log:open'; resizable height (drag handle on the top border) persisted under 'forge-log:height' (min 120px, max 70vh, default 320px).
- Drawer is mono-font ONLY (per repo typography rule); rest of the app stays sans-serif.
- NEW hook apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useLogStream.ts:
  - On mount: GET /api/internal/logs?limit=500 to seed history.
  - Subscribes to WS 'system' channel; appends 'system:log' envelopes to a ring (max 5000 client-side).
  - Reuses existing WsClient resume contract (forge-dgd) so reconnects don't lose entries.
  - Emits a derived 'unread errors/warns count since drawer was last opened' for the badge.
- NEW Zustand slice apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/logs.ts:
  - state: { entries: LogEntry[], filters: { level, components: Set<LogComponent>, search: string }, paused: boolean, lastOpenedAt: string|null }
  - actions: append, appendBatch, setLevel, toggleComponent, setSearch, pause, resume, clear, markOpened
- Drawer layout:
  - Header row (40px): level dropdown (debug/info/warn/error), component multi-select chips, search input, pause/resume button, clear button, close-drawer button.
  - Body (fills): virtualized list via @tanstack/react-virtual.
  - Row layout: `HH:MM:SS.mmm  [LEVEL chip]  [component chip]  event  msg…`
  - Click row → inline-expand the data JSON pretty-printed (max 8 lines, scroll if longer).
  - Color coding via CSS variables (no hardcoded hex): error=--accent-red, warn=--accent-yellow, debug=--text-muted, info=--text-primary.
  - Sticky bottom-scroll until user scrolls up >10px → auto-paused; a 'Resume → live' button appears at the bottom-right of the drawer.
  - Empty state when filters match nothing: small centered text 'No log entries match these filters.'
- Performance:
  - All renders MUST go through useMemo / selectors so filters don't recompute the full list on every WS event.
  - Virtualizer is the only place rows mount.
  - Filter pipeline is O(n) per filter change, NOT per append.
- Accessibility: drawer is a <section> with role='complementary', aria-label='Event log'; toggle button is a <button> with aria-expanded.
- Mobile (<768px): drawer takes 50vh, drag handle remains.

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/LogDrawer.tsx
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/LogRow.tsx (per-row component for virtualizer)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useLogStream.ts
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/logs.ts
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/log-client.ts (small fetch wrapper for /api/internal/logs)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TopBar.tsx — add drawer-toggle button with unread badge
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — mount <LogDrawer /> as sibling of <MainPane />
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css — .ide-log-drawer ruleset (mono font, drag handle, chip styling, virtualizer overrides) using CSS custom properties only
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/shell/LogDrawer.test.tsx — render-with-fixtures, filter behavior, pause/resume, badge counting
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/logs.test.ts — append/batch/filter logic, lastOpenedAt resets unread count

NON_GOALS
- No log export / download from the UI (use /api/internal/logs/files endpoint manually).
- No log forwarding to external sinks from the browser.
- No syntax highlighting of the JSON 'data' payload beyond pretty-printing.
- No persistent client-side log archive across reloads (the backend ring + disk is the source of truth).
- No multi-tab synchronization (each tab maintains its own ring; backend disk + ring is shared).
- No keyboard shortcuts in v1 (file a follow-up if it matters).
- No detail-page anchoring for individual log entries (no permalinks).

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict.
- No new runtime dependencies; reuse @tanstack/react-virtual + Zustand already in the project.
- All colors / spacing via CSS custom properties — NO hardcoded hex (per repo rule).
- Mono font scoped via .ide-log-drawer class — must NOT leak to the rest of the app.
- Drawer height resize must work via pointer events (mouse + touch) without library.
- Component must mount the toggle even before the WS connects (don't block UI on backend).
- LogRow must render in <2ms — measured with React DevTools Profiler before merge.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test → existing 219+ + new tests green.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build → clean, no new bundle warnings.
- Live smoke (manual on running server): open dashboard; click drawer toggle; verify entries stream in real time; flip level filter; verify badge counts unread errors when drawer is collapsed; resize drawer; reload page — collapsed/expanded state and height persist.
- Scrollback test: scroll up in a busy stream → confirm auto-pause; click resume → confirm tail catches up.
- Reconnect test: stop the backend, wait 5s, restart — confirm drawer recovers without duplicates (resume contract from forge-dgd).

OUTPUT
- File diff list.
- Screenshot or DOM excerpt of drawer in open + collapsed states.
- Per-row render time from Profiler (<2ms target).
- Confirmation that badge unread count behaves correctly (visible on collapsed drawer with new warns/errors; resets to 0 on open).

DEPENDS_ON forge-zpw8 (backend logger must ship first; UI consumes its WS event names + endpoint shapes).
notes: SUPERSEDED by forge-gud9. The unified bottom drawer (forge-gud9) hosts the Logs tab; this bead's UI consumer work folds into that. Backend (forge-zpw8) shipped on gkpr (logger singleton + /api/internal/logs + system WS channel). forge-gud9 will mount the LogsTab inside the drawer and consume those.

title: Structured event-log emitter + WS feed + disk retention (backend)
description:
PROBLEM
The server has rich operational state (poller cycles, watcher snapshot reads, Dolt query metrics, breaker transitions, WS connect/disconnect, source-health flips) but no unified way to capture or stream it. The forge-1qz health endpoint exposes a latency ring snapshot but everything else is invisible unless you tail stdout. Need a single structured logger with three sinks so the upcoming UI drawer (separate bead) and forensic disk archive both work, and so the docker-containerized service (forge-8kdc) leaves an audit trail beyond `docker logs`.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts — RealtimeEnvelope + 'system' channel already in ChannelName union
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts — ChannelRegistry.publish with seq + boot_id + ring buffer + resume contract from forge-dgd
- apps/gitboard/src/core/{github-poller,beads-change-watcher,dolt-client}.ts — emit call sites
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-dolt-health.ts — sibling /api/internal pattern for the new endpoint

SUCCESS
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts exports a singleton logger:
  - emit(entry: LogEntry): void — fan-out to all sinks
  - getRing(): LogEntry[] — current in-memory ring (most recent N=5000)
  - subscribe(filter?, fn): unsubscribe — local listener (not for WS, for tests + future API)
  - setDiskEnabled(boolean), setLogLevel('debug'|'info'|'warn'|'error')
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/types/log.ts:
  - LogEntry = { ts:string, level, component, event:string, msg?:string, data?:Record<string,unknown> }
  - LogLevel union, LogComponent union (poller|watcher|dolt|ws|api|breaker|system)
- types/realtime.ts extended: add 'system:log' to a new SystemRealtimeEvent union; payload SystemLogPayload = LogEntry. Bump nothing — additive only.
- SINK 1 (in-memory ring): fixed-size circular buffer (LOG_RING_SIZE=5000) preserved across the process lifetime; oldest dropped on overflow. O(1) push.
- SINK 2 (WS broadcast on 'system' channel): event name 'system:log', payload = entry, version = entry.ts. RESPECTS the LOG_LEVEL env var — only entries at >= configured level are broadcast (default 'info'). Debug entries STILL land in ring + disk.
- SINK 3 (disk, JSONL): /data/logs/<YYYY-MM-DD>.jsonl. Always writes ALL levels (full forensic capture). Daily rotation at UTC midnight. Retention: delete files older than 7 days on startup + once daily.
- Call-site instrumentation across the existing components:
  - github-poller.ts: cycle.start, cycle.complete (with counts + duration), rate_limit.changed, etag.hit_304, etag.miss, error
  - beads-change-watcher.ts: poll.skipped (hash unchanged fast-path — DEBUG), poll.snapshot_read (when snapshot actually runs — INFO), source_health.changed (on actual transition, not every emit), batch.published (with counts), drift.detected
  - dolt-client.ts: breaker.opened, breaker.closed, query.slow (>100ms only; the latency ring already records all), reconnect.attempt, reconnect.recovered
  - ws/channels.ts + handler.ts: client.connected, client.disconnected, resume.miss → emits warn, subscribe.version_mismatch (already a 4001 close — log it)
  - api/server.ts middleware: request.slow (>500ms only), request.error (non-2xx with stack if 5xx)
- NEW endpoint GET /api/internal/logs (gated to localhost): query params 'level', 'component', 'event', 'since' (ISO), 'limit' (default 200, max 1000). Reads from in-memory ring (fast). Returns JSON array.
- NEW endpoint GET /api/internal/logs/files: list available .jsonl files in /data/logs with size + date.

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts (~200 LOC)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/types/log.ts (~30 LOC)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-logs.ts (~80 LOC)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts — add SystemRealtimeEvent union + payload alias
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts — ~10 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts — ~6 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts — ~4 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/{channels.ts,handler.ts} — ~4 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts — request timing middleware + wire new /api/internal/logs routes
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/index.ts — set LOG_LEVEL from env at startup
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/core/logger.test.ts — ring overflow, level filter on broadcast, disk write/rotation/retention, instrumentation hooks fire
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/internal-logs.test.ts — query filters, localhost gate

NON_GOALS
- No UI work. The drawer is a separate bead (forge-???) depending on this one.
- No log aggregation system (loki, vector, etc.). Local disk only.
- No structured query language; query params on /api/internal/logs are flat string filters.
- No per-user / per-tenant log isolation.
- No compression of rotated files (gzip is a tiny follow-up if needed).
- No alerting / threshold rules.
- No replacement of stdout console.log calls — they keep working. New logger is ADDITIVE.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict, Bun runtime. Use Bun.file for disk writes (NOT node:fs/promises) to stay consistent.
- LOG_RING_SIZE, LOG_DEFAULT_LEVEL, LOG_DISK_DIR, LOG_RETENTION_DAYS as constants near top of logger.ts.
- Disk dir defaults to /data/logs (matches the docker volume from forge-8kdc); override via LOG_DIR env. Fall back to ./logs if /data is not writable (dev mode).
- Ring buffer push MUST be O(1) and allocation-light (called potentially 10x/sec).
- WS broadcast MUST go through the existing ChannelRegistry.publish; do NOT create a parallel transport.
- Reuse the realtime contract's seq/boot_id/resume — UI gets free reconnect-replay.
- No new runtime deps.
- All emit() call sites must be small (one-liners ideally); the logger does the structural work.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test → 219+ pass (existing) + new logger + internal-logs tests green.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build → clean.
- Live smoke (manual): start server, hit /api/internal/logs?limit=20 → returns JSON entries from cold-start (poller cycle.start, watcher first ticks, ws system events). Connect a WS client subscribed to 'system' channel → receives 'system:log' envelopes in real time. Kill Dolt for 30s → ring contains breaker.opened entry; restart → ring contains breaker.closed entry. Confirm /data/logs/<today>.jsonl exists and grows.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (~10 files).
- Logger interface (LogEntry, LogLevel, LogComponent unions) as the public contract.
- Sample 20-line JSONL excerpt from a real 60s run showing each component is wired.
- Retention test evidence (mock 8 day-files, run cleanup, confirm 1 deleted).

title: Dockerize gitboard as VPS service + Tailscale-only access
description:
PROBLEM
Developer works primarily from a VPS but wants the gitboard dashboard reachable from local laptop and desktop machines without per-session SSH-forward ceremony or public-internet exposure. The realtime stack now in place (forge-dgd/f0g/igg/1qz/3mdw) is worth running as a persistent service on the VPS — but right now there is no container, no compose, no documented access path.

SCOPE
- NEW: Dockerfile at repo root (or apps/gitboard/Dockerfile)
- NEW: docker-compose.yml at repo root (or apps/gitboard/docker-compose.yml)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/.dockerignore
- NEW: docs/deployment.md — 1-page setup README covering VPS install, Tailscale enrolment, firewall lockdown
- NO changes to runtime source code beyond what's required to honor PORT and AGENT_FORGE_DB env vars (these already work — verify, do not refactor)

SUCCESS
- `docker compose up -d` on the VPS builds and starts a single `gitboard` container.
- Container serves http://<vps-host>:3000/gitboard with the production dashboard build (NOT vite dev).
- BeadsChangeWatcher inside the container can reach the host Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:13839 (host-network mode) and successfully poll commit hashes.
- GithubPoller authenticates with GITHUB_TOKEN passed via env.
- State DB persists across container restarts via a named docker volume mounted at /data, configured via AGENT_FORGE_DB=/data/state.db.
- Host project directories (~/projects) are bind-mounted read-only at /projects so ProjectScanner can discover them.
- After Tailscale is installed on VPS + laptop + desktop and all are signed into the same tailnet, the dashboard is reachable from any signed-in device at http://<vps-tailscale-name>:3000/gitboard using MagicDNS.
- VPS firewall blocks public access to port 3000 (only the tailscale0 interface admits it).
- docs/deployment.md contains: prerequisites, build/up commands, Tailscale enrolment steps, ufw lockdown commands, troubleshooting (container can't reach Dolt / GitHub rate-limit / port already bound).

DESIGN DECISIONS (already agreed with operator — do NOT relitigate)
- Multi-stage Dockerfile: stage 1 oven/bun:1 → bun install + bun run build:dashboard; stage 2 oven/bun:1-slim → copy src, built dist, node_modules → CMD bun src/index.ts.
- Network mode: host (sees Dolt at 127.0.0.1:13839 with no extra_hosts gymnastics).
- Access pattern: Tailscale (multi-device, roaming, free for personal). NOT cloudflared, NOT bare SSH forward.
- Build target: production static-served dashboard. NO HMR/vite dev inside the container.
- Container does NOT run its own Dolt server — assumes the VPS shared dolt sql-server is already running externally (started by bd or systemd).

NON_GOALS
- Multi-container compose for Dolt itself. Out of scope; assume external.
- Reverse proxy in front of the container (nginx/Caddy). Tailscale + direct port is enough.
- TLS termination (tailscale cert is documented but not wired).
- Production-grade systemd unit on the host. `restart: unless-stopped` on compose covers reboot recovery.
- CI image publishing to a registry. Build on the VPS, no registry push.
- Beadboard frontend container — deprecated per CLAUDE.md.

CONSTRAINTS
- Final runtime image MUST be slim (target <200MB). Use oven/bun:1-slim for the runtime stage.
- .dockerignore MUST exclude .git, .beads, .xtrm, .worktrees, node_modules from the build context (relying on stage-1 install).
- Image MUST NOT bake in GITHUB_TOKEN or any other secret; pass via .env file referenced from compose.
- docker-compose.yml MUST bind the published port to all interfaces (0.0.0.0:3000:3000) so Tailscale routing works, with the public-internet firewall lockdown explicitly called out in the README as a required step.
- No new top-level runtime dependencies added to package.json.
- Bun runtime (per project rule). Do NOT switch to node.
- Volumes: named volume `gitboard-state` for /data; read-only bind for /projects.

VALIDATION
- `docker compose build` succeeds and image size < 200MB (`docker images gitboard:latest` confirms).
- `docker compose up -d` brings the container to healthy state; `docker compose logs gitboard` shows expected startup lines (Server running, Backfilling events, no Dolt connection errors).
- From inside the container: `curl http://localhost:3000/api/internal/dolt-health` returns JSON with breakerOpenUntil=0.
- From the VPS host: `curl http://127.0.0.1:3000/gitboard` returns the dashboard index.
- After Tailscale enrolment on VPS + at least one other device: that device opens http://<vps-tailscale-name>:3000/gitboard and the dashboard loads with live data.
- Container restart: `docker compose restart` → dashboard still loads, state.db survives, WS reconnects via existing resume protocol.
- docs/deployment.md walks a reader from zero to working setup in <10 minutes.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .dockerignore, docs/deployment.md).
- Built image size.
- Sample `docker compose logs gitboard | head -30` output proving Dolt connection + watcher + poller all healthy.
- Tailscale verification: screenshot path or curl from a second device.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/index.ts — entrypoint, reads PORT env (default 3000).
- apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md — runtime/Bun rules, env var inventory, port defaults.
- apps/gitboard/package.json — has build:dashboard script.
- apps/gitboard/vite.config.ts — base /gitboard/, proxy config not used in prod.
- Existing memory `dolt-connection-stability-forge-1qz-dolt-client-ts` — pool/breaker/probe constants matter at startup.

title: BeadsChangeWatcher: skip full snapshot when commit hash unchanged + collapse N+1 in toIssue
description:
PROBLEM
forge-1qz live smoke revealed BeadsChangeWatcher is hitting the 3s query timeout on roughly 1 in 3 polls against healthy Dolt — observed during the kill-Dolt failover test on 2026-05-17 (gitboard project, ~200 issues). Two compounding causes in apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts:

1. **Commit-hash poll is wasted.** poll() at line 60-68 calls getCommitHash(project) AND readSnapshot(project) every tick unconditionally. The whole point of commit-hash polling is that the hash IS the cheap 'did anything change' probe — if it matches the last seen hash, the snapshot read should be skipped. Today the expensive snapshot runs every 2s (active) / 10s (idle) regardless.

2. **N+1 fan-out in DoltClient.toIssue.** Each issue row triggers 3 separate awaited queries (getDependencies, getRelatedIds, getLabels). getIssues({limit:1000}) therefore performs ~3,001 sequential queries for a project with 1000 issues; gitboard has ~200 issues so it's ~600 queries per snapshot. Combined with the 3s wrapper this regularly times out, watcher silently falls through to JSONL via the catch on line 126-128, and the latency ring fills with 3000ms entries.

SCOPE
- apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (poll(), readSnapshot())
- apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (toIssue() and the three N+1 helpers OR a new batched getIssuesWithRelations)

SUCCESS
- poll() stores lastCommitHash per project. On each tick: cheap getCommitHash() call only. If hash unchanged AND we already have a previous snapshot, emit beads:source_health and return early — no readSnapshot, no diff, no batch.
- When the hash DOES change (or is null/first-tick), proceed with readSnapshot as today.
- DoltClient.getIssues collapses the per-issue N+1 into 4 total queries: one SELECT issues, one SELECT dependencies WHERE issue_id IN (...), one SELECT issue_related WHERE issue_id IN (...), one SELECT labels WHERE issue_id IN (...). Map them into the BeadIssue shape in JS.
- After fix: latency ring for the watcher path shows no 3000ms entries on healthy Dolt across a 60s window.

NON_GOALS
- No schema migration.
- Do not change DoltClient.getIssue(single) — keep the existing per-issue path for that route.
- Do not touch the breaker / pool / probe constants from forge-1qz.

CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve the public DoltClient.getIssues(filters) signature.
- TypeScript strict.
- Keep diff under ~80 lines.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test → still 219/219.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard test → still only pre-existing baseline failures (beads-reader.test.ts bun:sqlite + dolt-client.test.ts labels).
- Live smoke: with the gitboard server running and Dolt up, poll /api/internal/dolt-health for 60s; recentLatencies should contain NO 3000ms entries.

OUTPUT
- File diffs (2 files).
- Before/after latency-ring snapshot proving timeouts are gone.
close_reason: Watcher: lastCommitHash cache + skip-snapshot fast-path. DoltClient.getIssues/getClosedIssues: batched IN-clause hydration replacing N+1. Verified live: 60s watcher poll = 0 new timeouts vs ~33% pre-fix. Tests 219+ pass; pre-existing baseline failure unchanged
