description:
PROBLEM
There is no surface to view a repo's README or CHANGELOG inside gitboard/beadboard. Users have to leave the dashboard and open GitHub. Once /console exists, clicking a repo in the unified rail should open a repo dossier.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx (exports renderPrBodyText — the markdown renderer to reuse)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (existing /api/github/* endpoints)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (existing fetch path using gh CLI / GITHUB_TOKEN)
- GitHub Contents API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}

SUCCESS
- New /api/github/repo/:owner/:name/markdown?path=README.md (and =CHANGELOG.md) endpoint returns { content, sha, last_modified, encoding }.
- New RepoDossier overlay (modeled after IssueOverlay if forge-buv lands first) shows tabs: README · CHANGELOG · open PRs · open Issues · recent Releases. Each tab renders its content via renderPrBodyText for markdown or existing timeline components for PRs/Issues/Releases.
- Server-side cache: TTL 10 min per (repo, path) to avoid hammering GitHub rate limits.
- Clicking a repo in the rail opens RepoDossier; click outside / Escape closes.

SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (new GET /api/github/repo/:owner/:name/markdown)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts (new module: fetch + cache, reuses getGithubToken from github-poller)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoDossier.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoSidebar.tsx (wire click-to-open if not already opening repo filter)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (getRepoMarkdown)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/github.test.ts (test the new endpoint with mocked gh)

NON_GOALS
- No editing or pushing.
- No fork of renderPrBodyText; share the export.
- No multi-file browsing beyond README + CHANGELOG.
- No syntax-highlighted code rendering beyond what renderPrBodyText already does (fenced blocks plain).

CONSTRAINTS
- GitHub auth via existing getGithubToken() fallback (gh CLI → GITHUB_TOKEN env).
- Endpoint returns 404 when file does not exist (not 500).
- Body must run through SafeMarkdown / renderPrBodyText (script/style strip).
- Cache lives in process memory; no new dependency.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new endpoint test passes (mock gh).
- Manual: open dashboard, click a repo with a known README → RepoDossier shows rendered README.
- 404 case returns empty state, not error toast.

OUTPUT
- Files changed, /api contract, screenshot of RepoDossier with README expanded, residual risks (rate limit, large files).
close_reason: Superseded: integrated into gitboard TabBar + RepoContentPanels (forge-uqp / forge-xnk / forge-5rr)

title: Gitboard: add Releases tab alongside Activity/PRs/Issues
description:
PROBLEM
apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/TabBar segments are Activity / PRs / Issues. ReleaseEvent rows are already polled into the events table by GithubPoller, but there's no UI surface for release timeline (which tag, when, by whom, body).

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/{ActivityTimeline,PrTimeline,IssueTimeline}.tsx (row primitive + dossier pattern to mirror)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (existing /api/github/* endpoints)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (already inserts ReleaseEvent into events table)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubEvent shape)
- Verified data: /api/github/events returns rows where type === 'ReleaseEvent'

SUCCESS
- New ReleaseTimeline.tsx renders one row per ReleaseEvent: repo · tag · published date · author · expand-in-place dossier showing release notes body rendered via renderPrBodyText (PrTimeline's exported helper).
- New /api/github/releases endpoint returns releases (either by joining events table or filtering type='ReleaseEvent'), pageable like other endpoints.
- TabBar in GithubPanel.tsx gets a 'Releases' segment with count, sibling to Activity/PRs/Issues.
- Repo sidebar filter applies to Releases the same way it applies to other tabs (filter prop on the component).

SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ReleaseTimeline.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx (TabBar entry + ReleaseTimeline mount)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (new GET /api/github/releases)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (add releases slice + fetch)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (add getReleases method)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (add GithubRelease interface if release notes need fields beyond GithubEvent)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/github/ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx (smoke: renders empty state + renders a release)

NON_GOALS
- No release creation/publishing actions; read-only.
- No semver parsing or grouping; chronological only.
- No artifact downloads.
- No assets UI.

CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse renderPrBodyText for release notes markdown.
- Use the existing row primitive (.issue-row or equivalent) — no new card style.
- No new icon family; use Octicons (TagIcon for releases).
- Body sanitisation must run (script/style strip — already in renderPrBodyText).

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new ReleaseTimeline test passes.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build:dashboard green.
- Manual: /gitboard → Releases tab → shows ≥1 row if state.db has any ReleaseEvent rows; expand renders body.

OUTPUT
- Files changed, API contract for /api/github/releases, screenshot of Releases tab with one row expanded.
close_reason: Superseded: integrated into gitboard TabBar + RepoContentPanels (forge-uqp / forge-xnk / forge-5rr)

title: Beadboard Triage: render as Feed-style rows ordered by triage
description:
PROBLEM
The Triage tab in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx renders custom card grids (TriageIssueList, TriageStatCard) that diverge from the Feed row primitive shipped in forge-l85/forge-wky. Users can see top picks / quick wins / stale, but cannot expand any of them inline.

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (TriagePanel, TriageIssueList, TriageStatGrid, buildTriageView)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (IssueRow + IssueDossier — the row primitive to reuse)

SUCCESS
- Triage tab keeps Velocity + Project Health as small stat tiles at the top (no change required there).
- Top picks / Quick wins / Stale sections render as the SAME IssueRow component the Feed uses, grouped by section header.
- Clicking any row expands the same IssueDossier inline (parity with Feed).
- Ordering within each section preserved (top picks by score desc, quick wins by priority asc + score desc, stale by daysIdle desc).

SCOPE
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — replace TriageIssueList with IssueFeed-or-IssueRow reuse; keep StatCard/StatGrid components for Velocity + Project health.
- Possibly extract IssueRow + the selection-state pattern into a small reusable wrapper if App becomes too large.
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx — export IssueRow or a TriageGroupedFeed component if cleaner.

NON_GOALS
- No change to scoring heuristics (priority + staleness + open-load weighting stays).
- No change to Velocity / Project Health tile layout.
- No removal of the Triage tab; this is a structural/visual unification only.

CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse existing dossier interactions (loadProjectData + handleIssueSelect already in App).
- Single source of truth for the "selected issue" state — don't fork it.
- Section header style must match the Metadata / Description section title style used by IssueDossier.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard build:dashboard green.
- All three triage groups render at least one row when there are ≥1 open issue with deps; rows match Feed row density.
- Click a triage row → dossier opens inline (or via overlay if forge-buv has landed first); same content as Feed expand.

OUTPUT
- Diff summary, before/after screenshot of Triage tab, list of removed CSS rules and reused ones.
close_reason: Triage tab now renders Top picks/Quick wins/Stale as the same IssueRow Feed uses; rows expand inline via shared selectedIssueId state. Velocity + Project health stat tiles preserved.

title: Beadboard Kanban: shrink chips, expand-on-click via overlay
description:
PROBLEM
apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx + StatusColumn.tsx + BeadCard.tsx render columns with oversized cards and pill-style chips; visual density doesn't match the Feed rows shipped in forge-l85 / forge-wky. Clicking a card does not open a dossier in the kanban view (expand-in-place from forge-4wa is StatusColumn-local and shows only a small inline preview).

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/{KanbanBoard,StatusColumn,BeadCard}.tsx
- Existing IssueDossier in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (the full dossier — Metadata · Description · Notes · Labels · Related · Audit log · Dependency tree)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (row + dossier styles)

SUCCESS
- Kanban cards visually match Feed row density (same id+title+meta-cluster pattern; no oversized chips).
- Clicking a kanban card opens the SAME IssueDossier as the Feed view, in an overlay modal centered on the viewport (max-width clamp(560px, 75%, 1400px), backdrop dimmer rgba(0,0,0,0.45)).
- Overlay closes on (a) clicking the backdrop, (b) Escape key, (c) the existing chev / close icon.
- Body scroll is locked while overlay is open.
- Drag-and-drop (if currently wired in KanbanBoard) is preserved.

SCOPE
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/StatusColumn.tsx
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadCard.tsx
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (kanban styles + new .bead-overlay-*)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx if hoisting selectedIssueId is needed
- New component: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueOverlay.tsx (wraps IssueDossier in a portal+backdrop)
- Tests: apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/components/beads/StatusColumn.test.tsx (update expand-in-place expectations or migrate to overlay)

NON_GOALS
- No drag-and-drop changes (preserve as-is).
- No structural change to IssueDossier (reuse, don't fork).
- No mutation actions (claim/close); read-only overlay.
- No tooltips beyond what already exists.

CONSTRAINTS
- Portal must be Reach/Radix-compatible (we already use Radix for accordion); use ReactDOM.createPortal to document.body OR a Radix Dialog primitive.
- Overlay must trap focus (Tab cycles within overlay) and restore focus on close.
- Markdown rendering must still use SafeMarkdown (XSS safety preserved).
- Reuse .bead-expanded-stack and section CSS classes — no new dossier styling.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard build:dashboard green.
- Click a kanban card → overlay opens with full dossier; press Escape → closes; click outside → closes.
- Body cannot scroll while overlay open.
- Manual: navigate by keyboard alone (Tab/Shift+Tab) without escaping overlay.
- Existing StatusColumn test passes or is updated to the overlay model.

OUTPUT
- Files changed list, screenshots of card density (before/after), overlay open/close gif if convenient, residual risks (e.g. focus management quirks).
close_reason: Kanban cards shrunk + overlay modal wraps the existing IssueDossier (exported from IssueFeed). Drops inline BeadDossier from StatusColumn; KanbanBoard owns single selectedId state. Escape/click-outside/focus-trap/body-scroll-lock all working.

title: Beadboard: unify row anatomy + tighten id↔title (consistent across rows)
description: Move all variable meta (owner/date/children/deps/agent) into a single right-aligned meta cluster. Keep id (110px) | title (1fr) | meta-cluster (1fr) | type (64px) | state (88px) | chev (16px). id and title same 12px font, id mono-muted, title Inter-primary, 8px gap.
close_reason: id+title same size, single right-aligned meta cluster, type/state/chev fixed slots

title: Beadboard: widen expanded dossier to ~75% of viewport, centered
description: max-width 760px is too narrow on wide screens. Use clamp(640px, 75%, 1400px) so it scales with the viewport while staying readable and not flush against the edges.
close_reason: stack width clamp(560px, 75%, 1400px)

title: Beads kanban: expand-in-place for bead details
description: PROBLEM The new design's Beads kanban view is cleaner than the current beadboard implementation, but the design doesn't show how to expand a bead's description/details inline within a kanban column. We need to adapt the design's kanban while ADDING an expand-in-place dossier on each card, with safe markdown/html/xml rendering. REFERENCES - Current kanban: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx, StatusColumn.tsx, BeadCard.tsx - Design kanban: /tmp/xtrm-design/xtrm-console/project/src/beads-module.jsx (KanbanView function) - Existing safe markdown rendering pattern (used in PR view): grep for SafeMarkdown / dangerouslySetInnerHTML guard in apps/{gitboard,beadboard} - Forge-tm0 findings: drop "bd sync" button (does not exist); core affordances (status/type/priority/assignee/labels/deps/children/comments via bd) all supported SUCCESS - Kanban columns visually match design (no pills, square chrome, octicon type marks). - Each BeadCard can expand inline within its column to show: full description, dependencies, children list, agent if any, comments if loaded. - Markdown/HTML/XML in description renders safely (use SafeMarkdown component pattern or equivalent; no raw dangerouslySetInnerHTML with unsanitised input). - Expand state persisted per-card during view lifetime (collapsing on column switch is acceptable). - Smooth: only one card expanded per column at a time, click again to collapse. - DO NOT include any "bd sync" button (forge-tm0 verified it doesn't exist). SCOPE - apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx - apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/StatusColumn.tsx - apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadCard.tsx - apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (only if expand-in-place pattern needs reuse) - packages/ui if a shared SafeMarkdown / DossierBlock helper is needed - Adjacent tests in apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/components/beads/ NON_GOALS - Don't add features that don't map to a bd CLI command (per forge-tm0 audit). - No icon-family / font / token changes. - No drag-and-drop reordering unless trivial. CONSTRAINTS - Markdown rendering MUST be XSS-safe. If existing SafeMarkdown helper exists, reuse it. Otherwise use a sanitised library (e.g. DOMPurify-based wrapper) — do not just dangerouslySetInnerHTML on bead description. - Card expand must not break virtualization if used. - Match existing accent / token palette. VALIDATION - Manual: open kanban, click a card → expanded dossier appears in column → click again → collapses. - Markdown rendering: include a description with backtick code, *emphasis*, [links](...), and an or

The xtrm-console design contract calls for: bg #181818, single accent #8ed2dc, radius 0 (no pills), thin rgba(255,255,255,0.07–0.18) borders, no gradients, no decorative drop shadows. We need one canonical tokens layer the rest of the refresh slices build on.

CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve all existing visual functionality; this is a token + chrome pass.

- Keep accent overrides minimal — single accent token; no rainbow.

VALIDATION

description:
PROBLEM
The original gitboard PR expanded view is leaner and more readable than the design's verbose version. Keep the leaner spine but cherry-pick the genuinely cool features from the design (octicon meta lines, dossier section labels, conversation thread with markers, file diff rows, linked-beads block). Drop chart-like features (lifecycle ribbon, progress bars, sparklines).

REFERENCES
- Current PR view: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx (526 lines)

SUCCESS
- PR expanded view retains existing leaner layout (no two-column dossier sprawl).
- Adopts these design wins where they fit cleanly:

  * File diff rows with +/- diff numbers in mono, kept simple (no visual meter bar)
  * Linked-beads block surfacing related forge-* IDs from PR body if present
- DROP: lifecycle ribbon (Opened → Review → Mergeable → Deployed), epic progress segmented bars, file diff visual meter bar, any other chart-like ornament.
- Preserve safe markdown rendering (existing pattern from recent commit "Render PR detail bodies safely").

SCOPE

NON_GOALS

- No font-family changes (forge-m38 done).
- No token changes (forge-93p done).
- No charts, no progress bars, no sparklines, no lifecycle ribbons.
- Don't rewrite the layout from scratch — adapt incrementally.

CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve existing tests where they pertain to PrTimeline structure (or update them in lockstep).
- Markdown/HTML/XML rendering must remain XSS-safe (existing SafeMarkdown pattern).
- Section labels: 11-12px, uppercase tracking ~0.04em, Inter, accent-tinted octicon.

VALIDATION
- Snapshot the diff: changes localised to PrTimeline.tsx and at most 1-2 adjacent files.
- Visual: section labels use octicons; conversation thread items have type-marked octicons; no chart-like elements.

OUTPUT
- Manifest of design wins adopted vs deliberately skipped (with reason).
- Files changed, residual risks.

title: Octicon consistency pass across gitboard + beadboard
description:
PROBLEM
Mixed icon families across gitboard + beadboard: some files use @primer/octicons-react, others use unicode glyphs or custom SVGs. The xtrm-console contract requires one icon family — Octicons — everywhere.

REFERENCES
- Already in use: @primer/octicons-react (RepoSidebar, EventIcon, etc.)
- Design source icon set: /tmp/xtrm-design/xtrm-console/project/src/icons.jsx (octicon paths)
- bd remember: xtrm-console-visual-contract

SUCCESS
- Every visible glyph rendered via @primer/octicons-react components.
- No raw unicode glyphs in JSX text content used as icons (e.g. "✓", "●", "→", etc. used decoratively).
- No custom inline-SVG icons in components; if a needed glyph is missing from Octicons, document the exception.

SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/*.tsx
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/*.tsx
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx + apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
- packages/ui/src/components/* if any icon-like components live there

NON_GOALS
- No font changes (forge-m38).
- No CSS token changes (forge-93p is landed).
- No layout changes.

CONSTRAINTS
- Match design's icon usage in meta lines: clock, person, branch, comment, file, diff, tag, type marks (bug/feature/task/epic/chore/decision/PR variants/issue variants).
- Sizing: design uses 12px for inline meta icons, 14px for row primary icons, 16px for headers.
- Preserve existing accessibility (aria-labels on icon buttons).

VALIDATION
- grep "•|●|→|✓|✗|⏵|⏷|◯" in JSX text content of scoped files — should return 0 matches for decorative use (any remaining must be text content, not icons).
- grep for raw <svg> in scoped files — should only appear via Octicon components.

OUTPUT
- Manifest of icon swaps per file.
- Any unmatchable glyph noted with the chosen Octicon substitute (or kept).

title: Typography pass: less mono in UI text
description:
PROBLEM
JetBrains Mono is overused in UI text (titles, tabs, meta lines, labels). The xtrm-console contract restricts mono to IDs, SHAs, branch refs, diff numbers, and file paths — everything else uses Inter.

REFERENCES

- bd remember: xtrm-console-visual-contract

SUCCESS
- Mono font-family used only on these surfaces: bead IDs (forge-xxx), PR numbers (#412), SHA hashes, branch refs (origin/main), diff numbers (+142/-28), file paths, code blocks, command hints.
- Everything else (titles, tabs, meta lines, dossier prose, kv keys, segment labels, state tokens, day headers) uses Inter.
- Hierarchy still readable — restore Inter where mono was masking intended emphasis.

SCOPE
- packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (typography tokens if needed)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
- All *.tsx in apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/src/dashboard/components/* that set font-family inline

NON_GOALS
- No icon changes (forge-hl1).
- No layout reflow.
- No new tokens unless typography demands them.

CONSTRAINTS
- Don't add new font imports beyond Inter and JetBrains Mono.
- Preserve existing accessibility/contrast.
- Avoid mass replace — selectively change font-family in identified mono-leaking surfaces.

VALIDATION
- grep "JetBrains Mono\|monospace\|font-family.*mono" across scoped files — every remaining hit must be on one of the allowed surfaces (ID/SHA/branch/diff/path/code).

OUTPUT

### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job 8506cb) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `6401ecc` diff for `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx`, `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx`, `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/index.ts`
- Confidence: high

## Findings
- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx:153-164`
  - Issue: board surface gone. `IssueFeed` replaces `KanbanBoard`, and no UI path left to board view.
  - Why it matters: task asked preserve board access; this diff removes existing primary board behavior, so users lose old navigation/sort/scan flow.
  - Suggested executor instruction: restore board entrypoint or add explicit switch between feed and board; keep old board reachable from primary UI.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx:110-118`
  - Issue: issue row is clickable `<article>` only. No keyboard focus, no `role`, no `tabIndex`, no Enter/Space handler.
  - Why it matters: expansion becomes mouse-only. Accessibility regresses and inline dossier unusable for keyboard users.
  - Suggested executor instruction: make row a real `<button>` or add full keyboard semantics + focus styles.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx:83-99`
  - Issue: async detail fetch has stale-response race. Rapid click A→B can let A resolve after B and overwrite `selectedIssueDetail`.
  - Why it matters: expanded card can show wrong dossier under current selection; hard-to-repro state bug.
  - Suggested executor instruction: guard fetch with request token / abort controller and ignore out-of-date responses.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx:17-23, 49-57`
  - Issue: sort rank omits valid status `in_review`, so those issues fall to unknown rank 99, after closed/deferred.
  - Why it matters: feed order lies for existing status enum; review work gets buried.
  - Suggested executor instruction: add `in_review` to rank/label map or derive rank from full status enum.

## Non-findings
- Not flagging `any` in API client or inline style density. Existing pattern, not regression from diff.
- Not judging broader product design, rail/kanban semantics, or backend payload shape. Outside sanity pass.

## Handoff
Proceed to executor fix on 4 concrete regressions. Biggest hit: board removal, keyboard access, stale detail race, status sort hole.

apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts:50 readClosedIssues calls queryIssues() which orders by 'priority ASC, created_at DESC, issue_id ASC' (forge-bov4 change), then JS-filters status=='closed', then slice(0, limit).

Result: with hundreds of closed P0/P1 beads, recent P2/P3/P4 closes never make the dashboard's top-50 closed list. forge-dymv (P2 closed today) is invisible in the closed section even though it's the most recently closed bead.

## EVIDENCE (verified 2026-05-27)
- gitboard has 521 total closed beads
- /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues/closed?limit=50 returns forge-oxz2 (P1 closed today) but NOT forge-dymv (P2 closed today)
- Sort is priority ASC then created_at DESC → all 31 P0s + first 19 of 167 P1s fill the top 50

## SUCCESS
Recent closed beads visible regardless of priority. Either:
(a) readClosedIssues uses ORDER BY closed_at DESC instead of inheriting queryIssues' sort
(b) Dedicated SQL query in readClosedIssues with status='closed' WHERE clause + closed_at sort

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/substrate.ts — replace readClosedIssues implementation with a direct SQL query that sorts by closed_at DESC.

## SCRUTINY
low

## VALIDATION
- curl /api/beads/projects/<gitboard>/issues/closed?limit=10 returns the 10 most-recently closed beads (by closed_at), forge-dymv at top
- Existing materializer tests still pass

## DISCOVERED FROM
.27 check: operator reports closed beads stay visible on dashboard. Investigation showed neither the open filter nor the priority-sorted closed-50 includes recent P2/P3 closes.

title: Substrate parent_id always null — materializer doesn't resolve parent from dependencies(type=parent-child)
description:
## PROBLEM
Every issue in substrate_issues has parent_id=null. Verified forge-eorh.27 (which clearly has parent forge-eorh per bd) shows parent_id: null in xtrm.sqlite.

Root cause: bd's parent field is DERIVED from the dependencies array (filter type='parent-child', take depends_on_id). bd's JSON list output exposes 'parent: <id>' as a derived field. DoltClient.getIssues reads issues.parent_id column directly, which appears to be NULL because bd doesn't populate that column — it stores parent relationships in the dependencies table.

So normalizeIssue at apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts:157 reads issue.parent_id (always undefined for bd-sourced data) and writes null.

## SUCCESS
- substrate_issues.parent_id populated correctly: forge-eorh.27 → parent_id=forge-eorh
- Dashboard renders epic→child hierarchy

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-adapter.ts normalizeIssue: derive parent_id from issue.dependencies where type='parent-child'
- OR: read from the source's derived 'parent' field if BeadIssue type carries it
- Verify Dolt source path (DoltClient.assembleIssue) populates parent_id from deps array — line 187 already does .filter for parent-child children, but it doesn't reverse-resolve a parent for the issue itself

## SCRUTINY
medium

## DISCOVERED FROM
.27 Check 2 validation walkthrough
notes: Demoted to P3. Operator confirmed via Check 2 walkthrough: dashboard hierarchy renders correctly because it resolves parent-child via substrate_dependencies (which IS populated). The substrate_issues.parent_id column being null is a cosmetic redundancy gap — the underlying graph is correct.

title: internal-substrate /schema endpoint loopback-gated but service binds tailnet-only — endpoint unreachable
description: PROBLEM: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-substrate.ts gates /api/internal/substrate/schema with isLocalhost(host) requiring host startsWith 'localhost'|'127.0.0.1'|'[::1]'. But gitboard.service binds HOST=100.113.49.52 (tailnet IP only), so no caller can satisfy the loopback check — the endpoint always returns 403 or refuses connection. SUCCESS: orchestrator/admin can curl the endpoint to verify schema deploys; tailnet-only exposure remains. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-substrate.ts isLocalhost. title: Followup: p2-beads-adapter.ts smoke published-hint filter mismatch
description:
## PROBLEM
Discovered while addressing forge-eorh.46: tests/smoke/p2-beads-adapter.ts:21 filters published WS hints on (channel='system', event='materializer:hint') but the materializer's realtimeHintFor() emits beads sources on channels ['beads:changes', 'beads:project:<id>'] with event='beads:sync_hint'. The smoke's published.length assertion has never matched, only the cursor/row count assertions actually validate anything. Fixing this would also unmask a second issue: appended JSONL lines aren't being picked up by readSnapshot during the trigger cycle (active row count stays at 1 even after second trigger and 3+ seconds wait).

## SUCCESS
Smoke published-hint filter aligns with realtimeHintFor() output AND active row count tracks file changes correctly.

## SCOPE
apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p2-beads-adapter.ts
apps/gitboard/src/core/materializer/beads-snapshot-source.ts (if read caching is the second-issue cause)

## CONSTRAINTS
Out of scope for .46 (which is timeout bump only). Needs debugger investigation for the file-read part.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Smoke completes without timeout
- [ ] published.length matches hint cycle count
- [ ] After appendFileSync + trigger + waitFor, active row count = 2

## OUTPUT
Fixed smoke + root cause of read-not-seeing-append.

title: Flaky smoke: p2-beads-adapter.ts waitFor timeout=3000ms is too aggressive
description:

apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p2-beads-adapter.ts has a hard-coded waitFor(3000ms) at line 78 that times out before the materializer completes. The materializer itself works (proven by 26 passing tests + other smokes + p1-parity-cycle showing 5/5 cycles green). Flake is in the smoke timing, not in the materializer code.

apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p2-beads-adapter.ts:78 — waitFor() implementation.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Polling with a higher upper-bound (15s) + faster poll interval (50ms instead of probably 500ms), OR

## VALIDATION

Changed file, sample run output, residual risks.

title: Test P7: Renames + log move (migration smoke + build/test + log-path smoke)
description:
## PROBLEM
P7 is mechanical renames. Risk = data loss in the DB fold, broken builds in dir rename, or lost logs in path move. Smoke proof per task is the appropriate strategy; deep unit testing is overkill.

Layer: boundary (DB migration, FS path) + shell (build + test runners after dir rename).
Strategy: smoke per task + build/test smoke for dir rename.
Covers: forge-eorh.15, forge-eorh.16, forge-eorh.17.

## SUCCESS
Migration smoke shows row-count match + no data loss; build/test green after dir rename; logs appear in new path; legacy symlink works.

## SCOPE
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p7-fold-db.sh` (run migration; row-count both DBs; assert match).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p7-dir-rename.sh` (`bun run build && bun test` after rename).
- New: `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p7-logs-move.sh` (boot; verify new path; verify symlink if legacy exists).

## NON_GOALS
- Unit-test coverage of trivial path joins.
- Performance benchmarking.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Fold migration smoke must run on an actual install with pre-existing gitboard.sqlite (or a fixture copy).
- Dir rename smoke must run the FULL test suite, not a subset.
- Log smoke must verify NO destructive ops on `~/.agent-forge/`.

## VALIDATION

Assertions required:

**Background smoke (mandatory — all three):**
- [ ] `p7-fold-db.sh`: pre-migration row counts captured per github_* table; post-migration row counts in xtrm.sqlite match exactly; gitboard.sqlite renamed to `.migrated.<timestamp>`, not deleted.
- [ ] `p7-fold-db.sh`: second boot is no-op (idempotency).
- [ ] `p7-dir-rename.sh`: `bun run build:dashboard` passes; full `bun test` passes; no broken imports.
- [ ] `p7-dir-rename.sh`: `grep -r 'apps/gitboard' apps/xtrm/ tools/` returns zero functional refs (only historical mentions).
- [ ] `p7-logs-move.sh`: boot service; `~/.xtrm/logs/$(date +%F).jsonl` exists; if `~/.agent-forge/logs/` was present, `~/.xtrm/logs/legacy` symlink resolves to it.
- [ ] `p7-logs-move.sh`: NO files deleted from `~/.agent-forge/`.

**E2E (visible UI — for log path only since logs panel is visible):**
- [ ] After log move, internal Logs panel in dashboard shows today's log entries from the new path.

## OUTPUT
All three smoke logs, build/test pass output, row-count evidence.

## Critical paths covered
- DB fold idempotency + row-count match — risk: data loss on migration
- Build green post-rename — risk: broken imports
- Legacy symlink preservation — risk: historical log access lost

## Known deferred paths
- Cross-platform path edge cases (Windows, NFS) → out of scope; Linux-only target

title: P7/17: Move logs from ~/.agent-forge/ → ~/.xtrm/
description:

Per §5 + §16, the xtrm identity supersedes agent-forge. Logs move out of `~/.agent-forge/logs/` into `~/.xtrm/logs/` once the rename phase is in flight.

## SUCCESS

title: Telemetry smoke bead 20260523T064513Z
description: Temporary telemetry smoke bead: verify bead creation, API visibility, watcher logs, and claim flow.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job eec2b1) [WAITING — more output may follow]

Based on my GitNexus exploration, I now have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis of the codebase relevant to this telemetry smoke bead task.

## Summary

Gitboard/beadboard is a bead-focused project management system with two apps (gitboard + beadboard). Beads are persisted as JSONL files in `.beads/` directories, discovered by `ProjectScanner`, read by `BeadsReader`, and watched for changes by `BeadsChangeWatcher`. The `bd` CLI mentioned in the task is an EXTERNAL tool - NOT in the indexed codebase.

## Architecture Overview

**Two-app architecture:**
1. **gitboard** - Main app with GitHub integration, terminal, specialists dashboard
2. **beadboard** - Bead-focused standalone dashboard

**Key data flow:**
```
.beads/ directory (JSONL files)
    ↓
ProjectScanner.scanPath() / listBeadsFiles()
    ↓
BeadsReader.getIssues() / getIssue() / getDependencies()
    ↓
DoltClient (optional persistent storage)
    ↓
REST API routes (/api/beads/projects/:id/issues)
    ↓
WebSocket publish (via ChannelRegistry + WsHandler)
    ↓
Frontend UI (BeadsRepoView, IssueFeed)
```

**Real-time changes:**
```
BeadsChangeWatcher.poll() 
    → getCommitHash() / diffAndQueue() 
    → flush() 
    → ChannelRegistry.publish('beads-change', ...)
```

## Key Symbols

| Symbol | Location | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `BeadsChangeWatcher` | apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts | Polls `.beads/` for changes, publishes WS events |
| `BeadsReader` | apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts | Reads JSONL bead files |
| `ProjectScanner` | apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts | Discovers projects with `.beads/` directories |
| `WsHandler` | apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts | WebSocket connection management |
| `ChannelRegistry` | apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts | Pub/sub for real-time updates |
| `DoltClient` | apps/gitboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts | Alternative storage backend |
| `createApp` | apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts | Express app setup, routes registration |

## API Visibility

**Endpoints (from beads-api.ts + routes):**
- `GET /api/beads/projects` - list projects
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id` - get project
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/issues` - list issues for project
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/issues/:issueId` - single issue detail
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/closed` - closed issues
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/memories` - project memories
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/stats` - project statistics
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/connection` - connection status

**No POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints found for beads** - creation/modification would be via direct file system operations (hence `bd` CLI is external).

## Watcher Logs Flow

1. `BeadsChangeWatcher.start()` → initializes polling loop
2. `loop()` → calls `poll()` every 2s (ACTIVE_POLL_MS default)
3. `poll(path)` → checks `getCommitHash()` for changes
4. `diffAndQueue()` → compares snapshots, queues change events
5. `flush()` → publishes via `ChannelRegistry.publish('beads-change', ...)`

**Key implementation note (from memory context):** Watcher poll interval was previously 2s × 25 projects causing load issues. Optimized to use `fs.watch` for real-time signal + 30s fallback poll for stable projects.

## Claim Flow

**The `bd` CLI commands (`bd update --claim`, `bd close`, etc.) are NOT in this codebase.** The beadboard app READS beads but does NOT implement bead lifecycle management. External `bd` tool:
- Creates/modifies JSONL files in `.beads/` directories
- Beadboard detects changes via `BeadsChangeWatcher`
- UI reflects the changes via WS subscription

## Verification Checklist

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Bead creation | ✅ Found | Via filesystem (JSONL in `.beads/`) |
| API visibility | ✅ Found | REST routes in apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts |
| Watcher logs | ✅ Found | BeadsChangeWatcher class with poll/diff/flush |
| Claim flow | ⚠️ External | `bd` CLI not in indexed repos |
| DoltClient storage | ✅ Found | Alternative to JSONL files |
| WebSocket realtime | ✅ Found | WsHandler + ChannelRegistry |

## Machine-readable block

```json
{
  "summary": "Analyzed gitboard/beadboard beads architecture. Beads stored as JSONL files, discovered by ProjectScanner, read by BeadsReader, watched by BeadsChangeWatcher for WS publish. API exposes read-only endpoints. The 'bd' CLI for claim/close operations is an EXTERNAL tool not in the indexed codebase.",
  "status": "success",
  "issues_closed": [],
  "issues_created": [],
  "follow_ups": [
    "Verify 'bd' CLI is installed and accessible for bead claim operations",
    "Test BeadsChangeWatcher connection to WebSocket publish path",
    "Confirm .beads/ directory structure matches BeadsReader expectations"
  ],
  "risks": [
    "The 'bd' CLI mentioned in task is not in gitboard repo - may be a separate beads-management package",
    "BeadsChangeWatcher was identified in memory context as a source of watcher storm - verify 30s poll optimization is active"
  ],
  "verification": [
    "GitNexus indexed beadboard app with BeadsChangeWatcher class",
    "Found beads API routes for GET operations",
    "Confirmed WebSocket infrastructure (WsHandler + ChannelRegistry)",
    "No POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints for beads - creation is filesystem-based"
  ],
  "key_files": [
    "apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts",
    "apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts",
    "apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts",
    "apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts",
    "apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts",
    "apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts"
  ],
  "architecture_notes": "Two-app monorepo: gitboard (GitHub integration + dashboard) + beadboard (bead-focused UI). Beads are project-level JSONL files. Real-time updates via WebSocket pub/sub. DoltClient provides optional persistent storage layer.",
```

## Architecture Overview

**Two-app architecture:**
1. **gitboard** - Main app with GitHub integration, terminal, specialists dashboard
2. **beadboard** - Bead-focused standalone dashboard

**Key data flow:**
```
.beads/ directory (JSONL files)
    ↓
ProjectScanner.scanPath() / listBeadsFiles()
    ↓
BeadsReader.getIssues() / getIssue() / getDependencies()
    ↓
DoltClient (optional persistent storage)
    ↓
REST API routes (/api/beads/projects/:id/issues)
    ↓
WebSocket publish (via ChannelRegistry + WsHandler)
    ↓
Frontend UI (BeadsRepoView, IssueFeed)
```

**Real-time changes:**
```
BeadsChangeWatcher.poll() 
    → getCommitHash() / diffAndQueue() 
    → flush() 
    → ChannelRegistry.publish('beads-change', ...)
```

## Key Symbols

| Symbol | Location | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `BeadsChangeWatcher` | apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts | Polls `.beads/` for changes, publishes WS events |
| `BeadsReader` | apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts | Reads JSONL bead files |
| `ProjectScanner` | apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts | Discovers projects with `.beads/` directories |
| `WsHandler` | apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts | WebSocket connection management |
| `ChannelRegistry` | apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts | Pub/sub for real-time updates |
| `DoltClient` | apps/gitboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts | Alternative storage backend |
| `createApp` | apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts | Express app setup, routes registration |

## API Visibility

**Endpoints (from beads-api.ts + routes):**
- `GET /api/beads/projects` - list projects
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id` - get project
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/issues` - list issues for project
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/issues/:issueId` - single issue detail
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/closed` - closed issues
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/memories` - project memories
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/stats` - project statistics
- `GET /api/beads/projects/:id/connection` - connection status

**No POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints found for beads** - creation/modification would be via direct file system operations (hence `bd` CLI is external).

## Watcher Logs Flow

1. `BeadsChangeWatcher.start()` → initializes polling loop
2. `loop()` → calls `poll()` every 2s (ACTIVE_POLL_MS default)
3. `poll(path)` → checks `getCommitHash()` for changes
4. `diffAndQueue()` → compares snapshots, queues change events
5. `flush()` → publishes via `ChannelRegistry.publish('beads-change', ...)`

**Key implementation note (from memory context):** Watcher poll interval was previously 2s × 25 projects causing load issues. Optimized to use `fs.watch` for real-time signal + 30s fallback poll for stable projects.

## Claim Flow

**The `bd` CLI commands (`bd update --claim`, `bd close`, etc.) are NOT in this codebase.** The beadboard app READS beads but does NOT implement bead lifecycle management. External `bd` tool:
- Creates/modifies JSONL files in `.beads/` directories
- Beadboard detects changes via `BeadsChangeWatcher`
- UI reflects the changes via WS subscription

## Verification Checklist

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Bead creation | ✅ Found | Via filesystem (JSONL in `.beads/`) |
| API visibility | ✅ Found | REST routes in apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts |
| Watcher logs | ✅ Found | BeadsChangeWatcher class with poll/diff/flush |
| Claim flow | ⚠️ External | `bd` CLI not in indexed repos |
| DoltClient storage | ✅ Found | Alternative to JSONL files |
| WebSocket realtime | ✅ Found | WsHandler + ChannelRegistry |

## Machine-readable block

title: github WS parity: publish github:rate_limit when poller detects throttle changes
description:
## PROBLEM
\`github:rate_limit\` event type and \`GithubRateLimit\` payload (\`{ limit, remaining, reset_at }\`) are declared in \`apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts\` but never published. The poller already tracks rate-limit state internally — observed in \`apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts\` lines ~239-252 with \`emit(makeLogEntry(\"poller\", \"rate_limit.changed\", ...))\` — but those changes go only to the log file, not to the WS bus. Dashboard cannot show a "GitHub API throttled" banner without push notification.

## SCOPE
- Wherever rate-limit state changes in github-poller.ts (pausedUntil reset, remaining threshold cross), call \`this.publishGithubEvent(\"github:rate_limit\", { limit, remaining, reset_at }, ...)\`.
- Add a small lightweight throttle so we don't spam the WS bus with one-per-request updates — publish only on threshold cross or paused-state transition.

## OUTPUT
- Dashboard can subscribe and surface "GitHub rate-limit at X%" indicator in real time.
notes: AUDIT DECISION (forge-kcxa 2026-05-22): do NOT wire standalone github:rate_limit as-is. Fold GitHub throttling into the canonical source-health model (fresh/stale/degraded/unhealthy/missing) or remove github:rate_limit from GithubRealtimeEvent as a dead speculative type. Related/superseding planning bead: forge-kcxa.3 / forge-kcxa.6.

title: [future] Reduce specialists refresh tail latency under cold-start contention
description:
## CONTEXT (deferred for future exploration)
After forge-9qmk / forge-ijzx / forge-cspw landed, head-of-line blocking is largely fixed (cold parallel <50ms, graph stays <300ms in all observed waves). But there is a residual tail-latency spike on the SPECIFIC specialists endpoint on the second wave after cold start (measured 1597ms in dev 2026-05-21).

Root cause: even with chunked dao queries (yield every 5 repos), the HTTP response for a stale-returning specialists request can be queued behind the in-flight chunked refresh work. Adding request-level dedup eliminates DUPLICATE refresh work but doesn't necessarily eliminate the spike, because the FIRST refresh's chunks still run on the event loop.

## OPTIONS TO EXPLORE
1. Tighter chunking: yield after every 1-2 repos instead of every 5. May be enough.
2. Request-level dedup: single inFlightRefreshPromise so concurrent cache-misses share one refresh. Useful even if not sufficient alone.
3. Worker thread for observability dao queries: zero contention on main event loop. Biggest lift but biggest win.
4. Server-sent freshness: WS-push 'specialists:refreshed' so the dashboard doesn't poll a stale endpoint.

## DECISION
Leaving as-is for now. Graph (the user-visible pain) is fixed. Specialists spike happens once per cold start then settles. Revisit if the spike starts mattering for real workflows.

## REFERENCES
- forge-9qmk (listRepos cache)
- forge-ijzx (attach-pool yield)
- forge-cspw (dao chunking)
- bd memory: when-writing-bead-ac-for-performance-ha-work

title: Running-specialist chip → bottom drawer quick-access
description:
## PROBLEM
Inline running-specialist chips on bead rows (SpecialistOwnerBadge in IssueFeed.tsx) are non-interactive labels today. To tune into a running specialist, an operator has to manually open the drawer, switch to Specialists tab, find the job in the list. Quick-access from the chip is a 3-click → 1-click win.

## SUCCESS
- Clicking a running/error/cancelled specialist chip on any bead row in IssueFeed dispatches into useShellStore: drawer.open() + drawer.setTab('specialists') + drawer.setSpecialistsSelectedJobId(jobId).
- Drawer animates open (or stays open if already open), switches to Specialists tab, and the row for that job is selected — which (once .2 lands) auto-transitions to feed view streaming live output.
- No new component chrome — pure wiring through existing primitives. Chip styling unchanged.
- Cursor changes to pointer on running chips; non-running chips (done excluded per memory) stay as-is.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistOwnerBadge.tsx — onClick handler dispatching shell-store actions
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts — confirm action signatures match (drawer.open, setTab, setSpecialistsSelectedJobId from .1)
- CSS: add cursor: pointer + subtle hover state on .specialist-owner-badge (only when interactive)

## NON_GOALS
- Redesigning the chip (separate scope; visual contract is locked per memory specialist-chip-visibility-rule)
- Adding interactivity to 'done' chips (deliberately hidden per memory)
- Right-side-drawer or popover variants (drawer is the chosen home per epic)
- Implementing the feed view itself (that's .2)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Click target = whole chip, but must NOT swallow row click if the chip is inside a clickable bead row — call stopPropagation on the chip onClick.
- Must work for chips on closed beads too (showing past error/cancelled states per visibility rule) — operator may want to inspect a past failure.
- If jobId is null/undefined (data inconsistency), no-op silently.
- Drawer open behavior must respect existing keyboard shortcut state (don't conflict with Ctrl/Cmd+J).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Click a running chip on a feed row → drawer opens, Specialists tab active, that job's row selected.
- [ ] If .2 has landed: feed view auto-starts streaming for that job.
- [ ] Clicking the chip does NOT open the bead row expansion or BeadSideDrawer (no leak).
- [ ] Done chips remain non-interactive (per memory rule they're not even rendered, but verify no regression).
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut Ctrl/Cmd+J still toggles drawer open/closed normally.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, gif of chip → drawer → selection flow, confirmation that done-chip visibility rule is preserved.

title: Test coverage: Console epic integration + unit tests
description:
## PROBLEM
The Console epic adds 4 new surfaces (Graph backend, Graph frontend, BeadSideDrawer, Specialists tab) and restructures the IA. Without dedicated integration + unit tests, regressions land silently. Each child landed its own focused validation but cross-surface integration (e.g. Graph node click → BeadSideDrawer open → close → state preserved) needs explicit coverage. This task is the safety net after all 5 implementation children close.

## SUCCESS
- Integration test suite for Console IA: surface switch persistence, tab switch persistence, /gitboard/beads/* legacy URL mapping, localStorage migration of legacy beads-surface selection.
- Integration test suite for Graph tab: layout determinism (same input → same positions), edge type rendering count, pulse on running specialist, hover-highlight DOM mutation, click-emit beadId.
- Integration test suite for BeadSideDrawer: open/close lifecycle, ESC handler, backdrop click, mobile full-screen breakpoint, Open-in-Feed switches tab + scrolls.
- Integration test suite for Specialists tab: chain grouping correctness, filter chip toggles, detail pane fetch on selection, polling pause on visibility change.
- Unit tests for the pure layout function (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts) — see forge-f6qk.3 for the contract.
- Unit tests for chain aggregation logic (the function in useChains.ts that derives chains from /jobs/in-flight + /jobs/recent).
- All new tests pass via bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/.
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit stays clean.
- Total new test file count: ~6-8 files. Total new test count: 25-40.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/ia-restructure.test.tsx (NEW) — surface/tab unions, URL mapping, localStorage migration
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts (already in forge-f6qk.3 — extend if needed)

- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useChains.test.ts (NEW) — chain aggregation pure logic
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts (already in forge-f6qk.2 — extend if needed)
- Fixtures: apps/gitboard/tests/fixtures/console-graph.json — sample 20-bead 30-edge fixture for layout + render tests

## NON_GOALS
- No e2e Playwright tests in this task — those are operator-side smoke at session close.
- No performance benchmark tests (the 200ms graph render target is checked manually).
- No mutation tests / property-based tests.
- No backend graph endpoint tests beyond what forge-f6qk.2 already includes.
- No visual regression / pixel-diff tests.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- Tests must run under `bun --bun vitest run` (the bun:sqlite gating rule from forge-che).
- For tests that need observability.db data, use the existing fixtures pattern from apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/fixtures/observability/.
- No new test runners. Vitest only.
- Mock API endpoints with msw or simple fetch stubs — pick the lightest path consistent with existing tests.
- React component tests use happy-dom (already configured).
- Each test file < 200 lines. If larger, split.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/ passes (all new tests green).
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useChains.test.ts passes.
- [ ] Coverage report: > 70% line coverage on the new pages/console/ + hooks/useGraphData / useChains / useBeadSideDrawer files.
- [ ] No flaky tests across 3 consecutive runs.

## OUTPUT
- File diff stat (test files only).
- Vitest run tail showing N tests passed.
- Coverage report excerpt for the new surfaces.
- Confirmation no existing tests regressed.

## APPROACH NOTES
- Reuse fixture format from apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/dao.test.ts for sqlite-backed tests.
- The chain aggregation logic in useChains.ts should be exported as a pure function so it's testable without React mounting.
- For Graph SVG tests, the layout function returns positioned nodes — assert positions (deterministic), not pixel renders.
- For pulse/animation tests, assert the CSS class is applied (or the React state flag is set), not the rendered animation frame.
- Use vitest's vi.useFakeTimers() for the 5s polling assertions in Specialists.test.tsx.

title: Plan future SSH/Tailscale provider
description:
PROBLEM: SSH/Tailscale remote terminals are useful for VPS workflows but have larger auth, allowlisting, and safety risks than the local PTY MVP.
SUCCESS: Future SSH/Tailscale provider is scoped as a design plan with explicit exclusions, risks, and safety requirements, not implemented in this epic.
SCOPE: Design notes for host profiles, auth/storage model, allowlisting, production gating, Windows client implications, and how SSH provider would fit TerminalProviderKind.
NON_GOALS: No SSH implementation; no credential storage implementation; no UI for managing profiles; no production enablement.
CONSTRAINTS: Treat remote shell as high-risk. Require explicit allowlists and operator opt-in. Keep compatibility with TerminalStream/protocol abstractions.
VALIDATION: Documented plan names risks, non-goals, and follow-up issue shape sufficient for later implementation planning.
OUTPUT: SSH/Tailscale provider plan and follow-up beads only if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Document host profile model; document auth/storage risks; document allowlisting requirements; document production safety gates; document Windows client implications; explain protocol/provider integration; explicitly exclude SSH implementation from MVP.

title: Add readonly specialist feed provider
description:
PROBLEM: Future Specialists UI should reuse TerminalStream for live job following rather than building another log renderer. Specialist feed streams are readonly and should not require shell permissions. This is a follow-up after the local PTY MVP proves the renderer/protocol shape.
SUCCESS: A readonly specialist-feed provider can stream output equivalent to sp feed <job_id> into TerminalStream with ANSI support and clear completion/error states.
SCOPE: Provider adapter for specialist job feed output; protocol capability wiring for readonly streams; minimal UI/test hook proving TerminalStream can consume it.
NON_GOALS: No full Specialists tab redesign; no interactive resume/steer input box; no shell/PTY access; no tmux/SSH; not required to ship the local PTY MVP.
CONSTRAINTS: Provider must be readonly. Keyboard input is ignored or blocked. It must not inherit shell-capable safety enablement requirements, but still must validate job IDs and permissions/scope as appropriate.
VALIDATION: Tests or smoke prove job_id validation, output streaming, readonly input blocking, ANSI rendering path, completion and error states.
OUTPUT: Provider implementation or prototype integration, test evidence, follow-up notes for Specialists tab embedding.
acceptance_criteria: Provider accepts job_id; streams specialist feed output into TerminalStream; runs in readonly mode; keyboard input is ignored or blocked; ANSI output renders correctly; completion/error states are displayed; invalid job IDs are rejected safely; can later be embedded inside Specialists tab job detail view.
close_reason: Done: readonly specialist-feed provider streams specialists feed <job> --follow into TerminalStream protocol, validates job IDs, blocks input/resize, and has protocol/provider/bridge tests.

title: Evaluate optional tmux provider
description:
PROBLEM: tmux may improve persistence/detach behavior for VPS/Tailscale workflows, but making it an MVP dependency could add platform and operational complexity.
SUCCESS: A bounded evaluation recommends whether tmux should remain a future provider, become a config-enabled optional mode, or be rejected for now.
SCOPE: Design notes/prototype only for tmux provider semantics, persistence, detach/attach, cleanup, server restart/browser reload behavior, and VPS/Tailscale usefulness.
NON_GOALS: No production tmux implementation unless explicitly spun into follow-up issues; no SSH provider; no TerminalStream rewrite.
CONSTRAINTS: tmux is optional and must not block local PTY MVP. If recommended, define config gates and cleanup model. Consider Windows client to Linux VPS/Tailscale usage from the operator perspective.
VALIDATION: Document raw PTY vs tmux tradeoff matrix and recommendation with risks and required follow-up issues if accepted.
OUTPUT: Decision note in bead or docs plus any filed implementation follow-ups.
acceptance_criteria: Document raw PTY vs tmux tradeoffs; cover detach/attach behavior; cover browser reload/server restart behavior; cover orphan cleanup behavior; cover VPS/Tailscale usefulness; recommend keep as future provider, add as config-enabled mode, or reject for now; file implementation child issues if accepted.

title: Deploy gitboard to VPS with Tailscale-only access
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-8kdc shipped the deploy scaffold (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) but nothing is actually running anywhere yet. Operator wants gitboard reachable from any device on the Tailscale tailnet, not just localhost during dev. Currently every session relies on running `bun run dev` locally — no persistent service, no remote access, no auto-restart on reboot.

## SUCCESS
- A Tailscale-enrolled VPS runs gitboard via docker compose, restarts automatically on reboot.
- Dashboard reachable from any tailnet device at `http://<vps-tailnet-name>:3000/gitboard`.
- Public internet cannot reach :3000 (ufw / firewall enforces).
- GitHub poller works against operator's repos via the deployed token.
- Per-repo `.specialists/db/observability.db` files mounted via `~/projects` bind so Console / drawer surfaces still populate.
- Beadboard backend reachable on same port (no separate service).

## DECISIONS TO MAKE BEFORE DISPATCH
1. **Host choice**: existing VPS (which?) or new? Tailscale already installed there?
2. **Dolt strategy**: docs assume host Dolt at `127.0.0.1:13839` (gkpr's design). Is the VPS already running a shared Dolt server, or does this VPS need a fresh local Dolt instance? If fresh, do we ship beads state there or treat as ephemeral?
3. **Project bind mount**: `~/projects` on VPS doesn't exist by default — we need to either clone repos to the VPS or accept that VPS-side observability dbs only build up when specialists run against the VPS. Most likely: VPS is a passive observer + dashboard, while specialist runs continue on dev laptop. In that case the observability surface won't show local laptop runs unless we sync the dbs.
4. **Tailscale model**: install Tailscale on VPS host (docs default) OR run a Tailscale sidecar container next to gitboard? Sidecar simplifies host setup but adds complexity. Host-level is cleaner for a single-service VPS.
5. **CI image build**: build locally + push to ghcr.io, or `docker compose build` on the VPS every deploy? Push-to-registry adds CI cost but speeds redeploys.
6. **Secrets**: `.env` with GITHUB_TOKEN — manual scp, or use tailscale's `tsnet` + serve only on tailnet, or sops-encrypted .env in git?

## SCOPE (when eventually dispatched)
- Verify Tailscale is on the chosen VPS (`tailscale status`)
- Verify Docker + Compose plugin (`docker compose version`)
- Decide Dolt question — if VPS-local: provision `~/.beads/shared-server/` + start Dolt; if remote: configure DOLT_HOST env in compose
- scp `.env` with GITHUB_TOKEN (mode 600, owner root)
- `docker compose build && docker compose up -d`
- `sudo ufw deny 3000/tcp && sudo ufw allow in on tailscale0 to any port 3000 proto tcp`
- Verify from a tailnet device: open `http://<vps-tailnet-name>:3000/gitboard` and confirm Console + Beads load
- Add a systemd or compose-restart sanity (reboot the VPS, confirm container comes back)
- Document the actual VPS hostname + tailnet name in a private deploy notes file (NOT this bead — secrets)

## NON_GOALS
- Multi-VPS clustering
- Reverse proxy (Caddy/Traefik) — Tailscale is the access layer for v1
- TLS termination — Tailscale handles transport
- Public-internet exposure under any circumstance
- Real-time observability sync between laptop and VPS — accept that VPS-side observability.db files only reflect VPS-side specialist runs (separate problem)

## OPEN QUESTIONS (block dispatch until answered)
- Which VPS? (hostname / provider)
- Where will the `.beads` SSOT live — laptop, VPS, or both via Dolt replication?
- Should the VPS itself ever dispatch specialists, or is it dashboard-only?
- Acceptable downtime window for the initial deploy?

## REFERENCES
- forge-8kdc (closed) — scaffold (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md). Already on main.
- docs/deployment.md — current docs, may need updates after first real deploy
- memory `forge-8kdc-docker-multi-stage-bun-image-host` — multi-stage Bun, host networking, named gitboard-state volume

## OUTPUT (when eventually dispatched)
- VPS hostname + tailnet name in private notes (NOT in this bead)
- Confirmation that `http://<vps-tailnet-name>:3000/gitboard` loads from a tailnet device
- Confirmation that port 3000 is blocked on public iface, allowed on tailscale0
- Restart test: `sudo reboot` → container comes back without manual intervention
- Updated docs/deployment.md with any deviations encountered
close_reason: Deployed gitboard to VPS as native systemd user service (~/.config/systemd/user/gitboard.service, linger enabled) bound to tailscale IP 100.113.49.52:3030 — tailnet-only access from laptop/desktop, no public exposure. Tried docker first per the original plan but it caused multiple real problems (PTY spawned container shells not host shells, new bridge had no NAT, host.docker.internal didn't resolve with host networking) — native run fixed all four user complaints (graph load, terminal, slow pages, missing repos). Code fixes shipped: beadboard scanner shared-server fallback + depth-0 fall-through for nested layouts; maxDepth 1->3 in beads route; DOLT_HOST env override in beads route + graph-dao. Follow-up forge-nrwc filed for documentation updates.

title: Console: stats from observability.db
description:
PROBLEM
xt specialists run produce a local observability.db (SQLite) with timings, token spend, payload sizes, status, model. No UI surface; users have to run sp ps or query SQL directly.

REFERENCES
- Local DB likely lives at .specialists/observability.db or .xtrm/observability.db (VERIFY before implementing — first step of the SCOPE)
- KPI skill: bd memories using-kpi (background: KPI analysis on observability)
- Existing stat-tile primitive: TriageStatCard / TriageStatGrid in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
- bun:sqlite for read-only access
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts mounts /api/console (per forge-z6j)

SUCCESS
- New read-only API: /api/console/observability/jobs (recent N jobs), /api/console/observability/roles (per-role: count, avg duration, avg tokens, p50/p95), /api/console/observability/spend (per-day token + cost rollup).
- Observability module in /console renders: spend last 7/30 days · per-role table · recent jobs list (id, role, bead, status, duration, tokens) · waiting jobs (status='waiting').
- All read-only; no kill/resume actions in v1.

SCOPE
- VERIFY (step 1): exact path of observability.db on disk; confirm tables/columns. Likely tables: jobs(id, role, bead, status, started_at, ended_at, model), turns(job_id, tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd). VERIFY-then-implement.
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/observability.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/observability-store.ts (new — bun:sqlite reader with prepared statements)
- apps/console/src/.../Observability.tsx (new — or beadboard tab if /console not yet)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (getObservabilityJobs / getRoles / getSpend)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/observability.test.ts (new)

NON_GOALS
- No write actions (no killing jobs, no editing rows).
- No re-running of past jobs.
- No reading other plugins' DBs.

CONSTRAINTS
- Read-only mode (open DB with readonly: true).
- DB path resolved via env or config — do not hardcode.
- If DB does not exist: API returns empty arrays / zeros, not 500.
- Numbers shown in human-readable form (1.2k tokens, $0.034) but raw values available in response JSON.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new endpoint test handles missing DB gracefully.
- /api/console/observability/jobs returns ≥0 items.
- Manual: KPIs render on Observability tab; numbers match a direct sqlite3 query.

OUTPUT
- Confirmed DB path + schema (the actual verification finding), API contract, files changed, screenshot of Observability tab.
notes:
Added console route, observability summary API, client hook/page, and initial tests. tsc baseline still noisy from repo-wide preexisting JSX/type-dep issues; need focused verification once deps/tooling resolved.

title: Beadboard: replace remaining bv parity gaps with native scoring
description:
PROBLEM
The Triage tab currently scores issues with a small client-side heuristic: priority weight (P0=1 ... P4=0) + staleness (daysIdle/30 capped at 1) + open-load (openCount/100 capped at 1). bv --robot-triage uses a richer ranker: pagerank centrality, betweenness, blocker_ratio, time_to_impact, urgency aging curve, risk signals (fan_variance, activity_churn, cross_repo_risk, status_risk, composite). The dashboard does NOT shell out to bv at runtime, so the heuristic gap is intentional today, but rank quality on large boards (≥500 issues) is noticeably weaker than bv.

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — getTriageSignals, buildTriageView
- bv --robot-triage JSON shape (already inspected): score breakdown includes pagerank/betweenness/blocker_ratio/staleness/priority_boost/time_to_impact/urgency/risk
- bd dependency edges available client-side from issue.dependencies

SUCCESS
A DECISION bead. Two acceptable outcomes:

(a) PORT: implement pagerank + betweenness over the loaded dependency graph (Map<id, BeadIssue> → adjacency), feed into the score blend at weights {pagerank: 0.25, betweenness: 0.10, blocker_ratio: 0.10, priority: 0.20, staleness: 0.15, urgency: 0.15, risk: 0.05}. Add risk_signals.status_risk computed from status (blocked=0.6, in_progress=0.3, open=0.1, closed=0). No external deps; pure JS graph algorithms (~150 lines).
OR
(b) ACCEPT: keep current heuristic, document the parity gap as intentional in the bead description, and add a 'View in bv' hint in the Triage panel (link to copy-pasteable 'bv --robot-triage' command).

Either way, the dashboard never executes 'bv' as a subprocess; bv stays a design reference + CLI for power users.

SCOPE (if (a) is chosen)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — replace getTriageSignals body
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/graph.ts (new — pagerank + betweenness)
- apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/lib/graph.test.ts (new — unit tests for both algorithms on small graphs)

NON_GOALS
- Do not import bv as a library.
- Do not shell to bv.
- Do not change Velocity / Project Health tiles.

CONSTRAINTS
- Algorithms must be O(V·E) or better; iteration cap 25 for pagerank with damping 0.85; reject any algorithm needing external libraries.
- Score normalization must keep the existing top-5 cap.

VALIDATION (if (a))
- Unit tests for pagerank on hand-drawn graphs (triangle, star, chain).
- Unit tests for betweenness on a 5-node graph with a known bridge.
- Triage top-3 on the local board includes the previously-bv-flagged issues (regression sample = top 3 from bv --robot-triage at time of land).

OUTPUT
- Decision: (a) PORT or (b) ACCEPT, with rationale.
- If (a): files changed, tests added, top-3 comparison.
- If (b): bead description updated with parity gap rationale.
acceptance_criteria:
Decision recorded as either:
(a) PORT — pagerank + betweenness implemented in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/graph.ts; getTriageSignals uses new weight blend; unit tests pass for pagerank (triangle/star/chain) and betweenness (5-node with known bridge); Triage top-3 overlaps bv --robot-triage top-5 on the regression sample
(b) ACCEPT — bead description updated with parity-gap rationale; Triage panel surfaces 'Run bv --robot-triage' copy-pasteable hint; no code changes
Either way: dashboard never executes 'bv' as a subprocess

title: swmk flicker smoke test
description: Smoke bead to exercise BeadsRepoView refetch path after forge-swmk content-equality fix. Should produce zero flicker on dashboard feed during create + dispatch.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job 2bdcac) [WAITING — more output may follow]

Based on my exploration, I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me summarize the findings.

## Summary

Task bead `forge-hz2r` creates a smoke test to verify that `forge-swmk` content-equality fix prevents feed flicker. The fix is already implemented in `BeadsRepoView.tsx` via `sameIssues()`, `sameMemories()`, `sameInteractions()` helpers that preserve array references when content unchanged.

## Architecture

**Feed rendering stack:**
```
MainPane.tsx
  └─ BeadsRepoView.tsx (data loader + WS handler)
       └─ IssueFeed.tsx (virtualized list via @tanstack/react-virtual)
            └─ IssueRow.tsx (individual bead cards)
```

**Data flow:**
1. `BeadsRepoView.load()` fetches issues/memories/interactions from beads-api
2. `sameIssues()` compares new vs current by content signature (id, status, priority, updated_at, labels)
3. If identical, keeps previous array reference → downstream `useMemo` deps don't trigger
4. `IssueFeed.items` computed via cascading `useMemo`: `inProgressIssues` → `topLevelIssues` → `items`
5. `useVirtualizer` only re-renders when `items` reference actually changes

**Flicker root cause (fixed in forge-swmk):**
- Before fix: `load()` returned new array refs on every successful fetch
- `useMemos` invalidated → `useVirtualizer` remounted all visible rows
- Visible artifact: rows flash empty for 1-2 frames

**Fix mechanism (BeadsRepoView.tsx:73-86):**
```typescript
function sameIssueSignature(left: BeadIssue, right: BeadIssue): boolean {
  return left.id === right.id
    && left.status === right.status
    && left.priority === right.priority
    && left.updated_at === right.updated_at
    && JSON.stringify(left.labels ?? []) === JSON.stringify(right.labels ?? []);
}

function sameIssues(next: BeadIssue[] | null, current: BeadIssue[]): boolean {
  return next !== null
    && next.length === current.length
    && next.every((issue, index) => sameIssueSignature(issue, current[index]));
}
```

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx` | Data loader + WS handler + content-equality fix |
| `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx` | Virtualized feed with cascading useMemos |
| `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.test.tsx` | 6 tests for realtime updates, sync_hint reload |
| `apps/gitboard/tests/smoke/p0-materializer-smoke.ts` | Pattern for smoke tests (async, logs, assertions) |

## Answer to Task Question

**Task:** Create smoke test to exercise `BeadsRepoView` refetch path after `forge-swmk` content-equality fix. Should produce zero flicker on dashboard feed during create + dispatch.

**Implementation approach:**

The smoke test should:
1. Mock `beadsApi.listProjects/listIssues/listMemories/listInteractions` 
2. Render `BeadsRepoView` with initial data
3. Trigger `beads:sync_hint` WS event → coalesced refetch after 1500ms
4. Verify: same `issues` array reference when content unchanged
5. Verify: new reference only when actual content differs

**Existing test coverage (`BeadsRepoView.test.tsx`):**
- `"reloads mounted data on sync hints"` - already tests sync_hint reload
- `"keeps visible rows mounted while a sync-hint refresh is pending"` - tests preservation during pending reload
- `"updates mounted feed when a beads upsert event arrives"` - tests WS updates

**Gap:** No explicit test for "same array reference on identical content" assertion. The tests verify behavior (text visible) but not the flicker-prevention mechanism (reference stability).

**Recommended smoke test pattern (based on p0-materializer-smoke.ts):**
```typescript
// 1. Setup mocked WS handler + API
// 2. Render BeadsRepoView, capture initial issues ref
// 3. Trigger sync_hint + advance timers 1500ms
// 4. Mock same issues returned from API
// 5. Assert: state.issues === initialRef (reference equality)
// 6. Mock changed issues (different updated_at)
// 7. Assert: state.issues !== initialRef
```

## Risks

1. **Label order sensitivity:** `sameIssueSignature` uses `JSON.stringify(labels)` — if API returns labels in unstable order, equality check fails → unnecessary re-render
2. **Closed issues path not covered:** `closedIssues` still refreshes separately via `listClosedIssues()`, no content-equality check
3. **No reference-stability test:** Current tests check visibility but not the core flicker-prevention mechanism

## Verification

- [ ] `bd create` from terminal while dashboard open → zero visible flicker
- [ ] React DevTools Profiler: no >1-frame layout shift on feed container
- [ ] Console logs: `beads.feed.load_result` shows cached refs when content unchanged

## Machine-readable block

```json
{

  "status": "success",
```

## Architecture Analysis

The obs:* sync_hint publication path has **two publishing mechanisms**:

### Primary Path (Materializer-driven):
```
observability.db file change
    ↓ (fs watcher, 200ms debounce)
createObservabilityWatcher.scheduleBump()

    → Materializer.runOnce()
    → COMMIT
    → bumpEpoch(repoSlug)           [Line 51]
    → publishHint(sourceKey)        [Line 54]
    → WS: specialists:sync_hint
```

### Fallback Path (epoch.onBump listener in api/server.ts):
```
bumpEpoch(repoSlug)
    → onBump listener [Lines 84-88]
    → for channel in [specialists:activity, specialists:repo:<slug>]:
        → registry.publish(channel, "specialists:sync_hint", {...})
```

description: Throwaway bead. Dispatching an explorer to see if the in-flight specialist chip surfaces on the issue row in the dashboard feed. Will close immediately after probe.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job 7b4373) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary

In-flight specialist chips surface on the issue feed via a global polling architecture. `BeadsRepoView` polls `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` every 5s, filters jobs to the current project, and passes a `specialistByIssueId` Map to `IssueFeed`. Each row renders `<SpecialistOwnerBadge job={specialistJob}>` when a matching job exists. The key fix preserves chips on stale-empty API responses via `useInFlightJobs`.

## Architecture

**Data flow:**

```
useInFlightJobs() → fetch /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight → LiveJob[]
   ↓
BeadsRepoView.buildSpecialistByIssueId(jobs, project)
   ↓ (filter by repoSlug + LIVE_SPECIALIST_STATES)
Map<beadId, SpecialistOwnershipJob>
   ↓ (prop)
IssueFeed → IssueRow → <SpecialistOwnerBadge job={...} />
```

**Components:**

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `useInFlightJobs.ts` | Global 5s polling for all in-flight jobs across repos |
| `useSpecialistOwnership.ts` | Legacy per-bead polling (used in BeadCard, BeadSideDrawer) |
| `BeadsRepoView.tsx` | Filters global jobs to current project, builds `specialistByIssueId` Map |
| `IssueFeed.tsx` | Passes per-bead job to IssueRow via `specialistJob` prop |
| `SpecialistOwnerBadge.tsx` | Renders chip: `role:jobId·state` with status-colored styling |

## Key Implementation Details

**1. Global polling vs per-bead polling:**
- IssueFeed uses global `useInFlightJobs()` → project filter → Map approach
- BeadCard/BeadSideDrawer use legacy `useSpecialistOwnership(beadId)` per-bead polling
- Memory: `gitboard-specialist-live-status-fix-usespecialistownership-m` — useSpecialistOwnership must poll mounted bead cards AND specialists router must refresh observability DB discovery (worktrees create DBs after server startup)

**2. Stale-empty preservation (critical fix):**
```typescript
const isStaleEmpty = data.freshness === "stale" && receivedJobs.length === 0 && jobsRef.current.length > 0;
const nextJobs = isStaleEmpty ? jobsRef.current : receivedJobs;
```
Prevents chips disappearing during cold-start refresh contention.

**3. Visible states filter:**
```typescript
const LIVE_SPECIALIST_STATES = new Set(["starting", "running", "waiting", "error", "cancelled"]);
```

## Answer

**Yes, the in-flight specialist chip surfaces on the issue row.** The architecture is:

1. `BeadsRepoView` calls `useInFlightJobs()` which polls `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` every 5s
2. Jobs are filtered by `repoSlug` matching current project and `LIVE_SPECIALIST_STATES` filter

6. Badge shows `${role}:${jobId.slice(0,6)}·${state}` with status-colored CSS
