ROOT CAUSES from .47 incident (do not repeat): BeadsParityHarness ran on 30s timer with FS scan + new DoltClient per project + 19k JSON.stringify objects per cycle no eviction. Materializer.writeIssues hard-coded substrate columns broke obs writes silently.

LESSON: never flip a backend gate ON in prod without 5+ min memory watch first. CHECKPOINT bead structure is right, I skipped it by going straight to systemctl restart.

WORKTREE/BRANCH STATE: clean. Only main worktree. runtime/pre-rollback-2026-05-24 tag preserved as safety net.

Reviewer PASS via sp resume doesn't always land in the structured 'compliance_verdict' field that sp finalize / sp merge read. Symptom: 'No reviewer with PASS compliance verdict found in chain X' on finalize. Fix: re-prompt the reviewer with explicit format request (e.g. '## Compliance Verdict\n- Verdict: **PASS**'); the reviewer re-emits with the structured marker and finalize then succeeds. Also: NEVER delete the worktree/branch before sp merge succeeds — if you do, the dangling commit is recoverable via 'git branch <name> <SHA>' from checkpoint commits in reflog.

gitboard specialist live status fix: useSpecialistOwnership must poll mounted bead cards, and specialists router default DAO must refresh observability DB discovery because worktree orchestrators can create/write .specialists/db/observability.db after server startup.

forge-221d landed: ProjectScanner.loadProject (in both apps/beadboard and apps/gitboard) now prefers the shared-server Dolt port over the stale 'port:' value in config.yaml when shared-server: true. Before: gitboard's config had port:13839 (dead) AND shared-server:true; ProjectScanner read 13839 first and the materializer's DoltClient connected to a nonexistent port. readFromDolt silently failed (catch→null) → readFromJsonl fallback served stale data with ~2min latency. After: shared-server port (3308) is used directly. Verified: bd close → substrate state='closed' in ~5s (matched create latency). Also: per-page Dolt logs from forge-eorh.72 moved from info→debug, added one-shot source log in snapshotHash() showing dolt|jsonl path per cycle.

forge-eorh.72 landed: beads-snapshot-source.readFromDolt now paginates Dolt with limit=1000, offset=page*1000, until page<1000, 10k safety cap. Verified prod 2026-05-26: specialists project went from 1000 (capped) → 2264 issues; xtrm-tools from 1000 → 1253. Per-page logs use emit(makeLogEntry('system', 'beads-snapshot', ...)) which goes to in-process log ring, not journalctl — for journalctl visibility, would need to also write to stdout. snapshotHash() naturally computes over full accumulated set since readSnapshot() returns the merged array.

forge-f6qk.6 console epic test coverage: 6 new test files (ia-restructure, Graph, BeadSideDrawer, Specialists, useChains, fixtures/console-graph.json) + 13 tests. Pattern: tests fork off origin/main but need post-integration state — workflow is push xt/integration branch, dispatch with --force-stale-base, executor first git fetch origin <branch> && git reset --hard origin/<branch>. Reviewer pitfall: diff source defaults to main; for branches reset to non-main, rebut with explicit base (git diff --name-only origin/<branch>..feature/<x>).

forge-7xu unified shell: TopBar [GitHub|Beads] switch + tabs (7 github / 5 beads) + Sidebar single-level repos with RepoIcon + MainPane per-(surface,tab) dispatch. All 5 beads panels live in BeadsRepoView.tsx (Kanban=KanbanBoard, Feed=IssueFeed, Triage=inline TriagePanel ported from beadboard App.tsx:411, Closed=inline list, Memories=inline list). GitHub README/CHANGELOG/Reports use ReadmeView/ChangelogView/ReportsView from RepoContentPanels.tsx. CSS class prefix .ide-* in globals.css. Black bg via .ide-shell.
Shell-provider gate review: require explicit evidence for specialist-feed permission separation; status/policy fields alone insufficient.

BeadsChangeWatcher fast-path (forge-3mdw): cache lastCommitHash per project; if cheap getCommitHash() matches last seen AND we have a previous snapshot, emit beads:source_health and return WITHOUT readSnapshot. Drops poll-tick cost from a full N+1 hydration (~600 queries for ~200 issues) to a single SELECT current_commit() (~2ms). DoltClient.getIssues + getClosedIssues use hydrateIssues(rows) which does 1 issues SELECT + 3 IN-clause batched queries (dependencies/issue_related/labels) instead of per-row sub-queries. issue_related/labels remain optional (try/catch swallow); they're absent from some Dolt schemas.

Observability metrics-dao token_trajectory_json: entries are {t, source/turn_index, token_usage: {input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, cache_read_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd}}. The token fields are NESTED under .token_usage, not top-level. Easy gotcha when summing tokens.

Design handoffs from Claude Design (Anthropic) use --surface-1/2/3 and --danger token names, but this repo's packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css uses --surface-primary/secondary/tertiary and --accent-red. When porting design selectors that reference --surface-1/2/3 or --danger, either rename the selector to use the repo's tokens OR add aliases at the top of tokens.css. Failure to do this silently breaks any element using those vars (background collapses to transparent). Source: forge-pq6 structural alignment broke beadboard until forge-qct alias commit.

Theme gotcha: gitboard's --accent-blue is defined ONCE in :root (#7ed7e5/#8ed2dc pale cyan) and NOT overridden in [data-theme=light]. Anything using it directly will wash out on the white background. PR#10 left this asymmetry. When building a new themed UI element that needs a vibrant accent on BOTH themes, define your own component-scoped token at :root + override in .ide-shell[data-theme='light'] (pattern: see --specialist-chip-accent in globals.css). Theme-aware tokens that ARE properly overridden: --text-muted, --text-secondary, --text-primary, --accent-dim, --accent-soft, --bg-hover.

Unified expanded-row card pattern: when .pr-row.is-expanded (or .issue-row.is-expanded), strip bottom radius+border on row and zero its margin-bottom; on the .pr-expanded-body (or .issue-expanded-body), strip top border+radius. Result: row+body render as a single card with full rounded outer corners only. Required because the row and body are separate DOM siblings — without this they look like two stacked cards.

Reuse existing .pr-body-text + .pr-rich-text CSS for any markdown body rendered via renderPrBodyText — those classes carry the white-space:pre-wrap, font-size, line-height baseline that browser defaults need to look right. Custom .release-body-text style produced unstyled markdown output (no h3/list/p spacing).

bd dep rule: an epic cannot block a task (bd dep add <epic-id> <task-id> errors with 'epics can only block other epics, not tasks'). Use --parent on bd create instead to nest tasks under an epic, OR use bd dep relate for non-blocking links between epic and task. Tasks can block tasks. Epics can block epics. Source: forge-qct epic linking session.

forge-5w9.7 beads port: 5 components copied from apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/* into apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/. Import rewrites: { api } → { beadsApi as api }; ../../lib/api.ts → ../../lib/beads-api.ts; api.getInteractions → api.listInteractions. Backend /api/beads/projects/{id}/issues/closed returns 404 for some Dolt-backed projects — BeadsRepoView catch-isolates each load so partial data still renders. ProjectRail NOT ported (Sidebar tree supersedes it). OpenPr type lives in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts (type-only export).

Drawer resize/maximize tuning: the store's setDrawerHeight clamp upper bound (window.innerHeight - OFFSET) MUST equal the component's MAXIMIZED_OFFSET, OR clicking maximize is a silent no-op (it asks for a height the store rejects). Standardized OFFSET=24 — leaves a 24px hairline at top while keeping topbar visible. Also: BottomDrawer.tsx MUST NOT carry its own MAX_HEIGHT constant — leads to silent drag failure when default open height exceeds the local cap (operator-reported 'drag does nothing' on viewport > 800px). Store is single source of truth for height bounds.

graph-dao readIssues used to fetch limit:1000 unfiltered from Dolt, then filter status at app level. On projects with >1000 closed issues (specialists: 65 open + 2071 closed) the open ones were silently truncated — graph showed ~7 of 65 expected open nodes. Always push status filter to SQL when the consumer only renders non-closed (forge-afac fix). Pattern applies to any 'fetch then filter' code that has a row cap below the realistic dataset size.

sp merge intermittently fails with 'Merge conflict while merging' even when git merge-tree shows zero conflicts. Root cause: bd auto-export races with sp merge's working-tree check, writing .beads/issues.jsonl mid-operation. .beads/issues.jsonl IS in sp merge's ignore list per --help, but the race still triggers git's local-changes-would-be-overwritten guard. Workaround: fall back to manual squash-merge per cherry-pick playbook — git reset --hard origin/main, git checkout HEAD -- .beads/, git merge --squash <feature-branch>, then commit with conventional message. Do not retry sp merge in a loop — it will keep failing the same way.

html-preview package lives at packages/html-preview as a standalone Bun/Hono server; run with bun run --filter @xtrm/html-preview start -- --root /home/dawid/dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8787, then expose privately via tailscale serve --bg 8787.

sp merge fails with conflict on .beads/issues.jsonl when the orchestrator session mutates main's bd state (create child beads, add deps, claim, close) while a specialist worktree is open. Worktree forked from old main + main advanced + both touched issues.jsonl = unresolvable merge. Skill's chain-noise-filter applies to manual cherry-pick flows, NOT to sp merge — sp merge bails. Mitigations: (a) batch all orchestrator bd writes before dispatching the next chain, (b) commit + push main's bd state between dispatches so worktrees fork from current state, or (c) fall back to manual cherry-pick with --strategy-option=ours on .beads/. Discovered swarm-wide on forge-3ue5.1 dispatch.

xtrm-console visual contract: bg #181818, single accent #8ed2dc, radius 0 (no pills), thin rgba(255,255,255,0.07–0.18) borders, no gradients, Inter+JetBrains Mono with mono restricted to IDs/SHAs/branch refs/diff numbers/file paths, @primer/octicons-react as the only icon family, no charts/heatmaps/sparklines. Source: design handoff gDsOk8IG1FcsM7ojcdFJNg.

DASHBOARD BUILD: changes to apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/** require 'bun run --cwd apps/gitboard build:dashboard' BEFORE restart — the service serves static dist/dashboard/gitboard/. apps/gitboard/src/api/** changes take effect on service restart alone (run from source via bun). Forgetting to rebuild leaves the old SPA bundle in place even though source is correct. Also: SWR refetch pattern matters — any 'catch(()=>[])' fallback in a refetch path that writes to React state will visibly empty the list on transient API failure. Use null sentinels and merge: 'newDataResult ?? current.data' instead, to keep stale-while-revalidate semantics.

Code-sanity specialist must be dispatched with --job <exec-job>, never standalone. Its registry entry is worktree:none, so a solo dispatch has no filesystem to read and returns 'BLOCKED — source not retrievable' (confirmed on forge-zgny job a937b8). Correct invocation: chain off an executor's worktree via 'sp run code-sanity --bead <id> --job <executor-job-id>'. For ad-hoc sanity passes without a prior executor, run a no-op executor first to provision a worktree, then attach code-sanity via --job.

forge-f6qk.5 specialists tab live cockpit: replaces .1 placeholder Specialists.tsx with two-pane layout (60/40 split, card list + structured detail pane). New pages/console/specialists/{ChainCard,ChainDetailPane,FilterChips}.tsx + hooks/useChains.ts + useChainDetail.ts. Polling pauses on visibilitychange. Open-bead handler uses dynamic-import attempt of useBeadSideDrawer with fallback to /gitboard/console/feed scroll-into-view via data-bead-id. Wrapped try/catch + TODO for direct import once .4 lands. Integration: at merge into xt/1ers, take --theirs on Specialists.tsx (.5 cockpit overrides .1 placeholder).

MATERIALIZER BUG: beads-adapter.normalizeIssue (line 157) was conflating status='closed' with deleted_at=closed_at — making the substrate query's tombstone filter hide ALL closed issues. Fixed in commit (post-13d9c4a, see forge-115m). The bd model: closed and deleted are different — closed issues stay visible (state='closed', deleted_at=null), only tombstoned-missing rows from writeFull's tombstoneMissing get deleted_at set. After fix + wipe + repopulate: every project's substrate count exactly matches bd stats Total Issues (verified 6/6 projects: market-data 706, gitboard 548, specialists 2269, xtrm-tools 1253, darth-feedor 348, terminalbeta 29).

github releases ingestion pattern (forge-nwdr): the github_releases table is now populated by pollReleases() inside the standard pollRepo() loop — separate from the user-events stream that previously bounded release visibility to ~300 events / ~90 days. Watermark is published_at (drafts have null published_at and are skipped). ETag-gated like pollPullRequests; max 5 pages * 100 = 500 releases per cycle per repo. Result: 239 releases across 13 repos vs the prior 2 ReleaseEvent rows in github_events. Publishes github:release.upsert on github:activity for new/changed rows. The same pattern applies to any future github entity we add: dedicated table + per-repo poll method + watermark+etag in github_repo_poll_state + publish under github:activity. Three github WS event types remain declared-but-unpublished: github:event.append, github:rate_limit, and (until this PR) github:release.upsert.

Wave-8 T3 Playwright deferred: forge-3ue5.13 not closed in v1 auto-mode run. Trigger condition: when repo-wide vitest infra (@vitejs/plugin-react workspace dep) is fixed, dispatch T3 executor with test-only scope discipline. Bead state preserved with notes.

Markdown table parsing: pipe-row + separator row (|---|---|) is the trigger. Reject early if next line isn't dashes-with-pipes pattern. parseTableCells strips leading/trailing | then splits on | and trims. Fenced code blocks: detect (```|~~~) + optional lang, consume lines until matching fence.

forge-eorh.71 landed: substrate_issues schema extension (priority/issue_type/owner/labels/related_ids/parent_id/closed_at/close_reason/notes) + /api/internal/substrate/schema endpoint + verbose materializer logging. E2E verified: terminalbeta and gitboard projects now return varied priorities (p=0-4) and types (bug/chore/epic/feature/task) instead of hardcoded p=2/task. Migration via ALTER TABLE guards in ensureSubstrateIssuesColumns. Eager bootstrap on service restart auto-repopulates new columns from Dolt without needing manual wipe — schema migration + materializer rerun is sufficient. Pattern for next schema extensions: add columns + indices in xtrm-store.ts ensureXxxColumns, extend MaterializedIssue type, expand normalizeIssue + writeIssues in beads-adapter, drop hardcoded defaults in substrate.ts queryIssues, restart service. No wipe needed since materializer overwrites on next cycle.

Live e2e proof for forge-3ue5 (gitboard ownership view): with sp jobs running in 2+ repos, /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns 6 rows tagged by repoSlug (gitboard, xtrm-tools), and InFlightRail renders them grouped by repo with elapsed time. Screenshot: apps/gitboard/.playwright-mcp/e2e-rail-live.png

bd swarm integrates with specialists-v3 as the orchestration loop: validate epic → create swarm molecule → poll 'bd swarm status --json' for Ready fronts → dispatch 'sp run executor --worktree --bead <id> --background' per ready issue, reviewer via --job <exec-job>. Replaces hand-rolled bd ready polling with epic-scoped, wave-grouped status. v3 bead contracts/job rules unchanged.
Beadboard kanban dossier markdown should strip raw HTML tags and whitelist link protocols before rendering inline.

CSS grid + position:absolute child gotcha: an absolutely-positioned child of a 'display: grid' container is REMOVED from grid flow, so its template column gets filled by the NEXT in-flow child. If your design has N grid-template-columns but you include a position:absolute decoration as one of the spans, you actually have N-1 in-flow children filling N columns — everything shifts left and columns visually misalign (e.g. id renders in the rail slot, title renders in the id slot). Two fixes: either drop the absolute span (use a ::before pseudo instead) or keep it in-flow with grid-column:1. Source: forge-pq6 beadboard rows where <span className='rail' /> with position:absolute caused id+title to overlap until forge-xsk.

Reviewer rebuttal pattern: when reviewer flags 'unmet: parameterized queries' but actual interpolation is only schema identifiers (table names, column aliases), rebut by citing SQLite's hard limitation that table names/aliases cannot be parameter-bound. The contract constraint about 'parameterize bead/chain/epic ids' specifically named user-data ids — schema identifiers from PRAGMA database_list and registry-controlled slugs are not in that category. Cite file:line of the ? placeholders and the db.prepare(query).all(...params) call. Rebuttal accepted on forge-3ue5.4 (81→94).

forge-eorh.11 graph xtrm path: route GET /api/console/graph stays read-only for xtrm DAO; protected POST /api/console/graph/invalidate uses isAllowedMutationRequest + source cooldown to trigger beads materialization. Public graph source_health must not expose materialization_state.last_error or source_key; keep last_status/last_success_at/age_seconds only.

Beadboard /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns BeadDependency entries with title='' (the reader/exporter doesn't denormalise dep titles). Clients must resolve dep titles by id from the loaded issues list rather than trusting dep.title. Same for dep.status — prefer issueById.get(dep.id)?.status. Source: forge-3k5 dependency tree fix.

forge-yy27 production-bug fix root causes: (1) useBeadsLive read projectId from envelope top-level instead of msg.data — live sync silently dead until unwrap. (2) test mock used url.includes('page=N') which COLLIDES with 'per_page=100' substring causing infinite loops in pagination tests — always use new URL(url).searchParams.get('page') for accurate page matching. (3) getIssues/getPrs in github-store default LIMIT=100 — pagination tests must pass {limit: 200} or higher. (4) vitest happy-dom is the canonical jsdom-like env in this repo (jsdom package not installed). Use @vitest-environment happy-dom docblock, not jsdom.

html-preview visual alignment pass: use inline Octicon SVGs sourced from @primer/octicons-react paths (BrowserIcon brand, FileCodeIcon document rows/tab, RepoIcon sidebar, SearchIcon field, Sun/Moon theme toggle), and match Gitboard's IDE shell palette/topbar/sidebar spacing.
Octicon consistency pass: replace decorative glyphs in dashboard meta lines with LinkExternalIcon/CheckIcon/ChevronDownIcon; keep design-preview svg diagram as non-icon exception.
Terminal protocol validator must enforce capabilities enum values, not string[] only; otherwise unsafe capabilities drift.

Beadboard kanban column-width inconsistency root cause: StatusColumn used minWidth:240/maxWidth:300 as flex children with default flex:0 1 auto, so flexbox sized each column based on internal content — short-titled columns settled at minWidth, longer-content columns grew toward maxWidth. Fix: flex:'1 1 0' + minWidth:260 (no maxWidth). Now all columns share parent space equally and minWidth only kicks in when total < parent width (triggers horizontal scroll instead of squashing).

forge-eorh.12 WsClient grace: keep zero-ref channels in the subscription map during the 2000ms grace window, but skip count===0 entries during reconnect replay; otherwise socket flaps resubscribe channels that are pending unsubscribe.

Specialist chip visibility rule (per operator design feedback): chip surfaces ACTIONABLE state only. Show running/starting/waiting (live in-flight) + error/cancelled (failure traceability — operator needs to know which specialist died). HIDE 'done' — a successful past run is history, not an actionable signal, and belongs in the per-bead dossier timeline (forge-4hmt). Don't bloat the feed with success badges. Rule lives in SpecialistOwnerBadgeForBead.VISIBLE_STATES + useSpecialistOwnership fallback chain (live → error → cancelled, never done).

BeadsRepoView refetch coalescing — the third and final consumer of beads:changes that needed the same trailing-timer pattern. Verifies the 1.5s REFETCH_COALESCE_MS as the right constant for all three hooks (useGraphData, useChains, BeadsRepoView). If a fourth WS subscriber is added that also triggers refetch, extracting a shared useCoalescedRefetch(channel, fn) helper becomes worth doing (see forge-d3ix description) — for three consumers it's still below the rule-of-three abstraction threshold.
bv triage score uses pagerank, staleness, priority_boost, time_to_impact, urgency, and risk; top picks can be mirrored client-side with open issue idle time, priority cost, and open load.
Density pass: shrink spacing by token-level padding/gap changes, not font size; keep row height via --row-height and topbar via --topbar-height.

Observability persistence note (per forge-gud9 design): specialist_jobs rows in observability.db are RETENTION-FRIENDLY — sp keeps closed/error rows after a chain finishes. To surface a per-bead specialist history view, the only new API surface needed is GET /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=…&include_history=1 returning all rows (live+closed) DESC by updated_at_ms. No new write path or migration needed; data is already there.
review-forge-hl1-partial-missing-chevrondownicon-import

Beads source health is Dolt-first: JSONL must be shown/logged as degraded backup only. /connection should classify missing config, dead pid, unreachable server, and query failures distinctly while still allowing fallback reads.

forge-qc0 layout invariant: ANY MainPane content that uses @tanstack/react-virtual (ActivityTimeline, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ReleaseTimeline) needs a SIZED parent. Required CSS chain: html/body/#root height:100%; .ide-shell height:100vh + overflow:hidden; .ide-body flex:1 + min-height:0; .ide-main flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:hidden; .ide-main > * flex:1 + min-height:0. Without this chain virtualizer reports container as zero-height and renders nothing. min-height:100vh on shell is NOT enough — must be height:100vh.

gitboard already mounts beadboard's /api/beads/* routes via apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6,35 (imports beadsRoutes from '../../../beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts'). Cross-package import. forge-5w9.9 deprecation MUST move beadboard's api/routes/beads.ts + core/beads-*.ts into apps/gitboard before deleting beadboard, or gitboard's beads endpoints break. beadboard backend on port 3010 is NOT a runtime dependency for gitboard — gitboard serves beads itself on its own port (3000).

Real-world sp observability.db quirks discovered during live e2e (forge-xu1h test): (a) the slug derivation MUST strip the '.specialists/db' suffix from observability.db's parent — basename naively gives 'db' for every repo. (b) sp's schema_version values vary wildly across repos (0, 11, 68, 84, 26997) — a strict COMPATIBLE_RANGE rejects everything. Best contract check is table-shape presence (specialist_jobs exists), not version number. (c) Real observability.db paths live at both <repo>/.specialists/db/observability.db (modern) AND <repo>/.specialists/observability.db (legacy specialists repo) — the scan walks recursively which handles both.

Dashboard beads sync_hint refreshes should preserve current rows/selection and only swap data after the replacement list resolves; active specialist chips should be derived from the global /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight feed rather than per-row polling so open/unclaimed beads still show running jobs without list flicker.

forge-8kdc docker: multi-stage Bun image, host network mode (Dolt at 127.0.0.1:13839), named docker volume gitboard-state mounted at /data, ~/projects bind-mounted read-only, GITHUB_TOKEN via .env file, Tailscale-only access pattern documented in docs/deployment.md including ufw lockdown commands.

forge-kcxa.3 source health SSOT: canonical dashboard health vocabulary is fresh|stale|degraded|unhealthy|missing in apps/gitboard/src/types/source-health.ts; route responses should preserve old freshness/status fields while adding source_health metadata. GitHub rate-limit should use github:source_health, not github:rate_limit.
review forge-m38 fail when mono appears on non-identifier UI text in globals

forge-2vf5 realtime validation 2026-05-22: confirmed systemic gaps beyond WS refcount: graph cold-cache can return stale-empty then fresh-empty for gitboard live smoke; default observability roots only ~/dev/* miss ~/projects/* specialist DBs; github:release.upsert is published but client ignores it; github:rate_limit is declared dead type.

sp dispatch silently hangs when .specialists/jobs/ has many stale dead-PID job dirs. Symptom: 'sp run' returns a job ID, tmux session is created, but bun process sleeps in 'sleeping' state with 0 trace.jsonl entries and no DB observability entry. Cure: 'sp clean --processes' to remove dead-PID jobs and corresponding stale worktrees (DESTRUCTIVE — also removes worktrees). Also auto-closes beads of stopped keep-alive executors. Auto-mode workflow: dispatch a few chains, then 'sp clean --processes --dry-run' to inspect, then 'sp clean --processes' between chains to keep state fresh. Cross-project pollution (other repos' specialists holding observability.db WAL locks) compounds the hang.

Test-bead executor scope discipline lesson from T1: when dispatching executor on a test-only bead, the contract MUST say 'test files + fixtures ONLY — never edit production code. If a test fails because production code is wrong, REPORT do not edit.' Without this hard rule, executor expands scope to make tests pass. Also: never tell executor 'git checkout origin/main -- <file>' unless the file exists on origin/main — when local main is ahead of origin (common in auto-mode), this command deletes the file. Use 'git checkout main -- <file>' for local refs.
